## 4160: driving out (id: 4160)

Driving out demons
------------------

### In Jesus Christ's ministry

**[MRK 1:34](https://ref.ly/Mark1:34)** *See also* [MAT 8:16](https://ref.ly/Matt8:16), [MAT 9:32–9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32-Matt9:33) parallel [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14); [MRK 1:39](https://ref.ly/Mark1:39); [LUK 13:32](https://ref.ly/Luke13:32)

### Jesus Christ accused of using satanic power to drive out demons

**[MAT 12:24–12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24-Matt12:28)** parallels [MRK 3:22–3:26](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22-Mark3:26), [LUK 11:15–11:20](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15-Luke11:20) *See also* [MAT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Matt9:34)

### Jesus Christ commissioned his disciples to drive out demons

**[MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1)** parallel [LUK 9:1](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1) *See also* [MAT 10:8](https://ref.ly/Matt10:8); [MRK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:15), [MRK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:13)

### The failure of the disciples to drive out demons

[MRK 9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:18)

### Others attempted to use Jesus Christ's authority

[LUK 9:49](https://ref.ly/Luke9:49); ; [ACT 19:13](https://ref.ly/Acts19:13)

### Driving out demons is no substitute for obedience to the will of God

[MAT 7:22](https://ref.ly/Matt7:22)

### Driving out demons and Satan as an aspect of Jesus Christ's overall ministry

**[JHN 12:31](https://ref.ly/John12:31)**

Driving out people
------------------

**[NUM 33:51–33:52](https://ref.ly/Num33:51-Num33:52)** *See also* [EXO 10:11](https://ref.ly/Exod10:11); [PSA 44:2](https://ref.ly/Ps44:2); [PRO 19:26](https://ref.ly/Prov19:26), [PRO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Prov22:10); [JER 29:2](https://ref.ly/Jer29:2); [MAT 21:12](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 1:34; Matthew 8:16; Matthew 9:32-33; Luke 11:14; Mark 1:39; Luke 13:32; Matthew 12:24-28; Mark 3:22-26; Luke 11:15-20; Matthew 9:34; Matthew 10:1; Luke 9:1; Matthew 10:8; Mark 3:15; Mark 6:13; Mark 9:18; Luke 9:49; Acts 19:13; Matthew 7:22; John 12:31; Numbers 33:51-52; Exodus 10:11; Psalms 44:2; Proverbs 19:26; Proverbs 22:10; Jeremiah 29:2; Matthew 21:12
* **Associated Articles:** 2351: Jesus Christ, miracles of (ID: `2351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4134: demons, exorcism of (ID: `4134@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4165: exorcism (ID: `4165@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4165: exorcism (id: 4165)

The expulsion of an evil spirit or spirits from a person. Jesus Christ has unique authority as an exorcist. He delegated this authority to the disciples.

Exorcism was practised with varying degrees of success by Jews and pagans
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 7:22](https://ref.ly/Matt7:22), [MAT 12:27](https://ref.ly/Matt12:27) parallel [LUK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Luke11:19); ; [ACT 19:13–19:16](https://ref.ly/Acts19:13-Acts19:16)

Jesus Christ had unique authority as an exorcist
------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Mark1:27)** parallel [LUK 4:36](https://ref.ly/Luke4:36)**[MAT 12:25–12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:25-Matt12:28)** parallel [LUK 11:17–11:18](https://ref.ly/Luke11:17-Luke11:18) *The authority Jesus Christ exercised over evil spirits demonstrated that God's kingly authority was present in him.* *See also* [MAT 12:29](https://ref.ly/Matt12:29) parallels [MRK 3:27](https://ref.ly/Mark3:27), [LUK 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/Luke11:21-Luke11:22) *Jesus Christ is the “strong man”. His power over evil spirits shows that Satan is defeated.*

Jesus Christ had a general ministry of exorcism
-----------------------------------------------

[MAT 8:16](https://ref.ly/Matt8:16) parallels [MRK 1:34](https://ref.ly/Mark1:34), [LUK 4:41](https://ref.ly/Luke4:41); ; [MRK 1:39](https://ref.ly/Mark1:39); ; [LUK 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/Luke6:17-Luke6:18), [LUK 7:21](https://ref.ly/Luke7:21)

Jesus Christ gave the apostles power to cast out evil spirits
-------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1) parallels [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7), [LUK 9:1](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1); ; [MRK 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:15), [MRK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Mark16:17); ; [LUK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Luke10:17)

After the ascension the apostles cast out evil spirits
------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:16), [ACT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:12)

Various symptoms associated with the need for exorcism
------------------------------------------------------

### Violent or self\-destructive behaviour

[MRK 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/Mark5:1-Mark5:5) parallel [LUK 8:27–8:29](https://ref.ly/Luke8:27-Luke8:29)

### Deafness and muteness

[MAT 9:32](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32); ; [MRK 9:17](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17); ; [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14)

### Seizures

[MAT 17:14–17:15](https://ref.ly/Matt17:14-Matt17:15) parallels [MRK 9:17–9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17-Mark9:18), [LUK 9:38–9:39](https://ref.ly/Luke9:38-Luke9:39)

Various components of exorcism
------------------------------

### A word of command

**[MRK 1:25](https://ref.ly/Mark1:25)** parallel [LUK 4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:35) *See also* [MAT 8:32](https://ref.ly/Matt8:32); [MRK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25); [ACT 16:18](https://ref.ly/Acts16:18)

### The demons recognise the source of the authority addressing them

**[MRK 1:24](https://ref.ly/Mark1:24)** parallel [LUK 4:34](https://ref.ly/Luke4:34) *See also* [MAT 8:29](https://ref.ly/Matt8:29) parallels [MRK 5:7](https://ref.ly/Mark5:7), [LUK 8:28](https://ref.ly/Luke8:28); [ACT 16:17](https://ref.ly/Acts16:17)

### Exorcism was often accompanied by an element of violence or trauma

**[MRK 1:26](https://ref.ly/Mark1:26)** *See also* [MRK 9:26](https://ref.ly/Mark9:26); [MAT 8:32](https://ref.ly/Matt8:32) parallels [MRK 5:13](https://ref.ly/Mark5:13), [LUK 8:32–8:33](https://ref.ly/Luke8:32-Luke8:33); [ACT 19:16](https://ref.ly/Acts19:16)

### Exorcism changes the behaviour or condition of the person delivered

**[MRK 5:15](https://ref.ly/Mark5:15)** parallel [LUK 8:35](https://ref.ly/Luke8:35) *See also* [MAT 9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:33) parallel [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14)

### An exorcised person may be \`\`repossessed''

[MAT 12:43–12:45](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43-Matt12:45) parallel [LUK 11:24–11:26](https://ref.ly/Luke11:24-Luke11:26)

Methods of exorcism other than the word of command
--------------------------------------------------

### Exorcism may be achieved at a distance

[MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30); ; [ACT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:12)

### On rare occasions an object is used

[ACT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:12) *This was an unusual demonstration of power in a city noted for magic.*

### David's music brought relief to Saul

[1SA 16:14–16:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14-1Sam16:16), [1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23)

### Intensive prayer is sometimes necessary

[MRK 9:29](https://ref.ly/Mark9:29)

## 4175: mediums (id: 4175)

People who claim to be channels through whom the spirits of the dead speak to the living.

God's people must not be mediums
--------------------------------

**[DEU 18:10–18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:10-Deut18:12)**

God's people must not consult mediums
-------------------------------------

**[ISA 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19-Isa8:20)** *See also* [LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31), [LEV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Lev20:6); [2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24); [JER 27:9](https://ref.ly/Jer27:9)

---------------------------------------

### King Saul

[1SA 28:3–28:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:3-1Sam28:16); ; [1CH 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:13)

## 4180: seraphim (id: 4180)

An order of angels, mentioned only in one OT passage. Seraphim (the plural is also rendered seraphs) have a physical appearance and a moral and spiritual nature; they understand and use speech. Their functions include the worship of God and the communication of the assurance of forgiveness.

Their appearance
----------------

**[ISA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Isa6:2)** *The covering of faces and feet expresses reverence, humility and awe before God.*

Their functions
---------------

### Worship of God

**[ISA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Isa6:3)** parallel [REV 4:8](https://ref.ly/Rev4:8)

### Communication with the prophet

**[ISA 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Isa6:6-Isa6:7)**

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 6:2; Isaiah 6:3; Revelation 4:8; Isaiah 6:6-7
* **Associated Articles:** 4114: angels, and praise of God (ID: `4114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4150: cherubim (ID: `4150@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6653: forgiveness, divine (ID: `6653@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8625: worship, acceptable attitudes in (ID: `8625@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4185: sorcery and magic (id: 4185)

The attempt to discover or influence the future by means forbidden by God, including the use of divination, astrology or witchcraft. Such practices are strongly condemned in Scripture.

Examples of magic and sorcery
-----------------------------

### Magic practices

**[REV 18:23](https://ref.ly/Rev18:23)** *Babylon, here, represents the world as the centre of seduction.*

### Divination

**[ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2)**

### Spiritism

**[ISA 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19-Isa8:20)** *See also* [2CH 33:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:6)

### Astrology

**[2CH 33:3–33:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:3-2Chr33:5)** parallel [2KI 21:3–21:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3-2Kgs21:5)

Examples of those who practised sorcery
---------------------------------------

Egyptian magicians: [EXO 7:11](https://ref.ly/Exod7:11), [EXO 8:18](https://ref.ly/Exod8:18); [NUM 22:6](https://ref.ly/Num22:6), [NUM 23:23](https://ref.ly/Num23:23); [2KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:6) *Manasseh, king of Judah*; ; [ISA 47:9–47:13](https://ref.ly/Isa47:9-Isa47:13) *the Babylonians*; ; [ACT 8:9–8:11](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9-Acts8:11) *Simon of Samaria*; ; [ACT 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6-Acts13:8) *Bar\-Jesus of Cyprus*; ; [ACT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Acts19:19) *people in Ephesus*

All sorcery and magic is strongly forbidden
-------------------------------------------

**[DEU 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:9-Deut18:12)** *See also* [LEV 19:26](https://ref.ly/Lev19:26), [LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31); [JER 27:9–27:10](https://ref.ly/Jer27:9-Jer27:10); [EZK 13:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:18), [EZK 13:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:20)

God is stronger than sorcerers and magicians
--------------------------------------------

[EXO 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Exod7:11-Exod7:12), [EXO 8:16–8:19](https://ref.ly/Exod8:16-Exod8:19); ; [ISA 44:24–44:25](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24-Isa44:25)

God's judgment comes upon sorcerers and magicians
-------------------------------------------------

[LEV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Lev20:6); ; [MIC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Mic5:12); ; [ACT 13:6–13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6-Acts13:11); ; [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8); ; [GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21)

### God made participation in occult practices punishable by death

[EXO 22:18](https://ref.ly/Exod22:18); ; [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27)

### King Saul was judged for occult practices

[1CH 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:13-1Chr10:14)

Jesus Christ can deliver from occultism
---------------------------------------

[ACT 16:16–16:18](https://ref.ly/Acts16:16-Acts16:18), [ACT 19:18–19:19](https://ref.ly/Acts19:18-Acts19:19); ; [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39)

## 4190: spiritism (id: 4190)

The use of mediums or other spiritual intermediaries to communicate with the dead. Scripture forbids such practices, which are inconsistent with obedience to a living God.

Spiritism forbidden by God
--------------------------

**[LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31)** *See also* [DEU 18:10–18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:10-Deut18:12)

Punishment for spiritism
------------------------

**[LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27)** *See also* [LEV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Lev20:6)

Spiritism practised in Israel
-----------------------------

**[2KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:6)** parallel [2CH 33:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:6) *See also* [1SA 28:4–28:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:4-1Sam28:20)

Spiritists expelled from Israel
-------------------------------

**[2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24)** *See also* [1SA 28:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:3)

The futility of spiritism
-------------------------

**[ISA 8:19](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19)** *See also* [ISA 19:2–19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:2-Isa19:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:31; Deuteronomy 18:10-12; Leviticus 20:27; Leviticus 20:6; 2 Kings 21:6; 2 Chronicles 33:6; 1 Samuel 28:4-20; 2 Kings 23:24; 1 Samuel 28:3; Isaiah 8:19; Isaiah 19:2-3

## 4195: spirits (id: 4195)

Intelligent beings which exist separately from bodies. God is spirit and he has given human beings a spiritual entity. He has also created a realm of spiritual beings\-\-some have remained good, others have become evil. Evil spirits seek to control and destroy people, but Jesus Christ has the victory over them.

God is spirit
-------------

**[JHN 4:24](https://ref.ly/John4:24)** *See also* [GEN 1:2](https://ref.ly/Gen1:2); [JOB 33:4](https://ref.ly/Job33:4); [REV 1:4](https://ref.ly/Rev1:4), [REV 3:1](https://ref.ly/Rev3:1), [REV 4:5](https://ref.ly/Rev4:5), [REV 5:6](https://ref.ly/Rev5:6)

The role of spirits
-------------------

### As instruments of punishment

**[1SA 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14)** *See also* [JDG 9:23](https://ref.ly/Judg9:23); [1SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10), [1SA 19:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9); [1KI 22:20–22:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:20-1Kgs22:22) parallel [2CH 18:19–18:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:19-2Chr18:21)

### As instruments of testing to inspire faith in believers

**[1PE 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8-1Pet5:9)** *See also* [JOB 1:8–1:12](https://ref.ly/Job1:8-Job1:12), [JOB 1:22](https://ref.ly/Job1:22), [JOB 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/Job2:3-Job2:6), [JOB 2:10](https://ref.ly/Job2:10); [MAT 4:1](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1) parallels [MRK 1:13](https://ref.ly/Mark1:13), [LUK 4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:2); [JAS 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:7-Jas4:8)

Evil spirits and humanity
-------------------------

### Possession by spirits

**[MRK 9:17–9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17-Mark9:18)** parallel [LUK 9:39](https://ref.ly/Luke9:39) *See also* [MAT 8:28](https://ref.ly/Matt8:28) parallel [LUK 8:27](https://ref.ly/Luke8:27); [MAT 15:22](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22) parallel [MRK 7:25](https://ref.ly/Mark7:25); [MRK 1:23](https://ref.ly/Mark1:23) parallel [LUK 4:33](https://ref.ly/Luke4:33)

### Casting out spirits

**[MAT 8:16](https://ref.ly/Matt8:16)** parallels [MRK 1:34](https://ref.ly/Mark1:34), [LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40) *See also* [MAT 12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:28) parallel [LUK 11:20](https://ref.ly/Luke11:20); [MAT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Matt17:18) parallels [MRK 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25-Mark9:26), [LUK 9:42](https://ref.ly/Luke9:42); [ACT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:16), [ACT 8:7](https://ref.ly/Acts8:7)

### Consultation with spirits forbidden

**[DEU 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/Deut18:10-Deut18:11)** *See also* [LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31), [LEV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Lev20:6); [1SA 28:8–28:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:8-1Sam28:9); [2KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:6) parallel [2CH 33:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:6); [ISA 8:19](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19), [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3)

## 4203: earth, the (id: 4203)

Created and sustained by God, the world is both his possession and the dwelling\-place of his creatures. As such, it should bring him glory, and will one day be judged by him.

God created the earth from nothing
----------------------------------

**[EXO 20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:11)**; **[NEH 9:6](https://ref.ly/Neh9:6)**; **[ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15)** *See also* [GEN 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1-Gen1:2); [EXO 31:17](https://ref.ly/Exod31:17); [2KI 19:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:15); [JOB 26:7](https://ref.ly/Job26:7); [PSA 74:17](https://ref.ly/Ps74:17), [PSA 115:15](https://ref.ly/Ps115:15), [PSA 119:90](https://ref.ly/Ps119:90); [ISA 44:24](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24), [ISA 45:12](https://ref.ly/Isa45:12), [ISA 48:13](https://ref.ly/Isa48:13); [JER 10:12](https://ref.ly/Jer10:12), [JER 27:5](https://ref.ly/Jer27:5), [JER 32:17](https://ref.ly/Jer32:17), [JER 33:2](https://ref.ly/Jer33:2); [AMO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Amos4:13); [JHN 1:3](https://ref.ly/John1:3); [ACT 4:24](https://ref.ly/Acts4:24); [HEB 11:3](https://ref.ly/Heb11:3); [2PE 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:5); [REV 10:6](https://ref.ly/Rev10:6), [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7)

The earth belongs to God
------------------------

**[DEU 10:14](https://ref.ly/Deut10:14)**; **[PSA 24:1](https://ref.ly/Ps24:1), [PSA 95:4–95:5](https://ref.ly/Ps95:4-Ps95:5)** *See also* [EXO 9:29](https://ref.ly/Exod9:29); [JOS 3:13](https://ref.ly/Josh3:13); [1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8); [1CH 29:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:11); [PSA 47:9](https://ref.ly/Ps47:9), [PSA 89:11](https://ref.ly/Ps89:11); [1CO 10:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:26)

God controls and sustains the earth
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 135:6–135:7](https://ref.ly/Ps135:6-Ps135:7)** *See also* [GEN 9:16–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:16-Gen9:17); [JOB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Job5:10), [JOB 28:5](https://ref.ly/Job28:5); [PSA 104:13–104:14](https://ref.ly/Ps104:13-Ps104:14), [PSA 147:8](https://ref.ly/Ps147:8); [JER 10:13](https://ref.ly/Jer10:13), [JER 33:25](https://ref.ly/Jer33:25); [HOS 6:3](https://ref.ly/Hos6:3); [COL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:17); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3)

God's character seen on earth
-----------------------------

**[PSA 33:5](https://ref.ly/Ps33:5)** *See also* [PSA 85:11](https://ref.ly/Ps85:11), [PSA 119:64](https://ref.ly/Ps119:64); [ISA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Isa6:3), [ISA 11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:9); [JER 9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:24)

The earth should resound to God's glory and praise
--------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 96:1](https://ref.ly/Ps96:1)**; **[ISA 44:23](https://ref.ly/Isa44:23)** *See also* [PSA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Ps47:2), [PSA 66:4](https://ref.ly/Ps66:4), [PSA 72:9](https://ref.ly/Ps72:9), [PSA 108:5](https://ref.ly/Ps108:5), [PSA 148:7](https://ref.ly/Ps148:7); [ISA 49:13](https://ref.ly/Isa49:13); [HAB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Hab3:3); [REV 5:13](https://ref.ly/Rev5:13)

The earth is the habitation of human beings and the animals
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 115:16](https://ref.ly/Ps115:16)** *See also* [GEN 1:22](https://ref.ly/Gen1:22), [GEN 1:26](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26), [GEN 1:28–1:30](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28-Gen1:30), [GEN 8:17](https://ref.ly/Gen8:17), [GEN 9:1](https://ref.ly/Gen9:1); [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)

God judges the earth
--------------------

**[1CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:14)**; **[NAM 1:5](https://ref.ly/Nah1:5)** *See also* [GEN 18:25](https://ref.ly/Gen18:25); [DEU 32:22](https://ref.ly/Deut32:22); [1SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:10); [2SA 22:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:16); [JOB 9:6](https://ref.ly/Job9:6); [PSA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Ps46:6), [PSA 77:18](https://ref.ly/Ps77:18); [ISA 2:21](https://ref.ly/Isa2:21), [ISA 26:9](https://ref.ly/Isa26:9); [JER 10:10](https://ref.ly/Jer10:10)

God will destroy the earth one day
----------------------------------

God will create a new earth
---------------------------

## 4206: land (id: 4206)

Areas of territory, the possession of which was regarded as a sign of security and prosperity. The land of Israel was regarded as the land promised by God to his people and a sign of his covenant faithfulness.

* **Associated Articles:** 4207: land, as a divine gift (ID: `4207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4208: land, as a divine responsibility (ID: `4208@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4209: land, spiritual aspects of (ID: `4209@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4207: land, as a divine gift (id: 4207)

God's gift of the land of Israel is fundamental to the covenant promises made to Abraham and his descendants.

Land as part of God's creation
------------------------------

[GEN 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Gen1:9-Gen1:10)

The land (Canaan) promised to Abraham
-------------------------------------

**[GEN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Gen12:7)** *See also* [GEN 12:1](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1), [GEN 13:15–13:17](https://ref.ly/Gen13:15-Gen13:17), [GEN 17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:8), [GEN 24:7](https://ref.ly/Gen24:7)

The boundaries of the promised land
-----------------------------------

**[GEN 15:18–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:18-Gen15:21)** *See also* [EXO 23:31](https://ref.ly/Exod23:31); [NUM 34:1–34:12](https://ref.ly/Num34:1-Num34:12)

God's promise and its gradual fulfilment
----------------------------------------

### The promise made to the patriarchs

**[GEN 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Gen26:3-Gen26:4)** *See also* [GEN 23:17–23:20](https://ref.ly/Gen23:17-Gen23:20), [GEN 28:13](https://ref.ly/Gen28:13), [GEN 35:12](https://ref.ly/Gen35:12), [GEN 48:3–48:4](https://ref.ly/Gen48:3-Gen48:4), [GEN 50:24–50:25](https://ref.ly/Gen50:24-Gen50:25) *The patriarchs maintained only a very small part of the land. But Genesis closes with the prospect of much more.*

### The promise confirmed during the wilderness wanderings

**[EXO 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:8)** *See also* [EXO 6:4](https://ref.ly/Exod6:4), [EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8), [EXO 13:5](https://ref.ly/Exod13:5), [EXO 13:11](https://ref.ly/Exod13:11), [EXO 32:11–32:14](https://ref.ly/Exod32:11-Exod32:14), [EXO 33:1–33:3](https://ref.ly/Exod33:1-Exod33:3); [NUM 11:10–11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:10-Num11:12)

### Exploration of the promised land

**[NUM 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Num13:1-Num13:2)** *See also* [NUM 13:21–13:25](https://ref.ly/Num13:21-Num13:25)

### Entry delayed because of unbelief and rebellion

**[NUM 13:27–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:27-Num13:33)** *See also* [NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4), [NUM 14:26–14:35](https://ref.ly/Num14:26-Num14:35), [NUM 14:40–14:45](https://ref.ly/Num14:40-Num14:45)

### Criticial importance of obedience in the promised land

**[DEU 5:32–5:33](https://ref.ly/Deut5:32-Deut5:33)** *Moses, at the end of his life, urges all the Israelites always to remember the LORD and his commands; enjoyment of the land is dependent upon the people's obedience.* *See also* [DEU 6:10–6:12](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:12)

### Invasion of the land

**[JOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Josh1:2)** *The theme of the book of Joshua is the land: chapters 1–12 its invasion and conquest, chapters 13–21 its division among the Israelite tribes.* *See also* [JOS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Josh2:1)

### Gradual conquest of the land

**[JOS 13:1](https://ref.ly/Josh13:1)** *See also* [JOS 7:2](https://ref.ly/Josh7:2), [JOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Josh8:1), [JOS 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Josh9:1-Josh9:2), [JOS 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Josh10:29-Josh10:30), [JOS 13:2–13:6](https://ref.ly/Josh13:2-Josh13:6), [JOS 14:12](https://ref.ly/Josh14:12), [JOS 17:12–17:18](https://ref.ly/Josh17:12-Josh17:18)

### The promise fulfilled

**[JOS 21:43–21:45](https://ref.ly/Josh21:43-Josh21:45)**

### Division of the land

**[JOS 23:4–23:5](https://ref.ly/Josh23:4-Josh23:5)**

### Conflict with the Philistines over the land

**[1SA 7:7–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:7-1Sam7:14)** *Philistine aggression had imperilled the prospect of a full possession of the land until David overpowered them.* *See also* [JDG 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/Judg2:21-Judg2:22), [JDG 13:1](https://ref.ly/Judg13:1), [JDG 15:11](https://ref.ly/Judg15:11); [1SA 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:10-1Sam4:11), [1SA 13:17–13:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:17-1Sam13:19), [1SA 17:1–17:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:1-1Sam17:2), [1SA 31:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:1), [1SA 31:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:7)

### Jerusalem captured by David

[2SA 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:7)

The land at rest and prosperous
-------------------------------

### Under David

## 4208: land, as a divine responsibility (id: 4208)

Israel's land is a gift from God, though ultimately it remains under divine ownership. Life in the land is therefore characterised by both privilege and responsibility.

The land is a gift from God
---------------------------

**[DEU 26:5–26:9](https://ref.ly/Deut26:5-Deut26:9)**

### It is Israel's inheritance

**[DEU 26:1](https://ref.ly/Deut26:1)** *See also* [EXO 32:13](https://ref.ly/Exod32:13); [DEU 4:21](https://ref.ly/Deut4:21), [DEU 4:38](https://ref.ly/Deut4:38), [DEU 15:4](https://ref.ly/Deut15:4), [DEU 19:10](https://ref.ly/Deut19:10)

### Eleven full tribes share the inheritance

**[NUM 26:52–26:55](https://ref.ly/Num26:52-Num26:55)**

### Inherited land east of Jordan allotted by Moses

**[NUM 32:33](https://ref.ly/Num32:33)** *See also* [JOS 12:6](https://ref.ly/Josh12:6), [JOS 13:8](https://ref.ly/Josh13:8), [JOS 13:15](https://ref.ly/Josh13:15), [JOS 13:24](https://ref.ly/Josh13:24), [JOS 13:29](https://ref.ly/Josh13:29)

### Inherited land west of Jordan allotted by Joshua and others

**[NUM 34:17–34:29](https://ref.ly/Num34:17-Num34:29)** *See also* [JOS 14:1](https://ref.ly/Josh14:1), [JOS 14:13](https://ref.ly/Josh14:13), [JOS 15:1](https://ref.ly/Josh15:1), [JOS 15:20](https://ref.ly/Josh15:20), [JOS 16:4](https://ref.ly/Josh16:4), [JOS 18:11](https://ref.ly/Josh18:11), [JOS 19:1](https://ref.ly/Josh19:1), [JOS 19:10](https://ref.ly/Josh19:10), [JOS 19:17](https://ref.ly/Josh19:17), [JOS 19:24](https://ref.ly/Josh19:24), [JOS 19:32](https://ref.ly/Josh19:32), [JOS 19:40](https://ref.ly/Josh19:40), [JOS 19:51](https://ref.ly/Josh19:51)

### Levi receives no inheritance of land

[NUM 26:62](https://ref.ly/Num26:62); ; [DEU 18:1](https://ref.ly/Deut18:1); ; [JOS 13:14](https://ref.ly/Josh13:14), [JOS 13:33](https://ref.ly/Josh13:33)

### Israelite households to hold their inheritance in perpetuity

**[NUM 36:7–36:9](https://ref.ly/Num36:7-Num36:9)** *See also* [1KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:3); [EZK 46:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:18)

The land remains under divine ownership
---------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:23](https://ref.ly/Lev25:23)**

Responsibility to God for the land
----------------------------------

*See also* [MAT 21:33–21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:46)

### The tithe

**[LEV 27:30](https://ref.ly/Lev27:30)** *The tithe of the land's produce given to the LORD, is a kind of rent, a way of acknowledging his ownership.*

### Harvest festivals

**[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)** *See also* [LEV 23:15–23:21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:21), [LEV 23:33–23:43](https://ref.ly/Lev23:33-Lev23:43)

### Sabbath rest

**[LEV 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/Lev25:1-Lev25:7)**

### The Year of Jubilee

**[LEV 25:8–25:13](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:13)**

Responsibility to fellow Israelites in the land
-----------------------------------------------

### Individual property rights to be respected

**[DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14)** *See also* [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17); [PRO 15:25](https://ref.ly/Prov15:25), [PRO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Prov22:28), [PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10); [HOS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Hos5:10)

### Violation of these rights severely condemned

**[ISA 5:8](https://ref.ly/Isa5:8)** *See also* [1KI 21:17–21:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:17-1Kgs21:19); [JOB 20:19](https://ref.ly/Job20:19); [EZK 45:8–45:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:8-Ezek45:9); [MIC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:2), [MIC 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mic2:9)

Responsibility to the poor in the land
--------------------------------------

### The fallow (sabbath) year

**[EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11)** *See also* [LEV 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/Lev25:6-Lev25:7); [DEU 15:1–15:3](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1-Deut15:3) *Debts probably include the labour of a debtor's dependants, working to pay off his debt.*

### The Year of Jubilee

[LEV 25:8–25:13](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:13)

### Harvesting conventions

**[LEV 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:9-Lev19:10)** *See also* [LEV 23:22](https://ref.ly/Lev23:22); [DEU 24:19–24:22](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19-Deut24:22); [RUT 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:2-Ruth2:3)

------------------------------------------

### Blessing

## 4209: land, spiritual aspects of (id: 4209)

Scripture presents land in a spiritual perspective. The whole earth, once perfect but now contaminated by the fall, will one day be renewed. There will be a new heaven and earth where life is abundant and God's presence is experienced for ever.

Eden: the original good land
----------------------------

**[GEN 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Gen2:8-Gen2:9)**

Paradise lost: expulsion from the garden
----------------------------------------

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *See also* [ROM 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19-Rom8:20)

The promised land: an echo of Eden
----------------------------------

**[DEU 8:7–8:10](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:10)** *See also* [EXO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:8), [EXO 3:17](https://ref.ly/Exod3:17), [EXO 13:5](https://ref.ly/Exod13:5); [LEV 20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:24); [NUM 13:27](https://ref.ly/Num13:27); [DEU 6:3](https://ref.ly/Deut6:3), [DEU 11:8–11:12](https://ref.ly/Deut11:8-Deut11:12), [DEU 26:9](https://ref.ly/Deut26:9), [DEU 27:3](https://ref.ly/Deut27:3)

The land of Israel after the exile
----------------------------------

### Promise of a return to the land

**[AMO 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Amos9:14-Amos9:15)** *See also* [ISA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Isa14:1), [ISA 35:1–35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:1-Isa35:10); [EZK 20:41–20:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:41-Ezek20:42), [EZK 34:11–34:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11-Ezek34:13); [AMO 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11-Amos9:12); [ACT 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Acts15:16-Acts15:17)

### Promise of a restored environment

**[ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3)** *See also* [ZEC 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/Zech8:12-Zech8:13)

The final restoration of the land
---------------------------------

**[ISA 65:17](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17)** *See also* [ISA 66:22](https://ref.ly/Isa66:22); [ROM 8:21](https://ref.ly/Rom8:21)

### Jesus Christ will reign eternally over new heavens and a new earth

**[ZEC 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9-Zech9:10)** *See also* [MAT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Matt21:5); [JHN 12:15](https://ref.ly/John12:15); [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13); [REV 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:4), [REV 22:1–22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:5)

### Vision of life on the new earth

**[ISA 11:6–11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:6-Isa11:9)** *See also* [HOS 2:18–2:23](https://ref.ly/Hos2:18-Hos2:23); [MIC 4:2–4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:2-Mic4:4)

The new Eden: the Christian's inheritance
-----------------------------------------

**[HEB 11:13–11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13-Heb11:16)** *See also* [PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11); [MAT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Matt5:5); [ROM 8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:17); [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7), [REV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Rev21:7)LUK [23:43](https://ref.ly/Rev23:43); [2CO 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:1-2Cor12:4)

## 4212: astronomy (id: 4212)

The study of the stars and planets, with a view to understanding their movements and their use in fixing the time of significant events.

Astronomy as a study
--------------------

### It demonstrates the greatness of God and his creation

**[JOB 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/Job9:7-Job9:9)**; **[ISA 40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:26)** *See also* [GEN 2:1](https://ref.ly/Gen2:1); [DEU 10:14](https://ref.ly/Deut10:14); [1CH 16:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:26); [NEH 9:6](https://ref.ly/Neh9:6) *Nehemiah's prayer reviews God's grace and power in creation*; [JOB 22:12](https://ref.ly/Job22:12), [JOB 25:5](https://ref.ly/Job25:5), [JOB 26:9](https://ref.ly/Job26:9); [PSA 19:1–19:6](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:6), [PSA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Ps33:6), [PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16), [PSA 89:5](https://ref.ly/Ps89:5), [PSA 89:11](https://ref.ly/Ps89:11), [PSA 96:5](https://ref.ly/Ps96:5), [PSA 104:2](https://ref.ly/Ps104:2), [PSA 136:5](https://ref.ly/Ps136:5), [PSA 136:7–136:9](https://ref.ly/Ps136:7-Ps136:9), [PSA 148:3](https://ref.ly/Ps148:3); [PRO 3:19](https://ref.ly/Prov3:19) *Divine wisdom permeates the whole creation*; [ISA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:8) *the LORD's sign to Hezekiah*; [ISA 45:12](https://ref.ly/Isa45:12); [JER 31:35](https://ref.ly/Jer31:35); [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45); [HEB 1:10](https://ref.ly/Heb1:10) *credited to Jesus Christ*

### It demonstrates the humility of the human race

**[PSA 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3-Ps8:4)**

The use of astronomy to determine dates
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:3-Gen1:5)**; **[PSA 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3), [PSA 104:19](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19)**

The mysteries of astronomy are beyond human understanding
---------------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 38:31–38:33](https://ref.ly/Job38:31-Job38:33)**

God's judgment and astronomical disturbances
--------------------------------------------

**[JOL 2:31](https://ref.ly/Joel2:31)**; **[MAT 24:29](https://ref.ly/Matt24:29)** parallels [MRK 13:25](https://ref.ly/Mark13:25), [LUK 21:25](https://ref.ly/Luke21:25) *See also* [ISA 13:10](https://ref.ly/Isa13:10), [ISA 24:23](https://ref.ly/Isa24:23), [ISA 30:26](https://ref.ly/Isa30:26), [ISA 34:4](https://ref.ly/Isa34:4), [ISA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Isa51:6), [ISA 60:9–60:20](https://ref.ly/Isa60:9-Isa60:20) *the glory of Zion*; [EZK 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:7-Ezek32:8); [JOL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Joel2:10), [JOL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Joel3:15); [AMO 8:9](https://ref.ly/Amos8:9); [ACT 2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:20); [2PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10); [REV 8:12](https://ref.ly/Rev8:12)

## 4215: Babylon (id: 4215)

In OT times, the commercial, religious and political capital of Babylonia, which was the dominant power in the Near East in the sixth century B.C. In the NT, “Babylon” signifies the world and its forces in opposition to God. It is often contrasted with “the new Jerusalem”, in which God will finally reign supreme.

The founding of Babylon
-----------------------

**[GEN 10:8–10:10](https://ref.ly/Gen10:8-Gen10:10)** *Shinar was an alternative name for Babylon.*

Biblical events prior to the exile in Babylon
---------------------------------------------

### Inhabitants of Babylon exiled to Samaria

*See also* [2KI 17:24–17:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:24-2Kgs17:33)

### Envoys from Babylon visit Hezekiah

[2KI 20:12–20:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:12-2Kgs20:19) parallel [ISA 39:1–39:8](https://ref.ly/Isa39:1-Isa39:8) *Hezekiah's reception of the envoys was designed to lead to a coalition against Assyria, but in the end it brought about the very opposite of what he had hoped for.*

### Manasseh, king of Judah, exiled to Babylon

[2CH 33:10–33:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:10-2Chr33:11)

### Jehoiakim, king of Judah, becomes a vassal of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon

[2KI 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:1-2Kgs24:2) parallel [2CH 36:6–36:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:6-2Chr36:7)

### Nebuchadnezzar lays siege to Jerusalem

## 4218: cave (id: 4218)

A natural cavity in a rock face, often used as a place of refuge.

Caves as places of refuge
-------------------------

**[JDG 6:2](https://ref.ly/Judg6:2)**; **[1SA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:1-1Sam22:2)** *See also* [GEN 19:30](https://ref.ly/Gen19:30); [JOS 10:16–10:18](https://ref.ly/Josh10:16-Josh10:18), [JOS 10:22–10:23](https://ref.ly/Josh10:22-Josh10:23), [JOS 10:27](https://ref.ly/Josh10:27); [JDG 15:8](https://ref.ly/Judg15:8), [JDG 15:11](https://ref.ly/Judg15:11); [1SA 13:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:6), [1SA 24:2–24:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:2-1Sam24:22) *Saul comes to David's cave, but David spares his life*; [2SA 17:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:9), [2SA 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:13) parallel [1CH 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:15); [1KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:4), [1KI 18:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:13), [1KI 19:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:9); PSA 57:0, PSA 142:0; [ISA 2:19–2:21](https://ref.ly/Isa2:19-Isa2:21) *a place of refuge in the face of divine judgment*; [JER 48:28](https://ref.ly/Jer48:28), [JER 49:8](https://ref.ly/Jer49:8); [EZK 33:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:27); [HEB 11:38](https://ref.ly/Heb11:38) *a place of refuge for those being persecuted for their faith*; [REV 6:15](https://ref.ly/Rev6:15)

## 4221: cistern (id: 4221)

A reservoir for storing water collected from rainfall or from a spring. When empty, it was used for a variety of purposes. The word is also employed in a figurative sense.

-----------------------------------

## 4227: deep, the (id: 4227)

Unfathomed waters, sometimes seen as existing under the earth, held back by God's power. The term is also used of deep seas, and in metaphorical contexts.

## 4236: fountain (id: 4236)

A source of natural spring water. Figuratively, it symbolises a place of cleansing, tears or sexual fulfilment. Significantly, God is pictured as a fountain of life.

----------------------------------------

**[PRO 18:4](https://ref.ly/Prov18:4)**

---------------------------

### A place of cleansing

**[SNG 4:12](https://ref.ly/Song4:12)** *See also* [PRO 5:15–5:18](https://ref.ly/Prov5:15-Prov5:18); [SNG 4:15](https://ref.ly/Song4:15)

### A source of life

## 4239: garden (id: 4239)

A piece of land where flowers, fruit and vegetables may be grown. The Garden of Eden was the garden entrusted to the care of Adam and Eve.

---------------------------------

## 4241: Garden of Eden, the (id: 4241)

The garden created by God and entrusted to the care of Adam and Eve.

The \`\`garden of the LORD''
----------------------------

**[GEN 2:8](https://ref.ly/Gen2:8), [GEN 13:10](https://ref.ly/Gen13:10)** *See also* [GEN 2:9–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gen2:9-Gen2:14); [ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3); [EZK 31:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:9), [EZK 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:18) *Ancient Egypt is likened to Eden*; [EZK 36:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:35); [JOL 2:3](https://ref.ly/Joel2:3)

The place of fellowship with God
--------------------------------

**[GEN 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8)**

### Where God spoke to Adam

*See also* [GEN 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:16-Gen2:18)

### Where Adam was to work

[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15), [GEN 2:19](https://ref.ly/Gen2:19)

### Where God made Eve and gave her to Adam

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [GEN 2:20–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:24)

### Where Adam and Eve lived in innocence

[GEN 2:25](https://ref.ly/Gen2:25), [GEN 3:7](https://ref.ly/Gen3:7), [GEN 3:11](https://ref.ly/Gen3:11)

### Where God abundantly provided for Adam and Eve

[GEN 2:9–2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:9-Gen2:17) *The trees of Eden are important. They are both the evidence of God's love and one is the focus of Adam's test.*

The paradise that humanity has lost
-----------------------------------

### Where Adam and Eve disobeyed God

**[GEN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6)** *See also* [GEN 2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:17), [GEN 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:7), [GEN 3:11](https://ref.ly/Gen3:11)

### Where humanity came under God's curse

[GEN 3:16–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16-Gen3:19)

### The place from which Adam and Eve were banished

**[GEN 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gen3:23)** *See also* [GEN 3:22](https://ref.ly/Gen3:22), [GEN 3:24](https://ref.ly/Gen3:24)

The picture of God's blessing
-----------------------------

**[ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3)** *See also* [ISA 58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:11); [JER 31:12](https://ref.ly/Jer31:12)

### Where God first promised Satan's destruction

**[GEN 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14-Gen3:15)**

### Where God first provided a covering for sinners

*See also* [GEN 3:21](https://ref.ly/Gen3:21)

### Eden as an anticipation of heaven

**[LUK 23:43](https://ref.ly/Luke23:43)** *The word “paradise” (which means “park” or “walled garden”) clearly refers back to the idea of Eden.* *See also* [2CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:3), [2CO 12:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:4); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7), [REV 22:1–22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:3); [EZK 28:12–28:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:12-Ezek28:13) *the former glory of the king of Tyre likened to that of Adam in the Garden of Eden*

## 4248: islands (id: 4248)

Used of the islands and coastlands of the eastern Mediterranean and, especially by Isaiah, as a more general term indicating distant nations that are called upon to respond to God. Several specific islands are mentioned in the NT.

The islands and coastlands of the Mediterranean
-----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 11:11](https://ref.ly/Isa11:11)** *See also* [ISA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Isa42:10), [ISA 60:9](https://ref.ly/Isa60:9); [JER 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Jer2:10-Jer2:11), [JER 31:10](https://ref.ly/Jer31:10); [EZK 26:15–26:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:15-Ezek26:18), [EZK 27:3–27:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:3-Ezek27:7); [DAN 11:18](https://ref.ly/Dan11:18), [DAN 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Dan11:29-Dan11:30)

------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 40:15–40:17](https://ref.ly/Isa40:15-Isa40:17)**

--------------------------------------

**[ISA 66:19](https://ref.ly/Isa66:19)** *See also* [ISA 41:1](https://ref.ly/Isa41:1), [ISA 49:1](https://ref.ly/Isa49:1)

## 4254: mountains (id: 4254)

These natural features of Palestine are often used as poetic symbols when describing God or his dealings with his people. Specific mountains are remembered because of their association with important events in the history of Israel. Some of the key events in the life and ministry of Jesus Christ take place on mountain sites.

Mountains and the character of God
----------------------------------

### Mountains and the steadfastness of God

**[PSA 125:2](https://ref.ly/Ps125:2)** *See also* [PSA 11:1](https://ref.ly/Ps11:1), [PSA 90:2](https://ref.ly/Ps90:2); [ISA 54:10](https://ref.ly/Isa54:10)

### Mountains tremble at God's awesome presence

**[JDG 5:5](https://ref.ly/Judg5:5)** *The violent physical response to the presence of the holy God evokes a sense of his might and majesty.* *See also* [EXO 19:16–19:19](https://ref.ly/Exod19:16-Exod19:19); [PSA 97:5](https://ref.ly/Ps97:5); [ISA 64:1](https://ref.ly/Isa64:1); [MIC 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Mic1:3-Mic1:4); [HAB 3:3–3:6](https://ref.ly/Hab3:3-Hab3:6)

### Mountains cannot stand before the anger of God

**[PSA 18:7](https://ref.ly/Ps18:7)** *See also* [JER 4:23–4:26](https://ref.ly/Jer4:23-Jer4:26); [NAM 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Nah1:5-Nah1:6); [REV 6:12–6:14](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:14), [REV 16:19–16:20](https://ref.ly/Rev16:19-Rev16:20)

Mountains as places of encounter with God
-----------------------------------------

**[EXO 34:1–34:3](https://ref.ly/Exod34:1-Exod34:3)** *See also* [1KI 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:11-1Kgs19:13); [2PE 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:16-2Pet1:18)

Specific mountains as the sites of important events in Israel's history
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### The mountains of Abarim: site of Israel's camp

**[NUM 33:47–33:48](https://ref.ly/Num33:47-Num33:48)**

### Mount Ararat: resting place of Noah's ark

**[GEN 8:1–8:5](https://ref.ly/Gen8:1-Gen8:5)**

### Mount Carmel: scene of Elijah's defeat of the prophets of Baal

**[1KI 18:16–18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:16-1Kgs18:40)**

### Mount Ebal: site of cursing; Mount Gerizim: site of blessing

*See also* [DEU 11:29](https://ref.ly/Deut11:29), [DEU 27:12–27:13](https://ref.ly/Deut27:12-Deut27:13); [JOS 8:30–8:35](https://ref.ly/Josh8:30-Josh8:35)

### Mount Gilboa: place of Saul's suicide

**[1CH 10:1–10:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:1-1Chr10:12)** parallels [1SA 31:1–31:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:1-1Sam31:13), [2SA 1:4–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:4-2Sam1:12)

### Mount Hermon: a boundary of Israel

*See also* [DEU 3:8](https://ref.ly/Deut3:8); [JOS 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Josh11:16-Josh11:17); [PSA 42:6–42:7](https://ref.ly/Ps42:6-Ps42:7), [PSA 89:12](https://ref.ly/Ps89:12)

### Mount Hor: place of Aaron's death

**[NUM 33:37–33:39](https://ref.ly/Num33:37-Num33:39)** *See also* [NUM 20:22–20:29](https://ref.ly/Num20:22-Num20:29)

### Mount Moriah: testing place of Abraham

**[GEN 22:1–22:3](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:3)**

### Mount Moriah: site of Solomon's temple

**[2CH 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:1)**

### Mount Nebo: place of Moses' death

**[DEU 32:48–32:52](https://ref.ly/Deut32:48-Deut32:52)** *See also* [DEU 34:1–34:5](https://ref.ly/Deut34:1-Deut34:5)

### Mount Sinai: God gives Moses the law

**[EXO 24:12](https://ref.ly/Exod24:12)** *See also* [EXO 31:18](https://ref.ly/Exod31:18); [NEH 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Neh9:13-Neh9:14)

### Mount Tabor: scene of Sisera's defeat

**[JDG 4:12–4:16](https://ref.ly/Judg4:12-Judg4:16)**

Mountains as the sites of significant events in the NT
------------------------------------------------------

### On the Mount of Temptation the devil offers Jesus Christ the kingdoms of the world

**[MAT 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8-Matt4:9)** parallel [LUK 4:5–4:7](https://ref.ly/Luke4:5-Luke4:7)

### In the Sermon on the Mount Jesus Christ teaches about life in the kingdom of God

**[MAT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Matt5:1-Matt5:2)**

### On the Mount of Transfiguration Jesus Christ's glory is revealed

**[MAT 17:1–17:8](https://ref.ly/Matt17:1-Matt17:8)** parallels [MRK 9:2–9:8](https://ref.ly/Mark9:2-Mark9:8), [LUK 9:28–9:36](https://ref.ly/Luke9:28-Luke9:36)

### On the Mount of Olives Jesus Christ teaches about the destruction of the temple and the last things

**[MAT 24:3](https://ref.ly/Matt24:3)** parallel [MRK 13:3–13:4](https://ref.ly/Mark13:3-Mark13:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 125:2; Psalms 11:1; Psalms 90:2; Isaiah 54:10; Judges 5:5; Exodus 19:16-19; Psalms 97:5; Isaiah 64:1; Micah 1:3-4; Habakkuk 3:3-6; Psalms 18:7; Jeremiah 4:23-26; Nahum 1:5-6; Revelation 6:12-14; Revelation 16:19-20; Exodus 34:1-3; 1 Kings 19:11-13; 2 Peter 1:16-18; Numbers 33:47-48; Genesis 8:1-5; 1 Kings 18:16-40; Deuteronomy 11:29; Deuteronomy 27:12-13; Joshua 8:30-35; 1 Chronicles 10:1-12; 1 Samuel 31:1-13; 2 Samuel 1:4-12; Deuteronomy 3:8; Joshua 11:16-17; Psalms 42:6-7; Psalms 89:12; Numbers 33:37-39; Numbers 20:22-29; Genesis 22:1-3; 2 Chronicles 3:1; Deuteronomy 32:48-52; Deuteronomy 34:1-5; Exodus 24:12; Exodus 31:18; Nehemiah 9:13-14; Judges 4:12-16; Matthew 4:8-9; Luke 4:5-7; Matthew 5:1-2; Matthew 17:1-8; Mark 9:2-8; Luke 9:28-36; Matthew 24:3; Mark 13:3-4

## 4257: pit (id: 4257)

A hole in the ground
--------------------

**[MAT 15:14](https://ref.ly/Matt15:14)** *See also* [EXO 21:33–21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:33-Exod21:34); [2SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:17), [2SA 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:20); [MAT 12:11](https://ref.ly/Matt12:11); [MRK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1); [LUK 6:39](https://ref.ly/Luke6:39)

A pit used as a trap
--------------------

**[PSA 35:7–35:8](https://ref.ly/Ps35:7-Ps35:8)** *See also* [PSA 7:15](https://ref.ly/Ps7:15), [PSA 9:15](https://ref.ly/Ps9:15), [PSA 57:6](https://ref.ly/Ps57:6), [PSA 94:13](https://ref.ly/Ps94:13); [PRO 26:27](https://ref.ly/Prov26:27); [ECC 10:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:8); [ISA 24:17–24:18](https://ref.ly/Isa24:17-Isa24:18); [JER 18:20–18:22](https://ref.ly/Jer18:20-Jer18:22), [JER 48:43–48:44](https://ref.ly/Jer48:43-Jer48:44); [EZK 19:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:4), [EZK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:8)

A pit as a symbol of distress or grief
--------------------------------------

**[PSA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Ps40:2)** *See also* [JOB 9:30–9:31](https://ref.ly/Job9:30-Job9:31); [PSA 88:6](https://ref.ly/Ps88:6); [PRO 22:14](https://ref.ly/Prov22:14), [PRO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Prov23:27); [LAM 3:53–3:55](https://ref.ly/Lam3:53-Lam3:55); [ZEC 9:11](https://ref.ly/Zech9:11)

The pit as a term for the grave
-------------------------------

**[ISA 14:15](https://ref.ly/Isa14:15)** *See also* [JOB 33:18](https://ref.ly/Job33:18), [JOB 33:22](https://ref.ly/Job33:22), [JOB 33:24](https://ref.ly/Job33:24), [JOB 33:28](https://ref.ly/Job33:28), [JOB 33:30](https://ref.ly/Job33:30); [PSA 28:1](https://ref.ly/Ps28:1), [PSA 30:3](https://ref.ly/Ps30:3), [PSA 30:9](https://ref.ly/Ps30:9), [PSA 55:23](https://ref.ly/Ps55:23), [PSA 69:15](https://ref.ly/Ps69:15), [PSA 88:4](https://ref.ly/Ps88:4), [PSA 103:4](https://ref.ly/Ps103:4), [PSA 143:7](https://ref.ly/Ps143:7); [PRO 1:12](https://ref.ly/Prov1:12); [ISA 14:19](https://ref.ly/Isa14:19), [ISA 38:17–38:18](https://ref.ly/Isa38:17-Isa38:18); [EZK 26:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:20), [EZK 28:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:8), [EZK 31:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:14), [EZK 31:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:16), [EZK 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:18), [EZK 32:23–32:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:23-Ezek32:25), [EZK 32:29–32:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:29-Ezek32:30); [JON 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:6)

The pit as a symbol of judgment
-------------------------------

**[ISA 30:33](https://ref.ly/Isa30:33)**

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 15:14; Exodus 21:33-34; 2 Samuel 18:17; 2 Samuel 23:20; Matthew 12:11; Mark 12:1; Luke 6:39; Psalms 35:7-8; Psalms 7:15; Psalms 9:15; Psalms 57:6; Psalms 94:13; Proverbs 26:27; Ecclesiastes 10:8; Isaiah 24:17-18; Jeremiah 18:20-22; Jeremiah 48:43-44; Ezekiel 19:4; Ezekiel 19:8; Psalms 40:2; Job 9:30-31; Psalms 88:6; Proverbs 22:14; Proverbs 23:27; Lamentations 3:53-55; Zechariah 9:11; Isaiah 14:15; Job 33:18; Job 33:22; Job 33:24; Job 33:28; Job 33:30; Psalms 28:1; Psalms 30:3; Psalms 30:9; Psalms 55:23; Psalms 69:15; Psalms 88:4; Psalms 103:4; Psalms 143:7; Proverbs 1:12; Isaiah 14:19; Isaiah 38:17-18; Ezekiel 26:20; Ezekiel 28:8; Ezekiel 31:14; Ezekiel 31:16; Ezekiel 31:18; Ezekiel 32:23-25; Ezekiel 32:29-30; Jonah 2:6; Isaiah 30:33

## 4260: rivers and streams (id: 4260)

The term river is used to refer to all kinds of watercourses, including wadis (dried\-up desert river beds) and permanent rivers. It is also used symbolically to refer to things which threaten or overwhelm people and nations, such as invading nations, the peace of God and the Holy Spirit.

Different kinds of rivers and streams
-------------------------------------

### Wadis

**[1KI 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:7)** *Wadis were dry river beds in summer but became raging torrents in the rainy season.* *See also* [GEN 32:22](https://ref.ly/Gen32:22) *The Jabbok was a wadi, here in full flow*; [NUM 21:13–21:15](https://ref.ly/Num21:13-Num21:15); [JOB 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/Job6:15-Job6:17); [JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18)

### Springs

**[PSA 104:10](https://ref.ly/Ps104:10)** *See also* [GEN 16:7](https://ref.ly/Gen16:7); [EXO 15:27](https://ref.ly/Exod15:27); [JDG 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:14-Judg1:15); [2KI 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:19-2Kgs2:22)

### Permanent rivers

**[GEN 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gen2:10-Gen2:14)** *See also* [GEN 41:1–41:3](https://ref.ly/Gen41:1-Gen41:3); [NUM 22:1](https://ref.ly/Num22:1); [DEU 1:7](https://ref.ly/Deut1:7); [ISA 18:1](https://ref.ly/Isa18:1)

### Canals

**[DAN 8:2](https://ref.ly/Dan8:2)** *See also* [EXO 7:19](https://ref.ly/Exod7:19); [EZR 8:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:21); [EZK 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:1) *Although called a river here, the Kebar was a canal of the Euphrates.*

Some significant rivers and streams in Scripture
------------------------------------------------

### River Arnon

The Arnon flowed through Moab to the mid point of the Dead Sea: [NUM 21:13–21:15](https://ref.ly/Num21:13-Num21:15); ; [JDG 11:18](https://ref.ly/Judg11:18); ; [ISA 16:2](https://ref.ly/Isa16:2); ; [JER 48:20](https://ref.ly/Jer48:20)

### Rivers of Damascus

[2KI 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:12) *The Abana flowed through the city of Damascus and the Pharpar just to the south of it.*

### River Euphrates

The Euphrates flowed through Mesopotamia for some 1,700 miles (2,700 km) from its source in eastern Turkey to the Persian Gulf: [DEU 1:7](https://ref.ly/Deut1:7); ; [2KI 23:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:29) parallel [2CH 35:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:20); ; [ISA 11:15](https://ref.ly/Isa11:15); ; [JER 51:63–51:64](https://ref.ly/Jer51:63-Jer51:64); ; [REV 16:12](https://ref.ly/Rev16:12)

### River Habor

[2KI 17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:6) parallel [2KI 18:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:11) *The Habor was a tributary of the Euphrates.*

### River Jabbok

The Jabbok flowed westward through Gilead into the Jordan about 20 miles (32 km) north of the Dead Sea: [GEN 32:22](https://ref.ly/Gen32:22); ; [NUM 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/Num21:23-Num21:24); ; [DEU 3:16](https://ref.ly/Deut3:16); ; [JDG 11:13](https://ref.ly/Judg11:13)

### River Jordan

The Jordan flowed from the slopes of Mount Hermon through Lake Huleh and the Sea of Galilee, and on into the Dead Sea, providing a fertile basin on either side of the river: [GEN 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Gen13:10-Gen13:11), [GEN 32:9–32:10](https://ref.ly/Gen32:9-Gen32:10); ; [NUM 22:1](https://ref.ly/Num22:1); ; [JOS 3:14–4:9](https://ref.ly/Josh3:14-Josh4:9); ; [2KI 5:8–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:8-2Kgs5:14); ; [MRK 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4-Mark1:5) parallel [MAT 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:5-Matt3:6)

### River Kebar

The Kebar was a canal of the Euphrates in Babylon where the exiles gathered to pray: [EZK 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:1), [EZK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:15), [EZK 43:1–43:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:1-Ezek43:3)

### River Kerith

[1KI 17:2–17:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:2-1Kgs17:7) *The exact location of this wadi is uncertain, but it ran from the east into the Jordan or one of its tributaries.*

### River Kishon

The Kishon flowed north\-west across the Plain of Megiddo and could quickly flood as a result of storms: [JDG 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Judg4:6-Judg4:7), [JDG 4:13](https://ref.ly/Judg4:13), [JDG 5:21](https://ref.ly/Judg5:21); ; [1KI 18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:40); ; [PSA 83:9](https://ref.ly/Ps83:9)

### River Nile

The Nile flowed north some 3,500 miles (5,600 km) from Lake Victoria to the Mediterranean, providing a natural irrigation system in the desert through its annual floodings: [EXO 1:22–2:10](https://ref.ly/Exod1:22-Exod2:10), [EXO 7:17–7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:17-Exod7:21); ; [ISA 19:5–19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:5-Isa19:8); ; [EZK 29:3–29:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:3-Ezek29:5)

### River Tigris

The Tigris flowed from the Armenian mountains through Mesopotamia to join the Euphrates some 40 miles (65 km) north of the Persian Gulf: [GEN 2:14](https://ref.ly/Gen2:14); ; [DAN 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/Dan10:4-Dan10:5); ; [NAM 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Nah2:6-Nah2:8)

The crossing of rivers and streams
----------------------------------

### Rivers crossed by fords

[JOS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Josh2:7); ; [JDG 3:28](https://ref.ly/Judg3:28), [JDG 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Judg12:5-Judg12:6); ; [2SA 17:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:16)

### Rivers crossed by miraculous intervention from God

[JOS 3:14–3:17](https://ref.ly/Josh3:14-Josh3:17); ; [2KI 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:7-2Kgs2:8), [2KI 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:13-2Kgs2:14)

Human uses of rivers and streams
--------------------------------

### As boundaries

**[GEN 15:18](https://ref.ly/Gen15:18)** *See also* [JOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Josh1:2), [JOS 12:2](https://ref.ly/Josh12:2); [1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21)

### For irrigation

**[DEU 11:10](https://ref.ly/Deut11:10)** *The reference may be to a machine operated by foot, or to the foot breaking through the mud walls of the irrigation channel to let the water flow into the fields.* *See also* [EZK 17:5–17:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:5-Ezek17:8), [EZK 19:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10)

### As places of prayer

**[ACT 16:13](https://ref.ly/Acts16:13)** *See also* [PSA 137:1–137:3](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1-Ps137:3); [EZK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:15)

### For ritual washing and baptism

**[2KI 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:10)** *See also* [MRK 1:5](https://ref.ly/Mark1:5) parallel [MAT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:6); [ACT 8:36–8:38](https://ref.ly/Acts8:36-Acts8:38)

God as sovereign Creator is ruler and judge over all rivers and streams
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 74:15](https://ref.ly/Ps74:15)** *See also* [PSA 78:13–78:16](https://ref.ly/Ps78:13-Ps78:16), [PSA 107:33–107:35](https://ref.ly/Ps107:33-Ps107:35); [ISA 19:5–19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:5-Isa19:8); [REV 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Rev8:10-Rev8:11), [REV 16:12](https://ref.ly/Rev16:12)

The symbolic use of rivers and streams
--------------------------------------

### A symbol of threatening circumstances

**[PSA 124:1–124:5](https://ref.ly/Ps124:1-Ps124:5)** *See also* [PSA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Ps32:6), [PSA 88:16–88:17](https://ref.ly/Ps88:16-Ps88:17)

### A symbol of invading nations

**[JER 47:2](https://ref.ly/Jer47:2)** *See also* [ISA 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Isa8:7-Isa8:8); [JER 46:7–46:8](https://ref.ly/Jer46:7-Jer46:8)

### A symbol of God's judgment on his enemies

**[2SA 5:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:20)** *“Baal Perazim” means “the lord who breaks out”. The picture is of a river bursting its banks.* *See also* [JOB 20:28–20:29](https://ref.ly/Job20:28-Job20:29); [ISA 59:19](https://ref.ly/Isa59:19); [NAM 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Nah1:7-Nah1:8)

### A symbol of God's peace

**[ISA 48:18](https://ref.ly/Isa48:18)** *See also* [ISA 66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12)

### A symbol of God's sustaining power

**[PSA 46:4–46:5](https://ref.ly/Ps46:4-Ps46:5)** *See also* [ISA 8:6](https://ref.ly/Isa8:6)

### A symbol of God's provision and blessing

**[JOL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Joel3:18)** *See also* [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3), [PSA 36:8–36:9](https://ref.ly/Ps36:8-Ps36:9); [ISA 30:25](https://ref.ly/Isa30:25)

### A symbol of renewal and life

**[ISA 41:18](https://ref.ly/Isa41:18)** *See also* [EZK 47:1–47:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:1-Ezek47:12); [REV 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:2)

### A symbol of the Holy Spirit

**[JHN 7:37–7:39](https://ref.ly/John7:37-John7:39)** *See also* [ISA 44:3](https://ref.ly/Isa44:3); [JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 17:7; Genesis 32:22; Numbers 21:13-15; Job 6:15-17; Jeremiah 15:18; Psalms 104:10; Genesis 16:7; Exodus 15:27; Judges 1:14-15; 2 Kings 2:19-22; Genesis 2:10-14; Genesis 41:1-3; Numbers 22:1; Deuteronomy 1:7; Isaiah 18:1; Daniel 8:2; Exodus 7:19; Ezra 8:21; Ezekiel 1:1; Judges 11:18; Isaiah 16:2; Jeremiah 48:20; 2 Kings 5:12; 2 Kings 23:29; 2 Chronicles 35:20; Isaiah 11:15; Jeremiah 51:63-64; Revelation 16:12; 2 Kings 17:6; 2 Kings 18:11; Numbers 21:23-24; Deuteronomy 3:16; Judges 11:13; Genesis 13:10-11; Genesis 32:9-10; Joshua 3:14-4:9; 2 Kings 5:8-14; Mark 1:4-5; Matthew 3:5-6; Ezekiel 3:15; Ezekiel 43:1-3; 1 Kings 17:2-7; Judges 4:6-7; Judges 4:13; Judges 5:21; 1 Kings 18:40; Psalms 83:9; Exodus 1:22-2:10; Exodus 7:17-21; Isaiah 19:5-8; Ezekiel 29:3-5; Genesis 2:14; Daniel 10:4-5; Nahum 2:6-8; Joshua 2:7; Judges 3:28; Judges 12:5-6; 2 Samuel 17:16; Joshua 3:14-17; 2 Kings 2:7-8; 2 Kings 2:13-14; Genesis 15:18; Joshua 1:2; Joshua 12:2; 1 Kings 4:21; Deuteronomy 11:10; Ezekiel 17:5-8; Ezekiel 19:10; Acts 16:13; Psalms 137:1-3; 2 Kings 5:10; Mark 1:5; Matthew 3:6; Acts 8:36-38; Psalms 74:15; Psalms 78:13-16; Psalms 107:33-35; Revelation 8:10-11; Psalms 124:1-5; Psalms 32:6; Psalms 88:16-17; Jeremiah 47:2; Isaiah 8:7-8; Jeremiah 46:7-8; 2 Samuel 5:20; Job 20:28-29; Isaiah 59:19; Nahum 1:7-8; Isaiah 48:18; Isaiah 66:12; Psalms 46:4-5; Isaiah 8:6; Joel 3:18; Psalms 1:3; Psalms 36:8-9; Isaiah 30:25; Isaiah 41:18; Ezekiel 47:1-12; Revelation 22:1-2; John 7:37-39; Isaiah 44:3; John 4:13-14
* **Associated Articles:** 3215: Holy Spirit, and peace (ID: `3215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4236: fountain (ID: `4236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4266: sea (ID: `4266@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4293: water (ID: `4293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4296: wells (ID: `4296@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4844: rain (ID: `4844@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4849: snow (ID: `4849@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5580: thirst (ID: `5580@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4263: Rome (id: 4263)

The city state at the heart of the great world empire of NT times, pictured in the book of Revelation as the embodiment of corrupt earthly power. A large church had been established in Rome long before Paul arrived there, towards the end of his life and his mission. His letter to the believers in Rome includes a comprehensive explanation of the gospel.

Rome and the mission of Paul
----------------------------

### Paul plans to visit Rome

**[ACT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Acts19:21)** *See also* [ROM 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:13-Rom1:15), [ROM 15:23–15:29](https://ref.ly/Rom15:23-Rom15:29)

### Jesus Christ confirms that Paul will preach the gospel in Rome

**[ACT 23:11](https://ref.ly/Acts23:11)**

### Paul is sent for trial in Rome

**[ACT 25:10–25:12](https://ref.ly/Acts25:10-Acts25:12)** *See also* [ACT 27:1–27:2](https://ref.ly/Acts27:1-Acts27:2)

### Paul arrives in Rome

**[ACT 28:14–28:16](https://ref.ly/Acts28:14-Acts28:16)**

### Paul preaches in Rome

**[ACT 28:30–28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:30-Acts28:31)** *See also* [ACT 28:23–28:24](https://ref.ly/Acts28:23-Acts28:24); [PHP 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Phil1:12-Phil1:13)

The church in Rome existed long before Paul's arrival
-----------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 15:23–15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:23-Rom15:24)** *See also* [ACT 28:15](https://ref.ly/Acts28:15); [ROM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Rom1:8), [ROM 16:3–16:16](https://ref.ly/Rom16:3-Rom16:16) *Although Paul had never been to Rome, he seemed to be personally acquainted with 26 people associated with the church there, so its overall size was probably quite considerable.*

Paul's letter to the church in Rome presents the whole gospel
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Rom1:16-Rom1:17)** *The gospel is God's offer of salvation to all.*

### Sin and the universal need for salvation

**[ROM 3:23](https://ref.ly/Rom3:23)** *See also* [ROM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Rom3:10), [ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)

### God's \`\`rescue'' through Jesus Christ available to all

**[ROM 3:22](https://ref.ly/Rom3:22)** *See also* [ROM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rom1:16)

### Peace with God through believing in Jesus Christ

**[ROM 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1-Rom5:2)** *See also* [ROM 4:23–4:25](https://ref.ly/Rom4:23-Rom4:25), [ROM 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rom5:8-Rom5:10)

### Gentiles obtain salavation

**[ROM 10:11–10:13](https://ref.ly/Rom10:11-Rom10:13)**

Rome is pictured as the embodiment of corrupt earthly power
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[REV 17:1–17:18](https://ref.ly/Rev17:1-Rev17:18)** *Verse 9 clearly identifies the city as Rome, widely known then as the city built on seven hills.* *See also* [REV 14:8](https://ref.ly/Rev14:8) *Babylon was the great godless city of the OT and here is taken by most commentators to depict Rome, which in turn represents corrupt worldly power in general. However, some believe that only a general reference to the world system is in mind, and others that a literal reference to the actual city of Babylon is intended*; [REV 16:19](https://ref.ly/Rev16:19), [REV 18:1–18:24](https://ref.ly/Rev18:1-Rev18:24)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 19:21; Romans 1:13-15; Romans 15:23-29; Acts 23:11; Acts 25:10-12; Acts 27:1-2; Acts 28:14-16; Acts 28:30-31; Acts 28:23-24; Philippians 1:12-13; Romans 15:23-24; Acts 28:15; Romans 1:8; Romans 16:3-16; Romans 1:16-17; Romans 3:23; Romans 3:10; Romans 6:23; Romans 3:22; Romans 1:16; Romans 5:1-2; Romans 4:23-25; Romans 5:8-10; Romans 10:11-13; Revelation 17:1-18; Revelation 14:8; Revelation 16:19; Revelation 18:1-24
* **Associated Articles:** 2420: gospel (ID: `2420@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4215: Babylon (ID: `4215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5256: city (ID: `5256@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5305: empires (ID: `5305@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5457: power, human (ID: `5457@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5541: society, negative aspects of (ID: `5541@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6615: atonement, necessity and nature of (ID: `6615@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4266: sea (id: 4266)

The sea created and controlled by God
-------------------------------------

**[GEN 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Gen1:9-Gen1:10)**; **[JER 5:22](https://ref.ly/Jer5:22)** *See also* [PSA 95:5](https://ref.ly/Ps95:5), [PSA 107:29](https://ref.ly/Ps107:29), [PSA 146:6](https://ref.ly/Ps146:6); [ISA 50:2](https://ref.ly/Isa50:2); [AMO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8), [AMO 9:6](https://ref.ly/Amos9:6); [ACT 4:24](https://ref.ly/Acts4:24), [ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15)

## 4269: Sinai, Mount (id: 4269)

The mountain in the Sinai peninsula (probably present\-day Jebel Musa), also called Horeb in the OT, where God made a covenant with Israel, establishing them as his people and giving them his laws. In the NT Sinai is seen as symbolising the old covenant.

Mount Sinai and the call of Moses
---------------------------------

**[EXO 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Exod3:1-Exod3:10)** *Most scholars agree that the names Horeb and Sinai are used interchangeably of the same mountain, although some see them as referring to separate peaks within the same range.* *See also* [ACT 7:30–7:34](https://ref.ly/Acts7:30-Acts7:34)

God reveals himself to Israel at Mount Sinai
--------------------------------------------

### The people of Israel arrive at Mount Sinai three months after leaving Egypt

[EXO 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Exod19:1-Exod19:2)

### God promises to make Israel his people

**[EXO 19:3–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:3-Exod19:6)**

### God calls for holiness among his people

*See also* [EXO 19:10–19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:10-Exod19:15), [EXO 19:20–19:24](https://ref.ly/Exod19:20-Exod19:24)

### God appears in a theophany

**[EXO 19:16–19:19](https://ref.ly/Exod19:16-Exod19:19)** *See also* [DEU 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Deut4:10-Deut4:12); [HEB 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Heb12:18-Heb12:21)

God gives his laws to Israel on Mount Sinai
-------------------------------------------

### God gives Israel the Ten Commandments

**[EXO 20:1–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:1-Exod20:17)** parallel [DEU 5:6–5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:21)

### Israel responds with fear to God's revelation

*See also* [EXO 20:18–20:21](https://ref.ly/Exod20:18-Exod20:21); [DEU 5:23–5:27](https://ref.ly/Deut5:23-Deut5:27), [DEU 18:16](https://ref.ly/Deut18:16)

### God gives Israel the Book of the Covenant

[EXO 20:22–23:19](https://ref.ly/Exod20:22-Exod23:19) *These laws, largely an expansion and exposition of the Ten Commandments, are called “the Book of the Covenant” in [Ex 24:7](https://ref.ly/Exod24:7).*

God establishes the covenant with Israel on Mount Sinai
-------------------------------------------------------

### Moses performs the ceremony establishing the covenant

**[EXO 24:4–24:8](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4-Exod24:8)**

### Moses and other leaders ascend Mount Sinai to seal the covenant with a meal before God

**[EXO 24:9–24:11](https://ref.ly/Exod24:9-Exod24:11)**

### Moses ascends Mount Sinai further to receive the stone tablets and the requirements of covenant worship

**[EXO 24:12–24:18](https://ref.ly/Exod24:12-Exod24:18)** *During Moses' 40 days on Mount Sinai, God revealed to him the requirements of covenant worship, as outlined in [Ex 25:1–31:18](https://ref.ly/Exod25:1-Exod31:18).* *See also* [LEV 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/Lev7:37-Lev7:38), [LEV 26:46](https://ref.ly/Lev26:46)

Moses descends Mount Sinai and discovers Israel's idolatry
----------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19)** *See also* [EXO 32:1–32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:6), [EXO 32:21–32:35](https://ref.ly/Exod32:21-Exod32:35); [DEU 9:8–9:21](https://ref.ly/Deut9:8-Deut9:21); [PSA 106:19–106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19-Ps106:23)

God commands Israel to move on from Mount Sinai
-----------------------------------------------

[EXO 33:1–33:6](https://ref.ly/Exod33:1-Exod33:6), [EXO 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Exod33:15-Exod33:16)

God calls Moses up Mount Sinai again
------------------------------------

### Moses is commanded to prepare new stone tablets

[EXO 34:1–34:4](https://ref.ly/Exod34:1-Exod34:4)

### God reveals his nature and name to Moses

**[EXO 34:5–34:7](https://ref.ly/Exod34:5-Exod34:7)**

### God renews his covenant with Israel

*See also* [EXO 34:10–34:28](https://ref.ly/Exod34:10-Exod34:28)

### Moses descends Mount Sinai, his face radiant

[EXO 34:29–34:35](https://ref.ly/Exod34:29-Exod34:35)

The people of Israel prepare to move on from Mount Sinai
--------------------------------------------------------

### God commands Moses to take a census of Israel in the Desert of Sinai

[NUM 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Num1:1-Num1:4), [NUM 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Num3:14-Num3:16)

### The Israelites celebrate the Passover at Mount Sinai

[NUM 9:1–9:5](https://ref.ly/Num9:1-Num9:5)

### The Israelites leave Mount Sinai

[NUM 10:11–10:13](https://ref.ly/Num10:11-Num10:13) *The Israelites had been at Mount Sinai for almost a year (see also [Ex 19:1](https://ref.ly/Exod19:1))*; ; [DEU 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Deut1:6-Deut1:8)

Later generations look back to God's revelation on Mount Sinai
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[JDG 5:5](https://ref.ly/Judg5:5)** *Ascribing the title “the One of Sinai” to God, Deborah acknowledges the great importance of his revelation at Mount Sinai.* *See also* [1KI 19:8–19:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:8-1Kgs19:9) *Elijah's return to Horeb, the mountain of God, shows his need to hear God clearly again*; [NEH 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Neh9:13-Neh9:14); [PSA 68:7–68:8](https://ref.ly/Ps68:7-Ps68:8), [PSA 106:19–106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19-Ps106:23); [MAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mal4:4); [ACT 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/Acts7:37-Acts7:38)

Mount Sinai seen by NT writers as a symbol of the old covenant
--------------------------------------------------------------

[GAL 4:24–4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:24-Gal4:31) *Hagar, Mount Sinai and the present city of Jerusalem stand for the old covenant and bondage to the law, while Sarah and the new heavenly Jerusalem stand for the new covenant and living by faith*; ; [HEB 12:18–12:29](https://ref.ly/Heb12:18-Heb12:29)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 3:1-10; Acts 7:30-34; Exodus 19:1-2; Exodus 19:3-6; Exodus 19:10-15; Exodus 19:20-24; Exodus 19:16-19; Deuteronomy 4:10-12; Hebrews 12:18-21; Exodus 20:1-17; Deuteronomy 5:6-21; Exodus 20:18-21; Deuteronomy 5:23-27; Deuteronomy 18:16; Exodus 20:22-23:19; Exodus 24:7; Exodus 24:4-8; Exodus 24:9-11; Exodus 24:12-18; Exodus 25:1-31:18; Leviticus 7:37-38; Leviticus 26:46; Exodus 32:19; Exodus 32:1-6; Exodus 32:21-35; Deuteronomy 9:8-21; Psalms 106:19-23; Exodus 33:1-6; Exodus 33:15-16; Exodus 34:1-4; Exodus 34:5-7; Exodus 34:10-28; Exodus 34:29-35; Numbers 1:1-4; Numbers 3:14-16; Numbers 9:1-5; Numbers 10:11-13; Exodus 19:1; Deuteronomy 1:6-8; Judges 5:5; 1 Kings 19:8-9; Nehemiah 9:13-14; Psalms 68:7-8; Malachi 4:4; Acts 7:37-38; Galatians 4:24-31; Hebrews 12:18-29

## 4275: Sodom and Gomorrah (id: 4275)

Two neighbouring cities, probably located at the eastern or south\-eastern end of the Dead Sea, noted for their sinfulness. God's response in wrath and judgment serves as a warning to subsequent generations.

Sodom and Gomorrah's location
-----------------------------

[GEN 10:19](https://ref.ly/Gen10:19), [GEN 13:10–13:12](https://ref.ly/Gen13:10-Gen13:12)

Sodom became Lot's home
-----------------------

[GEN 13:10–13:12](https://ref.ly/Gen13:10-Gen13:12)

Sodom and Gomorrah at war
-------------------------

### Sodom and Gomorrah, with other cities of the plain, defeated by the alliance of four kings

[GEN 14:1–14:12](https://ref.ly/Gen14:1-Gen14:12)

### Abram rescues Lot and is honoured by the king of Sodom and his allies

[GEN 14:13–14:24](https://ref.ly/Gen14:13-Gen14:24)

The wickedness of Sodom and Gomorrah
------------------------------------

**[GEN 13:13](https://ref.ly/Gen13:13)** *See also* [GEN 18:20–18:21](https://ref.ly/Gen18:20-Gen18:21), [GEN 19:1–19:9](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:9)

Abraham pleads for the righteous in Sodom and Gomorrah
------------------------------------------------------

[GEN 18:16–18:32](https://ref.ly/Gen18:16-Gen18:32)

-----------------------------------------

**[GEN 19:13](https://ref.ly/Gen19:13)** *See also* [GEN 19:23–19:29](https://ref.ly/Gen19:23-Gen19:29); [DEU 29:23](https://ref.ly/Deut29:23); [LUK 17:29](https://ref.ly/Luke17:29)

The story of Sodom and Gomorrah becomes a picture of human sinfulness and divine judgment
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4278: spring of water (id: 4278)

Springs of water were important to the life of Israel, providing fresh water for the people. Both God and the gospel are often compared to springs of living water, on account of their life\-bringing and refreshing qualities.

The symbolic importance of springs of water to Israel
-----------------------------------------------------

### Living water as a sign of hope and prosperity

**[ZEC 14:8](https://ref.ly/Zech14:8)** *See also* [ISA 12:3](https://ref.ly/Isa12:3), [ISA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Isa49:10); [JER 31:9](https://ref.ly/Jer31:9)

### Living water as a source of fertility

**[JER 17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:8)** *See also* [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3)

### Living water as a sign of God's presence and power

[EXO 17:1–17:7](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1-Exod17:7); ; [NUM 20:1–20:11](https://ref.ly/Num20:1-Num20:11)

God as a spring of water
------------------------

**[JER 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:13)** *See also* [JER 17:13](https://ref.ly/Jer17:13)

The gospel as a spring of water
-------------------------------

**[JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14)** *See also* [PSA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:2); [ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1); [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [REV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

Other biblical uses of the image of a spring
--------------------------------------------

[PRO 25:26](https://ref.ly/Prov25:26) *spoiling what is good*; ; [JAS 3:9–3:11](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9-Jas3:11) *the tongue compared to a spring*; ; [2PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17) *a dry spring as a symbol of sterility*

* **Associated Articles:** 3120: Holy Spirit, descriptions of (ID: `3120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3290: Holy Spirit, the life-giver (ID: `3290@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4018: life, spiritual (ID: `4018@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4221: cistern (ID: `4221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4236: fountain (ID: `4236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4260: rivers and streams (ID: `4260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4293: water (ID: `4293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4296: wells (ID: `4296@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5580: thirst (ID: `5580@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4281: stars (id: 4281)

Heavenly luminaries other than the sun and moon, often worshipped as gods by pagan nations. Scripture stresses that the stars were created by God, and are subject to him. Stars are often treated as symbols of the generosity of God, and his faithfulness to his promises.

The relationship between God and the stars
------------------------------------------

### God is the creator

**[PSA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Ps33:6)** *See also* [GEN 1:16](https://ref.ly/Gen1:16); [NEH 9:6](https://ref.ly/Neh9:6); [PSA 136:7–136:9](https://ref.ly/Ps136:7-Ps136:9), [PSA 148:3–148:6](https://ref.ly/Ps148:3-Ps148:6)

### The stars demonstrate the power of their maker

**[ISA 40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:26)** *See also* [JOB 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/Job9:7-Job9:9), [JOB 38:31–38:33](https://ref.ly/Job38:31-Job38:33); [PSA 147:4–147:5](https://ref.ly/Ps147:4-Ps147:5); [ISA 45:11–45:12](https://ref.ly/Isa45:11-Isa45:12); [AMO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8)

Worship of the stars
--------------------

### Prohibited by God

**[DEU 4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:19)** *See also* [DEU 17:3](https://ref.ly/Deut17:3); [2KI 23:4–23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:4-2Kgs23:5)

### God's judgment of star worshippers

**[JER 8:2](https://ref.ly/Jer8:2)** *See also* [JER 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:12-Jer19:13); [AMO 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Amos5:25-Amos5:27); [ZEP 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:4-Zeph1:5); [ACT 7:42–7:43](https://ref.ly/Acts7:42-Acts7:43)

### Examples of star worship

[2KI 17:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:16), [2KI 21:3–21:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3-2Kgs21:5); ; [2CH 33:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:5); ; [ACT 28:11](https://ref.ly/Acts28:11)

Reading the stars
-----------------

**[ISA 47:13](https://ref.ly/Isa47:13)** *See also* [DAN 2:2–2:5](https://ref.ly/Dan2:2-Dan2:5), [DAN 4:7](https://ref.ly/Dan4:7)

Signs in the stars
------------------

**[ISA 34:4](https://ref.ly/Isa34:4)** *See also* [EZK 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:7-Ezek32:8); [REV 6:13](https://ref.ly/Rev6:13)

The star of Bethlehem
---------------------

**[MAT 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Matt2:9-Matt2:10)** *See also* [MAT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:2), [MAT 2:7](https://ref.ly/Matt2:7)

The stars illustrating God's promise to Abraham
-----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 15:5](https://ref.ly/Gen15:5)** *See also* [GEN 22:17](https://ref.ly/Gen22:17); [DEU 10:22](https://ref.ly/Deut10:22); [1CH 27:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:23); [NEH 9:23](https://ref.ly/Neh9:23); [JER 33:22](https://ref.ly/Jer33:22); [HEB 11:12](https://ref.ly/Heb11:12)

Metaphorical references to the stars
------------------------------------

### A coming ruler

**[NUM 24:17](https://ref.ly/Num24:17)**

### The morning star

**[REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Rev22:16)** *See also* [ISA 14:12–14:13](https://ref.ly/Isa14:12-Isa14:13); [2PE 1:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:19); [REV 2:28](https://ref.ly/Rev2:28)

### Wandering stars

[JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/Jude1:13)

### Stars representing angels

[REV 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rev1:16), [REV 1:20](https://ref.ly/Rev1:20)

### God's people shining like stars

**[PHP 2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:15)** *See also* [DAN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Dan12:3)
