## 4284: sun (id: 4284)

Created as a source of light and heat and an indicator of time and direction. The sun is also used as a metaphor for the splendour and light of God himself and the righteousness of his people.

Natural functions of the sun
----------------------------

### A source of light

**[GEN 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:16-Gen1:18)** *See also* [PSA 136:7–136:8](https://ref.ly/Ps136:7-Ps136:8)

### A marker of time

**[GEN 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14)** *See also* [GEN 29:7](https://ref.ly/Gen29:7); [EXO 22:26](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26); [JDG 9:33](https://ref.ly/Judg9:33); [1SA 11:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:9)

### An indicator of direction

**[NUM 21:11](https://ref.ly/Num21:11)** *See also* [EXO 27:13](https://ref.ly/Exod27:13); [DEU 11:30](https://ref.ly/Deut11:30); [JOS 19:12](https://ref.ly/Josh19:12); [PSA 113:3](https://ref.ly/Ps113:3); [ISA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Isa45:6)

### A blessing, necessary for life

**[ECC 11:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:7)** *See also* [DEU 33:13–33:14](https://ref.ly/Deut33:13-Deut33:14); [JOB 8:16](https://ref.ly/Job8:16); [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)

### The scorching heat causing discomfort and harm

**[JON 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:8)** *See also* [PSA 121:6](https://ref.ly/Ps121:6); [ISA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Isa49:10); [MAT 13:6](https://ref.ly/Matt13:6); [JAS 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11); [REV 7:16](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16)

Worship of the sun
------------------

### Forbidden to God's people

**[DEU 4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:19)** *See also* [DEU 17:2–17:5](https://ref.ly/Deut17:2-Deut17:5); [JOB 31:26–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:26-Job31:28)

### Practised in Israel and Egypt

**[EZK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16)** *See also* [2KI 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:5), [2KI 23:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:11); [JER 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Jer8:1-Jer8:2), [JER 43:13](https://ref.ly/Jer43:13)

Sunlight and God's judgment
---------------------------

**[JOL 2:31](https://ref.ly/Joel2:31)**; **[MAT 24:29–24:30](https://ref.ly/Matt24:29-Matt24:30)** parallels [MRK 13:24](https://ref.ly/Mark13:24), [LUK 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/Luke21:25-Luke21:26) *See also* [EXO 10:21–10:23](https://ref.ly/Exod10:21-Exod10:23); [ISA 13:9–13:10](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9-Isa13:10); [JER 4:28](https://ref.ly/Jer4:28); [EZK 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:7-Ezek32:8); [JOL 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Joel3:14-Joel3:15); [AMO 5:20](https://ref.ly/Amos5:20); [MAT 27:45](https://ref.ly/Matt27:45) parallels [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33), [LUK 23:44](https://ref.ly/Luke23:44) *the crucifixion*; [ACT 2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:20); [REV 6:12–6:17](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:17)

God and the sun
---------------

### God as creator and controller

**[JOB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Job9:7)**; **[PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16)** *See also* [JOS 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Josh10:12-Josh10:13); [2KI 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:8-2Kgs20:11); [PSA 19:1–19:6](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:6), [PSA 148:3](https://ref.ly/Ps148:3), [PSA 148:5](https://ref.ly/Ps148:5); [ISA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:8); [JER 31:35–31:36](https://ref.ly/Jer31:35-Jer31:36); [HAB 3:11](https://ref.ly/Hab3:11)

### God likened to the sun

**[LUK 1:78–1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78-Luke1:79)** *See also* [PSA 84:11](https://ref.ly/Ps84:11); [ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2); [HAB 3:4](https://ref.ly/Hab3:4); [MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2); [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2); [2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:6); [REV 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rev1:16)

### God's glory outshines the sun

**[REV 21:23](https://ref.ly/Rev21:23)** *See also* [ISA 24:23](https://ref.ly/Isa24:23), [ISA 60:19–60:20](https://ref.ly/Isa60:19-Isa60:20); [ACT 26:13](https://ref.ly/Acts26:13); [REV 22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:5)

Metaphorical allusions to the sun
---------------------------------

### In Paul's conversion experience

**[ACT 26:13](https://ref.ly/Acts26:13)**

### As righteousness

**[PSA 37:6](https://ref.ly/Ps37:6)**; **[MAT 13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:43)** *See also* [2SA 23:3–23:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:3-2Sam23:4); [PRO 4:18](https://ref.ly/Prov4:18); [ISA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8), [ISA 58:10](https://ref.ly/Isa58:10), [ISA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Isa62:1); [DAN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Dan12:3)

### As constancy and dependability

**[HOS 6:3](https://ref.ly/Hos6:3)** *See also* [PSA 72:5](https://ref.ly/Ps72:5), [PSA 72:17](https://ref.ly/Ps72:17), [PSA 89:36](https://ref.ly/Ps89:36)

### The setting sun signifying a premature end

[JER 15:9](https://ref.ly/Jer15:9); ; [AMO 8:9](https://ref.ly/Amos8:9); ; [MIC 3:6](https://ref.ly/Mic3:6)

## 4287: universe (id: 4287)

All existing things, including the earth and its creatures and all the heavenly bodies. The universe was made by God and is sustained by Jesus Christ.

God as the Maker of all things
------------------------------

**[PSA 115:15](https://ref.ly/Ps115:15)**; **[JER 10:16](https://ref.ly/Jer10:16)**; **[REV 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:11)** *See also* [GEN 14:19](https://ref.ly/Gen14:19); [2KI 19:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:15); [ECC 11:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:5); [ISA 37:16](https://ref.ly/Isa37:16), [ISA 44:24](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24); [ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15); [ROM 11:33–11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:33-Rom11:36); [1CO 8:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:6); [EPH 3:9](https://ref.ly/Eph3:9); [HEB 11:3](https://ref.ly/Heb11:3)

God as the ruler of the universe
--------------------------------

**[1CH 29:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:12)**; **[ACT 17:24](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24)** *See also* [PSA 119:91](https://ref.ly/Ps119:91); [JER 33:25](https://ref.ly/Jer33:25); [MAT 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25)

Jesus Christ, the one through whom the universe was made, now sustains all things
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 1:3](https://ref.ly/John1:3)**; **[COL 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:15-Col1:17)** *The title “firstborn” implies that Jesus Christ is the heir, that everything belongs to him*; **[HEB 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2-Heb1:3)** *See also* [JHN 13:3](https://ref.ly/John13:3); [1CO 8:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:6); [EPH 1:22](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22), [EPH 4:10](https://ref.ly/Eph4:10)

The universe is temporary
-------------------------

**[MAT 24:35](https://ref.ly/Matt24:35)**; **[2PE 3:10–3:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10-2Pet3:12)** *See also* [MAT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Matt5:18), [MAT 24:29](https://ref.ly/Matt24:29); [LUK 21:26](https://ref.ly/Luke21:26)

Future renewal of the universe
------------------------------

**[EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10)**; **[2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13)** *See also* [ISA 65:17](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17), [ISA 66:22](https://ref.ly/Isa66:22); [MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28); [ROM 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19-Rom8:22); [EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10)

Other references to the universe
--------------------------------

[DEU 4:25–4:26](https://ref.ly/Deut4:25-Deut4:26), [DEU 30:19](https://ref.ly/Deut30:19); ; [PHP 2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:15)

## 4290: valleys (id: 4290)

Valleys used as places of permanent settlement
----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 26:17](https://ref.ly/Gen26:17)** *See also* [NUM 14:25](https://ref.ly/Num14:25); [JOS 17:16](https://ref.ly/Josh17:16); [JDG 18:28](https://ref.ly/Judg18:28); [1CH 12:14–12:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:14-1Chr12:15)

Valleys as places for camp\-sites
---------------------------------

**[JDG 6:33](https://ref.ly/Judg6:33)** *See also* [NUM 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Num21:10-Num21:12); [DEU 3:29](https://ref.ly/Deut3:29); [JDG 7:8–7:12](https://ref.ly/Judg7:8-Judg7:12); [1CH 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:15)

Valleys used for pasture and crops
----------------------------------

**[1CH 4:39–4:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:39-1Chr4:40)** *Palestine was noted as a dry land, but valleys provided the necessary moisture for pastures and crop growth.* *See also* [1CH 27:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:29); [PSA 65:13](https://ref.ly/Ps65:13)

Valleys used for battles
------------------------

**[GEN 14:8](https://ref.ly/Gen14:8)** *See also* [JOS 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/Josh11:7-Josh11:8); [1SA 17:1–17:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:1-1Sam17:3); [2SA 8:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:13); [2CH 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:11-2Chr25:12) parallel [2KI 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:7)

Valleys as places of worship and sacrifice
------------------------------------------

**[2CH 28:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:3)** *See also* [DEU 21:1–21:9](https://ref.ly/Deut21:1-Deut21:9); [2CH 20:25–20:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:25-2Chr20:26); [JER 2:23–2:25](https://ref.ly/Jer2:23-Jer2:25), [JER 32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:35)

Valleys on the day of the LORD
------------------------------

[ISA 22:5](https://ref.ly/Isa22:5); ; [MIC 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Mic1:3-Mic1:4); ; [ZEC 14:3–14:5](https://ref.ly/Zech14:3-Zech14:5)

The metaphorical use of valleys
-------------------------------

### The valley of death

**[PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4)** *See also* [JOB 21:32–21:33](https://ref.ly/Job21:32-Job21:33)

### The valley of decision

**[JOL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Joel3:14)** *See also* [JOL 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Joel3:1-Joel3:2), [JOL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Joel3:12)

### The valley of dry bones

**[EZK 37:1–37:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:1-Ezek37:14)**

### The levelling of valleys as a preparation for the coming of the Lord

**[LUK 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Luke3:4-Luke3:5)** *See also* [ISA 40:3–40:4](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3-Isa40:4)

The Valley of Hinnom
--------------------

### The Valley of Hinnom as part of the boundary between Judah and Benjamin, lying to the south of Jerusalem

[JOS 15:8](https://ref.ly/Josh15:8), [JOS 18:16](https://ref.ly/Josh18:16)

### The Valley of Hinnom as a place of child sacrifice

**[2CH 28:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:3)** *See also* [JER 32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:35)

### The Valley of Hinnom as a place of God's judgment

**[JER 7:30–7:32](https://ref.ly/Jer7:30-Jer7:32)** *See also* [JER 19:1–19:15](https://ref.ly/Jer19:1-Jer19:15)

The Kidron Valley
-----------------

### The Kidron Valley as a boundary of Jerusalem

**[1KI 2:36–2:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:36-1Kgs2:37)** *See also* [2SA 15:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:23); [JER 31:38–31:40](https://ref.ly/Jer31:38-Jer31:40); [JHN 18:1](https://ref.ly/John18:1)

### The Kidron Valley as a place for the destruction of pagan cult objects

## 4293: water (id: 4293)

The colourless, odourless liquid that is essential for sustenance of life in human beings and animals. God is described as the spring of living water, being the source of life and salvation to those who come to him.

Water for drinking
------------------

### For human beings

**[2SA 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:15-2Sam23:16)** parallel [1CH 11:17–11:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:17-1Chr11:18) *See also* [GEN 21:14–21:19](https://ref.ly/Gen21:14-Gen21:19) *God provides water for Hagar and Ishmael*; [EXO 15:22–15:27](https://ref.ly/Exod15:22-Exod15:27), [EXO 17:1–17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1-Exod17:3); [NUM 20:1–20:11](https://ref.ly/Num20:1-Num20:11); [DEU 2:28](https://ref.ly/Deut2:28); [JDG 4:19](https://ref.ly/Judg4:19), [JDG 5:25](https://ref.ly/Judg5:25); [1SA 30:11–30:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:11-1Sam30:12); [1KI 17:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:10) *Elijah asks the widow at Zarephath for a drink of water*; [2KI 6:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:22); [2CH 18:25–18:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:25-2Chr18:26); [NEH 9:20](https://ref.ly/Neh9:20); [JOB 6:19](https://ref.ly/Job6:19); [ISA 33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:16); [DAN 1:8–1:16](https://ref.ly/Dan1:8-Dan1:16) *Daniel's resolve to live on vegetables and water*; [HOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hos2:5); [MAT 10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:42) parallel [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41); [JHN 4:7](https://ref.ly/John4:7); [1TI 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:23)

### For animals

**[2KI 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:9)** *See also* [GEN 24:13–24:19](https://ref.ly/Gen24:13-Gen24:19), [GEN 29:1–29:10](https://ref.ly/Gen29:1-Gen29:10); [EXO 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15-Exod2:16), [EXO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:3); [2KI 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:17); [PSA 104:10–104:11](https://ref.ly/Ps104:10-Ps104:11); [ISA 43:20](https://ref.ly/Isa43:20); [LUK 13:15](https://ref.ly/Luke13:15)

Living water
------------

## 4296: wells (id: 4296)

Artificial openings to tap underground water supplies. They were very important for life in a settled urban society and this is reflected in their symbolical use in Scripture.

The importance of wells in urban life
-------------------------------------

**[NEH 9:25](https://ref.ly/Neh9:25)** *See also* [DEU 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:11)

Women drew water for domestic use
---------------------------------

**[GEN 24:11–24:20](https://ref.ly/Gen24:11-Gen24:20)** *See also* [1SA 9:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:11); [JHN 4:7–4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:7-John4:9)

Livestock were watered at wells
-------------------------------

**[GEN 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/Gen29:2-Gen29:3)** *See also* [GEN 29:7–29:10](https://ref.ly/Gen29:7-Gen29:10); [EXO 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15-Exod2:17)

Wells were sometimes a source of contention
-------------------------------------------

**[GEN 21:25](https://ref.ly/Gen21:25)** *See also* [GEN 26:12–26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:12-Gen26:22); [NUM 20:17](https://ref.ly/Num20:17), [NUM 21:22](https://ref.ly/Num21:22)

Wells as landmarks
------------------

**[GEN 16:7](https://ref.ly/Gen16:7)** *Verse 14 refers to this spring as a well.* *See also* [GEN 16:14](https://ref.ly/Gen16:14), [GEN 21:31](https://ref.ly/Gen21:31) *“Beersheba” means either “well of seven” or “well of the oath”*; [GEN 26:20–26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:20-Gen26:22); [JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6)

Figurative use of wells
-----------------------

### Associated with God and his goodness

**[ISA 12:3](https://ref.ly/Isa12:3)**; **[JER 17:13](https://ref.ly/Jer17:13)** *See also* [JER 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:13); [JHN 4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:14); [REV 7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:17)

### Associated with human love

[PRO 5:15](https://ref.ly/Prov5:15)

### In warnings against and judgment of sin

**[JER 6:7](https://ref.ly/Jer6:7)** *See also* [PRO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Prov23:27); [JER 51:36](https://ref.ly/Jer51:36)

## 4303: metals (id: 4303)

Gold, silver, bronze, iron, copper, tin and lead are all mentioned in Scripture. Prized for their value, they were used in a variety of ways. Scripture uses as an image a metal refiner's fire for the purification of the lives of believers.

Gold
----

**[JOB 23:10](https://ref.ly/Job23:10)** *See also* [EXO 25:10–25:22](https://ref.ly/Exod25:10-Exod25:22), [EXO 38:21–38:24](https://ref.ly/Exod38:21-Exod38:24); [1KI 6:27–6:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:27-1Kgs6:35), [1KI 7:48–7:50](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:48-1Kgs7:50), [1KI 10:14–10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14-1Kgs10:21); [PSA 19:7–19:11](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7-Ps19:11); [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18), [REV 17:4](https://ref.ly/Rev17:4)

Silver
------

**[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)** *See also* [GEN 37:26–37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:26-Gen37:28); [EXO 38:25–38:28](https://ref.ly/Exod38:25-Exod38:28); [2CH 9:26–9:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:26-2Chr9:27); [PSA 119:72](https://ref.ly/Ps119:72); [MAT 27:3–27:10](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:10)

----

**[DEU 3:11](https://ref.ly/Deut3:11)** *See also* [DEU 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:9); [JDG 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Judg4:2-Judg4:3); [JOB 40:15–40:18](https://ref.ly/Job40:15-Job40:18); [EZK 27:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:19)

**[JOB 28:2](https://ref.ly/Job28:2)** *See also* [MRK 12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:44) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4)

Tin
---

**[EZK 27:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:12)** *See also* [EZK 22:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:18)

Lead
----

### It was an exceptionally heavy metal

**[EXO 15:10](https://ref.ly/Exod15:10)** *See also* [ZEC 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/Zech5:5-Zech5:8)

### It was refined before use

**[JER 6:29](https://ref.ly/Jer6:29)** *See also* [NUM 31:21–31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:21-Num31:23); [EZK 22:18–22:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:18-Ezek22:20)

### Inscriptions were sometimes made on lead

**[JOB 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/Job19:23-Job19:24)**

### It was traded between nations

**[EZK 27:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:12)**

The value of metals
-------------------

### All metals were of value

**[JOS 22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:8)** *See also* [1CH 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:10), [1CH 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14), [1CH 29:1–29:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1-1Chr29:7); [REV 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:12)

### The relative value of metals

**[ISA 60:17](https://ref.ly/Isa60:17)** *See also* [1KI 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:21) parallel [2CH 9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:20); [1KI 14:25–14:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:25-1Kgs14:28)

Trade with metals
-----------------

**[1KI 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:22)** parallel [2CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:21) *See also* [GEN 37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:28); [1KI 10:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:11), [1KI 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14-1Kgs10:15) parallel [2CH 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13-2Chr9:14); [1KI 22:48](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:48); [EZK 27:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:13), [EZK 27:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:22); [ZEP 1:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:11)

Idols were often made from various metals
-----------------------------------------

**[LEV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Lev19:4)** *See also* [1KI 14:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:9); [PSA 106:19](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19); [ISA 48:5](https://ref.ly/Isa48:5); [DAN 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:22-Dan5:23), [DAN 11:8](https://ref.ly/Dan11:8)

The refining of metals as an image of purification
--------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 48:10](https://ref.ly/Isa48:10)**; **[MAL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2)** *See also* [PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6); [ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9)

-----

Metals

## 4312: bronze (id: 4312)

A metal highly prized for its strength and durability. It had a variety of uses in biblical times and was employed extensively in both the tabernacle and temple.

Bronze as a material used by craftsmen
--------------------------------------

**[GEN 4:22](https://ref.ly/Gen4:22)** *See also* [EXO 31:1–31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:6) parallel [EXO 35:30–35:34](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:34); [1KI 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:13-1Kgs7:14); [1CH 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:15-1Chr22:16); [2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7), [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14), [2CH 24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:12)

Characteristics of bronze
-------------------------

### It was renowned for its strength

**[JER 15:12](https://ref.ly/Jer15:12)** *See also* [LEV 26:18–26:19](https://ref.ly/Lev26:18-Lev26:19); [DEU 28:23](https://ref.ly/Deut28:23); [JOB 6:12](https://ref.ly/Job6:12), [JOB 37:18](https://ref.ly/Job37:18), [JOB 40:18](https://ref.ly/Job40:18), [JOB 41:27](https://ref.ly/Job41:27); [ISA 48:4](https://ref.ly/Isa48:4); [JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18), [JER 15:20](https://ref.ly/Jer15:20); [DAN 7:19](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19); [MIC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:13)

### It was noted for its brilliance

**[DAN 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Dan10:5-Dan10:6)** *See also* [EZK 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:3); [REV 1:15](https://ref.ly/Rev1:15), [REV 2:18](https://ref.ly/Rev2:18)

The monetary value of bronze
----------------------------

### It was an expensive metal

**[REV 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:12)** *See also* [1CH 18:8–18:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:8-1Chr18:10); [EZR 8:26–8:27](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:26-Ezra8:27); [EZK 27:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:13)

### It was less valuable than gold or silver

**[ISA 60:17](https://ref.ly/Isa60:17)** *See also* [1KI 14:25–14:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:25-1Kgs14:28) parallel [2CH 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:9-2Chr12:11)

Offerings of bronze were made at various times
----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 35:24](https://ref.ly/Exod35:24)** *See also* [EXO 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/Exod25:1-Exod25:7) parallel [EXO 35:4–35:9](https://ref.ly/Exod35:4-Exod35:9); [EXO 38:29–38:31](https://ref.ly/Exod38:29-Exod38:31); [1CH 18:9–18:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:9-1Chr18:11), [1CH 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14), [1CH 29:6–29:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:6-1Chr29:7)

Bronze was used in the tabernacle
---------------------------------

### For the altar and its utensils

**[2CH 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:5-2Chr1:6)** *See also* [EXO 27:1–27:6](https://ref.ly/Exod27:1-Exod27:6) parallel [EXO 38:1–38:6](https://ref.ly/Exod38:1-Exod38:6); [EXO 35:16](https://ref.ly/Exod35:16) parallel [EXO 39:39](https://ref.ly/Exod39:39); [NUM 4:13](https://ref.ly/Num4:13), [NUM 16:39–16:40](https://ref.ly/Num16:39-Num16:40)

### For the laver

**[EXO 30:18](https://ref.ly/Exod30:18)** *See also* [EXO 35:16](https://ref.ly/Exod35:16), [EXO 38:8](https://ref.ly/Exod38:8)

### For the tent and the framework of the tabernacle

**[EXO 27:17–27:19](https://ref.ly/Exod27:17-Exod27:19)** parallel [EXO 38:17–38:20](https://ref.ly/Exod38:17-Exod38:20) *See also* [EXO 26:10–26:11](https://ref.ly/Exod26:10-Exod26:11), [EXO 26:36–26:37](https://ref.ly/Exod26:36-Exod26:37), [EXO 27:9–27:11](https://ref.ly/Exod27:9-Exod27:11) parallel [EXO 38:9–38:11](https://ref.ly/Exod38:9-Exod38:11); [EXO 36:18](https://ref.ly/Exod36:18), [EXO 36:37–36:38](https://ref.ly/Exod36:37-Exod36:38)

Bronze was used extensively in the temple
-----------------------------------------

**[2CH 4:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:18)** *See also* [1KI 7:23–7:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:23-1Kgs7:33), [1KI 7:38–7:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:38-1Kgs7:45) parallel [2CH 4:9–4:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:9-2Chr4:16); [1KI 8:64](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:64); [2KI 16:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:17), [2KI 25:13–25:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:13-2Kgs25:17) parallel [JER 52:17–52:23](https://ref.ly/Jer52:17-Jer52:23); [1CH 18:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:8), [1CH 29:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:2); [2CH 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:1)

Other uses of bronze
--------------------

### For armour

**[1SA 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5-1Sam17:6)** *See also* [1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38)

### For weapons

**[2SA 21:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:16)** *See also* [1SA 17:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:6); [2SA 22:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:35) parallel [PSA 18:34](https://ref.ly/Ps18:34)

### For shackles

**[JDG 16:21](https://ref.ly/Judg16:21)** *See also* [2KI 25:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:7) parallels [JER 39:7](https://ref.ly/Jer39:7), [JER 52:11](https://ref.ly/Jer52:11); [2CH 33:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:11), [2CH 36:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:6); [DAN 4:15](https://ref.ly/Dan4:15), [DAN 4:23](https://ref.ly/Dan4:23)

### For gates

**[PSA 107:16](https://ref.ly/Ps107:16)** *See also* [DEU 33:25](https://ref.ly/Deut33:25); [1KI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:13); [1CH 22:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:3); [ISA 45:2](https://ref.ly/Isa45:2)

### For the bronze snake

**[NUM 21:9](https://ref.ly/Num21:9)** *See also* [2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4)

### For idols

**[DAN 5:4](https://ref.ly/Dan5:4)** *See also* [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23); [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20)

### For mirrors

**[EXO 38:8](https://ref.ly/Exod38:8)** *See also* [JOB 37:18](https://ref.ly/Job37:18)

### For cooking utensils

**[LEV 6:28](https://ref.ly/Lev6:28)**

### For tools

**[GEN 4:22](https://ref.ly/Gen4:22)**

### For musical instruments

**[1CH 15:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:19)**

### For statues

*See also* [DAN 2:32](https://ref.ly/Dan2:32), [DAN 2:35](https://ref.ly/Dan2:35), [DAN 2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:45)

Bronze as booty in war
----------------------

**[JOS 22:7–22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:7-Josh22:8)** *See also* [NUM 31:21–31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:21-Num31:23); [JOS 6:24](https://ref.ly/Josh6:24); [2SA 8:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:8) parallel [1CH 18:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:8)

The metaphorical use of bronze
------------------------------

**[DAN 2:39](https://ref.ly/Dan2:39)** *See also* [LEV 26:19](https://ref.ly/Lev26:19); [DEU 28:23](https://ref.ly/Deut28:23); [JOB 40:18](https://ref.ly/Job40:18); [JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18), [JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18); [ZEC 6:1](https://ref.ly/Zech6:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 4:22; Exodus 31:1-6; Exodus 35:30-34; 1 Kings 7:13-14; 1 Chronicles 22:15-16; 2 Chronicles 2:7; 2 Chronicles 2:13-14; 2 Chronicles 24:12; Jeremiah 15:12; Leviticus 26:18-19; Deuteronomy 28:23; Job 6:12; Job 37:18; Job 40:18; Job 41:27; Isaiah 48:4; Jeremiah 1:18; Jeremiah 15:20; Daniel 7:19; Micah 4:13; Daniel 10:5-6; Ezekiel 40:3; Revelation 1:15; Revelation 2:18; Revelation 18:11-12; 1 Chronicles 18:8-10; Ezra 8:26-27; Ezekiel 27:13; Isaiah 60:17; 1 Kings 14:25-28; 2 Chronicles 12:9-11; Exodus 35:24; Exodus 25:1-7; Exodus 35:4-9; Exodus 38:29-31; 1 Chronicles 18:9-11; 1 Chronicles 22:14; 1 Chronicles 29:6-7; 2 Chronicles 1:5-6; Exodus 27:1-6; Exodus 38:1-6; Exodus 35:16; Exodus 39:39; Numbers 4:13; Numbers 16:39-40; Exodus 30:18; Exodus 38:8; Exodus 27:17-19; Exodus 38:17-20; Exodus 26:10-11; Exodus 26:36-37; Exodus 27:9-11; Exodus 38:9-11; Exodus 36:18; Exodus 36:37-38; 2 Chronicles 4:18; 1 Kings 7:23-33; 1 Kings 7:38-45; 2 Chronicles 4:9-16; 1 Kings 8:64; 2 Kings 16:17; 2 Kings 25:13-17; Jeremiah 52:17-23; 1 Chronicles 18:8; 1 Chronicles 29:2; 2 Chronicles 4:1; 1 Samuel 17:5-6; 1 Samuel 17:38; 2 Samuel 21:16; 1 Samuel 17:6; 2 Samuel 22:35; Psalms 18:34; Judges 16:21; 2 Kings 25:7; Jeremiah 39:7; Jeremiah 52:11; 2 Chronicles 33:11; 2 Chronicles 36:6; Daniel 4:15; Daniel 4:23; Psalms 107:16; Deuteronomy 33:25; 1 Kings 4:13; 1 Chronicles 22:3; Isaiah 45:2; Numbers 21:9; 2 Kings 18:4; Daniel 5:4; Daniel 5:23; Revelation 9:20; Leviticus 6:28; 1 Chronicles 15:19; Daniel 2:32; Daniel 2:35; Daniel 2:45; Joshua 22:7-8; Numbers 31:21-23; Joshua 6:24; 2 Samuel 8:8; Daniel 2:39; Leviticus 26:19; Zechariah 6:1

## 4315: clay (id: 4315)

Clay was a basic building material in biblical times and clay pottery was widely used.

Clay pottery
------------

### Moulded by potters

**[JER 18:3–18:4](https://ref.ly/Jer18:3-Jer18:4)** *See also* [ISA 29:16](https://ref.ly/Isa29:16), [ISA 41:25](https://ref.ly/Isa41:25), [ISA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9)

### Used to make pots

**[LEV 6:28](https://ref.ly/Lev6:28)** *See also* [LEV 11:33](https://ref.ly/Lev11:33), [LEV 14:5](https://ref.ly/Lev14:5), [LEV 14:50](https://ref.ly/Lev14:50), [LEV 15:12](https://ref.ly/Lev15:12); [LAM 4:2](https://ref.ly/Lam4:2)

### Used to make jars

**[JER 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/Jer19:1-Jer19:3)** *See also* [NUM 5:17](https://ref.ly/Num5:17); [JER 32:14](https://ref.ly/Jer32:14); [2CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7)

Other uses of clay
------------------

### To make idols

**[DAN 2:31–2:35](https://ref.ly/Dan2:31-Dan2:35)** *See also* [DAN 2:41–2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:41-Dan2:45)

### To make moulds

**[1KI 7:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:46)** parallel [2CH 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:17)

### To make bricks

**[NAM 3:14](https://ref.ly/Nah3:14)** *See also* [JER 43:9](https://ref.ly/Jer43:9)

### In plastering

**[LEV 14:42](https://ref.ly/Lev14:42)**

### To make furnaces

**[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)**

### To seal documents

**[JOB 38:14](https://ref.ly/Job38:14)**

### Clay tablets were used for writing on

**[EZK 4:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:1)**

Clay was a relatively weak material
-----------------------------------

**[JOB 13:12](https://ref.ly/Job13:12)** *See also* [JOB 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Job4:18-Job4:19)

Clay was a very common material
-------------------------------

**[JOB 27:16](https://ref.ly/Job27:16)** *See also* [2TI 2:20](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:20)

God and his people compared to a potter and the clay
----------------------------------------------------

## 4324: dross (id: 4324)

Impurities removed from molten metal during refining. In a spiritual sense, wicked people whom God removes in judgment, and also impurities in believers which are removed by discipline and trials.

God removes the wicked like dross
---------------------------------

**[PSA 119:119](https://ref.ly/Ps119:119)** *See also* [ISA 30:28](https://ref.ly/Isa30:28); [JER 6:28–6:30](https://ref.ly/Jer6:28-Jer6:30); [EZK 22:19–22:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:19-Ezek22:22)

Dross as a metaphor for impurity in God's people
------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 22:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:18)** *See also* [ISA 1:21–1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21-Isa1:23); [EZK 24:11–24:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:11-Ezek24:13), [EZK 36:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:25); [AMO 9:8–9:10](https://ref.ly/Amos9:8-Amos9:10); [JAS 1:21](https://ref.ly/Jas1:21)

God purges the dross from his people
------------------------------------

**[ISA 1:25–1:26](https://ref.ly/Isa1:25-Isa1:26)** *See also* [PRO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Prov17:3), [PRO 25:4](https://ref.ly/Prov25:4); [DAN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Dan12:10); [MAL 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2-Mal3:3); [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1); [1PE 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:7)

### By the blood of Jesus Christ

[ZEC 13:1](https://ref.ly/Zech13:1); ; [HEB 9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:14); ; [1JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/1John1:7)

### By testing and affliction

----------------------------------------------

## 4333: gold (id: 4333)

The most valuable of all metals, gold was indicative of wealth and prosperity in biblical times. It had a wide variety of uses and was employed extensively in both the tabernacle and the temple.

Gold was of great value
-----------------------

**[ISA 60:17](https://ref.ly/Isa60:17)** *See also* [JOS 6:18–6:19](https://ref.ly/Josh6:18-Josh6:19), [JOS 6:24](https://ref.ly/Josh6:24); [1KI 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:1-1Kgs20:6); [2KI 7:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:8), [2KI 14:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:14); [2CH 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:3); [JOB 28:12–28:19](https://ref.ly/Job28:12-Job28:19) *Wisdom cannot be bought with gold*; [PSA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ps45:9), [PSA 45:13](https://ref.ly/Ps45:13), [PSA 72:15](https://ref.ly/Ps72:15), [PSA 105:37](https://ref.ly/Ps105:37); [PRO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Prov20:15); [EZK 28:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:13); [DAN 11:8](https://ref.ly/Dan11:8), [DAN 11:38](https://ref.ly/Dan11:38); [JOL 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Joel3:4-Joel3:5); [2TI 2:20](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:20); [REV 21:18](https://ref.ly/Rev21:18), [REV 21:21](https://ref.ly/Rev21:21) *The streets of the new Jerusalem are to be paved with gold.*

Gold was indicative of wealth
-----------------------------

**[GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2)** *See also* [GEN 24:35](https://ref.ly/Gen24:35); [DEU 8:12–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:12-Deut8:14), [DEU 17:17](https://ref.ly/Deut17:17); [JOS 22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:8) *Joshua sends the eastern tribes home*; [2KI 20:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:13) parallel [ISA 39:2](https://ref.ly/Isa39:2); [2CH 1:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:15), [2CH 32:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27); [JOB 3:15](https://ref.ly/Job3:15), [JOB 31:24–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:24-Job31:28); [ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8); [ISA 2:7](https://ref.ly/Isa2:7); [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13) *the adornments of unfaithful Jerusalem*; [EZK 28:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:4); [ZEP 1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:18); [ZEC 9:3](https://ref.ly/Zech9:3), [ZEC 14:14](https://ref.ly/Zech14:14); [JAS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2); [REV 18:16](https://ref.ly/Rev18:16)

Gold was relatively rare
------------------------

**[ISA 13:12](https://ref.ly/Isa13:12)**

Gold was mined from the earth
-----------------------------

**[JOB 28:5–28:6](https://ref.ly/Job28:5-Job28:6)** *See also* [GEN 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Gen2:11-Gen2:12)

Gold was refined by fire
------------------------

**[REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)** *See also* [NUM 31:21–31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:21-Num31:23); [JOB 28:1](https://ref.ly/Job28:1); [PRO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Prov17:3), [PRO 27:21](https://ref.ly/Prov27:21); [ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9); [MAL 3:3](https://ref.ly/Mal3:3); [1PE 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:7)

-------------------------------

**[1KI 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:22)** parallel [2CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:21) *See also* [1KI 9:26–9:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:26-1Kgs9:28) parallel [2CH 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:17-2Chr8:18); [1KI 10:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:11) parallel [2CH 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9); [1KI 22:48](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:48); [ISA 60:6](https://ref.ly/Isa60:6); [JER 10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:9); [EZK 27:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:22); [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

Gold was worked by goldsmiths
-----------------------------

**[ISA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Isa46:6)** *See also* [1CH 29:4–29:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:4-1Chr29:5); [2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7), [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14); [JER 10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:9)

--------------------------------------

Gold was given to rulers as tribute and bribes
----------------------------------------------

**[1KI 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14-1Kgs10:15)** parallel [2CH 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13-2Chr9:14) *See also* [1KI 10:24–10:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:24-1Kgs10:25) parallel [2CH 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:23-2Chr9:24); [2KI 12:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:18), [2KI 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7-2Kgs16:9), [2KI 18:13–18:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:13-2Kgs18:16), [2KI 23:33–23:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:33-2Kgs23:35) parallel [2CH 36:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:3); [2CH 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:2-2Chr16:3)

-----------------------

**[JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11)** *See also* [GEN 24:53](https://ref.ly/Gen24:53); [2SA 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:9-2Sam8:10) parallel [1CH 18:9–18:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:9-1Chr18:10); [1KI 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:10) parallel [2CH 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9); [2KI 5:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:5); [MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11)

### Jewellery

**[EXO 35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:22)** *See also* [GEN 24:53](https://ref.ly/Gen24:53), [GEN 24:22](https://ref.ly/Gen24:22); [EXO 3:22](https://ref.ly/Exod3:22), [EXO 11:2](https://ref.ly/Exod11:2), [EXO 12:35](https://ref.ly/Exod12:35), [EXO 32:2](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2), [EXO 32:24](https://ref.ly/Exod32:24) *used to make the golden calf*; [NUM 31:50](https://ref.ly/Num31:50); [JDG 8:24–8:26](https://ref.ly/Judg8:24-Judg8:26) *used to make Gideon's ephod*; [2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24); [PRO 11:22](https://ref.ly/Prov11:22), [PRO 25:11](https://ref.ly/Prov25:11); [SNG 1:11](https://ref.ly/Song1:11); [JER 4:30](https://ref.ly/Jer4:30); [DAN 10:5](https://ref.ly/Dan10:5); [1PE 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:3)

## 4336: iron (id: 4336)

Iron ore is mined from the earth
--------------------------------

**[JOB 28:2](https://ref.ly/Job28:2)** *See also* [DEU 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:9)

Iron ore is refined by smelting in a furnace
--------------------------------------------

### Such imagery is often used of God's testing of his people

**[EZK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:20)** *See also* [NUM 31:21–31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:21-Num31:23); [JER 6:27–6:29](https://ref.ly/Jer6:27-Jer6:29); [EZK 22:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:18)

### Egypt is described as an iron\-smelting furnace

**[DEU 4:20](https://ref.ly/Deut4:20)** *See also* [1KI 8:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:51); [JER 11:4](https://ref.ly/Jer11:4)

Iron was a valuable metal
-------------------------

**[REV 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:12)** *See also* [JOS 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:6-Josh22:8); [ISA 60:17](https://ref.ly/Isa60:17); [EZK 27:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:12), [EZK 27:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:19)

Iron was prized for its great strength
--------------------------------------

**[DAN 2:40–2:43](https://ref.ly/Dan2:40-Dan2:43)** *See also* [LEV 26:19](https://ref.ly/Lev26:19); [DEU 28:23](https://ref.ly/Deut28:23)JOB [40:15–40:18](https://ref.ly/Deut40:15-Deut40:18), [JOB 41:27](https://ref.ly/Job41:27)PSA [2:9](https://ref.ly/Job2:9); [REV 2:27](https://ref.ly/Rev2:27), [REV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Rev19:15)[ISA 48:4](https://ref.ly/Isa48:4); [JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18), [JER 15:12](https://ref.ly/Jer15:12); [DAN 2:31–2:35](https://ref.ly/Dan2:31-Dan2:35)DAN [7:7](https://ref.ly/Dan7:7), [DAN 7:19](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19)[MIC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:13)

Iron objects were taken as booty in war
---------------------------------------

**[JOS 6:24](https://ref.ly/Josh6:24)** *See also* [JOS 6:15–6:19](https://ref.ly/Josh6:15-Josh6:19), [JOS 22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:8)

Iron was used in the construction of the temple
-----------------------------------------------

**[1CH 29:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:2)** *See also* [1CH 22:14–22:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14-1Chr22:16), [1CH 29:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:7); [2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7), [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14), [2CH 24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:12)

------------------

## 4339: ivory (id: 4339)

Ivory, a semiprecious substance derived from elephant tusks, was procured through trade
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:22)** parallel [2CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:21) *See also* [EZK 27:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:15); [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

Articles inlaid with ivory were a sign of wealth
------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 10:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:18)** parallel [2CH 9:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:17) *See also* [1KI 22:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:39); [PSA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8); [EZK 27:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:6); [AMO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Amos3:15), [AMO 6:4](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4)

The metaphorical use of ivory
-----------------------------

**[SNG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Song5:14)** *See also* [SNG 7:4](https://ref.ly/Song7:4)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 10:22; 2 Chronicles 9:21; Ezekiel 27:15; Revelation 18:11-13; 1 Kings 10:18; 2 Chronicles 9:17; 1 Kings 22:39; Psalms 45:8; Ezekiel 27:6; Amos 3:15; Amos 6:4; Song of Songs 5:14; Song of Songs 7:4

## 4342: jewels (id: 4342)

Precious stones, prized for their beauty and value are mentioned frequently in Scripture.

The breastpiece of the high priest was set with twelve jewels
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 28:15–28:21](https://ref.ly/Exod28:15-Exod28:21)** parallel [EXO 39:8–39:14](https://ref.ly/Exod39:8-Exod39:14)

The king of Tyre is depicted as having been adorned with jewels
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 28:11–28:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:11-Ezek28:13)**

The new Jerusalem is described as being constructed of jewels
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[REV 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Rev21:18-Rev21:21)**

Jewels in royal crowns
----------------------

**[2SA 12:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:30)** parallel [1CH 20:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:2) *See also* [ZEC 9:16](https://ref.ly/Zech9:16)

Jewels acquired through trade
-----------------------------

**[EZK 27:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:22)** *See also* [1KI 10:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:2) parallel [2CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1); [1KI 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:10-1Kgs10:11) parallel [2CH 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9-2Chr9:10); [REV 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:12)

Wisdom is said to be more precious than jewels
----------------------------------------------

**[PRO 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Prov8:10-Prov8:11)** *See also* [JOB 28:12–28:19](https://ref.ly/Job28:12-Job28:19); [PRO 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Prov3:13-Prov3:15), [PRO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Prov20:15)

## 4351: refining (id: 4351)

The process of heating metals to high temperatures in order to burn away impurities. It is often used in a metaphorical sense to depict God refining his people to get rid of impurities in their faith.

Precious metals produced by refining
------------------------------------

**[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)** *See also* [1CH 28:14–28:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:14-1Chr28:18), [1CH 29:3–29:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:3-1Chr29:5); [JOB 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Job28:1-Job28:2); [1PE 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:7); [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)

God refines his people
----------------------

### Refining involves suffering

**[PSA 66:10–66:12](https://ref.ly/Ps66:10-Ps66:12)** *See also* [ISA 48:9–48:11](https://ref.ly/Isa48:9-Isa48:11); [JER 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/Jer9:7-Jer9:9)

### Refining for purging and purification

**[ZEC 13:8–13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:8-Zech13:9)** *See also* [JER 6:27–6:30](https://ref.ly/Jer6:27-Jer6:30); [DAN 11:35](https://ref.ly/Dan11:35), [DAN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Dan12:10); [MAL 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2-Mal3:4)

## 4354: rock (id: 4354)

A symbol of strength and permanence and a place where people in danger often took refuge. God is depicted as the Rock of Israel, being the strength, security and deliverance for his people.

Rock as a symbol of strength, stability and permanence
------------------------------------------------------

**[ZEC 12:3](https://ref.ly/Zech12:3)** *See also* [JOB 18:4](https://ref.ly/Job18:4), [JOB 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/Job19:23-Job19:24), [JOB 41:24](https://ref.ly/Job41:24); [NAM 1:6](https://ref.ly/Nah1:6)

Rocks as places of refuge and shelter
-------------------------------------

[1SA 13:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:6), [1SA 23:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:25) *David's hiding\-place from Saul*; ; [JOB 24:8](https://ref.ly/Job24:8), [JOB 30:6](https://ref.ly/Job30:6); ; [PSA 27:5](https://ref.ly/Ps27:5), [PSA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Ps40:2); ; [SNG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Song2:14); ; [ISA 2:10](https://ref.ly/Isa2:10), [ISA 2:19](https://ref.ly/Isa2:19), [ISA 2:21](https://ref.ly/Isa2:21), [ISA 32:2](https://ref.ly/Isa32:2); ; [JER 4:29](https://ref.ly/Jer4:29), [JER 16:16](https://ref.ly/Jer16:16), [JER 48:28](https://ref.ly/Jer48:28), [JER 49:16](https://ref.ly/Jer49:16) parallel [OBA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3); ; [REV 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/Rev6:15-Rev6:16)

## 4357: salt (id: 4357)

A basic commodity, used for seasoning and preserving food in biblical times.

The importance of salt in the sacrificial system
------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lev2:13)** *See also* [EXO 30:34–30:35](https://ref.ly/Exod30:34-Exod30:35) *The addition of salt to incense was probably to make it burn more easily and quickly*; [EZR 6:6–6:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:6-Ezra6:10), [EZR 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:21-Ezra7:23); [EZK 43:22–43:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:22-Ezek43:24)

The covenant of salt
--------------------

**[NUM 18:17–18:19](https://ref.ly/Num18:17-Num18:19)** *See also* [LEV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lev2:13); [2CH 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:4-2Chr13:5)

Believers as the salt of the earth
----------------------------------

**[MAT 5:13](https://ref.ly/Matt5:13)** *This teaching reflects the importance of salt as a preservative and seasoning.* *See also* [MRK 9:50](https://ref.ly/Mark9:50); [LUK 14:34–14:35](https://ref.ly/Luke14:34-Luke14:35)

------------------------------

**[ZEP 2:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:9)** *See also* [DEU 29:23–29:25](https://ref.ly/Deut29:23-Deut29:25); [JOB 39:5–39:6](https://ref.ly/Job39:5-Job39:6); [PSA 107:33–107:34](https://ref.ly/Ps107:33-Ps107:34); [JER 17:5–17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5-Jer17:8)

-----------------------------------------------

**[JER 48:8–48:9](https://ref.ly/Jer48:8-Jer48:9)** *See also* [JDG 9:42–9:45](https://ref.ly/Judg9:42-Judg9:45)

---------------------------------------

----------------------------------------

## 4360: sand (id: 4360)

A fine granular substance formed by the erosion of rocks, and found especially in the deserts and by the sea. It is often used as a symbol of innumerability and occasionally as a symbol of instability.

Sand as a fine granular substance
---------------------------------

### In the desert

**[EXO 2:12](https://ref.ly/Exod2:12)** *See also* [JOB 39:13–39:15](https://ref.ly/Job39:13-Job39:15); [ISA 35:6–35:7](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6-Isa35:7)

### At the sea

**[JER 5:22](https://ref.ly/Jer5:22)** *See also* [DEU 33:18–33:19](https://ref.ly/Deut33:18-Deut33:19); [ACT 27:39](https://ref.ly/Acts27:39)

### Sand\-bars

**[ACT 27:41](https://ref.ly/Acts27:41)** *See also* [ACT 27:17](https://ref.ly/Acts27:17)

### The weight of sand

**[JOB 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Job6:2-Job6:3)** *See also* [PRO 27:3](https://ref.ly/Prov27:3)

Sand representing innumerability
--------------------------------

### God promised that Israel would be as innumerable as sand

**[GEN 22:15–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:15-Gen22:18)** *See also* [GEN 32:9–32:12](https://ref.ly/Gen32:9-Gen32:12); [1KI 4:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:20); [ISA 10:20–10:22](https://ref.ly/Isa10:20-Isa10:22), [ISA 48:17–48:19](https://ref.ly/Isa48:17-Isa48:19); [JER 33:19–33:22](https://ref.ly/Jer33:19-Jer33:22); [HOS 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/Hos1:8-Hos1:10); [ROM 9:22–9:27](https://ref.ly/Rom9:22-Rom9:27); [HEB 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/Heb11:11-Heb11:12)

### Armies described as being as innumerable as sand

**[1SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:5)** *See also* [JOS 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Josh11:1-Josh11:5); [JDG 7:12](https://ref.ly/Judg7:12); [2SA 17:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:11); [REV 20:7–20:8](https://ref.ly/Rev20:7-Rev20:8)

### Other comparisons with the innumerability of sand

**[GEN 41:49](https://ref.ly/Gen41:49)** *See also* [1KI 4:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:29); [JOB 29:18](https://ref.ly/Job29:18); [PSA 78:27–78:29](https://ref.ly/Ps78:27-Ps78:29), [PSA 139:17–139:18](https://ref.ly/Ps139:17-Ps139:18); [JER 15:8](https://ref.ly/Jer15:8); [HAB 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Hab1:8-Hab1:9)

The instability and impermanence of sand
----------------------------------------

**[MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27)** parallel [LUK 6:46–6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:46-Luke6:49)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 2:12; Job 39:13-15; Isaiah 35:6-7; Jeremiah 5:22; Deuteronomy 33:18-19; Acts 27:39; Acts 27:41; Acts 27:17; Job 6:2-3; Proverbs 27:3; Genesis 22:15-18; Genesis 32:9-12; 1 Kings 4:20; Isaiah 10:20-22; Isaiah 48:17-19; Jeremiah 33:19-22; Hosea 1:8-10; Romans 9:22-27; Hebrews 11:11-12; 1 Samuel 13:5; Joshua 11:1-5; Judges 7:12; 2 Samuel 17:11; Revelation 20:7-8; Genesis 41:49; 1 Kings 4:29; Job 29:18; Psalms 78:27-29; Psalms 139:17-18; Jeremiah 15:8; Habakkuk 1:8-9; Matthew 7:24-27; Luke 6:46-49

## 4363: silver (id: 4363)

A precious metal often surpassed in value only by gold. It had a variety of uses and by NT times was common in coinage.

Silver mined
------------

**[JOB 28:1–28:4](https://ref.ly/Job28:1-Job28:4)**

Silver acquired through trade
-----------------------------

**[1KI 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:22)** parallel [2CH 9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:20) *See also* [JER 10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:9); [EZK 27:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:12); [REV 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:12)

Possession of silver a sign of wealth
-------------------------------------

**[GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2)** *See also* [GEN 24:35](https://ref.ly/Gen24:35); [NUM 22:18](https://ref.ly/Num22:18); [DEU 8:13](https://ref.ly/Deut8:13), [DEU 17:17](https://ref.ly/Deut17:17); [JOS 22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:8); [2CH 32:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27) *Hezekiah's riches*; [JOB 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Job3:13-Job3:15), [JOB 27:16](https://ref.ly/Job27:16); [PSA 105:37](https://ref.ly/Ps105:37); [ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8); [ISA 2:7](https://ref.ly/Isa2:7), [ISA 60:17](https://ref.ly/Isa60:17); [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13) *the adornments of unfaithful Jerusalem*; [EZK 28:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:4); [DAN 11:43](https://ref.ly/Dan11:43); [HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8); [ZEC 9:3](https://ref.ly/Zech9:3), [ZEC 14:14](https://ref.ly/Zech14:14)

## 4366: stones (id: 4366)

Pieces of rock, used extensively for building.

Stones used in building
-----------------------

### The temple in Jerusalem

**[1KI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:17-1Kgs5:18)** *See also* [1KI 6:14–6:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:14-1Kgs6:18), [1KI 6:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:36), [1KI 7:9–7:121](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9-1Kgs7:121)CH [22:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:2), [1CH 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14), [1CH 29:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:2)[2KI 22:3–22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:3-2Kgs22:7) parallel [2CH 34:8–34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:8-2Chr34:11) *repairing the temple*; [EZR 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:1-Ezra6:4) *rebuilding the temple after the exile*; [MAT 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Matt24:1-Matt24:2) parallels [MRK 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Mark13:1-Mark13:2), [LUK 21:5–21:6](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5-Luke21:6) *Jesus Christ prophesies the temple's destruction.*

### Other references to building with stone

[GEN 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/Gen11:1-Gen11:3); ; [LEV 14:39–14:45](https://ref.ly/Lev14:39-Lev14:45); ; [2CH 16:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:6); ; [NEH 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Neh4:1-Neh4:3); ; [ISA 9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:10); ; [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11)

## 4369: sulphur (id: 4369)

A yellow, crystalline substance, also known as brimstone, which ignites and burns readily. It is invariably associated with divine punishment in Scripture.

Burning sulphur causes desolation
---------------------------------

**[DEU 29:23](https://ref.ly/Deut29:23)** *See also* [JOB 18:12–18:17](https://ref.ly/Job18:12-Job18:17)

Divine punishment by means of burning sulphur
---------------------------------------------

**[PSA 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/Ps11:5-Ps11:6)** *See also* [ISA 30:31–30:33](https://ref.ly/Isa30:31-Isa30:33), [ISA 34:8–34:10](https://ref.ly/Isa34:8-Isa34:10); [EZK 38:21–38:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:21-Ezek38:23); [REV 9:13–9:19](https://ref.ly/Rev9:13-Rev9:19), [REV 14:9–14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:9-Rev14:11)

Sodom and Gomorrah destroyed by burning sulphur
-----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 19:24–19:25](https://ref.ly/Gen19:24-Gen19:25)** *See also* [LUK 17:28–17:29](https://ref.ly/Luke17:28-Luke17:29)

The lake of burning sulphur
---------------------------

**[REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8)** *See also* [REV 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/Rev19:19-Rev19:21), [REV 20:7–20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:7-Rev20:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 29:23; Job 18:12-17; Psalms 11:5-6; Isaiah 30:31-33; Isaiah 34:8-10; Ezekiel 38:21-23; Revelation 9:13-19; Revelation 14:9-11; Genesis 19:24-25; Luke 17:28-29; Revelation 21:8; Revelation 19:19-21; Revelation 20:7-10

## 4402: plants (id: 4402)

Plants are an important aspect of God's creation, and a major source of food and materials. Scripture refers to many plants, with their various uses.

Plants as a source of food
--------------------------

**[GEN 1:29–1:30](https://ref.ly/Gen1:29-Gen1:30)** *See also* [GEN 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Gen1:11-Gen1:12), [GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19), [GEN 9:3](https://ref.ly/Gen9:3); [PSA 104:14](https://ref.ly/Ps104:14)

Growth of plants dependent upon soil, sun and irrigation
--------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 32:2](https://ref.ly/Deut32:2)** *See also* [GEN 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Gen2:4-Gen2:5); [2KI 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:26) parallel [ISA 37:27](https://ref.ly/Isa37:27); [JOB 8:16–8:19](https://ref.ly/Job8:16-Job8:19); [ISA 61:11](https://ref.ly/Isa61:11); [ZEC 10:1](https://ref.ly/Zech10:1); [MAT 13:3–13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:8) parallels [MRK 4:3–4:8](https://ref.ly/Mark4:3-Mark4:8), [LUK 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:5-Luke8:8); [JAS 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11)

### Myrtle

**[ISA 55:13](https://ref.ly/Isa55:13)** *See also* [ISA 41:18–41:20](https://ref.ly/Isa41:18-Isa41:20); [ZEC 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/Zech1:8-Zech1:10)

### Reed

**[EXO 2:3](https://ref.ly/Exod2:3)** *See also* [JOB 8:11](https://ref.ly/Job8:11); [ISA 19:6–19:7](https://ref.ly/Isa19:6-Isa19:7)

### Rose

**[SNG 2:1](https://ref.ly/Song2:1)**

### Rush

**[ISA 19:6–19:7](https://ref.ly/Isa19:6-Isa19:7)**

## 4404: food (id: 4404)

Scripture contains references to varied foodstuffs, which comprised the diet of the people of the Near East in biblical times.

God made provision for food in creation
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 1:29–1:30](https://ref.ly/Gen1:29-Gen1:30)**

Flour
-----

### Flour the main ingredient in bread

**[GEN 18:6](https://ref.ly/Gen18:6)** *See also* [EXO 29:2](https://ref.ly/Exod29:2); [JDG 6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:19); [1SA 28:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:24); [MAT 13:33](https://ref.ly/Matt13:33) parallel [LUK 13:21](https://ref.ly/Luke13:21)

### Flour figured largely in the staple diet

**[1KI 17:12–17:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:12-1Kgs17:16)** *See also* [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29); [1KI 4:22–4:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:22-1Kgs4:23); [2KI 4:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:41), [2KI 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:1), [2KI 7:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:16); [1CH 12:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:40)

### Fine flour more refined and more expensive than the common variety

**[EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13)** *See also* [EZK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:19); [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

### Flour in grain offerings

**[LEV 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Lev2:1-Lev2:10)** *See also* [LEV 6:14–6:23](https://ref.ly/Lev6:14-Lev6:23), [LEV 14:8–14:11](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:11); [NUM 5:11–5:15](https://ref.ly/Num5:11-Num5:15), [NUM 6:13–6:15](https://ref.ly/Num6:13-Num6:15), [NUM 15:1–15:4](https://ref.ly/Num15:1-Num15:4), [NUM 28:9](https://ref.ly/Num28:9), [NUM 29:7–29:11](https://ref.ly/Num29:7-Num29:11); [1CH 23:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:29); [EZK 46:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:14)

### Flour in other offerings

**[LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11)** *See also* [EXO 29:1–29:3](https://ref.ly/Exod29:1-Exod29:3), [EXO 29:22–29:25](https://ref.ly/Exod29:22-Exod29:25); [LEV 7:12](https://ref.ly/Lev7:12), [LEV 8:25–8:28](https://ref.ly/Lev8:25-Lev8:28), [LEV 23:15–23:17](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:17)

Honey
-----

### Honey a sweet delicacy

**[GEN 43:11](https://ref.ly/Gen43:11)** *See also* [EXO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Exod16:31); [DEU 32:13](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13); [JDG 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Judg14:8-Judg14:9); [1SA 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:25-1Sam14:26); [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29); [1KI 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:1-1Kgs14:3); [2KI 18:31–18:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:31-2Kgs18:32)PSA [19:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:10), [PSA 119:103](https://ref.ly/Ps119:103)[PSA 81:13–81:16](https://ref.ly/Ps81:13-Ps81:16); [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11), [SNG 5:1](https://ref.ly/Song5:1); [ISA 7:22](https://ref.ly/Isa7:22); [JER 41:8](https://ref.ly/Jer41:8); [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13)

### The promised land described as flowing with milk and honey

**[EXO 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:8)** *See also* [EXO 3:17](https://ref.ly/Exod3:17), [EXO 13:5](https://ref.ly/Exod13:5), [EXO 33:3](https://ref.ly/Exod33:3); [LEV 20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:24)NUM [13:27](https://ref.ly/Lev13:27), [NUM 14:8](https://ref.ly/Num14:8)[NUM 16:12–16:14](https://ref.ly/Num16:12-Num16:14); [DEU 6:3](https://ref.ly/Deut6:3), [DEU 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Deut11:8-Deut11:9), [DEU 26:9](https://ref.ly/Deut26:9), [DEU 31:20](https://ref.ly/Deut31:20); [JOS 5:6](https://ref.ly/Josh5:6); [JER 32:22](https://ref.ly/Jer32:22); [EZK 20:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:6), [EZK 20:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:15)

### Prohibition against burning honey on the altar

**[LEV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lev2:11)**

Raisins
-------

**[2SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:1)** *See also* [NUM 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:4); [1SA 25:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:18), [1SA 30:11–30:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:11-1Sam30:12); [2SA 6:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:19) parallel [1CH 16:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:3); [1CH 12:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:40); [SNG 2:5](https://ref.ly/Song2:5); [HOS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1)

Lentils
-------

**[GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34)** *See also* [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29), [2SA 23:11–23:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:11-2Sam23:12); [EZK 4:1–4:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:1-Ezek4:10)

Cheese
------

**[1SA 17:17–17:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17-1Sam17:18)** *See also* [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29); [JOB 10:8–10:11](https://ref.ly/Job10:8-Job10:11)

Believers should not eat food offered to idols
----------------------------------------------

**[ACT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:20)** *See also* [ACT 21:25](https://ref.ly/Acts21:25); [1CO 8:1–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:1-1Cor8:13); [REV 2:14](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:29-30; Genesis 18:6; Exodus 29:2; Judges 6:19; 1 Samuel 28:24; Matthew 13:33; Luke 13:21; 1 Kings 17:12-16; 2 Samuel 17:27-29; 1 Kings 4:22-23; 2 Kings 4:41; 2 Kings 7:1; 2 Kings 7:16; 1 Chronicles 12:40; Ezekiel 16:13; Ezekiel 16:19; Revelation 18:11-13; Leviticus 2:1-10; Leviticus 6:14-23; Leviticus 14:8-11; Numbers 5:11-15; Numbers 6:13-15; Numbers 15:1-4; Numbers 28:9; Numbers 29:7-11; 1 Chronicles 23:29; Ezekiel 46:14; Leviticus 5:11; Exodus 29:1-3; Exodus 29:22-25; Leviticus 7:12; Leviticus 8:25-28; Leviticus 23:15-17; Genesis 43:11; Exodus 16:31; Deuteronomy 32:13; Judges 14:8-9; 1 Samuel 14:25-26; 1 Kings 14:1-3; 2 Kings 18:31-32; 2 Kings 19:10; Psalms 119:103; Psalms 81:13-16; Song of Songs 4:11; Song of Songs 5:1; Isaiah 7:22; Jeremiah 41:8; Exodus 3:7-8; Exodus 3:17; Exodus 13:5; Exodus 33:3; Leviticus 20:24; Leviticus 13:27; Numbers 14:8; Numbers 16:12-14; Deuteronomy 6:3; Deuteronomy 11:8-9; Deuteronomy 26:9; Deuteronomy 31:20; Joshua 5:6; Jeremiah 32:22; Ezekiel 20:6; Ezekiel 20:15; Leviticus 2:11; 2 Samuel 16:1; Numbers 6:1-4; 1 Samuel 25:18; 1 Samuel 30:11-12; 2 Samuel 6:19; 1 Chronicles 16:3; Song of Songs 2:5; Hosea 3:1; Genesis 25:29-34; 2 Samuel 23:11-12; Ezekiel 4:1-10; 1 Samuel 17:17-18; Job 10:8-11; Acts 15:19-20; Acts 21:25; 1 Corinthians 8:1-13; Revelation 2:14
* **Associated Articles:** 4418: bread (ID: `4418@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4438: eating (ID: `4438@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4450: fruit (ID: `4450@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4474: manna (ID: `4474@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4476: meals (ID: `4476@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4478: meat (ID: `4478@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4480: milk (ID: `4480@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4532: vegetables (ID: `4532@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4612: birds (ID: `4612@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4642: fish (ID: `4642@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8770: idolatry, in NT (ID: `8770@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4406: agriculture (id: 4406)

The science of cultivation. The task, given to humanity, of cultivating the land is a reflection of God's own care for his creation.

A major activity for humanity
-----------------------------

**[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)** *See also* [GEN 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gen3:23), [GEN 4:2](https://ref.ly/Gen4:2), [GEN 4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:12), [GEN 9:20](https://ref.ly/Gen9:20), [GEN 47:19](https://ref.ly/Gen47:19); [DEU 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:8); [1KI 19:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19); [2CH 26:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:10); [ISA 37:30](https://ref.ly/Isa37:30)

Growth is according to the sovereign will of God
------------------------------------------------

### He has established the seasons

**[GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22)**; **[ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)** *See also* [LEV 26:4](https://ref.ly/Lev26:4); [JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24)

### He provides for the growth of the crops

**[PSA 65:9](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9)** *See also* [PSA 104:14](https://ref.ly/Ps104:14), [PSA 107:37–107:38](https://ref.ly/Ps107:37-Ps107:38), [PSA 147:8](https://ref.ly/Ps147:8); [EZK 34:26–34:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:26-Ezek34:27); [ZEC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:12); [MAT 6:30](https://ref.ly/Matt6:30); [2CO 9:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:10)

### OT laws for agriculture showed how to co\-operate with God

**[LEV 19:19](https://ref.ly/Lev19:19)** *See also* [LEV 19:23](https://ref.ly/Lev19:23), [LEV 25:3–25:4](https://ref.ly/Lev25:3-Lev25:4); [DEU 22:10](https://ref.ly/Deut22:10)

### Harvest festivals were for thanking God for his provision

**[EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22)** *See also* [LEV 23:10](https://ref.ly/Lev23:10), [LEV 23:39–23:41](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39-Lev23:41); [DEU 16:10](https://ref.ly/Deut16:10), [DEU 16:13](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13)

Cultivation was affected by the fall
------------------------------------

**[GEN 3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17)** *See also* [GEN 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:18-Gen3:19), [GEN 4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:12), [GEN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:29); [ROM 8:20](https://ref.ly/Rom8:20)

Agricultural images of God's action among his people
----------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:7)**; **[MAT 9:37](https://ref.ly/Matt9:37)**; **[JHN 12:24](https://ref.ly/John12:24)** *See also* [ISA 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/Isa17:5-Isa17:6), [ISA 24:13](https://ref.ly/Isa24:13); [JER 8:20](https://ref.ly/Jer8:20), [JER 50:16](https://ref.ly/Jer50:16), [JER 51:33](https://ref.ly/Jer51:33); [HOS 6:11](https://ref.ly/Hos6:11); [MAT 13:3–13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:8) parallels [MRK 4:3–4:8](https://ref.ly/Mark4:3-Mark4:8), [LUK 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:5-Luke8:8); [MAT 13:18–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:18-Matt13:23) parallels [MRK 4:13–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:13-Mark4:20), [LUK 8:11–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:15); [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30); [MRK 4:26–4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:26-Mark4:29); [JHN 4:35–4:38](https://ref.ly/John4:35-John4:38)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:15; Genesis 3:23; Genesis 4:2; Genesis 4:12; Genesis 9:20; Genesis 47:19; Deuteronomy 8:7-8; 1 Kings 19:19; 2 Chronicles 26:10; Isaiah 37:30; Genesis 8:22; Acts 14:17; Leviticus 26:4; Jeremiah 5:24; Psalms 65:9; Psalms 104:14; Psalms 107:37-38; Psalms 147:8; Ezekiel 34:26-27; Zechariah 8:12; Matthew 6:30; 2 Corinthians 9:10; Leviticus 19:19; Leviticus 19:23; Leviticus 25:3-4; Deuteronomy 22:10; Exodus 34:22; Leviticus 23:10; Leviticus 23:39-41; Deuteronomy 16:10; Deuteronomy 16:13; Genesis 3:17; Genesis 3:18-19; Genesis 5:29; Romans 8:20; Isaiah 5:7; Matthew 9:37; John 12:24; Isaiah 17:5-6; Isaiah 24:13; Jeremiah 8:20; Jeremiah 50:16; Jeremiah 51:33; Hosea 6:11; Matthew 13:3-8; Mark 4:3-8; Luke 8:5-8; Matthew 13:18-23; Mark 4:13-20; Luke 8:11-15; Matthew 13:24-30; Mark 4:26-29; John 4:35-38
* **Associated Articles:** 1325: God, the Creator (ID: `1325@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4017: life, animal and plant (ID: `4017@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4430: crops (ID: `4430@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4454: gleaning (ID: `4454@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4468: horticulture (ID: `4468@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4498: ploughing (ID: `4498@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4510: sowing and reaping (ID: `4510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4522: threshing (ID: `4522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4538: vineyard (ID: `4538@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4550: winnowing (ID: `4550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4970: seasons, of the year (ID: `4970@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4408: balm (id: 4408)

Balm was a herb valued for its healing properties
-------------------------------------------------

**[JER 8:22](https://ref.ly/Jer8:22)** *See also* [2CH 28:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:15); [JER 46:11](https://ref.ly/Jer46:11), [JER 51:8](https://ref.ly/Jer51:8)

Balm was exported from Israel to other nations
----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 43:11](https://ref.ly/Gen43:11)** *See also* [GEN 37:25](https://ref.ly/Gen37:25); [EZK 27:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 8:22; 2 Chronicles 28:15; Jeremiah 46:11; Jeremiah 51:8; Genesis 43:11; Genesis 37:25; Ezekiel 27:17
* **Associated Articles:** 4466: herbs and spices (ID: `4466@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4490: ointment (ID: `4490@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5587: trade (ID: `5587@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4410: banquets (id: 4410)

Grand, celebratory meals consisting usually of rich food and flowing wine. Banquets usually accompanied weddings, religious festivals and other celebrations. Prophecies of the Messianic age refer to a banquet to which the righteous will be invited.

Characteristics of banquets
---------------------------

### They were usually held in a hall

**[SNG 2:4](https://ref.ly/Song2:4)** *See also* [EST 7:8](https://ref.ly/Esth7:8); [DAN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Dan5:10)

### Honoured guests were given special places

**[LUK 14:7–14:11](https://ref.ly/Luke14:7-Luke14:11)** *See also* [PRO 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/Prov25:6-Prov25:7); [MAT 23:6](https://ref.ly/Matt23:6) parallels [MRK 12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:39), [LUK 20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:46)

### Guests reclined on couches

[EST 1:6](https://ref.ly/Esth1:6); ; [MAT 26:7](https://ref.ly/Matt26:7) parallels [MRK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3), [LUK 7:36](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36); ; [JHN 12:2](https://ref.ly/John12:2)

### Invitations were sent out

[LUK 14:17](https://ref.ly/Luke14:17) parallel [MAT 22:3](https://ref.ly/Matt22:3)

Occasions for holding banquets
------------------------------

### To celebrate weddings

**[MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2)** *See also* [GEN 29:21–29:22](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21-Gen29:22) *a feast celebrates Jacob's wedding*; [JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10) *Samson gives a wedding feast*; [EST 2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:18) *Xerxes celebrates Esther's choice as queen with a banquet*; [MAT 25:10](https://ref.ly/Matt25:10); [LUK 12:36](https://ref.ly/Luke12:36); [JHN 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/John2:8-John2:9); [REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9) *the marriage supper of the Lamb*

### To celebrate birthdays

[GEN 40:20](https://ref.ly/Gen40:20); ; [MRK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21)

### To celebrate other events

[GEN 21:8](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8) *Abraham celebrates Isaac's weaning.*; [EST 8:17](https://ref.ly/Esth8:17), [EST 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/Esth9:17-Esth9:19); [LUK 15:23](https://ref.ly/Luke15:23) *The father celebrates the prodigal son's return.*

### Religious celebrations

**[PSA 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ps22:29)** *See also* [2CH 30:21–30:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:21-2Chr30:22), [2CH 35:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:13); [ZEC 7:6](https://ref.ly/Zech7:6)

Activities at banquets
----------------------

### Good food and drink

**[ISA 25:6](https://ref.ly/Isa25:6)** *See also* [JOB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Job1:4); [ZEC 7:6](https://ref.ly/Zech7:6); [MAT 22:4](https://ref.ly/Matt22:4); [LUK 15:23](https://ref.ly/Luke15:23)

### Drunkenness and revelry

**[1SA 25:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:36)** *See also* [EST 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Esth1:7-Esth1:8), [EST 1:10](https://ref.ly/Esth1:10); [ECC 10:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:19); [JER 51:39](https://ref.ly/Jer51:39); [DAN 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Dan5:3-Dan5:4)

### Music and dancing

**[ISA 5:12](https://ref.ly/Isa5:12)** *See also* [MAT 14:6](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6) parallel [MRK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22); [LUK 15:25](https://ref.ly/Luke15:25)

Further examples of banquets
----------------------------

[GEN 26:30](https://ref.ly/Gen26:30) *Isaac holds a feast for Abimelech*; ; [GEN 43:34](https://ref.ly/Gen43:34) *Joseph feasts with his brothers*; ; [2SA 3:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:20) *David holds a feast for Abner*; ; [1KI 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:15) *Solomon gives a feast for all his court.*; [EST 1:3](https://ref.ly/Esth1:3), [EST 1:5](https://ref.ly/Esth1:5); [EST 1:9](https://ref.ly/Esth1:9) *Queen Vashti holds a banquet for the women of the palace.*; [EST 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Esth5:4-Esth5:5), [EST 5:8](https://ref.ly/Esth5:8); [DAN 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1-Dan5:2) *Belshazzar has a banquet for 1,000 nobles*; ; [LUK 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29) parallel [MAT 9:10](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10) *Levi holds a banquet for Jesus Christ*; ; [LUK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1) *Jesus Christ is the guest of a leading Pharisee.*

An invitation to a banquet seen as a sign of God's grace
--------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 14:12–14:14](https://ref.ly/Luke14:12-Luke14:14)** *See also* [2KI 6:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:23) *The king of Israel prepares a banquet for captured Aramean troops*; [MAT 22:8–22:10](https://ref.ly/Matt22:8-Matt22:10) parallel [LUK 14:21–14:23](https://ref.ly/Luke14:21-Luke14:23)

The Messianic banquet
---------------------

**[MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11)** *See also* [LUK 13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:29), [LUK 14:15](https://ref.ly/Luke14:15)

## 4416: branch (id: 4416)

The limb of a tree. The term is used literally, symbolically and metaphorically in Scripture. Branches are personified at times and the Messiah is referred to as the Branch.

Branches of various trees
-------------------------

### Vine branches

**[GEN 49:22](https://ref.ly/Gen49:22)** *See also* [NUM 13:23](https://ref.ly/Num13:23); [ISA 18:5](https://ref.ly/Isa18:5); [JER 5:10](https://ref.ly/Jer5:10), [JER 6:9](https://ref.ly/Jer6:9), [JER 48:32](https://ref.ly/Jer48:32); [EZK 17:5–17:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:5-Ezek17:8), [EZK 19:10–19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10-Ezek19:14); [JHN 15:5–15:6](https://ref.ly/John15:5-John15:6)

### Palm branches

**[REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9)** *See also* [NEH 8:15](https://ref.ly/Neh8:15); [ISA 9:14](https://ref.ly/Isa9:14), [ISA 19:15](https://ref.ly/Isa19:15)

**[JER 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jer1:11)** *See also* [GEN 30:37–30:39](https://ref.ly/Gen30:37-Gen30:39); [NEH 8:15](https://ref.ly/Neh8:15); [EZK 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:22-Ezek17:23), [EZK 31:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:3); [HEB 9:19](https://ref.ly/Heb9:19)

## 4418: bread (id: 4418)

The basic component of the staple diet in biblical times. Consecrated bread was continually laid out in the Holy Place of the tabernacle and temple. Jesus Christ called himself the bread of life.

Bread the basic component of the diet
-------------------------------------

**[MAT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Matt6:11)** parallel [LUK 11:3](https://ref.ly/Luke11:3) *See also* [GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18), [GEN 18:3–18:6](https://ref.ly/Gen18:3-Gen18:6) *Abraham entertains the three angels*; [GEN 25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:34), [GEN 27:17](https://ref.ly/Gen27:17), [GEN 45:23](https://ref.ly/Gen45:23); [DEU 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:9), [DEU 23:4](https://ref.ly/Deut23:4); [JDG 8:5–8:6](https://ref.ly/Judg8:5-Judg8:6) *Gideon asks for bread for his troops*; [JDG 19:19](https://ref.ly/Judg19:19); [RUT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:14); [1SA 2:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:36), [1SA 17:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17), [1SA 21:2–21:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:2-1Sam21:3), [1SA 25:7–25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:7-1Sam25:11) *Nabal refuses bread to David's men*; [1KI 13:7–13:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:7-1Kgs13:22), [1KI 17:10–17:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:10-1Kgs17:16) *The widow at Zarephath feeds Elijah and is rewarded*; [1KI 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5-1Kgs19:6); [JOB 23:12](https://ref.ly/Job23:12); [PSA 104:14–104:15](https://ref.ly/Ps104:14-Ps104:15); [PRO 30:8](https://ref.ly/Prov30:8); [ISA 33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:16), [ISA 51:14](https://ref.ly/Isa51:14), [ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2); [JER 37:21](https://ref.ly/Jer37:21) *Jeremiah's rations in prison*; [JER 42:14](https://ref.ly/Jer42:14); [LAM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Lam5:6); [EZK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:9); [DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3); [MAT 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Matt4:3-Matt4:4) parallel [LUK 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Luke4:3-Luke4:4) *the temptation of Jesus Christ*; [MRK 6:8](https://ref.ly/Mark6:8); [2CO 9:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:10)

A diet consisting solely of bread was indicative of deprivation
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27)** parallel [2CH 18:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:26) *See also* [PRO 6:26](https://ref.ly/Prov6:26); [LAM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Lam1:11), [LAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:12), [LAM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Lam4:4)

## 4422: brier (id: 4422)

Prickly vegetation, painful to the touch, often seen as a symbol of dereliction.

## 4424: cedar (id: 4424)

A tall, majestic tree, native to Lebanon. In OT times it was highly prized for its wood, which was used extensively in the construction of Solomon's temple.

Cedar trees associated with Lebanon
-----------------------------------

**[1KI 4:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:33)** *See also* [JDG 9:15](https://ref.ly/Judg9:15); [2KI 14:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:9) parallel [2CH 25:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:18); [2KI 19:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:23) parallel [ISA 37:24](https://ref.ly/Isa37:24); [PSA 29:5](https://ref.ly/Ps29:5), [PSA 92:12](https://ref.ly/Ps92:12), [PSA 104:16](https://ref.ly/Ps104:16); [SNG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Song5:15); [ISA 14:8](https://ref.ly/Isa14:8); [JER 22:23](https://ref.ly/Jer22:23); [EZK 27:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:5); [HOS 14:5–14:6](https://ref.ly/Hos14:5-Hos14:6); [ZEC 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Zech11:1-Zech11:2)

The cedar as a tall, majestic tree
----------------------------------

------------------------

---------------------------------

## 4426: chaff (id: 4426)

Husks of wheat or other cereal. It was separated from the grain by threshing, whereupon it usually blew away, as it was lighter than the grain. Sometimes however, it was burned as fuel. The wicked are frequently likened to chaff in Scripture.

Grain was threshed on outdoor threshing\-floors to separate the chaff
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[DAN 2:31–2:35](https://ref.ly/Dan2:31-Dan2:35)** *See also* [HOS 13:3](https://ref.ly/Hos13:3)

Characteristics of chaff
------------------------

### It is blown by the wind

**[JOB 13:25](https://ref.ly/Job13:25)**

### It is light

**[JOB 41:28](https://ref.ly/Job41:28)**

### It is useless

**[ISA 33:11](https://ref.ly/Isa33:11)**

The wicked are compared to chaff
--------------------------------

### They will disappear as chaff is blown away

**[PSA 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1-Ps1:6)** *See also* [JOB 21:17–21:18](https://ref.ly/Job21:17-Job21:18); [PSA 35:4–35:6](https://ref.ly/Ps35:4-Ps35:6), [PSA 83:13–83:15](https://ref.ly/Ps83:13-Ps83:15); [ISA 17:13](https://ref.ly/Isa17:13), [ISA 29:5](https://ref.ly/Isa29:5), [ISA 40:24–40:25](https://ref.ly/Isa40:24-Isa40:25); [JER 13:24](https://ref.ly/Jer13:24)

### Their punishment is compared to threshing

**[ISA 41:15–41:16](https://ref.ly/Isa41:15-Isa41:16)** *See also* [ISA 41:2](https://ref.ly/Isa41:2)

### Their end will be like chaff which is burned

**[MAT 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11-Matt3:12)** parallel [LUK 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Luke3:16-Luke3:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 2:31-35; Hosea 13:3; Job 13:25; Job 41:28; Isaiah 33:11; Psalms 1:1-6; Job 21:17-18; Psalms 35:4-6; Psalms 83:13-15; Isaiah 17:13; Isaiah 29:5; Isaiah 40:24-25; Jeremiah 13:24; Isaiah 41:15-16; Isaiah 41:2; Matthew 3:11-12; Luke 3:16-17
* **Associated Articles:** 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4522: threshing (ID: `4522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4524: threshing-floor (ID: `4524@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4550: winnowing (ID: `4550@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4428: corn (id: 4428)

A cereal crop cultivated for food use. An important agricultural crop in biblical times, it is mentioned frequently in Scripture.

The importance of corn
----------------------

### It was a basic foodstuff

**[PSA 65:9](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9)** *See also* [2KI 4:42–4:44](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:42-2Kgs4:44); [EZK 36:24–36:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:24-Ezek36:31); [MAT 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:8) parallels [MRK 2:23–2:28](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark2:28), [LUK 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:5)

### It was an important cereal crop

**[PSA 72:16](https://ref.ly/Ps72:16)** *See also* [JDG 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/Judg15:1-Judg15:6); [PSA 65:11–65:13](https://ref.ly/Ps65:11-Ps65:13); [ISA 36:16–36:17](https://ref.ly/Isa36:16-Isa36:17)

---------------

## 4430: crops (id: 4430)

Crops represent God's provision and are regulated by the Mosaic law. Their failure is associated with disobedience.

Crops related to the law of Moses
---------------------------------

**[DEU 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Deut7:12-Deut7:13)** *See also* [EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11), [EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16); [LEV 23:39](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39), [LEV 25:3](https://ref.ly/Lev25:3), [LEV 25:15–25:16](https://ref.ly/Lev25:15-Lev25:16), [LEV 25:20–25:22](https://ref.ly/Lev25:20-Lev25:22), [LEV 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Lev26:3-Lev26:4); [DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29), [DEU 16:13](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13) *the Feast of Tabernacles*; [DEU 22:9](https://ref.ly/Deut22:9), [DEU 26:12](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12), [DEU 28:11](https://ref.ly/Deut28:11), [DEU 30:8–30:9](https://ref.ly/Deut30:8-Deut30:9); [NEH 10:35–10:37](https://ref.ly/Neh10:35-Neh10:37); [PRO 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Prov3:9-Prov3:10); [MAL 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10-Mal3:11)

---------------------------------

**[PSA 65:9–65:10](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9-Ps65:10)**; **[ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)** *See also* [GEN 26:12](https://ref.ly/Gen26:12); [JER 2:7](https://ref.ly/Jer2:7); [EZK 34:27–34:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:27-Ezek34:29), [EZK 36:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:30); [ZEC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:12); [JAS 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Jas5:17-Jas5:18)

------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Deut11:16-Deut11:17)** *See also* [GEN 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:10-Gen4:12); [DEU 28:18](https://ref.ly/Deut28:18), [DEU 28:42](https://ref.ly/Deut28:42), [DEU 28:49–28:51](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49-Deut28:51); [JDG 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Judg6:3-Judg6:4); [JOB 31:5–31:8](https://ref.ly/Job31:5-Job31:8); [PSA 78:46](https://ref.ly/Ps78:46), [PSA 105:34–105:35](https://ref.ly/Ps105:34-Ps105:35); [AMO 7:1](https://ref.ly/Amos7:1); [HAG 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Hag1:9-Hag1:10)
