## 4432: dough (id: 4432)

A mixture of flour and water to which yeast is added and which is then baked to produce bread. Scripture makes several comparisons with it to teach spiritual lessons.

Dough and the process of making bread
-------------------------------------

### Yeast was normally added and the dough left to rise

**[EXO 12:33–12:34](https://ref.ly/Exod12:33-Exod12:34)** *See also* [EXO 12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:39)

### Dough was kneaded before baking

**[JER 7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:18)** *See also* [2SA 13:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:8); [HOS 7:4](https://ref.ly/Hos7:4)

Spiritual lessons from comparisons with dough
---------------------------------------------

### The dough offered as firstfruits

**[ROM 11:13–11:16](https://ref.ly/Rom11:13-Rom11:16)**

### The influence of yeast on the dough

**[MAT 13:33](https://ref.ly/Matt13:33)** parallel [LUK 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Luke13:20-Luke13:21) *See also* [1CO 5:1–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:8); [GAL 5:1–5:10](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1-Gal5:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 12:33-34; Exodus 12:39; Jeremiah 7:18; 2 Samuel 13:8; Hosea 7:4; Romans 11:13-16; Matthew 13:33; Luke 13:20-21; 1 Corinthians 5:1-8; Galatians 5:1-10
* **Associated Articles:** 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4418: bread (ID: `4418@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4554: yeast (ID: `4554@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5222: baking (ID: `5222@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5435: ovens (ID: `5435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7222: exodus, events of (ID: `7222@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4434: drinking (id: 4434)

Scripture lays down no general prohibition of the consumption of alcohol, but warns against its abuse.

* **Associated Articles:** 4435: drinking (ID: `4435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4436: drinking, abstention from (ID: `4436@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4435: drinking (id: 4435)

Wine as a gift of God
---------------------

### A sign of his blessing

**[DEU 7:13](https://ref.ly/Deut7:13)** *See also* [GEN 27:28](https://ref.ly/Gen27:28); [DEU 33:28](https://ref.ly/Deut33:28); [PRO 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Prov3:9-Prov3:10); [JOL 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/Joel2:18-Joel2:19), [JOL 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Joel2:22-Joel2:24), [JOL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Joel3:18); [AMO 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Amos9:13-Amos9:14)

Withdrawal of wine is evidence of divine judgment
-------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 24:6–24:7](https://ref.ly/Isa24:6-Isa24:7)** *See also* [JER 48:32–48:33](https://ref.ly/Jer48:32-Jer48:33)

### Of spiritual satisfaction

[JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14)

**[JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37)**

## 4436: drinking, abstention from (id: 4436)

Scripture indicates that the abuse of drinking, such as drunkenness, is unacceptable. It also indicates the advantages of abstention from drinking in certain situations.

For some, at certain times, total abstinence is right
-----------------------------------------------------

[LEV 10:8–10:9](https://ref.ly/Lev10:8-Lev10:9); ; [EZK 44:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:21) *priests*; ; [NUM 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:4), [NUM 6:20](https://ref.ly/Num6:20) *Nazirites*; ; [JDG 13:2–13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2-Judg13:5), [JDG 13:7](https://ref.ly/Judg13:7), [JDG 13:13–13:14](https://ref.ly/Judg13:13-Judg13:14); ; [PRO 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:5); ; [ISA 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12-Isa22:13); ; [JER 35:1–35:14](https://ref.ly/Jer35:1-Jer35:14); ; [DAN 1:5–1:16](https://ref.ly/Dan1:5-Dan1:16); [MAT 11:18](https://ref.ly/Matt11:18); ; [LUK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Luke1:15); [MAT 27:34](https://ref.ly/Matt27:34) *Jesus Christ*; ; [ROM 14:21](https://ref.ly/Rom14:21) *the Christian who does not want to give offence*

Warnings against the abuse of alcohol
-------------------------------------

**[ISA 5:11](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11)** *See also* [PRO 20:1](https://ref.ly/Prov20:1); [ISA 5:22](https://ref.ly/Isa5:22); [HAB 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hab2:15); [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34); [ROM 13:13](https://ref.ly/Rom13:13); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10), [1CO 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:21); [GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21); [EPH 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eph5:18); [1TH 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:7-1Thess5:8); [TIT 2:3](https://ref.ly/Titus2:3)

Christian leaders are to be sober
---------------------------------

**[1TI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:3)** *See also* [1TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8); [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7)

Examples of drunkenness
-----------------------

----------------------------------------------

## 4438: eating (id: 4438)

The consumption of food, necessary for survival. Scripture cites enjoyment of eating as a gift of God but emphasises that it is not the main priority in life, for God will provide abundantly for his children.

Eating for survival
-------------------

**[NEH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:2)** *See also* [GEN 28:20–28:22](https://ref.ly/Gen28:20-Gen28:22); [1KI 17:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:12), [1KI 19:3–19:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:8); [2KI 25:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:3)

Eating as a sign of life and health
-----------------------------------

**[DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28)** *See also* [2SA 13:5–13:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:5-2Sam13:10); [PSA 102:4](https://ref.ly/Ps102:4); [MRK 5:35–5:43](https://ref.ly/Mark5:35-Mark5:43) parallel [LUK 8:49–8:56](https://ref.ly/Luke8:49-Luke8:56); [LUK 24:36–24:43](https://ref.ly/Luke24:36-Luke24:43); [JHN 6:48–6:58](https://ref.ly/John6:48-John6:58)

Enjoyment of eating as a gift of God
------------------------------------

**[ECC 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:13)** *See also* [GEN 27:1–27:25](https://ref.ly/Gen27:1-Gen27:25); [ECC 2:24–2:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24-Eccl2:25), [ECC 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:18), [ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15), [ECC 9:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:7)

God blesses his people with sufficient food
-------------------------------------------

**[PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25)** *See also* [LEV 26:3–26:5](https://ref.ly/Lev26:3-Lev26:5), [LEV 26:23–26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:23-Lev26:26); [DEU 8:16](https://ref.ly/Deut8:16), [DEU 11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:15), [DEU 12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:7); [JOS 24:11–24:13](https://ref.ly/Josh24:11-Josh24:13); [PSA 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ps22:26), [PSA 78:23–78:29](https://ref.ly/Ps78:23-Ps78:29), [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2), [PSA 128:1–128:2](https://ref.ly/Ps128:1-Ps128:2); [PRO 13:25](https://ref.ly/Prov13:25); [ISA 1:19](https://ref.ly/Isa1:19), [ISA 7:22](https://ref.ly/Isa7:22), [ISA 65:13](https://ref.ly/Isa65:13), [ISA 65:21–65:22](https://ref.ly/Isa65:21-Isa65:22); [JOL 2:26](https://ref.ly/Joel2:26); [AMO 9:14](https://ref.ly/Amos9:14); [MIC 6:9–6:16](https://ref.ly/Mic6:9-Mic6:16); [HAG 1:5–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hag1:5-Hag1:11)

## 4440: fig-tree (id: 4440)

A tree common in the Near East, with soft, sweet fruit which can be eaten fresh or dried. The fruit was an important part of the diet in biblical times and the tree was symbolic of peace and security.

Figs
----

### Figs prized as a sweet, pleasant fruit

**[JDG 9:10–9:11](https://ref.ly/Judg9:10-Judg9:11)** *See also* [NUM 13:21–13:23](https://ref.ly/Num13:21-Num13:23)

### Figs a significant part of the diet in biblical times

**[NUM 20:5](https://ref.ly/Num20:5)** *See also* [DEU 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:9); [1SA 30:11–30:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:11-1Sam30:12); [NEH 13:15](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15)

### Figs given in gifts of food

**[1SA 25:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:18)** *See also* [2SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:1); [1CH 12:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:40)

### Other uses of figs and fig leaves

**[2KI 20:1–20:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:1-2Kgs20:7)** *See also* [GEN 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6-Gen3:7); [ISA 38:21](https://ref.ly/Isa38:21)

[JER 24:1–24:10](https://ref.ly/Jer24:1-Jer24:10), [JER 29:15–29:19](https://ref.ly/Jer29:15-Jer29:19)

## 4442: firstfruits (id: 4442)

Offerings of the produce of the land, being the first and best of the crops, given in acknowledgment of God's abundant blessings. The term is also used metaphorically to indicate the first of a much larger group that is to follow, such as those who will be raised from the dead.

Offering firstfruits
--------------------

**[EXO 23:19](https://ref.ly/Exod23:19)** *See also* [EXO 34:26](https://ref.ly/Exod34:26); [LEV 2:11–2:16](https://ref.ly/Lev2:11-Lev2:16); [DEU 26:1–26:11](https://ref.ly/Deut26:1-Deut26:11); [NEH 10:35–10:39](https://ref.ly/Neh10:35-Neh10:39), [NEH 13:31](https://ref.ly/Neh13:31); [PRO 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Prov3:9-Prov3:10); [ROM 11:16](https://ref.ly/Rom11:16)

----------------------------------------

**[NUM 18:8–18:13](https://ref.ly/Num18:8-Num18:13)** *See also* [DEU 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/Deut18:1-Deut18:5); [2CH 31:2–31:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:2-2Chr31:5); [NEH 12:44–12:47](https://ref.ly/Neh12:44-Neh12:47); [EZK 44:28–44:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:28-Ezek44:30)

## 4446: flowers (id: 4446)

The blossoming part of plants and trees, from which the seeds or fruit develop. Often admired for their beauty, Scripture uses their fleeting existence as an illustration of the transience of human life.

-------------------

### In the tabernacle

**[EXO 25:31–25:36](https://ref.ly/Exod25:31-Exod25:36)** parallel [EXO 37:17–37:22](https://ref.ly/Exod37:17-Exod37:22)

**[1KI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:18)** *See also* [1KI 6:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:29), [1KI 6:31–6:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:31-1Kgs6:35), [1KI 7:15–7:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:15-1Kgs7:22), [1KI 7:25–7:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:25-1Kgs7:26) parallel [2CH 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:4-2Chr4:5)

## 4450: fruit (id: 4450)

## 4456: grain (id: 4456)

The seed of cereals. Together with wine and oil, it was one of the three basic commodities in biblical times. Grain offerings were central to Israelite religion and tithes and firstfruits of grain were also given to God.

Grain was a basic foodstuff
---------------------------

**[DEU 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Deut7:12-Deut7:13)** *See also* [DEU 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:13-Deut11:15), [DEU 28:49–28:51](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49-Deut28:51), [DEU 33:28](https://ref.ly/Deut33:28); [RUT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:14); [1SA 17:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17), [1SA 25:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:18); [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29); [NEH 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:3), [NEH 5:10–5:11](https://ref.ly/Neh5:10-Neh5:11)

### The cultivation of barley

## 4458: grape (id: 4458)

Fruit which grows in clusters on a vine. It was very widely cultivated in the Near East in biblical times and was used mostly for making wine.

The cultivation of grapes indicative of rich and fertile land
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 20:2–20:5](https://ref.ly/Num20:2-Num20:5)** *See also* [NUM 13:21–13:25](https://ref.ly/Num13:21-Num13:25)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4460: grass (id: 4460)

Green vegetation, which grows rapidly after rainfall, and serves as good food for many animals. The withering of grass in hot weather is often used in Scripture as a symbol of the frailty and brevity of human life.

Grass is food for animals
-------------------------

**[1KI 18:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:5)** *See also* [NUM 22:4](https://ref.ly/Num22:4); [DEU 11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:15); [JOB 6:5](https://ref.ly/Job6:5), [JOB 40:15](https://ref.ly/Job40:15); [PSA 104:14](https://ref.ly/Ps104:14), [PSA 106:20](https://ref.ly/Ps106:20); [JER 14:5](https://ref.ly/Jer14:5); [DAN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Dan4:25), [DAN 4:32–4:33](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32-Dan4:33), [DAN 5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:21)

Rain is essential for the growth of grass
-----------------------------------------

**[JOB 38:25–38:27](https://ref.ly/Job38:25-Job38:27)** *See also* [DEU 32:2](https://ref.ly/Deut32:2); [2SA 23:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:4); [PSA 147:8](https://ref.ly/Ps147:8); [PRO 19:12](https://ref.ly/Prov19:12); [MIC 5:7](https://ref.ly/Mic5:7)

----------------------------------

**[ISA 44:3–44:4](https://ref.ly/Isa44:3-Isa44:4)** *See also* [JOB 5:25](https://ref.ly/Job5:25); [PSA 72:16](https://ref.ly/Ps72:16), [PSA 92:7](https://ref.ly/Ps92:7); [ISA 35:7](https://ref.ly/Isa35:7), [ISA 66:14](https://ref.ly/Isa66:14)

Grass withers, dies and burns easily
------------------------------------

**[ISA 15:6](https://ref.ly/Isa15:6)** *See also* [PSA 102:4](https://ref.ly/Ps102:4), [PSA 102:11](https://ref.ly/Ps102:11); [ISA 5:24](https://ref.ly/Isa5:24); [JER 12:4](https://ref.ly/Jer12:4); [REV 8:7](https://ref.ly/Rev8:7)

The life cycle of grass is short
--------------------------------

**[PSA 129:6](https://ref.ly/Ps129:6)** *See also* [ISA 37:27](https://ref.ly/Isa37:27); [MAT 6:28–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:28-Matt6:34) parallel [LUK 12:27–12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27-Luke12:31)

------------------------------------------------------

## 4464: harvest (id: 4464)

The gathering of mature crops from the land. The successful culmination of the agricultural year was evidence of God's goodness and consequently marked with festivity. Harvest is also used widely in a metaphorical sense.

The harvest is evidence of God's goodness
-----------------------------------------

**[GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22)**; **[2CO 9:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:10)** *See also* [LEV 26:5](https://ref.ly/Lev26:5), [LEV 26:9–26:10](https://ref.ly/Lev26:9-Lev26:10); [PSA 67:6](https://ref.ly/Ps67:6), [PSA 85:12](https://ref.ly/Ps85:12), [PSA 107:37](https://ref.ly/Ps107:37); [ISA 62:8–62:9](https://ref.ly/Isa62:8-Isa62:9); [JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24)

----------------------------

## 4466: herbs and spices (id: 4466)

Plants whose leaves or seeds are used as food or flavourings and sometimes for medicine. The rich variety of such plants in the Near East results in numerous references in Scripture.

## 4468: horticulture (id: 4468)

The art of garden cultivation, first practised in the Garden of Eden.

Horticulture in the Garden of Eden
----------------------------------

### The man and woman tended the garden

**[GEN 2:15–2:23](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15-Gen2:23)**

### The fall brought about a change in human priorities from horticulture to agriculture

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *See also* [GEN 4:2](https://ref.ly/Gen4:2)

Egyptian agriculture is likened to horticulture
-----------------------------------------------

**[DEU 11:10–11:11](https://ref.ly/Deut11:10-Deut11:11)**

Horticulture is totally dependent on good irrigation
----------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:11)** *See also* [GEN 2:4–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gen2:4-Gen2:14), [GEN 13:10](https://ref.ly/Gen13:10); [NUM 24:5–24:7](https://ref.ly/Num24:5-Num24:7); [JOB 8:16](https://ref.ly/Job8:16); [SNG 4:12](https://ref.ly/Song4:12), [SNG 4:15](https://ref.ly/Song4:15); [ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3); [JER 31:12](https://ref.ly/Jer31:12)

## 4474: manna (id: 4474)

The source of food provided miraculously for the children of Israel during their desert wanderings. The NT sees this heavenly food as a symbol of Jesus Christ as the bread of life.

Manna provided by God
---------------------

**[EXO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Exod16:31)**; **[JOS 5:12](https://ref.ly/Josh5:12)** *See also* [EXO 16:32–16:35](https://ref.ly/Exod16:32-Exod16:35); [NUM 11:6–11:9](https://ref.ly/Num11:6-Num11:9); [NEH 9:15](https://ref.ly/Neh9:15); [PSA 78:24–78:25](https://ref.ly/Ps78:24-Ps78:25); [JHN 6:31](https://ref.ly/John6:31), [JHN 6:49](https://ref.ly/John6:49); [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4)

Manna as a type
---------------

### A type of the word of God

**[DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3)** *See also* [DEU 8:16](https://ref.ly/Deut8:16); [NEH 9:20](https://ref.ly/Neh9:20); [MAT 4:4](https://ref.ly/Matt4:4)

### A type of Jesus Christ

**[JHN 6:50–6:51](https://ref.ly/John6:50-John6:51)** *See also* [JHN 6:32–6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:32-John6:35), [JHN 6:47–6:48](https://ref.ly/John6:47-John6:48), [JHN 6:52–6:58](https://ref.ly/John6:52-John6:58)

### A symbol of future blessing

[REV 2:17](https://ref.ly/Rev2:17)

## 4476: meals (id: 4476)

Food eaten at set times, on special occasions or following significant events. Sacrificial meals formed part of Israelite worship in the OT, as does the Lord's Supper for worship by believers in the NT.

## 4478: meat (id: 4478)

The flesh of animals, consumed as food. In biblical times it was a luxury, eaten on special occasions, usually in the context of sacrificial worship.

Meat a luxury food
------------------

**[EXO 16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:3)** *See also* [NUM 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/Num11:4-Num11:5); [1SA 25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:11); [ISA 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12-Isa22:13); [DAN 10:3](https://ref.ly/Dan10:3)

Meat served to honoured guests
------------------------------

**[1SA 28:21–28:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:21-1Sam28:25)** *See also* [GEN 18:1–18:8](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1-Gen18:8); [JDG 6:11–6:21](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11-Judg6:21), [JDG 13:9–13:16](https://ref.ly/Judg13:9-Judg13:16); [1SA 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:22-1Sam9:24); [2SA 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:1-2Sam12:4); [1KI 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19-1Kgs19:21); [PRO 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Prov9:1-Prov9:6); [ISA 25:6](https://ref.ly/Isa25:6); [LUK 15:20–15:24](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20-Luke15:24)

Meat provided by God
--------------------

**[1KI 17:2–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:2-1Kgs17:6)** *See also* [EXO 16:1–16:14](https://ref.ly/Exod16:1-Exod16:14); [NUM 11:4–11:34](https://ref.ly/Num11:4-Num11:34); [PSA 78:17–78:31](https://ref.ly/Ps78:17-Ps78:31)

Restrictions on eating certain types of meat
--------------------------------------------

### Unclean meat

**[LEV 11:1–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:47)** parallel [DEU 14:3–14:20](https://ref.ly/Deut14:3-Deut14:20) *See also* [ISA 65:2–65:5](https://ref.ly/Isa65:2-Isa65:5)

### Other prohibited meat

**[GEN 9:4](https://ref.ly/Gen9:4)** *See also* [EXO 21:28](https://ref.ly/Exod21:28), [EXO 22:31](https://ref.ly/Exod22:31); [LEV 19:26](https://ref.ly/Lev19:26); [DEU 12:23](https://ref.ly/Deut12:23), [DEU 14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:21); [1SA 14:33–14:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:33-1Sam14:34); [EZK 4:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:14), [EZK 33:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:25); [ACT 15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:20), [ACT 15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:29), [ACT 21:25](https://ref.ly/Acts21:25)

----------------------

**[EXO 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:1-Exod12:11)** *See also* [EXO 12:43–12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:43-Exod12:49); [DEU 16:1–16:8](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:8)

### Other sacrifices

**[EXO 29:31–29:34](https://ref.ly/Exod29:31-Exod29:34)** *See also* [LEV 7:11–7:18](https://ref.ly/Lev7:11-Lev7:18); [NUM 18:14–18:19](https://ref.ly/Num18:14-Num18:19); [DEU 12:27](https://ref.ly/Deut12:27); [1SA 1:3–1:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:3-1Sam1:8), [1SA 2:12–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:12-1Sam2:16); [JER 7:21](https://ref.ly/Jer7:21); [HOS 8:13](https://ref.ly/Hos8:13)

Non\-consecrated meat
---------------------

-----------------------------------------------

## 4480: milk (id: 4480)

A common source of food and nourishment, associated with rich and productive land. The term is also used figuratively as a form of spiritual food suitable for those who have yet to reach maturity in the Christian faith.

Milk as a source of nourishment
-------------------------------

**[DEU 32:13–32:14](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13-Deut32:14)** *See also* [GEN 18:8](https://ref.ly/Gen18:8); [JDG 4:19](https://ref.ly/Judg4:19); [2SA 17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:29); [PRO 27:27](https://ref.ly/Prov27:27); [EZK 25:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:4); [1CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7) *illustrating the support of the ministry*

Milk as an indication of prosperity and well\-being
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 6:3](https://ref.ly/Deut6:3)** *A frequent description of the promised land*; **[ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1)** *See also* [EXO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:8); [LEV 20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:24); [NUM 13:27](https://ref.ly/Num13:27); [ISA 7:22](https://ref.ly/Isa7:22), [ISA 60:16](https://ref.ly/Isa60:16); [EZK 20:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:6); [JOL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Joel3:18)

Laws concerning milk
--------------------

[EXO 23:19](https://ref.ly/Exod23:19), [EXO 34:26](https://ref.ly/Exod34:26); ; [DEU 14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:21)

Elementary Christian teaching likened to milk
---------------------------------------------

**[HEB 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/Heb5:12-Heb5:13)** *See also* [1CO 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:2); [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2)

Milk used figuratively
----------------------

**[PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33)** *See also* [GEN 49:12](https://ref.ly/Gen49:12); [JOB 10:10](https://ref.ly/Job10:10); [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11), [SNG 5:12](https://ref.ly/Song5:12); [LAM 4:7](https://ref.ly/Lam4:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 32:13-14; Genesis 18:8; Judges 4:19; 2 Samuel 17:29; Proverbs 27:27; Ezekiel 25:4; 1 Corinthians 9:7; Deuteronomy 6:3; Isaiah 55:1; Exodus 3:8; Leviticus 20:24; Numbers 13:27; Isaiah 7:22; Isaiah 60:16; Ezekiel 20:6; Joel 3:18; Exodus 23:19; Exodus 34:26; Deuteronomy 14:21; Hebrews 5:12-13; 1 Corinthians 3:2; 1 Peter 2:2; Proverbs 30:33; Genesis 49:12; Job 10:10; Song of Songs 4:11; Song of Songs 5:12; Lamentations 4:7
* **Associated Articles:** 4404: food (ID: `4404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5881: immaturity (ID: `5881@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7257: promised land, the (ID: `7257@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4482: millstones (id: 4482)

Pairs of upper and lower millstones were used for grinding grain in the ancient East. Millstones were common features of the daily life of ordinary people.

Millstones as a means of earning a living
-----------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6)** *See also* [ISA 47:1–47:2](https://ref.ly/Isa47:1-Isa47:2); [JER 25:10](https://ref.ly/Jer25:10); [REV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Rev18:22)

Millstones were heavy and burdensome
------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Matt18:6)** parallels [MRK 9:42](https://ref.ly/Mark9:42), [LUK 17:2](https://ref.ly/Luke17:2)

Millstones as potential weapons
-------------------------------

**[JDG 9:50–9:53](https://ref.ly/Judg9:50-Judg9:53)** *See also* [2SA 11:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:21)

Millstones in figurative use
----------------------------

**[JOB 41:24](https://ref.ly/Job41:24)**

Millstones as symbols of destructive force
------------------------------------------

**[REV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Rev18:21)**

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 24:6; Isaiah 47:1-2; Jeremiah 25:10; Revelation 18:22; Matthew 18:6; Mark 9:42; Luke 17:2; Judges 9:50-53; 2 Samuel 11:21; Job 41:24; Revelation 18:21
* **Associated Articles:** 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4462: grinding (ID: `4462@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5295: destruction (ID: `5295@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4484: mustard seed (id: 4484)

A very small seed which grew rapidly into a substantial plant. It was used in parables by Jesus Christ to encourage the faith of the individual and to illustrate the growth of the kingdom of God.

The mustard seed and the faith of the individual
------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/Luke17:5-Luke17:6)** *See also* [MAT 17:14–17:20](https://ref.ly/Matt17:14-Matt17:20)

The mustard seed and the kingdom of God
---------------------------------------

**[MAT 13:31–13:32](https://ref.ly/Matt13:31-Matt13:32)** parallels [MRK 4:30–4:32](https://ref.ly/Mark4:30-Mark4:32), [LUK 13:18–13:19](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18-Luke13:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 17:5-6; Matthew 17:14-20; Matthew 13:31-32; Mark 4:30-32; Luke 13:18-19
* **Associated Articles:** 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4402: plants (ID: `4402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4506: seed (ID: `4506@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4528: trees (ID: `4528@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5438: parables (ID: `5438@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8443: growth (ID: `8443@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4486: myrrh (id: 4486)

A resinous substance derived from certain trees and bushes native to the Near East. It was a valuable commodity and had a variety of uses.

Myrrh was traded by merchants
-----------------------------

**[GEN 37:25](https://ref.ly/Gen37:25)** *See also* [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

Myrrh was presented as a gift
-----------------------------

**[MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11)** *See also* [GEN 43:11](https://ref.ly/Gen43:11)

Uses of myrrh
-------------

### In the sacred anointing oil

**[EXO 30:22–30:28](https://ref.ly/Exod30:22-Exod30:28)**

### As a perfume

**[SNG 1:13](https://ref.ly/Song1:13)** *See also* [PSA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8); [PRO 7:10–7:18](https://ref.ly/Prov7:10-Prov7:18); [SNG 3:6](https://ref.ly/Song3:6)

### In beauty treatments

**[EST 2:12](https://ref.ly/Esth2:12)** *See also* [SNG 5:5](https://ref.ly/Song5:5)

### As a painkiller

**[MRK 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/Mark15:22-Mark15:23)**

### In embalming

**[JHN 19:38–19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:38-John19:40)**

The metaphorical use of myrrh
-----------------------------

**[SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13)** *See also* [SNG 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/Song4:1-Song4:7), [SNG 4:12–4:14](https://ref.ly/Song4:12-Song4:14), [SNG 5:1](https://ref.ly/Song5:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 37:25; Revelation 18:11-13; Matthew 2:11; Genesis 43:11; Exodus 30:22-28; Song of Songs 1:13; Psalms 45:8; Proverbs 7:10-18; Song of Songs 3:6; Esther 2:12; Song of Songs 5:5; Mark 15:22-23; John 19:38-40; Song of Songs 5:13; Song of Songs 4:1-7; Song of Songs 4:12-14; Song of Songs 5:1

## 4488: oil (id: 4488)

Normally olive oil, which was in plentiful supply in Israel and in general household use. Its ritual uses included the anointing of priests and kings and ceremonial cleansing; in the NT oil was used to anoint the sick. As a sign of God's provision for his people, oil was associated with prosperity and gladness, and was a mark of spiritual blessing.

The production of oil
---------------------

[JOB 24:11](https://ref.ly/Job24:11) *Olives were pressed either with a pestle and mortar or in a stone press*; ; [MAT 26:36](https://ref.ly/Matt26:36) *“Gethsemane” means “oil press”, named after the stone presses associated with the Mount of Olives.*

General uses of oil
-------------------

### As a necessary provision

[1CH 12:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:40); ; [2CH 11:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:11)

### As a fuel for lamps

**[EXO 27:20](https://ref.ly/Exod27:20)** parallel [LEV 24:2](https://ref.ly/Lev24:2) *See also* [EXO 35:14](https://ref.ly/Exod35:14) parallel [EXO 39:37](https://ref.ly/Exod39:37); [NUM 4:9](https://ref.ly/Num4:9); [MAT 25:1–25:10](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:10)

### Mixed with flour in baking

**[1KI 17:12–17:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:12-1Kgs17:16)** *See also* [EXO 29:23](https://ref.ly/Exod29:23); [NUM 11:8](https://ref.ly/Num11:8)

### As an ointment

**[ISA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:6)** *See also* [PSA 55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:21); [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34)

Oil for use in the sanctuary
----------------------------

**[NEH 10:39](https://ref.ly/Neh10:39)** *See also* [EXO 25:6](https://ref.ly/Exod25:6) parallel [EXO 35:8](https://ref.ly/Exod35:8); [NUM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Num4:16); [1CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:29); [EZR 6:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:9)

Oil used for ceremonial anointing
---------------------------------

### Instructions for making and using the anointing oil

**[EXO 30:23–30:33](https://ref.ly/Exod30:23-Exod30:33)** *See also* [EXO 31:11](https://ref.ly/Exod31:11), [EXO 37:29](https://ref.ly/Exod37:29)

### Holy objects were consecrated by anointing with oil

Jacob twice anoints a pillar at Bethel: [GEN 28:18](https://ref.ly/Gen28:18), [GEN 31:13](https://ref.ly/Gen31:13), [GEN 35:14](https://ref.ly/Gen35:14); [EXO 40:9–40:11](https://ref.ly/Exod40:9-Exod40:11); ; [LEV 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Lev8:10-Lev8:11)

### Priests were ordained by anointing with oil

**[LEV 8:12](https://ref.ly/Lev8:12)** parallel [EXO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Exod29:7) *See also* [EXO 29:21](https://ref.ly/Exod29:21) parallel [LEV 8:30](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30); [LEV 10:7](https://ref.ly/Lev10:7), [LEV 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:12); [PSA 133:2](https://ref.ly/Ps133:2); [ZEC 4:12–4:14](https://ref.ly/Zech4:12-Zech4:14)

### Kings were anointed with oil

[1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1) *Saul*; [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13); ; [PSA 89:20](https://ref.ly/Ps89:20); [1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39) *Solomon*; ; [2KI 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1-2Kgs9:6) *Jehu*

Further ceremonial uses of oil
------------------------------

### In ceremonial cleansing

[LEV 14:15–14:18](https://ref.ly/Lev14:15-Lev14:18), [LEV 14:24–14:29](https://ref.ly/Lev14:24-Lev14:29)

### In offerings

At the consecration of priests: [EXO 29:2](https://ref.ly/Exod29:2); ; [NUM 8:8](https://ref.ly/Num8:8); [EXO 29:40](https://ref.ly/Exod29:40); ; [NUM 28:5](https://ref.ly/Num28:5); [LEV 2:1–2:7](https://ref.ly/Lev2:1-Lev2:7), [LEV 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Lev6:20-Lev6:21); ; [NUM 7:13](https://ref.ly/Num7:13); [LEV 7:12](https://ref.ly/Lev7:12) *with the thank offering*; ; [NUM 6:15](https://ref.ly/Num6:15) *at the completion of the Nazirite's vow*; [NUM 28:9](https://ref.ly/Num28:9), [NUM 28:28](https://ref.ly/Num28:28); ; [EZK 46:4–46:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:4-Ezek46:5)

### Oil was excluded from certain offerings

[LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11); ; [NUM 5:15](https://ref.ly/Num5:15)

Oil used to anoint the sick
---------------------------

**[JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)** *See also* [MRK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:13)

God's provision of oil
----------------------

### Oil as a sign of God's blessing

**[DEU 32:13](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13)** *See also* [DEU 7:13](https://ref.ly/Deut7:13), [DEU 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:8), [DEU 11:14](https://ref.ly/Deut11:14); [HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8)

### Oil withdrawn as a sign of judgment

**[DEU 28:40](https://ref.ly/Deut28:40)** *See also* [DEU 28:51](https://ref.ly/Deut28:51); [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10); [MIC 6:15](https://ref.ly/Mic6:15); [HAG 1:11](https://ref.ly/Hag1:11)

### Renewed provision of oil following restoration

**[JER 31:12](https://ref.ly/Jer31:12)** *See also* [HOS 2:22](https://ref.ly/Hos2:22); [JOL 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/Joel2:18-Joel2:19), [JOL 2:24](https://ref.ly/Joel2:24)

### The firstfruits of the oil harvest

[NUM 18:12](https://ref.ly/Num18:12); ; [DEU 18:4](https://ref.ly/Deut18:4); ; [2CH 31:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:5); ; [NEH 10:37](https://ref.ly/Neh10:37)

### Tithes of oil

[DEU 12:17](https://ref.ly/Deut12:17), [DEU 14:23](https://ref.ly/Deut14:23); ; [NEH 13:12](https://ref.ly/Neh13:12)

### Oil as a commodity for trade

[1KI 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:11) parallel [2CH 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:10); ; [EZR 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:7); ; [EZK 27:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:17); ; [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1)

### Oil as a sign of prosperity

**[JOB 29:6](https://ref.ly/Job29:6)** *See also* [DEU 33:24](https://ref.ly/Deut33:24); [2KI 20:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:13) parallel [ISA 39:2](https://ref.ly/Isa39:2); [PRO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Prov21:20); [ISA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Isa57:9); [EZK 16:18–16:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:18-Ezek16:19)

### Oil associated with celebration

**[PSA 45:7](https://ref.ly/Ps45:7)** *The joy with which God anoints his servants is likened to the oil which anoints the bridegroom in preparation for his wedding.* *See also* [PSA 104:15](https://ref.ly/Ps104:15); [ECC 9:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:8); [ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3); [MAT 6:17](https://ref.ly/Matt6:17) *Oil draws attention away from the fact that a person is fasting*; [HEB 1:9](https://ref.ly/Heb1:9)

### Oil as a sign of honour

**[PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5)** *Oil was used to anoint the heads of honoured guests at a meal.* *See also* [PSA 141:5](https://ref.ly/Ps141:5); [LUK 7:46](https://ref.ly/Luke7:46)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 24:11; Matthew 26:36; 1 Chronicles 12:40; 2 Chronicles 11:11; Exodus 27:20; Leviticus 24:2; Exodus 35:14; Exodus 39:37; Numbers 4:9; Matthew 25:1-10; 1 Kings 17:12-16; Exodus 29:23; Numbers 11:8; Isaiah 1:6; Psalms 55:21; Luke 10:34; Nehemiah 10:39; Exodus 25:6; Exodus 35:8; Numbers 4:16; 1 Chronicles 9:29; Ezra 6:9; Exodus 30:23-33; Exodus 31:11; Exodus 37:29; Genesis 28:18; Genesis 31:13; Genesis 35:14; Exodus 40:9-11; Leviticus 8:10-11; Leviticus 8:12; Exodus 29:7; Exodus 29:21; Leviticus 8:30; Leviticus 10:7; Leviticus 21:10-12; Psalms 133:2; Zechariah 4:12-14; 1 Samuel 10:1; 1 Samuel 16:13; Psalms 89:20; 1 Kings 1:39; 2 Kings 9:1-6; Leviticus 14:15-18; Leviticus 14:24-29; Exodus 29:2; Numbers 8:8; Exodus 29:40; Numbers 28:5; Leviticus 2:1-7; Leviticus 6:20-21; Numbers 7:13; Leviticus 7:12; Numbers 6:15; Numbers 28:9; Numbers 28:28; Ezekiel 46:4-5; Leviticus 5:11; Numbers 5:15; James 5:14; Mark 6:13; Deuteronomy 32:13; Deuteronomy 7:13; Deuteronomy 8:7-8; Deuteronomy 11:14; Hosea 2:8; Deuteronomy 28:40; Deuteronomy 28:51; Joel 1:10; Micah 6:15; Haggai 1:11; Jeremiah 31:12; Hosea 2:22; Joel 2:18-19; Joel 2:24; Numbers 18:12; Deuteronomy 18:4; 2 Chronicles 31:5; Nehemiah 10:37; Deuteronomy 12:17; Deuteronomy 14:23; Nehemiah 13:12; 1 Kings 5:11; 2 Chronicles 2:10; Ezra 3:7; Ezekiel 27:17; Hosea 12:1; Job 29:6; Deuteronomy 33:24; 2 Kings 20:13; Isaiah 39:2; Proverbs 21:20; Isaiah 57:9; Ezekiel 16:18-19; Psalms 45:7; Psalms 104:15; Ecclesiastes 9:8; Isaiah 61:3; Matthew 6:17; Hebrews 1:9; Psalms 23:5; Psalms 141:5; Luke 7:46

## 4490: ointment (id: 4490)

A liquid blend of aromatic substances, usually spices, used as a cosmetic and also as a medicinal balm. Lotions were applied to the body to refresh, to improve appearance and to give a pleasing fragrance. Balm, especially from Gilead, was used to soothe pain and to heal wounds.

Preparation of ointment
-----------------------

**[JOB 41:31](https://ref.ly/Job41:31)** *One way of making ointment was to crush solid ingredients and boil them together in olive oil.*

### Preparation of the holy anointing oil

**[EXO 35:28](https://ref.ly/Exod35:28)** *See also* [EXO 25:6](https://ref.ly/Exod25:6) parallel [EXO 35:8](https://ref.ly/Exod35:8); [EXO 30:23–30:25](https://ref.ly/Exod30:23-Exod30:25), [EXO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Exod30:33) *The holy anointing oil was to be prepared only by those authorised to do so.*

### Ointments made from blended spices

[2CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:14); ; [SNG 3:6](https://ref.ly/Song3:6); ; [LUK 23:56](https://ref.ly/Luke23:56)

Containers for ointment
-----------------------

[ISA 3:20](https://ref.ly/Isa3:20); [MAT 26:7](https://ref.ly/Matt26:7) parallel [MRK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3); [LUK 7:37](https://ref.ly/Luke7:37)

Uses of ointment
----------------

### As a cosmetic lotion

**[RUT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:3)** *See also* [EST 2:12](https://ref.ly/Esth2:12); [SNG 1:12](https://ref.ly/Song1:12); [EZK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:9)**[PSA 104:15](https://ref.ly/Ps104:15)** *See also* [2SA 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:20), [2SA 14:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:2); [ECC 9:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:8); [DAN 10:3](https://ref.ly/Dan10:3); [MAT 6:17](https://ref.ly/Matt6:17); [EXO 30:32](https://ref.ly/Exod30:32)

### To anoint the heads of guests

**[PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5)** *See also* [PSA 133:2](https://ref.ly/Ps133:2) *This may reflect the custom of anointing a guest with perfumed ointment which melted and ran down the face and clothes to produce a pleasing fragrance*; [LUK 7:46](https://ref.ly/Luke7:46)

### To anoint the dead

**[MAT 26:12](https://ref.ly/Matt26:12)** parallel [MRK 14:8](https://ref.ly/Mark14:8) *See also* [JHN 12:7](https://ref.ly/John12:7); [GEN 50:2–50:3](https://ref.ly/Gen50:2-Gen50:3), [GEN 50:26](https://ref.ly/Gen50:26); [MRK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Mark16:1) parallel [LUK 24:1](https://ref.ly/Luke24:1); [JHN 19:39–19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:39-John19:40)

Expensive ointments
-------------------

**[MAT 26:8–26:9](https://ref.ly/Matt26:8-Matt26:9)** parallel [MRK 14:4–14:5](https://ref.ly/Mark14:4-Mark14:5) *(Spike)nard was an expensive import from India.* *See also* [PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17); [SNG 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Song4:13-Song4:14); [AMO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:6) *The use of expensive ointments was a mark of luxury*; [JHN 12:5](https://ref.ly/John12:5)

Ointment used in healing
------------------------

### Oil used to soothe and heal

**[ISA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:6)** *See also* [PSA 55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:21); [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34)

### Medicinal balm

**[JER 8:22](https://ref.ly/Jer8:22)** *Gilead was famous for its healing balsam.* *See also* [2CH 28:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:15); [JER 46:11](https://ref.ly/Jer46:11), [JER 51:8](https://ref.ly/Jer51:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 41:31; Exodus 35:28; Exodus 25:6; Exodus 35:8; Exodus 30:23-25; Exodus 30:33; 2 Chronicles 16:14; Song of Songs 3:6; Luke 23:56; Isaiah 3:20; Matthew 26:7; Mark 14:3; Luke 7:37; Ruth 3:3; Esther 2:12; Song of Songs 1:12; Ezekiel 16:9; Psalms 104:15; 2 Samuel 12:20; 2 Samuel 14:2; Ecclesiastes 9:8; Daniel 10:3; Matthew 6:17; Exodus 30:32; Psalms 23:5; Psalms 133:2; Luke 7:46; Matthew 26:12; Mark 14:8; John 12:7; Genesis 50:2-3; Genesis 50:26; Mark 16:1; Luke 24:1; John 19:39-40; Matthew 26:8-9; Mark 14:4-5; Proverbs 21:17; Song of Songs 4:13-14; Amos 6:6; John 12:5; Isaiah 1:6; Psalms 55:21; Luke 10:34; Jeremiah 8:22; 2 Chronicles 28:15; Jeremiah 46:11; Jeremiah 51:8

## 4492: olive (id: 4492)

An evergreen tree native to the Near East. Also the fruit of this tree, for which it is cultivated. The fruit can be eaten or pressed to produce oil for food or other uses.

Olive oil
---------

### Olives pressed to produce oil

**[MIC 6:15](https://ref.ly/Mic6:15)** *See also* [EXO 29:40](https://ref.ly/Exod29:40); [LEV 24:2](https://ref.ly/Lev24:2); [NUM 28:5](https://ref.ly/Num28:5)

### Olive oil in food use

**[EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13)** *See also* [NUM 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Num18:11-Num18:13); [DEU 8:6–8:9](https://ref.ly/Deut8:6-Deut8:9); [1CH 27:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:28); [2CH 11:5–11:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:5-2Chr11:11); [JOB 29:4–29:6](https://ref.ly/Job29:4-Job29:6)

### Olive oil used in the tabernacle

**[LEV 24:2](https://ref.ly/Lev24:2)** *See also* [EXO 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/Exod25:1-Exod25:7), [EXO 30:22–30:33](https://ref.ly/Exod30:22-Exod30:33), [EXO 35:4–35:9](https://ref.ly/Exod35:4-Exod35:9), [EXO 35:28](https://ref.ly/Exod35:28)

### Offerings of olive oil

**[EXO 29:38–29:41](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:41)** *See also* [NUM 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Num18:12-Num18:13), [NUM 28:3–28:8](https://ref.ly/Num28:3-Num28:8); [EZR 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:21-Ezra7:23); [EZK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:19)

### Payment, tribute and trade in olive oil

**[1KI 5:10–5:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:10-1Kgs5:11)** *See also* [2CH 2:8–2:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:8-2Chr2:10), [2CH 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:15-2Chr2:16); [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1); [LUK 16:1–16:8](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1-Luke16:8); [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

Olive trees
-----------

### Olive trees cultivated for their fruit

**[HAG 2:19](https://ref.ly/Hag2:19)** *See also* [GEN 8:11](https://ref.ly/Gen8:11); [DEU 28:38–28:40](https://ref.ly/Deut28:38-Deut28:40); [2KI 18:31–18:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:31-2Kgs18:32); [ISA 41:19](https://ref.ly/Isa41:19); [AMO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Amos4:9)

### Wild olive trees

**[NEH 8:15](https://ref.ly/Neh8:15)** *See also* [ROM 11:24](https://ref.ly/Rom11:24)

### Olive trees in visions

**[REV 11:3–11:6](https://ref.ly/Rev11:3-Rev11:6)** *See also* [ZEC 4:1–4:14](https://ref.ly/Zech4:1-Zech4:14)

### Metaphorical references to olive trees

**[PSA 52:8](https://ref.ly/Ps52:8)** *See also* [JDG 9:8–9:9](https://ref.ly/Judg9:8-Judg9:9); [JOB 15:33](https://ref.ly/Job15:33); [PSA 128:3](https://ref.ly/Ps128:3); [ISA 17:4–17:6](https://ref.ly/Isa17:4-Isa17:6), [ISA 24:10–24:13](https://ref.ly/Isa24:10-Isa24:13); [JER 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Jer11:16-Jer11:17); [HOS 14:5–14:6](https://ref.ly/Hos14:5-Hos14:6); [ROM 11:1–11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:1-Rom11:36) *the grafting of olive trees*

Olive groves
------------

**[EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11)** *See also* [DEU 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:11); [JOS 24:13](https://ref.ly/Josh24:13); [JDG 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/Judg15:4-Judg15:5); [1SA 8:11–8:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11-1Sam8:14); [NEH 5:9–5:11](https://ref.ly/Neh5:9-Neh5:11), [NEH 9:25](https://ref.ly/Neh9:25); [JHN 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/John18:1-John18:3)

----------

## 4494: papyrus (id: 4494)

A large plant growing in marshland, sometimes to a height of six metres.

Papyrus was used for making boats
---------------------------------

[EXO 2:3](https://ref.ly/Exod2:3); ; [JOB 9:26](https://ref.ly/Job9:26); ; [ISA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Isa18:2)

Papyrus indicates an ample water supply
---------------------------------------

[JOB 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Job8:11-Job8:13); ; [ISA 35:7](https://ref.ly/Isa35:7) *Papyrus growing in what was once desert depicts the blessing coming on God's redeemed people.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 2:3; Job 9:26; Isaiah 18:2; Job 8:11-13; Isaiah 35:7
* **Associated Articles:** 4402: plants (ID: `4402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5515: scroll (ID: `5515@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4496: perfume (id: 4496)

Substances used to make perfumes
--------------------------------

### By blending ingredients

[EXO 30:23–30:24](https://ref.ly/Exod30:23-Exod30:24), [EXO 30:34](https://ref.ly/Exod30:34)

### Substances used as perfumes

**[SNG 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Song4:13-Song4:14)** *See also* [SNG 1:12–1:14](https://ref.ly/Song1:12-Song1:14), [SNG 3:6](https://ref.ly/Song3:6), [SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13); [MRK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3); [JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3)

### Perfume ingredients as traded commodities

[GEN 37:25](https://ref.ly/Gen37:25); ; [EZK 27:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:19); ; [REV 18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:13)

The attractive fragrance of perfume
-----------------------------------

### Its tempting allure

**[PRO 7:17](https://ref.ly/Prov7:17)** *See also* [ISA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Isa57:9); [EZK 23:40–23:41](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:40-Ezek23:41)

### Its pleasing fragrance

[PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9); ; [SNG 1:3](https://ref.ly/Song1:3), [SNG 4:10](https://ref.ly/Song4:10)

### Fragrance used metaphorically

**[2CO 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14-2Cor2:16)** *See also* [EPH 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eph5:2); [PHP 4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:18)

Examples of the use of perfume
------------------------------

### As a cosmetic

**[EST 2:12](https://ref.ly/Esth2:12)** *See also* [RUT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:3)

### To give fragrance to clothes

**[PSA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8)** *See also* [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11)

### In burying the dead

**[MAT 26:12](https://ref.ly/Matt26:12)** parallel [MRK 14:8](https://ref.ly/Mark14:8) *See also* [2CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:14); [MRK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Mark16:1) parallel [LUK 23:56–24:1](https://ref.ly/Luke23:56-Luke24:1); [JHN 12:7](https://ref.ly/John12:7), [JHN 19:39–19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:39-John19:40)

Perfumers
---------

### Commissioned to make oil and incense for the sanctuary

**[EXO 37:29](https://ref.ly/Exod37:29)** *See also* [EXO 30:25](https://ref.ly/Exod30:25), [EXO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Exod30:33) *The manufacture of the sacred anointing oil by unauthorised persons was forbidden*; [EXO 30:35](https://ref.ly/Exod30:35); [1CH 9:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:30)

### Other references to perfumers

## 4498: ploughing (id: 4498)

Cutting up the furrows in soil and turning it up before work can be done on the land.

Ploughing as a way of life
--------------------------

**[1KI 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19-1Kgs19:21)**; **[LUK 17:7](https://ref.ly/Luke17:7)** *See also* [1SA 8:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:12), [1SA 13:19–13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:19-1Sam13:21); [JOB 1:14](https://ref.ly/Job1:14); [PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4); [ISA 28:24](https://ref.ly/Isa28:24); [EZK 36:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:9); [AMO 9:13](https://ref.ly/Amos9:13)

Rules concerning ploughing
--------------------------

[EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11), [EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21); ; [DEU 22:10](https://ref.ly/Deut22:10)

Ploughing used figuratively
---------------------------

**[JOB 4:8](https://ref.ly/Job4:8)**; **[LUK 9:62](https://ref.ly/Luke9:62)** *See also* [JDG 14:18](https://ref.ly/Judg14:18); [PSA 129:3](https://ref.ly/Ps129:3), [PSA 141:7](https://ref.ly/Ps141:7); [ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4); [JER 4:3](https://ref.ly/Jer4:3); [HOS 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Hos10:11-Hos10:12); [AMO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Amos6:12); [1CO 9:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:10)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 19:19-21; Luke 17:7; 1 Samuel 8:12; 1 Samuel 13:19-21; Job 1:14; Proverbs 20:4; Isaiah 28:24; Ezekiel 36:9; Amos 9:13; Exodus 23:10-11; Exodus 34:21; Deuteronomy 22:10; Job 4:8; Luke 9:62; Judges 14:18; Psalms 129:3; Psalms 141:7; Isaiah 2:4; Jeremiah 4:3; Hosea 10:11-12; Amos 6:12; 1 Corinthians 9:10

## 4500: poison (id: 4500)

A substance obtained from plants or animals that can cause illness, pain, disability or death. It is often used symbolically to refer to distress or bitterness.

Kinds of poison
---------------

### Poison from plants, water or food

[EXO 15:23](https://ref.ly/Exod15:23); ; [DEU 32:32](https://ref.ly/Deut32:32); ; [2KI 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:19-2Kgs2:22), [2KI 4:39–4:40](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:39-2Kgs4:40); ; [HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4)

### Poison from animals or reptiles

[NUM 21:6](https://ref.ly/Num21:6); ; [DEU 32:24](https://ref.ly/Deut32:24), [DEU 32:33](https://ref.ly/Deut32:33)

Poison used symbolically
------------------------

### To refer to distress

In these incidences God himself is portrayed as the one who brings such bitter experiences: [JOB 6:4](https://ref.ly/Job6:4); ; [JER 8:14](https://ref.ly/Jer8:14), [JER 9:15](https://ref.ly/Jer9:15), [JER 23:15](https://ref.ly/Jer23:15)

### To refer to evil and its effects

**[JOB 20:12–20:16](https://ref.ly/Job20:12-Job20:16)** *See also* [DEU 29:18](https://ref.ly/Deut29:18), [DEU 32:32–32:33](https://ref.ly/Deut32:32-Deut32:33); [PSA 58:4](https://ref.ly/Ps58:4); [PRO 23:32](https://ref.ly/Prov23:32); [ISA 14:29](https://ref.ly/Isa14:29); [JER 8:17](https://ref.ly/Jer8:17); [AMO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Amos6:12); [ACT 14:2](https://ref.ly/Acts14:2); [ROM 3:13](https://ref.ly/Rom3:13); [PSA 140:1–140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:1-Ps140:3); [JAS 3:8](https://ref.ly/Jas3:8); [REV 9:5](https://ref.ly/Rev9:5)

## 4504: roots (id: 4504)

The source and foundation of a tree or plant, anchoring it to the soil, usually found beneath the surface of the ground. Scripture contains literal, metaphorical and figurative references to roots.

Roots of trees and plants
-------------------------

**[JOB 30:4](https://ref.ly/Job30:4)** *See also* [JOB 14:7–14:9](https://ref.ly/Job14:7-Job14:9); [MAT 13:3–13:6](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:6) parallel [MRK 4:2–4:6](https://ref.ly/Mark4:2-Mark4:6); [MRK 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/Mark11:20-Mark11:21)

Jesus Christ as the \`\`Root of David''
---------------------------------------

**[REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Rev22:16)** *See also* [ISA 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1-Isa11:5), [ISA 11:10–11:12](https://ref.ly/Isa11:10-Isa11:12), [ISA 53:1–53:3](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1-Isa53:3); [ROM 15:8–15:12](https://ref.ly/Rom15:8-Rom15:12); [REV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5)

The metaphorical use of roots
-----------------------------

### Individuals and nations depicted as trees or plants with roots

**[JOB 29:18–29:20](https://ref.ly/Job29:18-Job29:20)** *See also* [JDG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Judg5:14); [JOB 8:11–8:19](https://ref.ly/Job8:11-Job8:19), [JOB 18:5–18:17](https://ref.ly/Job18:5-Job18:17); [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3), [PSA 80:14–80:15](https://ref.ly/Ps80:14-Ps80:15); [ISA 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Isa5:22-Isa5:24), [ISA 14:30](https://ref.ly/Isa14:30), [ISA 37:30–37:32](https://ref.ly/Isa37:30-Isa37:32) *the LORD's sign to Hezekiah*; [JER 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7-Jer17:8); [EZK 17:1–17:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:1-Ezek17:10), [EZK 31:3–31:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:3-Ezek31:9)DAN [4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:13-Ezek4:16), [DAN 4:23–4:26](https://ref.ly/Dan4:23-Dan4:26)[HOS 9:16](https://ref.ly/Hos9:16), [HOS 14:5](https://ref.ly/Hos14:5); [MAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mal4:1); [MAT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:10) parallel [LUK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:9); [MAT 13:18–13:21](https://ref.ly/Matt13:18-Matt13:21) parallels [MRK 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Mark4:13-Mark4:17), [LUK 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:13) *the parable of the sower*; [MAT 15:13](https://ref.ly/Matt15:13); [ROM 11:13–11:21](https://ref.ly/Rom11:13-Rom11:21)

### Sources and foundations described as roots

**[1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)** *See also* [JOB 19:28](https://ref.ly/Job19:28), [JOB 28:9](https://ref.ly/Job28:9); [PRO 12:12](https://ref.ly/Prov12:12); [ISA 14:29](https://ref.ly/Isa14:29); [AMO 2:9](https://ref.ly/Amos2:9); [JON 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:6)

### Bitter roots

**[HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Heb12:15)** *See also* [DEU 29:18](https://ref.ly/Deut29:18)

Roots as a symbol of stability and growth
-----------------------------------------

### Taking root

**[COL 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:6-Col2:7)** *See also* [2KI 19:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:30); [JOB 5:3](https://ref.ly/Job5:3); [PSA 80:8–80:9](https://ref.ly/Ps80:8-Ps80:9); [ISA 27:6](https://ref.ly/Isa27:6), [ISA 40:24](https://ref.ly/Isa40:24); [JER 12:2](https://ref.ly/Jer12:2); [EPH 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Eph3:14-Eph3:19)

### Being uprooted

## 4506: seed (id: 4506)

The means of propagating life from one generation to another. Figuratively, the term illustrates principles of spiritual life and growth, especially in later parts of the OT and throughout the NT.

How seed was used
-----------------

### Seed for eating

[GEN 1:29](https://ref.ly/Gen1:29), [GEN 42:1–42:2](https://ref.ly/Gen42:1-Gen42:2); ; [NEH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:2); ; [ISA 28:28](https://ref.ly/Isa28:28); ; [ECC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:6)

### Seed as an offering

[LEV 23:15–23:21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:21) parallel [NUM 28:26–28:31](https://ref.ly/Num28:26-Num28:31); ; [2CH 31:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:5)

### Seed to measure value

[LEV 27:16](https://ref.ly/Lev27:16)

How seed was appreciated
------------------------

### The presence of seed was a blessing

**[ZEC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:12)** *See also* [GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22); [PSA 67:6](https://ref.ly/Ps67:6), [PSA 85:12](https://ref.ly/Ps85:12)

### The absence of seed was a curse

**[JOL 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Joel1:16-Joel1:17)** *See also* [DEU 28:38–28:42](https://ref.ly/Deut28:38-Deut28:42)

Regulations in the law concerning seed
--------------------------------------

**[DEU 22:9](https://ref.ly/Deut22:9)** parallel [LEV 19:19](https://ref.ly/Lev19:19) *See also* [LEV 11:37–11:38](https://ref.ly/Lev11:37-Lev11:38), [LEV 27:30](https://ref.ly/Lev27:30)

Seed used figuratively to illustrate kingdom life
-------------------------------------------------

### To illustrate those ready to enter the kingdom of God

**[JHN 4:35–4:38](https://ref.ly/John4:35-John4:38)**

### To illustrate birth into eternal life

**[1PE 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:23)** *See also* [1JN 3:9](https://ref.ly/1John3:9)

### To illustrate the growth of the kingdom of God

**[MAT 13:31–13:32](https://ref.ly/Matt13:31-Matt13:32)** parallels [MRK 4:30–4:32](https://ref.ly/Mark4:30-Mark4:32), [LUK 13:18–13:19](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18-Luke13:19)**[MRK 4:26–4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:26-Mark4:29)** *See also* [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30), [MAT 13:36–13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:36-Matt13:43)

### To illustrate the growth of the church

**[1CO 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:6-1Cor3:7)**

### To illustrate God's word and people's response to it

*See also* [MAT 13:1–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:1-Matt13:23) parallels [MRK 4:1–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:1-Mark4:20), [LUK 8:4–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:4-Luke8:15) *the parable of the sower*

### To illustrate faith

**[MAT 17:20](https://ref.ly/Matt17:20)**

### To illustrate the principle that death comes before life

**[JHN 12:23–12:25](https://ref.ly/John12:23-John12:25)** *See also* [1CO 15:36](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:36)

### To illustrate the resurrection body

**[1CO 15:35–15:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:35-1Cor15:38)**

God calls his people to sow seeds of righteousness
--------------------------------------------------

**[JAS 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jas3:18)** *See also* [PSA 126:5–126:6](https://ref.ly/Ps126:5-Ps126:6); [PRO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Prov22:8); [JER 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Jer4:3-Jer4:4); [HOS 10:12](https://ref.ly/Hos10:12); [2CO 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6), [2CO 9:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:10); [GAL 6:7–6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:7-Gal6:9)

## 4508: sickle (id: 4508)

A cutting tool with a short handle used in harvesting grain. Early blades were made of wood lined with flint teeth; these were later replaced with curved iron blades. The cutting down of grain at harvest with a sickle is used as a symbol of God's judgment.

Sickles used to harvest grain
-----------------------------

**[MRK 4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:29)** *See also* [DEU 16:9](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9), [DEU 23:25](https://ref.ly/Deut23:25) *Sickles were not to be used in another person's field*; [1SA 13:19–13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:19-1Sam13:21); [JER 50:16](https://ref.ly/Jer50:16)

Cutting down grain as a symbol of judgment
------------------------------------------

**[JOL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Joel3:13)**; **[REV 14:14–14:19](https://ref.ly/Rev14:14-Rev14:19)** *See also* [ISA 17:5](https://ref.ly/Isa17:5); [JER 51:33](https://ref.ly/Jer51:33); [MIC 4:12](https://ref.ly/Mic4:12); [MAT 13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:30)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 4:29; Deuteronomy 16:9; Deuteronomy 23:25; 1 Samuel 13:19-21; Jeremiah 50:16; Joel 3:13; Revelation 14:14-19; Isaiah 17:5; Jeremiah 51:33; Micah 4:12; Matthew 13:30
* **Associated Articles:** 4406: agriculture (ID: `4406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4428: corn (ID: `4428@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4510: sowing and reaping (ID: `4510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5583: tools (ID: `5583@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4510: sowing and reaping (id: 4510)

OT references are mainly agricultural with regulatory laws covering crop production and festivals of thanksgiving. God's blessing was reflected in the harvest. The NT refers more to the spiritual sowing and reaping that takes place in the kingdom of God.

Regulations concerning the sowing and reaping of crops
------------------------------------------------------

### Laws of purity related to planting

[LEV 19:23–19:25](https://ref.ly/Lev19:23-Lev19:25); ; [DEU 22:9](https://ref.ly/Deut22:9)

### Reapers are to leave gleanings for the poor

[LEV 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:9-Lev19:10), [LEV 23:22](https://ref.ly/Lev23:22); ; [DEU 24:19](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19); ; [RUT 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:2-Ruth2:3), [RUT 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:15-Ruth2:16)

### Harvest celebrations

**[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)** *See also* [LEV 23:9–23:11](https://ref.ly/Lev23:9-Lev23:11), [LEV 23:39–23:41](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39-Lev23:41); [DEU 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:10), [DEU 16:13–16:15](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13-Deut16:15)

### The Sabbath\-rest for the land and its cultivators

[EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11), [EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21); ; [LEV 25:3–25:5](https://ref.ly/Lev25:3-Lev25:5), [LEV 25:11](https://ref.ly/Lev25:11)

Reaping a good harvest is a blessing from God
---------------------------------------------

### It is a consequence of obedience

[GEN 26:12](https://ref.ly/Gen26:12); ; [LEV 25:18–25:22](https://ref.ly/Lev25:18-Lev25:22), [LEV 26:3–26:5](https://ref.ly/Lev26:3-Lev26:5), [LEV 26:10](https://ref.ly/Lev26:10)

### It is a promise to the remnant of God's people

**[AMO 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Amos9:13-Amos9:14)** *See also* [ISA 30:23](https://ref.ly/Isa30:23), [ISA 62:8–62:9](https://ref.ly/Isa62:8-Isa62:9), [ISA 65:21–65:22](https://ref.ly/Isa65:21-Isa65:22); [JER 31:5](https://ref.ly/Jer31:5); [ZEC 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:11-Zech8:12)

God's judgment on Israel is reflected in reaping a poor harvest
---------------------------------------------------------------

### A poor yield of crops

[LEV 26:18–26:20](https://ref.ly/Lev26:18-Lev26:20); ; [DEU 28:15](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15), [DEU 28:38–28:40](https://ref.ly/Deut28:38-Deut28:40); ; [ISA 5:10](https://ref.ly/Isa5:10), [ISA 17:10–17:11](https://ref.ly/Isa17:10-Isa17:11); ; [JER 8:13](https://ref.ly/Jer8:13), [JER 12:13](https://ref.ly/Jer12:13); ; [HAG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hag1:6), [HAG 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hag1:10-Hag1:11)

### The harvesting of one's crops by others

**[LEV 26:15–26:16](https://ref.ly/Lev26:15-Lev26:16)** *See also* [NEH 9:36–9:37](https://ref.ly/Neh9:36-Neh9:37); [JOB 31:7–31:8](https://ref.ly/Job31:7-Job31:8); [JER 5:17](https://ref.ly/Jer5:17); [MIC 6:15](https://ref.ly/Mic6:15)

Proverbs and poems of sowing and harvest
----------------------------------------

[PRO 10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:5), [PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4); ; [ISA 28:23–28:29](https://ref.ly/Isa28:23-Isa28:29)

Moral and spiritual sowing and reaping
--------------------------------------

### Good and evil

**[JOB 4:8](https://ref.ly/Job4:8)** *See also* [PRO 11:18](https://ref.ly/Prov11:18), [PRO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Prov22:8); [HOS 8:7](https://ref.ly/Hos8:7), [HOS 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Hos10:12-Hos10:13); [GAL 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Gal6:7-Gal6:8); [JAS 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jas3:18)

### Sowing and reaping in the kingdom of God

**[MRK 4:3–4:8](https://ref.ly/Mark4:3-Mark4:8)** parallels [MAT 13:3–13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:8), [LUK 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:5-Luke8:8) *See also* [MAT 13:18–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:18-Matt13:23) parallels [MRK 4:14–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:14-Mark4:20), [LUK 8:11–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:15); [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30), [MAT 13:37–13:39](https://ref.ly/Matt13:37-Matt13:39); [MRK 4:26–4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:26-Mark4:29)

### The certainty of reaping

**[PSA 126:5–126:6](https://ref.ly/Ps126:5-Ps126:6)**; **[GAL 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9)**

### Reaping where others have sown

*See also* [JHN 4:35–4:38](https://ref.ly/John4:35-John4:38); [ROM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Rom1:13); [1CO 3:5–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:5-1Cor3:9)

### Reaping a material harvest from spiritual sowing

[1CO 9:9–9:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:9-1Cor9:11)

People and nations are reaped for judgment
------------------------------------------

**[JOL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Joel3:13)** *See also* [JER 51:33](https://ref.ly/Jer51:33); [HOS 6:11](https://ref.ly/Hos6:11); [REV 14:15–14:19](https://ref.ly/Rev14:15-Rev14:19)

Metaphorical use of sowing and reaping
--------------------------------------

## 4512: staff (id: 4512)

The staff was a basic item of personal property
-----------------------------------------------

### It was carried at most times

**[1SA 14:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:27)** *See also* [GEN 47:31](https://ref.ly/Gen47:31); [JDG 6:21](https://ref.ly/Judg6:21); [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4)

### It was carried by travellers

**[GEN 32:10](https://ref.ly/Gen32:10)** *See also* [EXO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:11); [MAT 10:5–10:10](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:10) parallels [MRK 6:7–6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7-Mark6:13), [LUK 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:6)

### It was carried by shepherds

**[MIC 7:14](https://ref.ly/Mic7:14)** *See also* [PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4); [ZEC 11:7](https://ref.ly/Zech11:7), [ZEC 11:10–11:14](https://ref.ly/Zech11:10-Zech11:14)

The staff was sometimes a symbol of authority
---------------------------------------------

**[GEN 49:10](https://ref.ly/Gen49:10)** *See also* [GEN 38:18](https://ref.ly/Gen38:18), [GEN 38:25](https://ref.ly/Gen38:25); [NUM 21:17–21:18](https://ref.ly/Num21:17-Num21:18); [JDG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Judg5:14); [2KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:21) parallel [ISA 36:6](https://ref.ly/Isa36:6); [MAT 27:28–27:30](https://ref.ly/Matt27:28-Matt27:30) parallel [MRK 15:19](https://ref.ly/Mark15:19)

God's miraculous power was manifest through Moses' and Aaron's staffs
---------------------------------------------------------------------

[EXO 4:2–4:5](https://ref.ly/Exod4:2-Exod4:5), [EXO 4:15–4:17](https://ref.ly/Exod4:15-Exod4:17), [EXO 7:8–7:12](https://ref.ly/Exod7:8-Exod7:12), [EXO 7:14–7:20](https://ref.ly/Exod7:14-Exod7:20) *the plague of blood*; ; [EXO 8:5–8:6](https://ref.ly/Exod8:5-Exod8:6) *the plague of frogs*; ; [EXO 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Exod8:16-Exod8:17) *the plague of gnats*; ; [EXO 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/Exod9:22-Exod9:24) *the plague of hail*; ; [EXO 10:12–10:14](https://ref.ly/Exod10:12-Exod10:14) *the plague of locusts*; ; [EXO 14:15–14:16](https://ref.ly/Exod14:15-Exod14:16) *the parting of the Red Sea*; ; [EXO 17:5–17:13](https://ref.ly/Exod17:5-Exod17:13); [NUM 17:1–17:10](https://ref.ly/Num17:1-Num17:10); ; [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4); [NUM 20:7–20:13](https://ref.ly/Num20:7-Num20:13)

## 4522: threshing (id: 4522)

The process of beating out or separating grain from harvested stalks.

Threshing and the harvest
-------------------------

**[LEV 26:5](https://ref.ly/Lev26:5)** *See also* [JDG 6:11](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11); [1CH 21:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:20); [1CO 9:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:9); [DEU 25:4](https://ref.ly/Deut25:4); [1TI 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:18) *Paul is adapting a humane OT law to the support of Christian workers.*

Threshing implements
--------------------

**[2SA 24:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:22)** parallel [1CH 21:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:23) *A threshing\-sledge was a board studded underneath with sharp stones which was dragged by oxen over the wheat ears in order to separate the kernels.* *See also* [JOB 41:30](https://ref.ly/Job41:30); [ISA 28:27–28:28](https://ref.ly/Isa28:27-Isa28:28), [ISA 41:15](https://ref.ly/Isa41:15)

Threshing used figuratively
---------------------------

**[ISA 27:12](https://ref.ly/Isa27:12)**; **[HOS 10:11](https://ref.ly/Hos10:11)** *See also* [2KI 13:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:7); [PRO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Prov20:26); [JER 50:11](https://ref.ly/Jer50:11); [MIC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:13)

* **Associated Articles:** 4426: chaff (ID: `4426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4510: sowing and reaping (ID: `4510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4524: threshing-floor (ID: `4524@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4550: winnowing (ID: `4550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5530: sifting (ID: `5530@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5621: wheel (ID: `5621@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

## 4528: trees (id: 4528)

Large, woody plants with trunk, branches, leaves and roots, noted for their long lives. Scripture refers to many different varieties of tree.

-----------------------

**[GEN 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Gen1:11-Gen1:12)**

Acacia
------

## 4530: unleavened bread (id: 4530)

Bread baked without yeast, often made because of insufficient time to prepare bread in the conventional manner. It was also eaten at the Passover Feast and was presented with sacrifices and offerings.

Unleavened bread prepared in haste
----------------------------------

**[EXO 12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:39)** *See also* [GEN 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:3); [EXO 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:8-Exod12:11); [JDG 6:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:17-Judg6:19); [1SA 28:21–28:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:21-1Sam28:25)

Unleavened bread eaten to commemorate the Passover
--------------------------------------------------

[EXO 12:1–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:1-Exod12:20), [EXO 13:3–13:7](https://ref.ly/Exod13:3-Exod13:7), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15), [EXO 34:18](https://ref.ly/Exod34:18); ; [LEV 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5-Lev23:6) parallel [NUM 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Num28:16-Num28:17); ; [NUM 9:9–9:11](https://ref.ly/Num9:9-Num9:11); ; [DEU 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:4), [DEU 16:8](https://ref.ly/Deut16:8); ; [EZK 45:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:21)

## 4534: vine (id: 4534)

A plant on which grapes grow, cultivated primarily for making wine. Jesus Christ described himself as the true vine, the source of life and growth for all believers.

The vine as an image of peace and security
------------------------------------------

**[1KI 4:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:25)** *See also* [2KI 18:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:31) parallel [ISA 36:16](https://ref.ly/Isa36:16); [MIC 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:4); [ZEC 3:10](https://ref.ly/Zech3:10)

----------

## 4538: vineyard (id: 4538)

A cultivated field or garden, sometimes surrounded by a wall, in which vines and other trees were grown. Israel is sometimes compared to a vineyard. Some of Jesus Christ's parables concern vineyards.

Vineyards were sometimes surrounded by walls
--------------------------------------------

**[MAT 21:33](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33)** parallel [MRK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1) *See also* [NUM 22:24](https://ref.ly/Num22:24)

Vineyards were associated with a settled, peaceful life
-------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 65:20–65:22](https://ref.ly/Isa65:20-Isa65:22)** *See also* [GEN 9:20](https://ref.ly/Gen9:20); [DEU 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:11); [2KI 18:31–18:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:31-2Kgs18:32) parallel [ISA 36:16–36:17](https://ref.ly/Isa36:16-Isa36:17); [2KI 19:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:29) parallel [ISA 37:30](https://ref.ly/Isa37:30); [2CH 26:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:10); [NEH 9:25](https://ref.ly/Neh9:25); [PSA 107:37–107:38](https://ref.ly/Ps107:37-Ps107:38); [SNG 7:12](https://ref.ly/Song7:12); [JER 31:3–31:5](https://ref.ly/Jer31:3-Jer31:5), [JER 32:15](https://ref.ly/Jer32:15), [JER 35:6–35:10](https://ref.ly/Jer35:6-Jer35:10); [EZK 28:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:26); [AMO 9:14](https://ref.ly/Amos9:14)

Vineyards were important for agriculture
----------------------------------------

**[2KI 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:11-2Kgs25:12)** parallels [JER 39:9–39:10](https://ref.ly/Jer39:9-Jer39:10), [JER 52:15–52:16](https://ref.ly/Jer52:15-Jer52:16) *See also* [NUM 20:17](https://ref.ly/Num20:17), [NUM 21:22](https://ref.ly/Num21:22); [DEU 20:6](https://ref.ly/Deut20:6); [JDG 14:5](https://ref.ly/Judg14:5), [JDG 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/Judg15:4-Judg15:5); [1SA 8:10–8:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:10-1Sam8:14); [1CH 27:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:27); [NEH 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:5), [NEH 5:11](https://ref.ly/Neh5:11); [ISA 61:5](https://ref.ly/Isa61:5)

---------------------------------------------

Naboth's vineyard
-----------------

**[1KI 21:1–21:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1-1Kgs21:19)**

---------------------

**[MAT 21:28–21:32](https://ref.ly/Matt21:28-Matt21:32)** *See also* [SNG 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Song8:11-Song8:12); [MAT 20:1–20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:16) *the parable of the workers in the vineyard*; [MAT 21:33–21:44](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:44) parallels [MRK 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:11), [LUK 20:9–20:18](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:18) *the parable of the tenants*; [LUK 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/Luke13:6-Luke13:9); [1CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7)

## 4540: weeds (id: 4540)

Uncultivated plants or shrubs, growing wild, often among cultivated crops or plants. Jesus Christ used the image of weeds in some of his parables to illustrate sin and worldliness.

The presence of weeds indicates desolation and neglect
------------------------------------------------------

**[ZEP 2:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:9)** *See also* [PRO 24:30–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:30-Prov24:34); [ISA 5:3–5:6](https://ref.ly/Isa5:3-Isa5:6), [ISA 7:18–7:25](https://ref.ly/Isa7:18-Isa7:25), [ISA 32:11–32:15](https://ref.ly/Isa32:11-Isa32:15), [ISA 34:8–34:14](https://ref.ly/Isa34:8-Isa34:14), [ISA 55:13](https://ref.ly/Isa55:13); [HOS 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Hos9:5-Hos9:6)

Weeds are worthless
-------------------

**[ISA 7:23](https://ref.ly/Isa7:23)** *See also* [MIC 7:4](https://ref.ly/Mic7:4); [LUK 6:44](https://ref.ly/Luke6:44)

Weeds were a serious problem for the farmer
-------------------------------------------

**[HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4)** *See also* [JOB 31:38–31:40](https://ref.ly/Job31:38-Job31:40); [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30)

Some varieties of weed were painful to the touch
------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 33:55](https://ref.ly/Num33:55)** *See also* [JOS 23:9–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:9-Josh23:13); [JDG 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Judg2:1-Judg2:3); [2SA 23:6–23:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:6-2Sam23:7); [EZK 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:3-Ezek2:6), [EZK 28:20–28:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:20-Ezek28:24)

Weeds were destroyed by burning
-------------------------------

**[ISA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Isa27:4)** *See also* [EXO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Exod22:6); [ISA 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/Isa9:18-Isa9:19), [ISA 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/Isa10:16-Isa10:17)

Weeds in Jesus Christ's parables
--------------------------------

The parable of the sower and its explanation: [MAT 13:3–13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:8) parallels [MRK 4:3–4:8](https://ref.ly/Mark4:3-Mark4:8), [LUK 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:5-Luke8:8); ; [MAT 13:18–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:18-Matt13:23) parallels [MRK 4:13–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:13-Mark4:20), [LUK 8:11–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:15); [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30), [MAT 13:36–13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:36-Matt13:43)

## 4542: wheat (id: 4542)

A cereal widely cultivated for use as food. One of the main agricultural crops in biblical times, it is referred to throughout the Scriptures and features several times in Jesus Christ's teaching.

The cultivation of wheat
------------------------

### Sowing wheat

**[ISA 28:24–28:25](https://ref.ly/Isa28:24-Isa28:25)** *See also* [JER 12:13](https://ref.ly/Jer12:13); [1CO 15:37–15:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:37-1Cor15:38)

### The growth of the plant

**[EXO 9:31–9:32](https://ref.ly/Exod9:31-Exod9:32)** *See also* [JHN 12:24](https://ref.ly/John12:24)

### The wheat harvest

**[1SA 6:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:13)** *See also* [GEN 30:14](https://ref.ly/Gen30:14); [JDG 15:1](https://ref.ly/Judg15:1); [RUT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:23); [1SA 12:16–12:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:16-1Sam12:18); [JOL 1:8–1:11](https://ref.ly/Joel1:8-Joel1:11)

### Threshing wheat

**[JDG 6:11](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11)** *See also* [1CH 21:18–21:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:18-1Chr21:28)

### The parable of the wheat and the weeds

[MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30), [MAT 13:36–13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:36-Matt13:43)

-------------

**[PSA 81:13–81:16](https://ref.ly/Ps81:13-Ps81:16)** *See also* [DEU 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:8), [DEU 32:13–32:14](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13-Deut32:14); [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29); [PSA 147:12–147:14](https://ref.ly/Ps147:12-Ps147:14); [JER 41:4–41:8](https://ref.ly/Jer41:4-Jer41:8); [EZK 4:9–4:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:9-Ezek4:13)

## 4544: wine (id: 4544)

Drink made from fermented grape juice, often drunk instead of water in biblical times. Scripture recognises the richness of wine and its value in bringing joy to people; however, it condemns the excessive drinking of wine, and recommends total abstinence in some situations.

Offerings of wine
-----------------

**[NUM 15:5](https://ref.ly/Num15:5)** *See also* [EXO 29:40](https://ref.ly/Exod29:40); [LEV 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Lev23:12-Lev23:13); [NUM 28:14](https://ref.ly/Num28:14); [DEU 32:37–32:38](https://ref.ly/Deut32:37-Deut32:38); [1SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:24); [EZR 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:8-Ezra6:10)

------------------------------

**[NEH 13:12](https://ref.ly/Neh13:12)** *See also* [NUM 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Num18:11-Num18:12); [DEU 12:17](https://ref.ly/Deut12:17), [DEU 14:23–14:26](https://ref.ly/Deut14:23-Deut14:26), [DEU 18:4–18:5](https://ref.ly/Deut18:4-Deut18:5); [2CH 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:4-2Chr31:5)

Lack of wine as a sign of God's displeasure
-------------------------------------------

**[JER 48:33](https://ref.ly/Jer48:33)** *See also* [DEU 28:38–28:51](https://ref.ly/Deut28:38-Deut28:51); [ISA 24:7–24:9](https://ref.ly/Isa24:7-Isa24:9); [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10); [HAG 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Hag2:14-Hag2:16)

-------------------------------

**[1TI 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:23)** *See also* [GEN 14:17–14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:17-Gen14:18); [JDG 19:19](https://ref.ly/Judg19:19); [LAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:12); [MAT 27:48](https://ref.ly/Matt27:48) parallels [MRK 15:36](https://ref.ly/Mark15:36), [LUK 23:36](https://ref.ly/Luke23:36), [JHN 19:28–19:29](https://ref.ly/John19:28-John19:29)

---------------

--------------------------------------

----------------------------

------------

------------------------

## 4546: winepress (id: 4546)

The vat in which grapes were trodden to produce juice for making wine. Scripture sometimes uses the image of treading grapes at the winepress to depict scenes of divine wrath and punishment.

Winepresses were widely used
----------------------------

**[DEU 15:12–15:14](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:14)** *See also* [NUM 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/Num18:25-Num18:27), [NUM 18:30](https://ref.ly/Num18:30); [JDG 6:11](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11); [2KI 6:24–6:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:24-2Kgs6:27)

Treading grapes at winepresses
------------------------------

### Literal references

**[NEH 13:15](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15)** *See also* [JOB 24:11](https://ref.ly/Job24:11); [ISA 16:10](https://ref.ly/Isa16:10); [JER 48:33](https://ref.ly/Jer48:33)

### Metaphorical references

**[LAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Lam1:15)** *See also* [ISA 63:1–63:6](https://ref.ly/Isa63:1-Isa63:6); [JOL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Joel3:13); [REV 14:17–14:20](https://ref.ly/Rev14:17-Rev14:20), [REV 19:11–19:16](https://ref.ly/Rev19:11-Rev19:16)

## 4548: wineskin (id: 4548)

A container or sack, made from animal skin to hold wine. Jesus Christ used the image of old and new wineskins to explain the differences between himself and John the Baptist.

Wineskins used to store or carry wine
-------------------------------------

**[1SA 10:2–10:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:2-1Sam10:4)** *See also* [1SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:24); [PSA 119:83](https://ref.ly/Ps119:83); [JER 13:12–13:14](https://ref.ly/Jer13:12-Jer13:14); [HAB 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Hab2:15-Hab2:16)

Old and new wineskins
---------------------

**[JOB 32:17–32:20](https://ref.ly/Job32:17-Job32:20)** *New wine was stored in new wineskins, which expanded with the wine as it fermented.* *See also* [JOS 9:3–9:13](https://ref.ly/Josh9:3-Josh9:13)

Wineskins as gifts
------------------

**[2SA 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:1-2Sam16:2)** *See also* [1SA 16:14–16:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14-1Sam16:20), [1SA 25:14–25:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:14-1Sam25:20)

The parable of old and new wineskins
------------------------------------

**[MAT 9:14–9:17](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:17)** parallels [MRK 2:18–2:22](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:22), [LUK 5:33–5:39](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33-Luke5:39)

* **Associated Articles:** 4544: wine (ID: `4544@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4546: winepress (ID: `4546@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5234: bottle (ID: `5234@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5325: gifts (ID: `5325@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4550: winnowing (id: 4550)

The exposing of grain to the air by either tossing or fanning so that the loose outer part is blown away. It is also used figuratively to refer to God's judgment.

Winnowing took place at the threshing\-floor
--------------------------------------------

*See also* [RUT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:2)

Winnowing used figuratively
---------------------------

**[JER 15:7](https://ref.ly/Jer15:7)**; **[MAT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:12)** parallel [LUK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Luke3:17) *See also* [PRO 20:8](https://ref.ly/Prov20:8), [PRO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Prov20:26); [ISA 41:16](https://ref.ly/Isa41:16); [JER 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jer4:11), [JER 51:2](https://ref.ly/Jer51:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Ruth 3:2; Jeremiah 15:7; Matthew 3:12; Luke 3:17; Proverbs 20:8; Proverbs 20:26; Isaiah 41:16; Jeremiah 4:11; Jeremiah 51:2
* **Associated Articles:** 4426: chaff (ID: `4426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4522: threshing (ID: `4522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4524: threshing-floor (ID: `4524@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5530: sifting (ID: `5530@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4552: wood (id: 4552)

Trunks and branches of trees used as raw material for various objects and as fuel for burning. Acacia wood was used extensively in the tabernacle and olive wood in the temple.

---------------------

## 4554: yeast (id: 4554)

A raising agent commonly used in bread. Bread that was required quickly was baked without yeast, as at the first Passover, commemorated annually in the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Although some non\-meat offerings contained yeast, it could not be burned on the altar.

Unleavened bread was made without yeast
---------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:39)** *See also* [GEN 19:3](https://ref.ly/Gen19:3); [EXO 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:8-Exod12:11), [EXO 12:34](https://ref.ly/Exod12:34); [JDG 6:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:17-Judg6:19); [1SA 28:24–28:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:24-1Sam28:25)

The consumption of yeast was prohibited during the Feast of Unleavened Bread
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:14–12:15](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14-Exod12:15)** *See also* [EXO 12:17–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17-Exod12:20), [EXO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Exod13:3), [EXO 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Exod13:6-Exod13:7), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15), [EXO 23:18](https://ref.ly/Exod23:18), [EXO 34:18](https://ref.ly/Exod34:18); [LEV 23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:6); [NUM 28:17](https://ref.ly/Num28:17); [DEU 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:4); [EZK 45:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:21)

Sacrifices burned on the altar were not to contain yeast
--------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lev2:11)** *See also* [EXO 29:1–29:3](https://ref.ly/Exod29:1-Exod29:3), [EXO 29:22–29:24](https://ref.ly/Exod29:22-Exod29:24), [EXO 34:25](https://ref.ly/Exod34:25); [LEV 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Lev2:4-Lev2:5), [LEV 6:16–6:17](https://ref.ly/Lev6:16-Lev6:17), [LEV 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Lev7:12-Lev7:13), [LEV 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Lev8:2-Lev8:3), [LEV 8:26](https://ref.ly/Lev8:26), [LEV 10:12](https://ref.ly/Lev10:12); [NUM 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Num6:14-Num6:15), [NUM 6:19](https://ref.ly/Num6:19)

--------------------------------------------------
