## 4603: animals (id: 4603)

A category of living creatures that dwell mainly on the land, distinguished from fishes, birds and insects. Scripture stresses that all animals are created by God and are subordinate to human beings.

* **Associated Articles:** 4604: animals, nature of (ID: `4604@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4605: animals, religious role of (ID: `4605@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4604: animals, nature of (id: 4604)

As a class within the created order, their variety reflects God's generous giving. He makes human beings their stewards, though they themselves come into the category of creatures.

Animals in their profusion
--------------------------

**[GEN 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Gen1:24-Gen1:25)** *See also* [GEN 7:1–7:3](https://ref.ly/Gen7:1-Gen7:3), [GEN 7:13–7:16](https://ref.ly/Gen7:13-Gen7:16), [GEN 8:15–8:19](https://ref.ly/Gen8:15-Gen8:19); [PSA 50:9–50:11](https://ref.ly/Ps50:9-Ps50:11), [PSA 148:7–148:10](https://ref.ly/Ps148:7-Ps148:10); [ISA 43:20](https://ref.ly/Isa43:20); [EZK 36:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:11)

Animals as illustrated by their variety
---------------------------------------

**[ISA 11:6–11:8](https://ref.ly/Isa11:6-Isa11:8)** *See also* [EXO 23:12](https://ref.ly/Exod23:12) *Ox, donkey*; [EXO 25:3–25:5](https://ref.ly/Exod25:3-Exod25:5) *Goat, sea cow*; [LEV 11:4–11:7](https://ref.ly/Lev11:4-Lev11:7) *Rabbit, pig*; [LEV 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Lev11:29-Lev11:30) *Weasel, rat*; [NUM 19:2](https://ref.ly/Num19:2) *Heifer*; [DEU 14:4–14:5](https://ref.ly/Deut14:4-Deut14:5) *Deer, gazelle, antelope*; [1SA 17:34–17:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:34-1Sam17:35) *Sheep, bear, lion*; [JOB 40:15–40:24](https://ref.ly/Job40:15-Job40:24) *possibly the hippopotamus*; [JOB 41:1–41:34](https://ref.ly/Job41:1-Job41:34) *possibly the crocodile*; [PSA 58:4](https://ref.ly/Ps58:4) *Snake*; [PSA 105:30](https://ref.ly/Ps105:30) *Frog*; [SNG 2:15](https://ref.ly/Song2:15) *Fox*; [ISA 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/Isa13:21-Isa13:22) *Jackal, wild goat, hyena*; [ISA 51:8](https://ref.ly/Isa51:8) *Worm*

## 4605: animals, religious role of (id: 4605)

Central to the OT sacrificial system, they were governed by strict laws within the Mosaic code.

Animals as defined by the Law of Moses
--------------------------------------

### Animals for food

**[LEV 20:25–20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:25-Lev20:26)** *See also* [EXO 22:31](https://ref.ly/Exod22:31); [LEV 22:8](https://ref.ly/Lev22:8); [DEU 12:15–12:16](https://ref.ly/Deut12:15-Deut12:16), [DEU 12:21](https://ref.ly/Deut12:21), [DEU 14:3–14:8](https://ref.ly/Deut14:3-Deut14:8); [EZK 4:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:14), [EZK 44:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:31); [ACT 10:9–10:14](https://ref.ly/Acts10:9-Acts10:14), [ACT 11:6](https://ref.ly/Acts11:6), [ACT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:20)

### Relationships with animals forbidden

### Animal carcasses

-----------------------------------------------------

## 4609: beast, the (id: 4609)

In apocalyptic literature, symbol of the ultimate adversary of God in the great final spiritual battle.

Beasts represent rulers and nations opposed to God
--------------------------------------------------

[DAN 7:3–7:7](https://ref.ly/Dan7:3-Dan7:7), [DAN 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Dan7:11-Dan7:12), [DAN 7:17](https://ref.ly/Dan7:17), [DAN 7:19–7:23](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19-Dan7:23)

The beast represents the antichrist in the final spiritual battle
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### The emergence of the beast

[REV 11:7](https://ref.ly/Rev11:7), [REV 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rev13:1), [REV 13:11](https://ref.ly/Rev13:11), [REV 17:3](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3)

### The power and authority of the beast

[REV 13:2–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rev13:2-Rev13:7), [REV 13:12–13:15](https://ref.ly/Rev13:12-Rev13:15)

### Those who will worship the beast, and those who will not

[REV 13:8](https://ref.ly/Rev13:8), [REV 13:16–13:18](https://ref.ly/Rev13:16-Rev13:18), [REV 14:9–14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:9-Rev14:11), [REV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Rev16:2), [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Rev20:4)

### The defeat of the beast and the fulfilment of God's purposes

[REV 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10-Rev16:11), [REV 17:7–17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:7-Rev17:14), [REV 17:16–17:17](https://ref.ly/Rev17:16-Rev17:17), [REV 19:19–19:20](https://ref.ly/Rev19:19-Rev19:20)

## 4612: birds (id: 4612)

Part of the created world, used for food. In OT times they were also used in sacrifices. In Scripture, birds are used in many metaphors.

Part of creation
----------------

**[GEN 1:21](https://ref.ly/Gen1:21)** *See also* [GEN 1:20–1:30](https://ref.ly/Gen1:20-Gen1:30), [GEN 2:20](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20); [1CO 15:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:39); [JAS 3:7](https://ref.ly/Jas3:7)

### Cared for by God

**[LUK 12:24](https://ref.ly/Luke12:24)** parallel [MAT 6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26) *See also* [JOB 38:41](https://ref.ly/Job38:41); [PSA 50:11](https://ref.ly/Ps50:11), [PSA 147:9](https://ref.ly/Ps147:9)

[DEU 22:6–22:7](https://ref.ly/Deut22:6-Deut22:7)

## 4615: bull (id: 4615)

Bulls were particularly prized as sacrifices on account of their considerable value. The sacrifice of a bull was especially associated with the sin offering.

Bulls offered as sacrifices
---------------------------

**[EXO 29:36](https://ref.ly/Exod29:36)**; **[2SA 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:12-2Sam6:13)**; **[ACT 14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:13)** *Also used in pagan sacrifices.* *See also* [NUM 23:1–23:4](https://ref.ly/Num23:1-Num23:4); [JOB 42:7–42:8](https://ref.ly/Job42:7-Job42:8); [PSA 66:15](https://ref.ly/Ps66:15); [HEB 9:13](https://ref.ly/Heb9:13)

### Instructions for the offering of a bull

**[LEV 4:13–4:21](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13-Lev4:21)** *See also* [LEV 1:3–1:9](https://ref.ly/Lev1:3-Lev1:9)

### Bulls sacrificed on significant occasions

**[NUM 28:16–28:20](https://ref.ly/Num28:16-Num28:20)** *The Passover.* *See also* [EXO 24:4–24:5](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4-Exod24:5) *the covenant confirmed*; [EXO 29:10–29:14](https://ref.ly/Exod29:10-Exod29:14); [LEV 9:2](https://ref.ly/Lev9:2), [LEV 16:11–16:14](https://ref.ly/Lev16:11-Lev16:14) *the Day of Atonement*; [NUM 8:5–8:12](https://ref.ly/Num8:5-Num8:12), [NUM 29:12–29:13](https://ref.ly/Num29:12-Num29:13); [1SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:24); [1CH 15:25–15:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:25-1Chr15:26), [1CH 29:21–29:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:21-1Chr29:22); [2CH 29:20–29:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:20-2Chr29:24); [EZR 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:9-Ezra6:10), [EZR 8:35](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:35); [EZK 43:19–43:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:19-Ezek43:27), [EZK 45:18–45:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:18-Ezek45:24)

## 4618: calf (id: 4618)

A young animal prized as the source of tender meat, suitable for celebrations. However, it also had associations with both the OT sacrificial system and idolatry.

-----------------------------------------

**[EXO 32:2–32:8](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:8)**; **[1KI 12:28–12:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28-1Kgs12:29)** *See also* [EXO 32:19–32:24](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19-Exod32:24); [DEU 9:16–9:21](https://ref.ly/Deut9:16-Deut9:21); [2KI 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:28-2Kgs10:29), [2KI 17:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:16); [2CH 11:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:15); [NEH 9:16–9:18](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16-Neh9:18); [PSA 106:19–106:20](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19-Ps106:20); [HOS 8:5–8:6](https://ref.ly/Hos8:5-Hos8:6), [HOS 10:5](https://ref.ly/Hos10:5), [HOS 13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:2); [ACT 7:41](https://ref.ly/Acts7:41)

## 4624: cow (id: 4624)

------------------------------

## 4627: creatures (id: 4627)

Generally, living beings within God's creation, especially the exotic animals seen in visions, which often have a deep symbolic significance.

Creatures representing angelic beings who worship God
-----------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:5-Ezek1:6)**; **[REV 4:6–4:8](https://ref.ly/Rev4:6-Rev4:8)** *The strange creatures of Ezekiel and Revelation seem to stand for the fulness and variety of God's creation.* *See also* [EZK 1:7–1:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:7-Ezek1:25), [EZK 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:12-Ezek3:13), [EZK 10:15–10:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:15-Ezek10:22) *The creatures are here identified as cherubim*; [REV 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:9-Rev4:11), [REV 5:6–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:6-Rev5:14), [REV 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Rev6:1-Rev6:3), [REV 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Rev6:5-Rev6:6), [REV 7:11](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11), [REV 14:3](https://ref.ly/Rev14:3), [REV 15:7](https://ref.ly/Rev15:7), [REV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Rev19:4)

Creatures representing kings or kingdoms
----------------------------------------

**[DAN 7:17](https://ref.ly/Dan7:17), [DAN 8:20–8:21](https://ref.ly/Dan8:20-Dan8:21)** *The “beasts” here are unnatural, and represent the hostility of human empires to the rule of God. The horns represent individual kings within an imperial dynasty.*

Creatures representing Satan
----------------------------

The beast here represents Satan, with the horns representing royal authority at a time of Roman persecution of the church: [REV 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Rev12:3-Rev12:4), [REV 12:7–12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:7-Rev12:9), [REV 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rev13:1); [DAN 7:2–7:8](https://ref.ly/Dan7:2-Dan7:8), [DAN 7:19–7:26](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19-Dan7:26), [DAN 8:3–8:12](https://ref.ly/Dan8:3-Dan8:12); ; [REV 11:7](https://ref.ly/Rev11:7), [REV 13:2–13:8](https://ref.ly/Rev13:2-Rev13:8), [REV 13:11–13:18](https://ref.ly/Rev13:11-Rev13:18), [REV 17:3](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3), [REV 17:7–17:13](https://ref.ly/Rev17:7-Rev17:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 1:5-6; Revelation 4:6-8; Ezekiel 1:7-25; Ezekiel 3:12-13; Ezekiel 10:15-22; Revelation 4:9-11; Revelation 5:6-14; Revelation 6:1-3; Revelation 6:5-6; Revelation 7:11; Revelation 14:3; Revelation 15:7; Revelation 19:4; Daniel 7:17; Daniel 8:20-21; Revelation 12:3-4; Revelation 12:7-9; Revelation 13:1; Daniel 7:2-8; Daniel 7:19-26; Daniel 8:3-12; Revelation 11:7; Revelation 13:2-8; Revelation 13:11-18; Revelation 17:3; Revelation 17:7-13
* **Associated Articles:** 1469: visions (ID: `1469@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4110: angels (ID: `4110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4150: cherubim (ID: `4150@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4603: animals (ID: `4603@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4609: beast, the (ID: `4609@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9105: last things (ID: `9105@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

## 4636: dove (id: 4636)

A common species of bird. In Scripture the dove symbolises the Spirit of God, and also certain kinds of human behaviour.

--------------

## 4642: fish (id: 4642)

A basic ingredient of diet in biblical times. Fishing was regarded as a trade rather than a recreation.

Fish as part of God's creation
------------------------------

**[GEN 1:20–1:22](https://ref.ly/Gen1:20-Gen1:22)** *See also* [GEN 1:26](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26), [GEN 9:2](https://ref.ly/Gen9:2); [1KI 4:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:33); [PSA 8:8](https://ref.ly/Ps8:8); [1CO 15:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:39)

Fish as food
------------

**[MAT 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:10)** parallel [LUK 11:11](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11)**[MAT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Matt14:17)** parallels [MRK 6:38](https://ref.ly/Mark6:38), [LUK 9:16](https://ref.ly/Luke9:16), [JHN 6:9](https://ref.ly/John6:9) *See also* [NUM 11:5](https://ref.ly/Num11:5); [NEH 13:16](https://ref.ly/Neh13:16); [MAT 15:34](https://ref.ly/Matt15:34) parallel [MRK 8:7](https://ref.ly/Mark8:7); [LUK 24:42](https://ref.ly/Luke24:42); [JHN 21:13](https://ref.ly/John21:13)

Rules about eating fish
-----------------------

[LEV 11:9–11:12](https://ref.ly/Lev11:9-Lev11:12); ; [DEU 14:9–14:10](https://ref.ly/Deut14:9-Deut14:10)

Fish related to judgment
------------------------

**[EXO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Exod7:18)**; **[ISA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:8)** *See also* [PSA 105:29](https://ref.ly/Ps105:29); [ISA 50:2](https://ref.ly/Isa50:2); [HOS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:3); [ZEP 1:3](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:3)

-----------------------------------

**[DEU 4:15–4:18](https://ref.ly/Deut4:15-Deut4:18)** *See also* [JDG 16:23](https://ref.ly/Judg16:23) *Dagon was the fish god.*

Fish involved in miracles
-------------------------

**[JON 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:17)**; **[LUK 5:4–5:7](https://ref.ly/Luke5:4-Luke5:7)** *See also* [MAT 14:19–14:21](https://ref.ly/Matt14:19-Matt14:21) parallels [MRK 6:39–6:44](https://ref.ly/Mark6:39-Mark6:44), [LUK 9:14–9:17](https://ref.ly/Luke9:14-Luke9:17), [JHN 6:10–6:13](https://ref.ly/John6:10-John6:13); [MAT 15:35–15:38](https://ref.ly/Matt15:35-Matt15:38) parallel [MRK 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Mark8:7-Mark8:9); [MAT 17:24–17:27](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:27); [JHN 21:5–21:6](https://ref.ly/John21:5-John21:6)

-----------------------

**[MAT 13:47–13:48](https://ref.ly/Matt13:47-Matt13:48)** *See also* [EZK 47:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:10)

## 4648: goad (id: 4648)

A spike used to drive animals on. The term is also figuratively applied to prompting and guiding people into action.

-------------------------------

### Used with animals

[1SA 13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:21)

### Used as a weapon

[JDG 3:31](https://ref.ly/Judg3:31)

Goading as a symbol of discipline
---------------------------------

### God's word as a goad

**[ECC 12:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:11)** *See also* [ACT 2:37](https://ref.ly/Acts2:37)

### Conscience as a goad

**[ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/Acts26:14)** *See also* [EXO 25:2](https://ref.ly/Exod25:2); [JOB 20:2](https://ref.ly/Job20:2); [ROM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Rom2:15)

### Encouragement as a goad

**[HEB 10:24](https://ref.ly/Heb10:24)** *See also* [1TH 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:3) *The promptings of love and faith may be seen as symbolic goads.*

People goaded into doing wrong
------------------------------

## 4651: goat (id: 4651)

A common animal used both for food and within the sacrificial system.

The economic importance of goats
--------------------------------

### For food

**[GEN 27:9](https://ref.ly/Gen27:9)**; **[JDG 13:15](https://ref.ly/Judg13:15)** *See also* [DEU 14:4](https://ref.ly/Deut14:4), [DEU 14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:21); [JDG 6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:19); [PRO 27:27](https://ref.ly/Prov27:27); [LUK 15:29](https://ref.ly/Luke15:29)

### For fabric

**[EXO 35:26](https://ref.ly/Exod35:26)** *The tabernacle curtains were spun from goat hair, the ordinary material for making tents.* *See also* [EXO 26:7](https://ref.ly/Exod26:7), [EXO 36:14](https://ref.ly/Exod36:14); [REV 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12)

### As a measure of wealth and payment

**[GEN 30:32](https://ref.ly/Gen30:32)**; **[1SA 25:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:2)** *See also* [GEN 32:5](https://ref.ly/Gen32:5), [GEN 32:13–32:14](https://ref.ly/Gen32:13-Gen32:14), [GEN 47:17](https://ref.ly/Gen47:17); [PRO 27:26](https://ref.ly/Prov27:26); [EZK 27:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:21)

-----------------------

### For worship

### For atonement for sin

**[LEV 4:23](https://ref.ly/Lev4:23)**; **[EZR 8:35](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:35)** *See also* [LEV 3:12](https://ref.ly/Lev3:12), [LEV 4:28](https://ref.ly/Lev4:28), [LEV 5:6](https://ref.ly/Lev5:6), [LEV 9:3](https://ref.ly/Lev9:3); [NUM 7:16](https://ref.ly/Num7:16), [NUM 7:22](https://ref.ly/Num7:22), [NUM 7:28](https://ref.ly/Num7:28), [NUM 15:27](https://ref.ly/Num15:27)

### As a fellowship offering

[NUM 7:17](https://ref.ly/Num7:17), [NUM 7:23](https://ref.ly/Num7:23), [NUM 7:29](https://ref.ly/Num7:29)

### At the dedication of the temple

**[1KI 8:63](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:63)** parallel [2CH 7:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:5) *See also* [2CH 30:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:24); [EZR 6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:17)

### Flawless goats were required

**[EXO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Exod12:5)** *See also* [LEV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Lev1:10); [EZK 43:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:22)

--------------------------------------------------------

Goats are symbolic of sinful humanity
-------------------------------------

**[MAT 25:32](https://ref.ly/Matt25:32)**

## 4654: horn (id: 4654)

The hard, pointed growth on the heads of certain animals; a projection resembling such a growth. The horns of animals were regarded as symbols of strength. In apocalyptic writings, horns may denote various powers and enemies of God. Altars were made with horn\-like corner projections.

Horns of animals
----------------

*See also* [GEN 22:13](https://ref.ly/Gen22:13)LEV [11:16](https://ref.ly/Gen11:16); [DEU 14:15](https://ref.ly/Deut14:15)[PSA 22:21](https://ref.ly/Ps22:21), [PSA 69:31](https://ref.ly/Ps69:31)

Uses of animal horns
--------------------

### As musical instruments

[EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13); ; [JOS 6:4](https://ref.ly/Josh6:4); ; [1CH 15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:28); ; [2CH 15:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:14); ; [PSA 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3), [PSA 98:6](https://ref.ly/Ps98:6); ; [DAN 3:5](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5), [DAN 3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:7), [DAN 3:10](https://ref.ly/Dan3:10), [DAN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:15); ; [HOS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Hos5:8)

### As oil containers

[1SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1), [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13); ; [1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39)

Uses of imitation horns
-----------------------

### As projections at corners of altars

[EXO 27:2](https://ref.ly/Exod27:2), [EXO 29:12](https://ref.ly/Exod29:12), [EXO 30:2–30:3](https://ref.ly/Exod30:2-Exod30:3), [EXO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:10), [EXO 37:25–37:26](https://ref.ly/Exod37:25-Exod37:26), [EXO 38:2](https://ref.ly/Exod38:2); ; [LEV 4:7](https://ref.ly/Lev4:7), [LEV 4:18](https://ref.ly/Lev4:18), [LEV 4:25](https://ref.ly/Lev4:25), [LEV 4:30](https://ref.ly/Lev4:30), [LEV 4:34](https://ref.ly/Lev4:34), [LEV 8:15](https://ref.ly/Lev8:15), [LEV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Lev9:9), [LEV 16:18](https://ref.ly/Lev16:18); ; [1KI 1:50–1:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:50-1Kgs1:51), [1KI 2:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:28); ; [PSA 118:27](https://ref.ly/Ps118:27); ; [JER 17:1](https://ref.ly/Jer17:1); ; [EZK 43:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:15), [EZK 43:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:20); ; [AMO 3:14](https://ref.ly/Amos3:14); ; [REV 9:13](https://ref.ly/Rev9:13)

### As prophetic signs

**[1KI 22:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:11)** parallel [2CH 18:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:10)

Horns as symbols of strength
----------------------------

### Describing God's saving power

**[2SA 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2-2Sam22:3)** parallel [PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2) *See also* [LUK 1:69](https://ref.ly/Luke1:69)

### Describing human strength

**[DEU 33:17](https://ref.ly/Deut33:17)** *See also* [1SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1), [1SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:10); [PSA 75:10](https://ref.ly/Ps75:10), [PSA 89:17](https://ref.ly/Ps89:17), [PSA 89:24](https://ref.ly/Ps89:24), [PSA 92:10](https://ref.ly/Ps92:10), [PSA 112:9](https://ref.ly/Ps112:9), [PSA 132:17](https://ref.ly/Ps132:17), [PSA 148:14](https://ref.ly/Ps148:14); [JER 48:25](https://ref.ly/Jer48:25); [LAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Lam2:3), [LAM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Lam2:17); [EZK 29:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:21), [EZK 34:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:21); [MIC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:13)

## 4657: horse (id: 4657)

The animal most commonly associated with war and battle, the horse also appears figuratively in poetic and prophetic literature. Its domesticated usage was mainly for personal travel and the haulage of heavy loads.

The military use of horses
--------------------------

**[EXO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1)**; **[JOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Josh11:4)**; **[2KI 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:14-2Kgs6:15)** [EXO 14:9](https://ref.ly/Exod14:9), [EXO 14:28](https://ref.ly/Exod14:28); ; [DEU 11:4](https://ref.ly/Deut11:4); ; [ISA 43:16–43:17](https://ref.ly/Isa43:16-Isa43:17); [1KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:1), [1KI 20:20–20:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:20-1Kgs20:21); [JOB 39:19–39:25](https://ref.ly/Job39:19-Job39:25); ; [PSA 76:6](https://ref.ly/Ps76:6); ; [ISA 5:28](https://ref.ly/Isa5:28); ; [JER 4:13](https://ref.ly/Jer4:13), [JER 46:9](https://ref.ly/Jer46:9), [JER 51:21](https://ref.ly/Jer51:21); ; [EZK 38:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:4); ; [HAB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Hab1:8)

-----------------------------------

-----------------------------------------------------

## 4660: insects (id: 4660)

A wide variety of insects are mentioned in Scripture. Some are useful in the provision of food, while others are viciously destructive. Their behaviour is used figuratively in the poetic and prophetic literature.

Regulations concerning insects as food
--------------------------------------

**[LEV 11:20–11:23](https://ref.ly/Lev11:20-Lev11:23)** *See also* [DEU 14:19](https://ref.ly/Deut14:19)

Kinds of insect
---------------

### The ant

**[PRO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6)** *See also* [PRO 30:25](https://ref.ly/Prov30:25)

### The bee

**[JDG 14:8](https://ref.ly/Judg14:8)** *See also* [DEU 1:44](https://ref.ly/Deut1:44); [PSA 118:12](https://ref.ly/Ps118:12); [ISA 7:18](https://ref.ly/Isa7:18)

### The flea

[1SA 24:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:14), [1SA 26:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:20)

### The fly

**[EXO 8:24](https://ref.ly/Exod8:24)** *See also* [EXO 8:21–8:22](https://ref.ly/Exod8:21-Exod8:22), [EXO 8:29–8:31](https://ref.ly/Exod8:29-Exod8:31); [PSA 78:45](https://ref.ly/Ps78:45); [ECC 10:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:1); [ISA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Isa51:6)

### The gnat

### The hornet

[EXO 23:28](https://ref.ly/Exod23:28); ; [DEU 7:20](https://ref.ly/Deut7:20); ; [JOS 24:12](https://ref.ly/Josh24:12)

### The locust

### The moth

### The spider

Although not strictly an insect the spider is most conveniently included under this category: [JOB 8:14](https://ref.ly/Job8:14); ; [ISA 59:5](https://ref.ly/Isa59:5)

## 4663: lamb (id: 4663)

A common source of clothing and meat, this animal was given heightened significance by its place in the Jewish sacrificial system and was used figuratively of Jesus Christ.

The economic importance of lambs
--------------------------------

### As food

**[2SA 12:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:4)** *See also* [DEU 28:4](https://ref.ly/Deut28:4); [AMO 6:4](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4)

### As a means of exchange

**[GEN 30:32](https://ref.ly/Gen30:32)** *See also* [2KI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:4)

The sacrificial significance of lambs
-------------------------------------

### Their general use

**[GEN 22:7](https://ref.ly/Gen22:7)** *See also* [EXO 29:38](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38); [LEV 3:7](https://ref.ly/Lev3:7), [LEV 14:12](https://ref.ly/Lev14:12), [LEV 14:24](https://ref.ly/Lev14:24); [1SA 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:9)

### The Passover lamb

**[EXO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Exod12:21)** *See also* [EXO 12:3](https://ref.ly/Exod12:3), [EXO 12:7](https://ref.ly/Exod12:7); [2CH 30:15–30:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:15-2Chr30:17); [EZR 6:20](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:20); [LUK 22:7](https://ref.ly/Luke22:7)

### The best had to be chosen

**[NUM 6:14](https://ref.ly/Num6:14)** *See also* [LEV 4:32](https://ref.ly/Lev4:32), [LEV 23:12](https://ref.ly/Lev23:12); [NUM 28:3–28:4](https://ref.ly/Num28:3-Num28:4), [NUM 28:11](https://ref.ly/Num28:11); [EZK 46:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:4)

### They were costly

**[LEV 12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:8)** *See also* [EXO 34:20](https://ref.ly/Exod34:20)

The figurative use of lambs
---------------------------

### In general

**[PSA 114:4](https://ref.ly/Ps114:4)**; **[LUK 10:3](https://ref.ly/Luke10:3)** *See also* [ISA 40:11](https://ref.ly/Isa40:11), [ISA 65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:25); [JER 11:19](https://ref.ly/Jer11:19); [JHN 21:15](https://ref.ly/John21:15)

### Describing Jesus Christ and his work

**[JHN 1:36](https://ref.ly/John1:36)**; **[1PE 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18-1Pet1:19)** *See also* [ACT 8:32](https://ref.ly/Acts8:32); [ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7); [1CO 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7); [REV 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rev5:12), [REV 22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3)

## 4666: lion (id: 4666)

An important animal in the natural history of Palestine; a symbol of power and strength.

Lions seen as a threat to life
------------------------------

**[DAN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7)** *See also* [JDG 14:5](https://ref.ly/Judg14:5); [1KI 13:24–13:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:24-1Kgs13:28), [1KI 20:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:36); [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24)

God's deliverance from lions
----------------------------

**[DAN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Dan6:22)** *See also* [1SA 17:34–17:37](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:34-1Sam17:37); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17); [HEB 11:33](https://ref.ly/Heb11:33)

---------------------------

**[JOB 10:16](https://ref.ly/Job10:16)** *See also* [GEN 49:9](https://ref.ly/Gen49:9); [PSA 22:13](https://ref.ly/Ps22:13), [PSA 22:21](https://ref.ly/Ps22:21)

### As fearsome

[JER 12:8](https://ref.ly/Jer12:8), [JER 51:38](https://ref.ly/Jer51:38)

### As voracious

**[PSA 7:2](https://ref.ly/Ps7:2)** *See also* [PSA 35:17](https://ref.ly/Ps35:17), [PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4); [JER 2:30](https://ref.ly/Jer2:30); [ZEP 3:3](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:3)

### They are not invincible

**[PSA 34:10](https://ref.ly/Ps34:10)** *See also* [JOB 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Job4:10-Job4:11); [PSA 58:6](https://ref.ly/Ps58:6), [PSA 91:13](https://ref.ly/Ps91:13)

### Jesus Christ is likened to a lion

**[REV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5)**

### Satan is likened to a lion

**[1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)**

------------------------------

**[ISA 11:6](https://ref.ly/Isa11:6)** *See also* [ISA 65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:25)

## 4669: locust (id: 4669)

The most destructive of all biblical insects, also used figuratively as a devouring pest.

There was no defence against locusts
------------------------------------

**[JOL 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Joel2:4-Joel2:5)** *See also* [PRO 30:27](https://ref.ly/Prov30:27)

Locusts were frequently a sign of God's judgment
------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 28:38](https://ref.ly/Deut28:38)**; **[AMO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Amos4:9)** [EXO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Exod10:4), [EXO 10:12–10:14](https://ref.ly/Exod10:12-Exod10:14), [EXO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Exod10:19); [DEU 28:42](https://ref.ly/Deut28:42) *curses for disobedience*; ; [1KI 8:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:37) parallel [2CH 6:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:28); ; [2CH 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:13-2Chr7:14); ; [PSA 78:46](https://ref.ly/Ps78:46), [PSA 105:34](https://ref.ly/Ps105:34); ; [JOL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Joel1:4), [JOL 2:25](https://ref.ly/Joel2:25); ; [AMO 7:1](https://ref.ly/Amos7:1)

Locusts were a source of food
-----------------------------

**[LEV 11:22](https://ref.ly/Lev11:22)** *See also* [MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) parallel [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6)

Locusts used figuratively
-------------------------

### Of numerous and destructive invaders

**[JDG 7:12](https://ref.ly/Judg7:12)**; **[ISA 33:4](https://ref.ly/Isa33:4)** *See also* [JDG 6:5](https://ref.ly/Judg6:5); [JER 46:23](https://ref.ly/Jer46:23), [JER 51:14](https://ref.ly/Jer51:14), [JER 51:27](https://ref.ly/Jer51:27); [NAM 3:15–3:17](https://ref.ly/Nah3:15-Nah3:17)

## 4672: manger (id: 4672)

A feeding trough in a stable or stall, filled with fodder for donkeys, oxen, etc. The term is also used to refer to the stall in which animals were kept. The newborn Jesus was laid in a manger because there was no room at an inn.

A feeding place for livestock
-----------------------------

### Recognised by the animals of the household

**[ISA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Isa1:3)** *Israel is like a wild animal which does not recognise or accept God's provision.* *See also* [JOB 39:9](https://ref.ly/Job39:9)

### Provision for farm animals

**[PRO 14:4](https://ref.ly/Prov14:4)** *See also* [PRO 12:10](https://ref.ly/Prov12:10); [ISA 30:24](https://ref.ly/Isa30:24)

### Provision for beasts of burden

**[GEN 24:32](https://ref.ly/Gen24:32)** *See also* [GEN 24:25](https://ref.ly/Gen24:25), [GEN 42:27](https://ref.ly/Gen42:27); [JDG 19:19](https://ref.ly/Judg19:19)

Jesus Christ laid in a manger
-----------------------------

**[LUK 2:7](https://ref.ly/Luke2:7)** *See also* [LUK 2:12](https://ref.ly/Luke2:12), [LUK 2:16](https://ref.ly/Luke2:16)

Farm animals kept in stalls
---------------------------

### Cattle kept in stalls

**[1KI 4:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:23)** *See also* [2CH 32:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:28); [PSA 50:9](https://ref.ly/Ps50:9); [HAB 3:17](https://ref.ly/Hab3:17); [MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2)

### Other animals kept in stalls

[1KI 4:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:26) parallel [2CH 9:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:25); ; [LUK 13:15](https://ref.ly/Luke13:15)

* **Associated Articles:** 2036: Jesus Christ, humility of (ID: `2036@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2515: Jesus Christ, birth of (ID: `2515@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4406: agriculture (ID: `4406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4603: animals (ID: `4603@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4675: nest (id: 4675)

The nest as the home of a bird rearing its young
------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 84:3](https://ref.ly/Ps84:3)** *See also* [DEU 22:6–22:7](https://ref.ly/Deut22:6-Deut22:7); [JOB 39:27–39:30](https://ref.ly/Job39:27-Job39:30); [PSA 104:12](https://ref.ly/Ps104:12), [PSA 104:17](https://ref.ly/Ps104:17); [ISA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Isa34:11), [ISA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Isa34:15); [JER 48:28](https://ref.ly/Jer48:28); [EZK 17:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:23)

The nest as a place of security
-------------------------------

**[MAT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:20)** parallel [LUK 9:58](https://ref.ly/Luke9:58) *See also* [DEU 32:10–32:11](https://ref.ly/Deut32:10-Deut32:11); [PRO 27:8](https://ref.ly/Prov27:8); [HAB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hab2:9)

The nest may not always be safe
-------------------------------

**[ISA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Isa10:14)** *See also* [NUM 24:21–24:22](https://ref.ly/Num24:21-Num24:22); [ISA 16:2](https://ref.ly/Isa16:2); [JER 49:16](https://ref.ly/Jer49:16); [OBA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Obad1:4)

The nest as the home of the viper
---------------------------------

[ISA 11:8](https://ref.ly/Isa11:8)

## 4678: pigeon (id: 4678)

A bird used, along with doves, for sacrifices, especially by those who could not afford lambs.

A pigeon offered as sacrifice
-----------------------------

**[LEV 1:14](https://ref.ly/Lev1:14)** *See also* [GEN 15:9](https://ref.ly/Gen15:9); [LEV 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Lev1:15-Lev1:17), [LEV 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Lev14:21-Lev14:22), [LEV 15:14](https://ref.ly/Lev15:14), [LEV 15:29](https://ref.ly/Lev15:29); [NUM 6:10](https://ref.ly/Num6:10)

Pigeons offered as a sacrifice by the poor
------------------------------------------

**[LEV 5:7](https://ref.ly/Lev5:7)** *See also* [LUK 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22-Luke2:24)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 1:14; Genesis 15:9; Leviticus 1:15-17; Leviticus 14:21-22; Leviticus 15:14; Leviticus 15:29; Numbers 6:10; Leviticus 5:7; Luke 2:22-24
* **Associated Articles:** 4612: birds (ID: `4612@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4636: dove (ID: `4636@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4681: ram (id: 4681)

The male sheep, part of an agrarian economy and used in sacrifice. Its skin had value and its horn was used in worship.

Rams as part of an agrarian economy
-----------------------------------

**[GEN 31:38](https://ref.ly/Gen31:38)** *See also* [GEN 32:14](https://ref.ly/Gen32:14); [DEU 32:14](https://ref.ly/Deut32:14); [2KI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:4); [EZK 27:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:21), [EZK 34:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:17)

Rams were used for religious purposes
-------------------------------------

### As sacrificial victims

**[GEN 22:13](https://ref.ly/Gen22:13)** *See also* [NUM 23:2](https://ref.ly/Num23:2); [1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22); [1CH 15:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:26); [MIC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mic6:7)

### In the making of a covenant

[GEN 15:9–15:10](https://ref.ly/Gen15:9-Gen15:10)

### For guilt offerings

**[LEV 5:15](https://ref.ly/Lev5:15)** *See also* [LEV 5:16–5:19](https://ref.ly/Lev5:16-Lev5:19), [LEV 6:6](https://ref.ly/Lev6:6), [LEV 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Lev19:21-Lev19:22); [NUM 5:8](https://ref.ly/Num5:8); [2CH 29:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:21); [EZR 10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:19)

### For fellowship or burnt offerings

**[LEV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Lev9:4)** *See also* [LEV 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/Lev9:1-Lev9:3), [LEV 16:3–16:5](https://ref.ly/Lev16:3-Lev16:5); [NUM 6:14–6:17](https://ref.ly/Num6:14-Num6:17), [NUM 7:17](https://ref.ly/Num7:17), [NUM 15:6–15:7](https://ref.ly/Num15:6-Num15:7), [NUM 28:11](https://ref.ly/Num28:11), [NUM 29:2](https://ref.ly/Num29:2); [EZK 43:23–43:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:23-Ezek43:24), [EZK 46:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:4)

For Aaron's ordination

## 4684: sheep (id: 4684)

Sheep were a major feature of ancient Israel's rural economy. Although they were of importance as a source of food and wool, they also symbolise helplessness and the need for guidance.

The economic importance of sheep
--------------------------------

### As a measure of wealth and God's blessing

**[1SA 25:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:2)**; **[PSA 144:13](https://ref.ly/Ps144:13)** *See also* [GEN 12:16](https://ref.ly/Gen12:16), [GEN 30:43](https://ref.ly/Gen30:43); [EXO 9:3](https://ref.ly/Exod9:3), [EXO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Exod22:1); [DEU 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Deut22:1-Deut22:2); [JOS 6:21](https://ref.ly/Josh6:21); [2SA 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:2-2Sam12:3); [JOB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Job1:3), [JOB 42:12](https://ref.ly/Job42:12)

### As a source of food

**[1SA 25:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:18)** *See also* [LEV 7:23](https://ref.ly/Lev7:23); [NUM 18:17–18:18](https://ref.ly/Num18:17-Num18:18); [1SA 14:32](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:32); [2SA 17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:29); [1CH 12:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:40)

### As a source of wool

**[2KI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:4)** *See also* [GEN 31:19](https://ref.ly/Gen31:19), [GEN 38:12–38:13](https://ref.ly/Gen38:12-Gen38:13); [DEU 18:4](https://ref.ly/Deut18:4); [1SA 25:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:7); [2SA 13:23–13:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:23-2Sam13:24); [EZK 27:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:18)

### Sheep were used in striking a bargain

**[GEN 21:27](https://ref.ly/Gen21:27)** *See also* [GEN 20:14](https://ref.ly/Gen20:14), [GEN 30:25–30:43](https://ref.ly/Gen30:25-Gen30:43)

### The care of sheep was a recognised occupation

**[1SA 16:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:11)** *See also* [GEN 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/Gen29:2-Gen29:3), [GEN 37:13](https://ref.ly/Gen37:13); [1SA 17:34–17:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:34-1Sam17:35); [SNG 1:7](https://ref.ly/Song1:7); [HOS 12:12](https://ref.ly/Hos12:12)

Sheep were used in sacrifice
----------------------------

**[EXO 20:24](https://ref.ly/Exod20:24)** *See also* [LEV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Lev1:10), [LEV 22:19](https://ref.ly/Lev22:19), [LEV 22:23](https://ref.ly/Lev22:23); [NUM 22:40](https://ref.ly/Num22:40); [DEU 18:3](https://ref.ly/Deut18:3); [1KI 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:9), [1KI 8:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:5); [JHN 2:14](https://ref.ly/John2:14)

The symbolic importance of sheep
--------------------------------

### Israel without a leader

**[1KI 22:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:17)** *See also* [NUM 27:16–27:17](https://ref.ly/Num27:16-Num27:17); [2SA 24:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:17); [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2), [ZEC 13:7](https://ref.ly/Zech13:7); [MAT 26:31](https://ref.ly/Matt26:31)

### Helplessness

**[ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7)**; **[MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36)** *See also* [PSA 44:11](https://ref.ly/Ps44:11), [PSA 49:14](https://ref.ly/Ps49:14); [JER 12:3](https://ref.ly/Jer12:3); [MAT 10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:16); [ROM 8:36](https://ref.ly/Rom8:36); [PSA 44:22](https://ref.ly/Ps44:22)

### Judgment

**[MAT 25:32–25:33](https://ref.ly/Matt25:32-Matt25:33)** *See also* [EZK 34:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:17)

### Sin

**[ISA 53:6](https://ref.ly/Isa53:6)**; **[1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:25)** *See also* [PSA 119:176](https://ref.ly/Ps119:176); [EZK 34:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:16); [MAT 18:12](https://ref.ly/Matt18:12)

### The people that belong to God

**[PSA 100:3](https://ref.ly/Ps100:3)** *See also* [PSA 74:1](https://ref.ly/Ps74:1), [PSA 78:52](https://ref.ly/Ps78:52), [PSA 79:13](https://ref.ly/Ps79:13); [JER 23:1](https://ref.ly/Jer23:1), [JER 50:6](https://ref.ly/Jer50:6); [EZK 34:11–34:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11-Ezek34:15); [MIC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mic2:12)

### Jesus Christ's care for his own

**[LUK 12:32](https://ref.ly/Luke12:32)**; **[JHN 10:1–10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:1-John10:16), [JHN 10:26–10:30](https://ref.ly/John10:26-John10:30)** *See also* [JHN 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:16-John21:17); [HEB 13:20](https://ref.ly/Heb13:20)

### A picture of God's future blessings

**[ISA 5:17](https://ref.ly/Isa5:17)**

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 25:2; Psalms 144:13; Genesis 12:16; Genesis 30:43; Exodus 9:3; Exodus 22:1; Deuteronomy 22:1-2; Joshua 6:21; 2 Samuel 12:2-3; Job 1:3; Job 42:12; 1 Samuel 25:18; Leviticus 7:23; Numbers 18:17-18; 1 Samuel 14:32; 2 Samuel 17:29; 1 Chronicles 12:40; 2 Kings 3:4; Genesis 31:19; Genesis 38:12-13; Deuteronomy 18:4; 1 Samuel 25:7; 2 Samuel 13:23-24; Ezekiel 27:18; Genesis 21:27; Genesis 20:14; Genesis 30:25-43; 1 Samuel 16:11; Genesis 29:2-3; Genesis 37:13; 1 Samuel 17:34-35; Song of Songs 1:7; Hosea 12:12; Exodus 20:24; Leviticus 1:10; Leviticus 22:19; Leviticus 22:23; Numbers 22:40; Deuteronomy 18:3; 1 Kings 1:9; 1 Kings 8:5; John 2:14; 1 Kings 22:17; Numbers 27:16-17; 2 Samuel 24:17; Zechariah 10:2; Zechariah 13:7; Matthew 26:31; Isaiah 53:7; Matthew 9:36; Psalms 44:11; Psalms 49:14; Jeremiah 12:3; Matthew 10:16; Romans 8:36; Psalms 44:22; Matthew 25:32-33; Ezekiel 34:17; Isaiah 53:6; 1 Peter 2:25; Psalms 119:176; Ezekiel 34:16; Matthew 18:12; Psalms 100:3; Psalms 74:1; Psalms 78:52; Psalms 79:13; Jeremiah 23:1; Jeremiah 50:6; Ezekiel 34:11-15; Micah 2:12; Luke 12:32; John 10:1-16; John 10:26-30; John 21:16-17; Hebrews 13:20; Isaiah 5:17

## 4687: snake (id: 4687)

A reptile, often associated with cunning and danger, or occasionally with satanic temptation.

Snakes as crawling reptiles
---------------------------

**[GEN 3:14](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14)** *See also* [ISA 65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:25); [MIC 7:17](https://ref.ly/Mic7:17)

Snakes are dangerous
--------------------

### Both to animals and human beings

**[GEN 49:17](https://ref.ly/Gen49:17)** *See also* [GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15); [EXO 4:3](https://ref.ly/Exod4:3), [EXO 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Exod7:9-Exod7:13); [PRO 23:32](https://ref.ly/Prov23:32); [ECC 10:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:8), [ECC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:11); [ISA 30:6](https://ref.ly/Isa30:6); [ACT 28:3–28:6](https://ref.ly/Acts28:3-Acts28:6)

### Some are poisonous

**[ISA 14:29](https://ref.ly/Isa14:29)** *See also* [DEU 8:15](https://ref.ly/Deut8:15), [DEU 32:33](https://ref.ly/Deut32:33); [JOB 20:14](https://ref.ly/Job20:14), [JOB 20:16](https://ref.ly/Job20:16); [REV 9:19](https://ref.ly/Rev9:19)

### They were sometimes used to bring God's judgment

**[1CO 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:9)** *See also* [NUM 21:4–21:6](https://ref.ly/Num21:4-Num21:6); [AMO 5:19](https://ref.ly/Amos5:19), [AMO 9:3](https://ref.ly/Amos9:3)

### They may be overcome

**[PSA 91:13](https://ref.ly/Ps91:13)** *See also* [MRK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Mark16:18); [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Luke10:19)

### One day they will be rendered harmless

**[ISA 11:8](https://ref.ly/Isa11:8)**

Snakes used symbolically
------------------------

### Of Israel's enemies

*See also* [ISA 27:1](https://ref.ly/Isa27:1); [JER 46:22](https://ref.ly/Jer46:22), [JER 51:34](https://ref.ly/Jer51:34)

### Of cunning and intelligence

**[MAT 10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:16)**

### Of something harmful

**[MAT 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:10)** parallel [LUK 11:11](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11)

### Of the ungodly

**[PSA 58:4–58:5](https://ref.ly/Ps58:4-Ps58:5)** *See also* [PSA 140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:3); [MAT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7), [MAT 12:34](https://ref.ly/Matt12:34), [MAT 23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:33); [ROM 3:13](https://ref.ly/Rom3:13)

### Of Satan

**[REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:13](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:13); [2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3); [REV 20:2](https://ref.ly/Rev20:2)

### Of salvation

**[JHN 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/John3:14-John3:15)** *See also* [NUM 21:7–21:9](https://ref.ly/Num21:7-Num21:9); [2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4)

## 4690: wings (id: 4690)

Limbs which enable flight. In Scripture these are mainly used either of angelic beings or in various symbolic senses, especially of God's protection.

Wings of birds or insects
-------------------------

[GEN 7:14](https://ref.ly/Gen7:14); ; [LEV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Lev1:17); ; [JOB 39:13](https://ref.ly/Job39:13), [JOB 39:26](https://ref.ly/Job39:26); ; [ISA 18:1](https://ref.ly/Isa18:1)

### Clean and unclean winged creatures

[LEV 11:13–11:25](https://ref.ly/Lev11:13-Lev11:25); ; [DEU 14:11–14:20](https://ref.ly/Deut14:11-Deut14:20); ; [ACT 10:12–10:15](https://ref.ly/Acts10:12-Acts10:15)

Wings of angelic beings
-----------------------

### Cherubim

**[EXO 25:20](https://ref.ly/Exod25:20)** *See also* [EXO 37:9](https://ref.ly/Exod37:9); [1KI 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:6-1Kgs8:7) parallel [2CH 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:7-2Chr5:8); [1CH 28:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:18); [2CH 3:11–3:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:11-2Chr3:13); [EZK 1:5–1:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:5-Ezek1:25), [EZK 3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:13), [EZK 10:5–10:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:5-Ezek10:22)

### Other angelic beings

**[ISA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Isa6:2)** *See also* [ISA 6:6](https://ref.ly/Isa6:6); [REV 4:8](https://ref.ly/Rev4:8)

Wings as a metaphor of protection
---------------------------------

### Birds protect their young

[DEU 32:11](https://ref.ly/Deut32:11); ; [ISA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Isa34:15); ; [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37) parallel [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34)

### God's protection likened to a bird's wings

**[PSA 91:4](https://ref.ly/Ps91:4)** *See also* [EXO 19:4](https://ref.ly/Exod19:4); [RUT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:12); [PSA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8), [PSA 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ps36:7), [PSA 57:1](https://ref.ly/Ps57:1), [PSA 61:4](https://ref.ly/Ps61:4), [PSA 63:7](https://ref.ly/Ps63:7); [ISA 31:5](https://ref.ly/Isa31:5)

### Egypt's protection likened to an eagle's wings

[EZK 17:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:7) *Zedekiah's fruitless appeal to Egypt for help against Babylon*

Wings as a metaphor for God's judgment
--------------------------------------

### Judgment on Israel

**[DEU 28:49](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49)** *See also* [ISA 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Isa8:7-Isa8:8); [EZK 17:3–17:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:3-Ezek17:4); [HOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Hos8:1); [HAB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Hab1:8); [ZEC 5:9](https://ref.ly/Zech5:9)

### Judgment on others

[JER 48:40](https://ref.ly/Jer48:40), [JER 49:22](https://ref.ly/Jer49:22); ; [REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Rev9:9)

Other figurative uses of wings
------------------------------

### God rides on the wings of the wind

**[2SA 22:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:11)** parallel [PSA 18:10](https://ref.ly/Ps18:10) *See also* [PSA 104:3](https://ref.ly/Ps104:3)

### God renews his people's strength

**[ISA 40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:31)**

### Israel described as a winged bird

*See also* [PSA 68:13](https://ref.ly/Ps68:13); [ISA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Isa10:14)

### Wings symbolise the ability to escape

**[PSA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Ps55:6)** *See also* [REV 12:14](https://ref.ly/Rev12:14)

### Wings symbolise instability

**[PRO 23:5](https://ref.ly/Prov23:5)** *See also* [ECC 10:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:20)

### Wings symbolise strength

**[DAN 7:6](https://ref.ly/Dan7:6)** *See also* [DAN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Dan7:4)

### Wings of the dawn

[PSA 139:9](https://ref.ly/Ps139:9)

### Wings of the sun

[MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2) *God is likened to the rays of the sun.*

## 4696: yoke (id: 4696)

The wooden collar which joins two animals, usually oxen, to enable them to pull together. Used metaphorically the term may denote the partnership of those who share a yoke or, more usually, the subjection of those under a yoke. As his covenant partners God's people are yoked to him and he will break the oppressive yoke of their enemies.

Literal references to yokes
---------------------------

**[1SA 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:7)**; **[1KI 19:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19)** *See also* [JOB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Job1:3), [JOB 42:12](https://ref.ly/Job42:12); [LUK 14:19](https://ref.ly/Luke14:19)NUM [19:2](https://ref.ly/Luke19:2); [DEU 21:32](https://ref.ly/Deut21:32)SA [24:22](https://ref.ly/Deut24:22); [1KI 19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:21)

God's yoke
----------

**[JER 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jer2:20)** *See also* [JER 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jer5:5)**[MAT 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:29-Matt11:30)**

The yoke as a symbol of partnership
-----------------------------------

### Partnership in Jesus Christ's service

**[PHP 4:3](https://ref.ly/Phil4:3)**

### Unsuitable partnerships

**[2CO 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14-2Cor6:16)** [GEN 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Gen24:3-Gen24:4); ; [1KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4); ; [1CO 7:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:39); [NUM 25:2–25:3](https://ref.ly/Num25:2-Num25:3); ; [PSA 106:28](https://ref.ly/Ps106:28); ; [1CO 10:18–10:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:18-1Cor10:21); [1CO 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:10) *too close association with the immoral*

The yoke as a symbol of subjection
----------------------------------

### The yoke of the law

**[ACT 15:10](https://ref.ly/Acts15:10)** *See also* [MAT 23:4](https://ref.ly/Matt23:4); [GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1)

### Israel under the yoke of enemies

**[DEU 28:47–28:48](https://ref.ly/Deut28:47-Deut28:48)**; **[JER 27:2](https://ref.ly/Jer27:2)** *Jeremiah symbolically portrays the coming Babylonian exile.* *See also* [ISA 47:6](https://ref.ly/Isa47:6); [JER 27:8](https://ref.ly/Jer27:8), [JER 27:11–27:13](https://ref.ly/Jer27:11-Jer27:13)JER [28:2–28:4](https://ref.ly/Jer28:2-Jer28:4), [JER 28:10–28:14](https://ref.ly/Jer28:10-Jer28:14)[LAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Lam1:14), [LAM 3:27](https://ref.ly/Lam3:27); [HOS 10:11](https://ref.ly/Hos10:11)

### The yoke of other masters

[GEN 27:40](https://ref.ly/Gen27:40) *Esau will throw off the yoke of Jacob.*; [1KI 12:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:4) parallel [2CH 10:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:4); ; [1KI 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:9-1Kgs12:11) parallel [2CH 10:9–10:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:9-2Chr10:11); ; [1KI 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:14) parallel [2CH 10:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:14); [ISA 58:6](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6), [ISA 58:9](https://ref.ly/Isa58:9); [1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:1) *Slaves are to respect their masters.*

### God breaks the yoke of oppression

**[EZK 34:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:27)**; **[HOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:4)** [EXO 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6-Exod6:7); ; [LEV 26:13](https://ref.ly/Lev26:13); ; [EZK 30:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:18); [ISA 9:4](https://ref.ly/Isa9:4), [ISA 10:27](https://ref.ly/Isa10:27), [ISA 14:25](https://ref.ly/Isa14:25); ; [NAM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Nah1:13); [JER 30:8](https://ref.ly/Jer30:8) *Babylon's yoke broken*

## 4801: black (id: 4801)

A dark colour often associated with impending judgment or with evil.

Black as descriptive of colour
------------------------------

**[MAT 5:36](https://ref.ly/Matt5:36)** *See also* [EXO 10:15](https://ref.ly/Exod10:15) *the plague of locusts*; [LEV 11:13–11:14](https://ref.ly/Lev11:13-Lev11:14) parallel [DEU 14:12–14:13](https://ref.ly/Deut14:12-Deut14:13) *unclean food*; [LEV 13:31](https://ref.ly/Lev13:31); [DEU 4:11](https://ref.ly/Deut4:11); [1KI 18:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:45); [JOB 28:3](https://ref.ly/Job28:3), [JOB 30:28](https://ref.ly/Job30:28), [JOB 30:30](https://ref.ly/Job30:30); [SNG 5:11](https://ref.ly/Song5:11); [LAM 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Lam4:7-Lam4:8)ZEC [6:2](https://ref.ly/Lam6:2), [ZEC 6:6](https://ref.ly/Zech6:6); [REV 6:5](https://ref.ly/Rev6:5)[REV 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12)

Black as the opposite of light
------------------------------

**[JOB 3:5](https://ref.ly/Job3:5)** *Job cursing the day of his birth.* *See also* [ISA 5:30](https://ref.ly/Isa5:30), [ISA 45:7](https://ref.ly/Isa45:7); [AMO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8)

Black in the setting of impending judgment
------------------------------------------

**[JOL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Joel2:2)** *See also* [AMO 8:9](https://ref.ly/Amos8:9); [ZEP 1:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:15)

Black as a symbol of evil
-------------------------

**[JOB 34:22](https://ref.ly/Job34:22)** *See also* [JHN 3:19](https://ref.ly/John3:19); [2PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17); [JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/Jude1:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:36; Exodus 10:15; Leviticus 11:13-14; Deuteronomy 14:12-13; Leviticus 13:31; Deuteronomy 4:11; 1 Kings 18:45; Job 28:3; Job 30:28; Job 30:30; Song of Songs 5:11; Lamentations 4:7-8; Lamentations 6:2; Zechariah 6:6; Revelation 6:5; Revelation 6:12; Job 3:5; Isaiah 5:30; Isaiah 45:7; Amos 5:8; Joel 2:2; Amos 8:9; Zephaniah 1:15; Job 34:22; John 3:19; 2 Peter 2:17; Jude 1:13

## 4802: blight (id: 4802)

A disease ruinous to crops. In the OT blight was seen as evidence of God's judgment.

Blight as punishment from God
-----------------------------

**[DEU 28:22](https://ref.ly/Deut28:22)** *See also* [HOS 9:16](https://ref.ly/Hos9:16); [AMO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Amos4:9); [HAG 2:17](https://ref.ly/Hag2:17)

Deliverance from blight
-----------------------

**[1KI 8:37–8:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:37-1Kgs8:39)** parallel [2CH 6:28–6:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:28-2Chr6:30)

Blight as a symbol of sorrow
----------------------------

**[PSA 102:4](https://ref.ly/Ps102:4)**

## 4804: breath (id: 4804)

The imagery of breath is often used to convey spiritual essence and power, unseen except in its effects, including aspects of the person and work of the Holy Spirit. The main Hebrew and Greek words translated as “breath” in the NIV are also translated as “spirit” and “wind”.

The breath of life
------------------

### Life imparted by God

**[GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7)**; **[ACT 17:25](https://ref.ly/Acts17:25)** *See also* [GEN 1:30](https://ref.ly/Gen1:30), [GEN 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gen6:17), [GEN 7:15](https://ref.ly/Gen7:15), [GEN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Gen7:22); [JOB 27:3](https://ref.ly/Job27:3), [JOB 33:4](https://ref.ly/Job33:4); [PSA 104:30](https://ref.ly/Ps104:30); [ISA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Isa42:5), [ISA 57:16](https://ref.ly/Isa57:16); [JER 38:16](https://ref.ly/Jer38:16); [REV 11:11](https://ref.ly/Rev11:11)

### Life recalled by his sovereign will

**[ECC 12:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:7)** *See also* [1KI 17:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:17); [JOB 34:14–34:15](https://ref.ly/Job34:14-Job34:15); [PSA 104:29](https://ref.ly/Ps104:29); [ISA 40:7](https://ref.ly/Isa40:7)

The breath of God
-----------------

### An emblem of the Holy Spirit

**[JHN 20:21–20:22](https://ref.ly/John20:21-John20:22)** *See also* [EZK 37:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:5), [EZK 37:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:14)

### God's creative breath

**[PSA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Ps33:6)** *See also* [GEN 1:2](https://ref.ly/Gen1:2); [JOB 26:13](https://ref.ly/Job26:13), [JOB 33:4](https://ref.ly/Job33:4)

### God's enlightening breath

**[JOB 32:8](https://ref.ly/Job32:8)** *See also* [JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26), [JHN 16:13–16:15](https://ref.ly/John16:13-John16:15); [2TI 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16-2Tim3:17); [2PE 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:20-2Pet1:21)

### God's regenerating breath

**[EZK 37:4–37:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:4-Ezek37:14)** *See also* [JHN 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:5-John3:8); [1TH 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:4-1Thess1:5); [TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5)

### God's empowering breath

**[ACT 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:4)** *See also* [ISA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Isa11:2), [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [JOL 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28-Joel2:29); [JHN 20:21–20:22](https://ref.ly/John20:21-John20:22); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)

### God's destroying breath

## 4805: clouds (id: 4805)

A natural feature of the sky, often associated with God's judgment, presence and glory.

Clouds as a natural phenomenon
------------------------------

### Rain clouds

**[1KI 18:44–18:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:44-1Kgs18:45)** *See also* [GEN 9:13–9:16](https://ref.ly/Gen9:13-Gen9:16); [JOB 36:28](https://ref.ly/Job36:28); [ECC 11:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:3); [LUK 12:54](https://ref.ly/Luke12:54)

### Clouds representing the sky

[JOB 20:6](https://ref.ly/Job20:6), [JOB 35:5](https://ref.ly/Job35:5); ; [ISA 14:14](https://ref.ly/Isa14:14); ; [JER 51:9](https://ref.ly/Jer51:9)

### Clouds subject to God's sovereign control

**[JOB 37:13](https://ref.ly/Job37:13)** *See also* [JDG 5:4](https://ref.ly/Judg5:4); [JOB 26:8](https://ref.ly/Job26:8); [PSA 135:7](https://ref.ly/Ps135:7), [PSA 147:8](https://ref.ly/Ps147:8); [PRO 3:20](https://ref.ly/Prov3:20), [PRO 8:28](https://ref.ly/Prov8:28); [ISA 5:6](https://ref.ly/Isa5:6); [JER 10:13](https://ref.ly/Jer10:13), [JER 51:16](https://ref.ly/Jer51:16); [ZEC 10:1](https://ref.ly/Zech10:1)

### Clouds expressing the mystery of God's world

**[JOB 37:15–37:16](https://ref.ly/Job37:15-Job37:16)** *See also* [JOB 22:14](https://ref.ly/Job22:14), [JOB 26:9](https://ref.ly/Job26:9), [JOB 36:29](https://ref.ly/Job36:29), [JOB 38:9](https://ref.ly/Job38:9), [JOB 38:37](https://ref.ly/Job38:37); [ECC 11:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:4)

### Clouds of smoke

[LEV 16:13](https://ref.ly/Lev16:13); ; [JDG 20:38](https://ref.ly/Judg20:38)

Clouds as symbols of spiritual truths
-------------------------------------

### A symbol of judgment

**[EZK 30:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:3)** *See also* [EZK 30:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:18), [EZK 34:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:12); [JOL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Joel2:2); [ZEP 1:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:15)

### A symbol of blessing

[ISA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Isa45:8)

### A symbol of impermanence

**[JOB 7:9](https://ref.ly/Job7:9)** *See also* [JOB 30:15](https://ref.ly/Job30:15); [PRO 16:15](https://ref.ly/Prov16:15); [HOS 6:4](https://ref.ly/Hos6:4), [HOS 13:3](https://ref.ly/Hos13:3)

### Other positive symbolic uses

[ISA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:5), [ISA 44:22](https://ref.ly/Isa44:22); ; [HEB 12:1](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1)

### Other negative symbolic uses

[PRO 25:14](https://ref.ly/Prov25:14); ; [ECC 12:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:2); ; [JUD 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12)

Clouds as a symbol of God's presence and glory
----------------------------------------------

### Revealing God's majesty

**[NAM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Nah1:3)** *See also* [JOB 37:11](https://ref.ly/Job37:11); [PSA 97:2](https://ref.ly/Ps97:2); [ISA 30:27](https://ref.ly/Isa30:27), [ISA 30:30](https://ref.ly/Isa30:30)DEU [33:26](https://ref.ly/Isa33:26); [PSA 68:4](https://ref.ly/Ps68:4), [PSA 104:3](https://ref.ly/Ps104:3); [JER 4:13](https://ref.ly/Jer4:13)

### The cloud of God's presence

**[EXO 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/Exod13:21-Exod13:22)** [NUM 9:15–9:22](https://ref.ly/Num9:15-Num9:22), [NUM 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Num10:11-Num10:12), [NUM 10:34](https://ref.ly/Num10:34), [NUM 11:25](https://ref.ly/Num11:25), [NUM 14:14](https://ref.ly/Num14:14); ; [DEU 31:15](https://ref.ly/Deut31:15); ; [NEH 9:12](https://ref.ly/Neh9:12), [NEH 9:19](https://ref.ly/Neh9:19); ; [PSA 78:14](https://ref.ly/Ps78:14), [PSA 99:7](https://ref.ly/Ps99:7), [PSA 105:39](https://ref.ly/Ps105:39); [EXO 14:19–14:20](https://ref.ly/Exod14:19-Exod14:20), [EXO 14:24](https://ref.ly/Exod14:24); ; [1CO 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:1-1Cor10:2); ; [REV 14:14](https://ref.ly/Rev14:14)

### The cloud of God's awesomeness

**[LEV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2)** *See also* [EXO 19:9](https://ref.ly/Exod19:9), [EXO 19:16](https://ref.ly/Exod19:16), [EXO 34:5](https://ref.ly/Exod34:5); [NUM 12:5](https://ref.ly/Num12:5), [NUM 12:10](https://ref.ly/Num12:10); [DEU 4:11](https://ref.ly/Deut4:11), [DEU 5:22](https://ref.ly/Deut5:22)

### The cloud of God's glory

**[1KI 8:10–8:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:10-1Kgs8:12)** parallel [2CH 5:13–6:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13-2Chr6:1) *See also* [EXO 16:10](https://ref.ly/Exod16:10), [EXO 24:15–24:18](https://ref.ly/Exod24:15-Exod24:18), [EXO 33:9–33:10](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9-Exod33:10), [EXO 40:34–40:38](https://ref.ly/Exod40:34-Exod40:38); [NUM 16:42](https://ref.ly/Num16:42); [EZK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:28)

## 4807: colours (id: 4807)

A wide range of colours are mentioned in Scripture, some being used symbolically.

Colours mentioned in Scripture
------------------------------

### White

**[GEN 49:12](https://ref.ly/Gen49:12)**; **[2KI 5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:27)**

### Blue

**[EST 1:6](https://ref.ly/Esth1:6)**; **[EZK 27:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:24)** *See also* [EXO 26:1](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1), [EXO 39:22](https://ref.ly/Exod39:22)

### Yellow

**[REV 9:17](https://ref.ly/Rev9:17)** *See also* [LEV 13:30](https://ref.ly/Lev13:30)

### Green

**[MRK 6:39](https://ref.ly/Mark6:39)** *An indication as to the season; the grass is green in Galilee in spring.* [GEN 1:30](https://ref.ly/Gen1:30); ; [PSA 23:2](https://ref.ly/Ps23:2); ; [JER 17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:8); ; [REV 8:7](https://ref.ly/Rev8:7)

### Red

**[2KI 3:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:22)**; **[MAT 16:2](https://ref.ly/Matt16:2)** *See also* [GEN 25:30](https://ref.ly/Gen25:30); [EXO 36:19](https://ref.ly/Exod36:19); [REV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rev12:3)

### Purple

**[MRK 15:17](https://ref.ly/Mark15:17)** [NUM 4:13](https://ref.ly/Num4:13); ; [PRO 31:22](https://ref.ly/Prov31:22); ; [DAN 5:7](https://ref.ly/Dan5:7); ; [ACT 16:14](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14)

### Scarlet

**[ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18)** *See also* [GEN 38:28](https://ref.ly/Gen38:28); [EXO 35:23](https://ref.ly/Exod35:23) *Purple, scarlet, crimson and red are closely related and sometimes interchangeable in Scripture*; [NUM 4:8](https://ref.ly/Num4:8); [SNG 4:3](https://ref.ly/Song4:3); [MAT 27:28](https://ref.ly/Matt27:28); [REV 17:4](https://ref.ly/Rev17:4)

### Crimson

**[ISA 63:1](https://ref.ly/Isa63:1)** *See also* [2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7)

### Black

**[SNG 5:11](https://ref.ly/Song5:11)** *See also* [REV 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12)

### The whole spectrum

**[EZK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:28)** *See also* [REV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Rev4:3), [REV 10:1](https://ref.ly/Rev10:1)

Colours used symbolically
-------------------------

**[REV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Rev6:2)** *White here symbolises conquest*; **[REV 17:3](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3)** *See also* [REV 6:4](https://ref.ly/Rev6:4) *bloodshed and war*; [REV 6:8](https://ref.ly/Rev6:8) *death*[REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Rev19:14), [REV 19:11](https://ref.ly/Rev19:11)[REV 20:11](https://ref.ly/Rev20:11) *holiness*

## 4808: crimson (id: 4808)

A deep red colour used interchangeably with scarlet and sometimes with purple. It has associations with blood and richness.

The richness of crimson used for ornamentation
----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 25:3–25:4](https://ref.ly/Exod25:3-Exod25:4), [EXO 28:6](https://ref.ly/Exod28:6)** *It was used extensively in the construction of the tabernacle*; **[2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7)** *See also* [2CH 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:14), [2CH 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:14)

Crimson associated with splendour and prosperity
------------------------------------------------

**[2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24)**; **[REV 18:16](https://ref.ly/Rev18:16)** *See also* [PRO 31:21](https://ref.ly/Prov31:21); [JER 4:30](https://ref.ly/Jer4:30); [NAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Nah2:3); [MAT 27:28](https://ref.ly/Matt27:28) parallel [MRK 15:17](https://ref.ly/Mark15:17); [REV 18:12](https://ref.ly/Rev18:12)

Crimson associated with blood and sin
-------------------------------------

**[ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18)** *See also* [LEV 14:3–14:6](https://ref.ly/Lev14:3-Lev14:6), [LEV 14:49–14:53](https://ref.ly/Lev14:49-Lev14:53); [NUM 19:6](https://ref.ly/Num19:6); [ISA 63:1–63:3](https://ref.ly/Isa63:1-Isa63:3); [HEB 9:19](https://ref.ly/Heb9:19); [REV 17:3–17:4](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3-Rev17:4)

Everyday use of the colour crimson
----------------------------------

[GEN 38:28](https://ref.ly/Gen38:28); ; [JOS 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Josh2:17-Josh2:18); ; [SNG 4:3](https://ref.ly/Song4:3)

## 4809: darkness (id: 4809)

The absence of light. Often used figuratively as a symbol of sin, chaos or the absence of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 4810: darkness, natural (ID: `4810@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin (ID: `4811@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4812: darkness, and God's judgment (ID: `4812@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4810: darkness, natural (id: 4810)

Scripture stresses that God is able to be present and known, even in the darkest aspects of the world.

Darkness as a symbol of the terrifying majesty of God
-----------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:18–20:21](https://ref.ly/Exod20:18-Exod20:21)**; **[PSA 97:2](https://ref.ly/Ps97:2)** *See also* [DEU 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Deut4:11-Deut4:12), [DEU 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Deut5:22-Deut5:24); [HEB 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Heb12:18-Heb12:21)

God is the source of natural darkness
-------------------------------------

**[ISA 45:7](https://ref.ly/Isa45:7)** *See also* [GEN 1:2–1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:2-Gen1:5), [GEN 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:16-Gen1:18); [JOB 26:10](https://ref.ly/Job26:10); [PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16), [PSA 104:19–104:20](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19-Ps104:20)

God's eye penetrates the darkness
---------------------------------

**[JOB 34:21–34:22](https://ref.ly/Job34:21-Job34:22)**; **[PSA 139:11–139:12](https://ref.ly/Ps139:11-Ps139:12)** *See also* [DAN 2:22](https://ref.ly/Dan2:22); [AMO 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8-Amos5:9); [2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:6)

Miracles involving natural darkness
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 27:45](https://ref.ly/Matt27:45)** parallels [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33), [LUK 23:44–23:45](https://ref.ly/Luke23:44-Luke23:45) *See also* [EXO 10:21–10:23](https://ref.ly/Exod10:21-Exod10:23), [EXO 14:19–14:20](https://ref.ly/Exod14:19-Exod14:20); [JOS 24:6–24:7](https://ref.ly/Josh24:6-Josh24:7); [PSA 105:26–105:28](https://ref.ly/Ps105:26-Ps105:28); [ISA 50:3](https://ref.ly/Isa50:3); [ACT 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:19-Acts2:20); [JOL 2:30–2:32](https://ref.ly/Joel2:30-Joel2:32); [ACT 13:9–13:12](https://ref.ly/Acts13:9-Acts13:12)

--------------------------------

## 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin (id: 4811)

Darkness is often used in Scripture as a symbol of sin and its effects. It is often contrasted with light, as a symbol of forgiveness and the presence of God.

Darkness as a symbol of sin
---------------------------

**[JHN 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:19-John3:20)** *See also* [JOB 24:16–24:17](https://ref.ly/Job24:16-Job24:17); [PRO 2:12–2:15](https://ref.ly/Prov2:12-Prov2:15), [PRO 4:19](https://ref.ly/Prov4:19); [ISA 5:20](https://ref.ly/Isa5:20), [ISA 29:15](https://ref.ly/Isa29:15); [MAT 6:23](https://ref.ly/Matt6:23); [LUK 22:53](https://ref.ly/Luke22:53) *Jesus Christ arrested*; [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21)ROM [13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12); [2CO 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14); [EPH 5:8–5:12](https://ref.ly/Eph5:8-Eph5:12)[1JN 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/1John1:5-1John1:6)

Darkness as a symbol of the effects of sin
------------------------------------------

### Darkness symbolising ignorance of the truth

**[2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:4)** *See also* [PSA 82:5](https://ref.ly/Ps82:5); [ISA 8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:20); [2CO 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:14-2Cor3:15); [EPH 4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:18)

### Darkness symbolising the inability to find the right way

**[JHN 12:35](https://ref.ly/John12:35)** *See also* [JOB 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/Job12:24-Job12:25); [ISA 59:9–59:10](https://ref.ly/Isa59:9-Isa59:10); [MAT 5:14](https://ref.ly/Matt5:14); [1JN 2:9](https://ref.ly/1John2:9), [1JN 2:11](https://ref.ly/1John2:11)

### Darkness symbolises times of trial

[JOB 3:3–3:6](https://ref.ly/Job3:3-Job3:6), [JOB 19:8](https://ref.ly/Job19:8), [JOB 23:16–23:17](https://ref.ly/Job23:16-Job23:17), [JOB 30:26](https://ref.ly/Job30:26); ; [PSA 107:10](https://ref.ly/Ps107:10), [PSA 143:3](https://ref.ly/Ps143:3); ; [ECC 11:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:8)

### Darkness symbolises death and the grave

**[JOB 10:20–10:22](https://ref.ly/Job10:20-Job10:22)** *See also* [PSA 49:19](https://ref.ly/Ps49:19), [PSA 88:10–88:12](https://ref.ly/Ps88:10-Ps88:12)

God's provision for the darkness of sin
---------------------------------------

### God himself is a remedy for darkness

**[2SA 22:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:29)** *See also* [PSA 18:28](https://ref.ly/Ps18:28), [PSA 30:5](https://ref.ly/Ps30:5), [PSA 91:4–91:7](https://ref.ly/Ps91:4-Ps91:7); [ISA 42:16](https://ref.ly/Isa42:16), [ISA 50:10](https://ref.ly/Isa50:10); [MIC 7:8](https://ref.ly/Mic7:8)

### God will deliver from darkness

**[ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2)** *See also* [MAT 4:16](https://ref.ly/Matt4:16); [PSA 107:13–107:14](https://ref.ly/Ps107:13-Ps107:14); [ISA 29:18](https://ref.ly/Isa29:18), [ISA 49:8–49:9](https://ref.ly/Isa49:8-Isa49:9); [LUK 1:78–1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78-Luke1:79); [ACT 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:17-Acts26:18); [COL 1:13](https://ref.ly/Col1:13); [1TH 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:4-1Thess5:5); [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)

### God's Son saves believers from darkness

**[JHN 8:12](https://ref.ly/John8:12)** *See also* [JHN 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/John1:4-John1:5), [JHN 12:46](https://ref.ly/John12:46); [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5); [2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:6); [GEN 1:3](https://ref.ly/Gen1:3); [EPH 5:8](https://ref.ly/Eph5:8); [2PE 1:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:19); [1JN 2:8](https://ref.ly/1John2:8)

### God's people rescue others from darkness

**[ISA 42:6–42:7](https://ref.ly/Isa42:6-Isa42:7)** *See also* [ISA 60:1–60:5](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1-Isa60:5)

* **Associated Passages:** John 3:19-20; Job 24:16-17; Proverbs 2:12-15; Proverbs 4:19; Isaiah 5:20; Isaiah 29:15; Matthew 6:23; Luke 22:53; Romans 1:21; Romans 13:12; 2 Corinthians 6:14; Ephesians 5:8-12; 1 John 1:5-6; 2 Corinthians 4:4; Psalms 82:5; Isaiah 8:20; 2 Corinthians 3:14-15; Ephesians 4:18; John 12:35; Job 12:24-25; Isaiah 59:9-10; Matthew 5:14; 1 John 2:9; 1 John 2:11; Job 3:3-6; Job 19:8; Job 23:16-17; Job 30:26; Psalms 107:10; Psalms 143:3; Ecclesiastes 11:8; Job 10:20-22; Psalms 49:19; Psalms 88:10-12; 2 Samuel 22:29; Psalms 18:28; Psalms 30:5; Psalms 91:4-7; Isaiah 42:16; Isaiah 50:10; Micah 7:8; Isaiah 9:2; Matthew 4:16; Psalms 107:13-14; Isaiah 29:18; Isaiah 49:8-9; Luke 1:78-79; Acts 26:17-18; Colossians 1:13; 1 Thessalonians 5:4-5; 1 Peter 2:9; John 8:12; John 1:4-5; John 12:46; 1 Corinthians 4:5; 2 Corinthians 4:6; Genesis 1:3; Ephesians 5:8; 2 Peter 1:19; 1 John 2:8; Isaiah 42:6-7; Isaiah 60:1-5
* **Associated Articles:** 4027: world, as fallen (ID: `4027@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4846: shadow (ID: `4846@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5560: suffering (ID: `5560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5831: depression (ID: `5831@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6139: deadness, spiritual (ID: `6139@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6181: ignorance (ID: `6181@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6184: imagination (ID: `6184@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8718: disobedience (ID: `8718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8734: evil (ID: `8734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4812: darkness, and God's judgment (id: 4812)

Darkness is used figuratively of God's present and final judgment upon individuals, unfaithful Israel and the heathen nations.

Darkness as a symbol of judgment upon ungodly individuals
---------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:30)** *See also* [1SA 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9-1Sam2:10); [JOB 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/Job5:13-Job5:14), [JOB 12:25](https://ref.ly/Job12:25), [JOB 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/Job15:22-Job15:23), [JOB 15:30](https://ref.ly/Job15:30), [JOB 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Job18:5-Job18:6), [JOB 18:18](https://ref.ly/Job18:18), [JOB 20:24–20:26](https://ref.ly/Job20:24-Job20:26), [JOB 22:10–22:11](https://ref.ly/Job22:10-Job22:11) *Eliphaz reasons on the assumption that Job's suffering proves his wickedness*; [PSA 35:5–35:6](https://ref.ly/Ps35:5-Ps35:6); [ISA 8:22](https://ref.ly/Isa8:22); [JER 23:11–23:12](https://ref.ly/Jer23:11-Jer23:12); [MAT 22:13](https://ref.ly/Matt22:13); [2PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17); [JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/Jude1:13); [REV 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10-Rev16:11)

Darkness as a symbol of judgment upon unfaithful Israel
-------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 28:28–28:29](https://ref.ly/Deut28:28-Deut28:29)**; **[ISA 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Isa5:29-Isa5:30)** *This will be fulfilled at the time of Israel's captivity.* *See also* [ISA 59:9–59:10](https://ref.ly/Isa59:9-Isa59:10); [ROM 11:10](https://ref.ly/Rom11:10); [JER 4:27–4:28](https://ref.ly/Jer4:27-Jer4:28), [JER 13:16](https://ref.ly/Jer13:16); [LAM 3:2](https://ref.ly/Lam3:2), [LAM 3:6](https://ref.ly/Lam3:6); [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12)

Darkness as a symbol of judgment upon heathen nations
-----------------------------------------------------

[ISA 47:5–47:7](https://ref.ly/Isa47:5-Isa47:7); ; [EZK 30:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:18), [EZK 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:7-Ezek32:8); ; [NAM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Nah1:8)

Darkness as a symbol of judgment at the day of the Lord
-------------------------------------------------------

**[JOL 2:31](https://ref.ly/Joel2:31)** *See also* [ISA 13:9–13:10](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9-Isa13:10), [ISA 34:4](https://ref.ly/Isa34:4); [JOL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Joel2:1-Joel2:2), [JOL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Joel2:10), [JOL 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Joel3:14-Joel3:15); [AMO 5:18–5:20](https://ref.ly/Amos5:18-Amos5:20), [AMO 8:9](https://ref.ly/Amos8:9); [ZEP 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14-Zeph1:15); [ZEC 14:6](https://ref.ly/Zech14:6); [MAT 24:29](https://ref.ly/Matt24:29); [ACT 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:19-Acts2:20); [1TH 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:1-1Thess5:3); [REV 6:12–6:17](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 25:30; 1 Samuel 2:9-10; Job 5:13-14; Job 12:25; Job 15:22-23; Job 15:30; Job 18:5-6; Job 18:18; Job 20:24-26; Job 22:10-11; Psalms 35:5-6; Isaiah 8:22; Jeremiah 23:11-12; Matthew 22:13; 2 Peter 2:17; Jude 1:13; Revelation 16:10-11; Deuteronomy 28:28-29; Isaiah 5:29-30; Isaiah 59:9-10; Romans 11:10; Jeremiah 4:27-28; Jeremiah 13:16; Lamentations 3:2; Lamentations 3:6; Matthew 8:12; Isaiah 47:5-7; Ezekiel 30:18; Ezekiel 32:7-8; Nahum 1:8; Joel 2:31; Isaiah 13:9-10; Isaiah 34:4; Joel 2:1-2; Joel 2:10; Joel 3:14-15; Amos 5:18-20; Amos 8:9; Zephaniah 1:14-15; Zechariah 14:6; Matthew 24:29; Acts 2:19-20; 1 Thessalonians 5:1-3; Revelation 6:12-17

## 4814: dew (id: 4814)

The nightly condensation of moisture from the atmosphere
--------------------------------------------------------

**[SNG 5:2](https://ref.ly/Song5:2)** *See also* [JDG 6:36–6:40](https://ref.ly/Judg6:36-Judg6:40) *Gideon's sign*[DAN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Dan4:25), [DAN 5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:21)

Dew as a sign of God's blessing and provision
---------------------------------------------

**[DEU 33:13](https://ref.ly/Deut33:13)** *See also* [GEN 27:28](https://ref.ly/Gen27:28); [JOB 38:28](https://ref.ly/Job38:28); [PRO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Prov3:19-Prov3:20); [HOS 14:5](https://ref.ly/Hos14:5); [ZEC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:12)

Lack of dew a sign of God's judgment
------------------------------------

-----------------------

* **Associated Passages:** Song of Songs 5:2; Judges 6:36-40; Daniel 4:25; Daniel 5:21; Deuteronomy 33:13; Genesis 27:28; Job 38:28; Proverbs 3:19-20; Hosea 14:5; Zechariah 8:12; Exodus 16:13-14; Numbers 11:9; 1 Kings 17:1; Genesis 27:39; 2 Samuel 1:21; Haggai 1:10; Deuteronomy 32:2; Psalms 110:3; Isaiah 26:19; 2 Samuel 17:12; Job 29:19; Psalms 133:3; Proverbs 19:12; Isaiah 18:4; Hosea 6:4; Hosea 13:3; Micah 5:7

## 4815: drought (id: 4815)

A severe long\-term absence of water or drink of any kind, leading to disaster for the people affected in this way. Scripture provides examples of such droughts, while also indicating the possibility and dangers of long\-term spiritual thirst arising through disobedience or lack of faith.

* **Associated Articles:** 4816: drought, physical (ID: `4816@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4817: drought, spiritual (ID: `4817@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4816: drought, physical (id: 4816)

Drought arises through the absence of rain, leading to severe hardship for those who are affected by it.

Drought may be a sign of God's displeasure
------------------------------------------

**[DEU 11:17](https://ref.ly/Deut11:17)** *See also* [DEU 28:22–28:24](https://ref.ly/Deut28:22-Deut28:24); [2SA 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:21); [1KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:1); [PSA 107:33–107:34](https://ref.ly/Ps107:33-Ps107:34); [ISA 5:6](https://ref.ly/Isa5:6), [ISA 5:13](https://ref.ly/Isa5:13); [JER 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2-Jer2:3), [JER 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/Jer14:1-Jer14:6), [JER 50:38](https://ref.ly/Jer50:38); [EZK 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:23-Ezek22:24); [AMO 4:7](https://ref.ly/Amos4:7); [HAG 1:11](https://ref.ly/Hag1:11); [JAS 5:17](https://ref.ly/Jas5:17)

The response of believers to drought
------------------------------------

### It tests their faith

**[1KI 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:7)**; **[HAB 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Hab3:17-Hab3:18)** *See also* [DEU 8:15](https://ref.ly/Deut8:15); [ISA 41:17](https://ref.ly/Isa41:17), [ISA 48:20–48:21](https://ref.ly/Isa48:20-Isa48:21); [JER 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5-Jer2:6); [HOS 13:5](https://ref.ly/Hos13:5)

### It gives opportunity to help others

**[ACT 11:28–11:29](https://ref.ly/Acts11:28-Acts11:29)** *See also* [GEN 41:56–41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:56-Gen41:57); [1KI 17:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:9), [1KI 18:1–18:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:1-1Kgs18:4)

Rain is a sign of God's general favour or of his forgiveness
------------------------------------------------------------

### His general favour

**[ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)** *See also* [GEN 27:28](https://ref.ly/Gen27:28); [JOB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Job5:10), [JOB 38:25–38:28](https://ref.ly/Job38:25-Job38:28); [PSA 65:9–65:10](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9-Ps65:10), [PSA 147:8](https://ref.ly/Ps147:8); [JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24), [JER 14:22](https://ref.ly/Jer14:22); [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)

### His response to repentance

**[JOL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12), [JOL 2:23](https://ref.ly/Joel2:23)** *See also* [MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10)

### His response to obedience

**[DEU 11:13–11:14](https://ref.ly/Deut11:13-Deut11:14)** *See also* [LEV 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Lev26:3-Lev26:4)

### His response to prayer

**[JAS 5:18](https://ref.ly/Jas5:18)** *See also* [1KI 8:35–8:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:35-1Kgs8:36), [1KI 18:36–18:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:36-1Kgs18:46); [ZEC 10:1](https://ref.ly/Zech10:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 11:17; Deuteronomy 28:22-24; 2 Samuel 1:21; 1 Kings 17:1; Psalms 107:33-34; Isaiah 5:6; Isaiah 5:13; Jeremiah 2:2-3; Jeremiah 14:1-6; Jeremiah 50:38; Ezekiel 22:23-24; Amos 4:7; Haggai 1:11; James 5:17; 1 Kings 17:7; Habakkuk 3:17-18; Deuteronomy 8:15; Isaiah 41:17; Isaiah 48:20-21; Jeremiah 2:5-6; Hosea 13:5; Acts 11:28-29; Genesis 41:56-57; 1 Kings 17:9; 1 Kings 18:1-4; Acts 14:17; Genesis 27:28; Job 5:10; Job 38:25-28; Psalms 65:9-10; Psalms 147:8; Jeremiah 5:24; Jeremiah 14:22; Matthew 5:45; Joel 2:12; Joel 2:23; Malachi 3:10; Deuteronomy 11:13-14; Leviticus 26:3-4; James 5:18; 1 Kings 8:35-36; 1 Kings 18:36-46; Zechariah 10:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1330: God, the provider (ID: `1330@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4293: water (ID: `4293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4819: dryness (ID: `4819@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4822: famine (ID: `4822@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4844: rain (ID: `4844@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5569: suffering, hardship (ID: `5569@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8358: weakness, physical (ID: `8358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4817: drought, spiritual (id: 4817)

Scripture indicates that believers, both as individuals and communities, may go through periods in which they fail to experience the refreshing and life\-giving presence of God. One cause of such spiritual thirst is unbelief. Scripture also indicates ways in which this thirst may be relieved.

Spiritual drought in unbelievers
--------------------------------

**[PSA 68:6](https://ref.ly/Ps68:6)** *See also* [ISA 29:7–29:8](https://ref.ly/Isa29:7-Isa29:8)

### The remedy for spiritual drought is to be found in Jesus Christ

**[JHN 7:37–7:39](https://ref.ly/John7:37-John7:39)** *See also* [ISA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Isa45:8), [ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1); [JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14); [TIT 3:5–3:7](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5-Titus3:7); [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

The experience of spiritual drought in believers
------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 63:1](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1)** *See also* [PSA 143:6](https://ref.ly/Ps143:6); [EZK 19:10–19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10-Ezek19:14)

The cause of spiritual drought in believers
-------------------------------------------

### Unconfessed sin

**[PSA 32:3–32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:4)** *See also* [PSA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Ps66:18); [ISA 1:28–1:30](https://ref.ly/Isa1:28-Isa1:30)

### Spiritual depression

**[PSA 42:1–42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:1-Ps42:2)**

### Self\-reliance

**[JER 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:13)** *See also* [JER 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5-Jer17:6)

### God's judgment

**[AMO 8:11](https://ref.ly/Amos8:11)**

The remedy for spiritual drought in the lives of believers
----------------------------------------------------------

### Through confession

**[1JN 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:8-1John1:9)** *See also* [PSA 32:5](https://ref.ly/Ps32:5), [PSA 51:12](https://ref.ly/Ps51:12); [1JN 3:19–3:22](https://ref.ly/1John3:19-1John3:22)

### Through Scripture

**[PSA 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2-Ps1:3)** *See also* [DEU 32:1–32:2](https://ref.ly/Deut32:1-Deut32:2); [ISA 55:10–55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10-Isa55:11)

### Through Jesus Christ

**[JHN 6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:35)** *See also* [JHN 7:37–7:39](https://ref.ly/John7:37-John7:39); [JAS 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7-Jas5:8)

### Through the Holy Spirit

**[ISA 44:3](https://ref.ly/Isa44:3)** *See also* [ISA 32:13–32:15](https://ref.ly/Isa32:13-Isa32:15); [ROM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Rom15:13)

### Finally in heaven

**[REV 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16-Rev7:17)**
