## 4819: dryness (id: 4819)

A lack of moisture, often due to lack of rain or a period of drought, leading to vegetation drying up. Dryness is often used as a symbol of hopelessness or of the absence of God's blessing.

The dryness of river beds
-------------------------

### Evaporation and recession of floodwaters

**[GEN 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/Gen8:13-Gen8:14)** *See also* [GEN 8:1–8:11](https://ref.ly/Gen8:1-Gen8:11)

### God's drying up of rivers to allow his people to cross

**[JOS 4:23](https://ref.ly/Josh4:23)** *See also* [EXO 14:10–14:31](https://ref.ly/Exod14:10-Exod14:31); [JOS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Josh2:10), [JOS 3:1–3:17](https://ref.ly/Josh3:1-Josh3:17), [JOS 5:1](https://ref.ly/Josh5:1); [PSA 106:7–106:11](https://ref.ly/Ps106:7-Ps106:11); [ISA 11:15](https://ref.ly/Isa11:15), [ISA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Isa51:10)

### Other instances of rivers drying up

**[1KI 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:7)** *See also* [2KI 19:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:24) parallel [ISA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Isa37:25); [PSA 74:15](https://ref.ly/Ps74:15); [ISA 15:6](https://ref.ly/Isa15:6), [ISA 19:4–19:10](https://ref.ly/Isa19:4-Isa19:10), [ISA 42:15](https://ref.ly/Isa42:15), [ISA 44:24–44:27](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24-Isa44:27), [ISA 50:2](https://ref.ly/Isa50:2); [JER 48:34](https://ref.ly/Jer48:34), [JER 51:36](https://ref.ly/Jer51:36); [EZK 30:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:12); [JOL 1:20](https://ref.ly/Joel1:20); [NAM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Nah1:4)

The dryness of vegetation
-------------------------

**[PSA 37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:2)** *See also* [JOB 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Job8:11-Job8:12), [JOB 8:19](https://ref.ly/Job8:19), [JOB 14:2](https://ref.ly/Job14:2), [JOB 15:30](https://ref.ly/Job15:30); [ISA 34:4](https://ref.ly/Isa34:4) *on the day of the LORD*; [ISA 40:7–40:8](https://ref.ly/Isa40:7-Isa40:8); [JER 8:13](https://ref.ly/Jer8:13), [JER 12:4](https://ref.ly/Jer12:4); [EZK 19:10–19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10-Ezek19:14); [HOS 9:16](https://ref.ly/Hos9:16); [MAT 13:3–13:6](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:6) parallels [MRK 4:2–4:6](https://ref.ly/Mark4:2-Mark4:6), [LUK 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/Luke8:4-Luke8:6) *the parable of the sower*; [MAT 21:19–21:20](https://ref.ly/Matt21:19-Matt21:20) parallel [MRK 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/Mark11:20-Mark11:21) *the fig\-tree withers*

Physical dryness
----------------

**[EZK 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:11)** *See also* [PSA 102:4](https://ref.ly/Ps102:4), [PSA 102:11](https://ref.ly/Ps102:11); [PRO 17:22](https://ref.ly/Prov17:22); [ZEC 11:17](https://ref.ly/Zech11:17)

Dryness indicative of desolation and hopelessness
-------------------------------------------------

**[JOL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Joel1:12)** *See also* [ISA 24:7](https://ref.ly/Isa24:7), [ISA 33:9](https://ref.ly/Isa33:9); [JER 23:10](https://ref.ly/Jer23:10); [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10), [JOL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Joel1:17); [ZEC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Zech9:5), [ZEC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Zech10:11)

Dryness indicating God's withholding of moisture due to sin
-----------------------------------------------------------

[DEU 28:22–28:24](https://ref.ly/Deut28:22-Deut28:24); ; [1KI 8:35–8:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:35-1Kgs8:36)

-----------------------------

Earthquakes remain under God's sovereignty

## 4821: east (id: 4821)

The geographical direction often identified by the position of the rising sun. Many of Israel's invaders came from the east, often giving the term a negative association in OT writings.

East as a point of the compass
------------------------------

**[GEN 2:8](https://ref.ly/Gen2:8)**; **[MAT 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:2)** *See also* [GEN 4:16](https://ref.ly/Gen4:16), [GEN 13:11](https://ref.ly/Gen13:11), [GEN 13:14](https://ref.ly/Gen13:14); [DEU 3:27](https://ref.ly/Deut3:27); [PSA 107:3](https://ref.ly/Ps107:3); [LUK 13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:29); [REV 21:13](https://ref.ly/Rev21:13)

East and west used to demonstrate vast extent
---------------------------------------------

**[GEN 28:14](https://ref.ly/Gen28:14)**; **[ISA 43:5](https://ref.ly/Isa43:5)** *See also* [1CH 12:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:15); [JOB 23:8](https://ref.ly/Job23:8); [PSA 75:6](https://ref.ly/Ps75:6), [PSA 103:12](https://ref.ly/Ps103:12); [ZEC 8:7](https://ref.ly/Zech8:7); [MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11), [MAT 24:27](https://ref.ly/Matt24:27); [LUK 13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:29)

East identified by the rising sun
---------------------------------

**[NUM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Num2:3)** *See also* [JOS 1:15](https://ref.ly/Josh1:15), [JOS 19:12](https://ref.ly/Josh19:12); [PSA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Ps50:1), [PSA 113:3](https://ref.ly/Ps113:3); [ISA 41:25](https://ref.ly/Isa41:25), [ISA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Isa45:6)

The east wind
-------------

**[JOB 15:2](https://ref.ly/Job15:2)** *The east wind blows straight out of the desert*; **[PSA 48:7](https://ref.ly/Ps48:7)** *See also* [GEN 41:6](https://ref.ly/Gen41:6); [EXO 10:13](https://ref.ly/Exod10:13); [ISA 27:8](https://ref.ly/Isa27:8); [JER 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jer4:11), [JER 18:17](https://ref.ly/Jer18:17); [EZK 17:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:10), [EZK 27:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:26); [HOS 13:15](https://ref.ly/Hos13:15); [JON 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:8)

Military threat from the east
-----------------------------

**[JDG 6:33](https://ref.ly/Judg6:33)** *See also* [JDG 10:8](https://ref.ly/Judg10:8); [1CH 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:10); [ISA 9:12](https://ref.ly/Isa9:12), [ISA 46:11](https://ref.ly/Isa46:11)

## 4822: famine (id: 4822)

A severe long\-term absence of food, leading to disaster for the people affected in this way. Scripture provides examples of such famines, while also indicating the possibility and dangers of spiritual famine arising through disobedience or lack of faith.

* **Associated Articles:** 4823: famine, physical (ID: `4823@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4824: famine, spiritual (ID: `4824@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4823: famine, physical (id: 4823)

A serious shortage of food caused by drought, war or natural disasters. Famine is seen as a sign of God's judgment on Israel.

Examples of famines
-------------------

### In the days of Abraham

**[GEN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10)** *See also* [GEN 26:1](https://ref.ly/Gen26:1)

### In the days of Joseph

[GEN 41:53–41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:53-Gen41:57); ; [ACT 7:11](https://ref.ly/Acts7:11)

### In the days of Ruth

[RUT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:1)

### In the days of David

[2SA 21:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1)

### In the days of Elijah

[1KI 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:1-1Kgs18:5); ; [LUK 4:25](https://ref.ly/Luke4:25)

### In the days of Elisha

[2KI 4:38](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:38), [2KI 6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25)

### In the days of the prophets

[ISA 51:19](https://ref.ly/Isa51:19); ; [JER 44:13](https://ref.ly/Jer44:13); ; [EZK 7:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:15); ; [JOL 1:10–1:12](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10-Joel1:12)

### In the days of Claudius

[ACT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Acts11:28)

### In the last days

**[MAT 24:7](https://ref.ly/Matt24:7)** parallels [MRK 13:8](https://ref.ly/Mark13:8), [LUK 21:11](https://ref.ly/Luke21:11)

Natural causes of famine
------------------------

*See also* [DEU 28:22](https://ref.ly/Deut28:22) *crop disease*[1KI 18:1–18:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:1-1Kgs18:2); [JER 14:1–14:62](https://ref.ly/Jer14:1-Jer14:62)KI [6:24–6:25](https://ref.ly/Jer6:24-Jer6:25), [2KI 25:1–25:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1-2Kgs25:3)[JOL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Joel1:4) *locusts*

Spiritual causes of famine
--------------------------

### The disobedience of God's people

**[DEU 28:15–28:18](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:18)** *See also* [DEU 28:22–28:24](https://ref.ly/Deut28:22-Deut28:24), [DEU 28:38–28:40](https://ref.ly/Deut28:38-Deut28:40), [DEU 28:42](https://ref.ly/Deut28:42), [DEU 28:51](https://ref.ly/Deut28:51), [DEU 28:53](https://ref.ly/Deut28:53), [DEU 32:24](https://ref.ly/Deut32:24); [JDG 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Judg6:1-Judg6:5); [2SA 21:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1), [2SA 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:13) parallel [1CH 21:11–21:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:11-1Chr21:12); [ISA 51:19–51:20](https://ref.ly/Isa51:19-Isa51:20); [JER 5:17](https://ref.ly/Jer5:17), [JER 11:22](https://ref.ly/Jer11:22), [JER 14:11–14:18](https://ref.ly/Jer14:11-Jer14:18), [JER 18:21](https://ref.ly/Jer18:21), [JER 29:17–29:18](https://ref.ly/Jer29:17-Jer29:18), [JER 32:24](https://ref.ly/Jer32:24); [EZK 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:16-Ezek5:17), [EZK 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:11-Ezek6:12), [EZK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:13)

### God's judgment on the ungodly

[GEN 41:28–41:30](https://ref.ly/Gen41:28-Gen41:30); ; [ISA 14:29–14:30](https://ref.ly/Isa14:29-Isa14:30); ; [REV 6:8](https://ref.ly/Rev6:8), [REV 18:8](https://ref.ly/Rev18:8)

The effects of famine
---------------------

### Desperation and death

**[LAM 4:9](https://ref.ly/Lam4:9)** *See also* [GEN 47:13](https://ref.ly/Gen47:13), [GEN 47:20](https://ref.ly/Gen47:20); [DEU 28:53](https://ref.ly/Deut28:53); [2KI 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:3-2Kgs7:4); [NEH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Neh5:3); [JER 52:6](https://ref.ly/Jer52:6); [LUK 15:14–15:16](https://ref.ly/Luke15:14-Luke15:16), [LUK 23:28–23:29](https://ref.ly/Luke23:28-Luke23:29)

### God's people seek him and return to him

**[JDG 6:6](https://ref.ly/Judg6:6)** *See also* [2SA 21:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1); [LUK 15:17–15:18](https://ref.ly/Luke15:17-Luke15:18)

God's provision in famine
-------------------------

### He gives warnings beforehand

[GEN 41:25–41:27](https://ref.ly/Gen41:25-Gen41:27); ; [1KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:1); ; [2KI 8:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:1); ; [ACT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Acts11:28)

### He gives wisdom to men

[GEN 41:33–41:36](https://ref.ly/Gen41:33-Gen41:36), [GEN 42:1–42:2](https://ref.ly/Gen42:1-Gen42:2), [GEN 45:11](https://ref.ly/Gen45:11), [GEN 47:4](https://ref.ly/Gen47:4); ; [PSA 105:16–105:22](https://ref.ly/Ps105:16-Ps105:22); ; [ACT 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/Acts7:10-Acts7:11)

### He provides for his people

**[JOB 5:20](https://ref.ly/Job5:20)** *See also* [GEN 26:1–26:3](https://ref.ly/Gen26:1-Gen26:3); [1KI 17:2–17:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:2-1Kgs17:16) *Sometimes he provides miraculously*; [PSA 33:18–33:19](https://ref.ly/Ps33:18-Ps33:19), [PSA 37:19](https://ref.ly/Ps37:19); [EZK 34:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:29), [EZK 36:29–36:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:29-Ezek36:30)

### He sends deliverance

[2KI 7:1–7:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:1-2Kgs7:9), [2KI 7:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:16); ; [JER 32:36–32:37](https://ref.ly/Jer32:36-Jer32:37)

Responses to famine
-------------------

### Accepting God's chastisements

[JER 27:12–27:13](https://ref.ly/Jer27:12-Jer27:13), [JER 38:2–38:3](https://ref.ly/Jer38:2-Jer38:3), [JER 42:13–42:17](https://ref.ly/Jer42:13-Jer42:17), [JER 44:27](https://ref.ly/Jer44:27)

### Caring for one's family

[GEN 45:11](https://ref.ly/Gen45:11); ; [RUT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:1); ; [2KI 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:1-2Kgs8:2)

### Helping those in need

[ACT 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29-Acts11:30)

### Praying

[1KI 8:35–8:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:35-1Kgs8:40) parallel [2CH 6:26–6:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:26-2Chr6:31)

### Trusting in the Lord

**[ROM 8:35–8:37](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:37)**

## 4824: famine, spiritual (id: 4824)

A state of spiritual hunger arising from a failure to experience the presence and joy of God. Scripture describes such experiences, indicates one ultimate cause to be unbelief and points to ways in which this famine can be relieved.

The experience of spiritual famine
----------------------------------

### God's word is withheld

**[AMO 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Amos8:11-Amos8:12)** *See also* [1SA 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:1); [PSA 74:9](https://ref.ly/Ps74:9); [LAM 2:9](https://ref.ly/Lam2:9); [EZK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:26); [LUK 23:8–23:9](https://ref.ly/Luke23:8-Luke23:9)

### God's Spirit is withheld

**[PSA 51:11](https://ref.ly/Ps51:11)** *See also* [PSA 107:33–107:34](https://ref.ly/Ps107:33-Ps107:34)

Causes of spiritual famine
--------------------------

### Sin

**[ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2)**

### Unbelief

**[ACT 13:46](https://ref.ly/Acts13:46)** *See also* [ACT 28:25–28:28](https://ref.ly/Acts28:25-Acts28:28)

### Worldliness

[ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2)

### False beliefs

[ISA 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19-Isa8:22)

### Self\-satisfaction

**[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)** *It is possible to be well\-fed physically but famished spiritually.*

### Testing God

*See also* [PSA 78:18–78:19](https://ref.ly/Ps78:18-Ps78:19), [PSA 78:29–78:31](https://ref.ly/Ps78:29-Ps78:31)

### God testing his servants

[JOB 23:3](https://ref.ly/Job23:3), [JOB 23:8–23:10](https://ref.ly/Job23:8-Job23:10)

Consequences of spiritual famine
--------------------------------

### Spiritual weakness

**[PSA 119:81](https://ref.ly/Ps119:81)** *See also* [PSA 84:2](https://ref.ly/Ps84:2)

### Loss of hope and joy

**[JOL 1:16](https://ref.ly/Joel1:16)** *See also* [PSA 51:12](https://ref.ly/Ps51:12)

Relief of spiritual famine
--------------------------

### Relief is promised to God's people

**[PSA 107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:9)** *See also* [PSA 85:8–85:13](https://ref.ly/Ps85:8-Ps85:13); [ISA 58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:11), [ISA 65:21](https://ref.ly/Isa65:21); [AMO 9:13–9:15](https://ref.ly/Amos9:13-Amos9:15)

### Relief comes through Jesus Christ

**[JHN 6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:35)** *See also* [ISA 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/Isa9:1-Isa9:3); [LUK 1:53](https://ref.ly/Luke1:53); [JHN 6:32–6:33](https://ref.ly/John6:32-John6:33), [JHN 6:48](https://ref.ly/John6:48), [JHN 6:50](https://ref.ly/John6:50), [JHN 6:58](https://ref.ly/John6:58); [1CO 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:3-1Cor10:4)

### Relief comes by the word

**[ISA 55:10–55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10-Isa55:11)** *See also* [MAT 4:4](https://ref.ly/Matt4:4) parallel [LUK 4:4](https://ref.ly/Luke4:4); [DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3)

### Relief comes by the Spirit

**[ACT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17)** *See also* [JOL 2:28](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28); [PSA 107:35–107:38](https://ref.ly/Ps107:35-Ps107:38); [ISA 35:1–35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:1-Isa35:10), [ISA 44:3](https://ref.ly/Isa44:3)

### Final relief is found in heaven

**[REV 7:16](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16)** *See also* [ISA 49:9–49:10](https://ref.ly/Isa49:9-Isa49:10); [PSA 16:11](https://ref.ly/Ps16:11); [REV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6)

Conditions of relief from spiritual famine
------------------------------------------

### It is offered freely

**[ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1)** *See also* [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

### It is for those who desire it

**[MAT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6)** *See also* [LUK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Luke6:21)

### It is for those who pray

[PSA 107:4–107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:4-Ps107:9); ; [JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16)

### It is for those who repent

[PSA 81:8–81:10](https://ref.ly/Ps81:8-Ps81:10); ; [ISA 64:9–64:10](https://ref.ly/Isa64:9-Isa64:10); ; [HOS 14:1–14:8](https://ref.ly/Hos14:1-Hos14:8); ; [JOL 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Joel2:13-Joel2:14)

### It is for those who have patience

**[JAS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7)** *See also* [PSA 126:4–126:6](https://ref.ly/Ps126:4-Ps126:6); [ECC 11:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:1); [JHN 4:35](https://ref.ly/John4:35); [JAS 5:16–5:18](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16-Jas5:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Amos 8:11-12; 1 Samuel 3:1; Psalms 74:9; Lamentations 2:9; Ezekiel 7:26; Luke 23:8-9; Psalms 51:11; Psalms 107:33-34; Isaiah 59:2; Acts 13:46; Acts 28:25-28; Isaiah 55:2; Isaiah 8:19-22; Revelation 3:17; Psalms 78:18-19; Psalms 78:29-31; Job 23:3; Job 23:8-10; Psalms 119:81; Psalms 84:2; Joel 1:16; Psalms 51:12; Psalms 107:9; Psalms 85:8-13; Isaiah 58:11; Isaiah 65:21; Amos 9:13-15; John 6:35; Isaiah 9:1-3; Luke 1:53; John 6:32-33; John 6:48; John 6:50; John 6:58; 1 Corinthians 10:3-4; Isaiah 55:10-11; Matthew 4:4; Luke 4:4; Deuteronomy 8:3; Acts 2:17; Joel 2:28; Psalms 107:35-38; Isaiah 35:1-10; Isaiah 44:3; Revelation 7:16; Isaiah 49:9-10; Psalms 16:11; Revelation 21:6; Isaiah 55:1; John 7:37; Revelation 22:17; Matthew 5:6; Luke 6:21; Psalms 107:4-9; James 5:16; Psalms 81:8-10; Isaiah 64:9-10; Hosea 14:1-8; Joel 2:13-14; James 5:7; Psalms 126:4-6; Ecclesiastes 11:1; John 4:35; James 5:16-18

## 4826: fire (id: 4826)

Scripture shows fire being used in a variety of ways in this world. Fire is often mentioned as a symbol, denoting God's presence and power, his judgment or his purifying work.

Domestic uses of fire
---------------------

[ISA 50:11](https://ref.ly/Isa50:11); ; [JER 36:22](https://ref.ly/Jer36:22); ; [MAT 5:15](https://ref.ly/Matt5:15); ; [MRK 14:67](https://ref.ly/Mark14:67) parallels [LUK 22:55](https://ref.ly/Luke22:55), [JHN 18:18](https://ref.ly/John18:18); ; [JHN 21:9](https://ref.ly/John21:9); ; [ACT 28:2](https://ref.ly/Acts28:2)

Cremation
---------

**[1SA 31:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:12)** *See also* [JOS 7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25); [AMO 6:10](https://ref.ly/Amos6:10)

Fire as an instrument of punishment
-----------------------------------

**[LEV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Lev20:14)** *See also* [GEN 38:24](https://ref.ly/Gen38:24); [LEV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Lev20:14), [LEV 21:9](https://ref.ly/Lev21:9); [JDG 9:49](https://ref.ly/Judg9:49); [DAN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Dan3:6)

Fire as an instrument of divine judgment
----------------------------------------

### In general terms

**[2PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10)** *See also* [GEN 19:24](https://ref.ly/Gen19:24); [LEV 10:2](https://ref.ly/Lev10:2); [NUM 11:1](https://ref.ly/Num11:1), [NUM 16:35](https://ref.ly/Num16:35); [2TH 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:7-2Thess1:8)

### Eternal fire

**[REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10)** *See also* [ISA 33:14](https://ref.ly/Isa33:14), [ISA 66:24](https://ref.ly/Isa66:24); [MAT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:12) parallel [LUK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Luke3:17); [MAT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Matt18:8) parallel [MRK 9:43](https://ref.ly/Mark9:43); [MAT 25:41](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41); [REV 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:10-Rev14:11)

Fire used in worship
--------------------

**[NUM 28:2](https://ref.ly/Num28:2)** *See also* [LEV 24:7](https://ref.ly/Lev24:7); [1SA 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:9); [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11); [EZR 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:3)

Prayers answered by fire
------------------------

**[1CH 21:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:26)** *See also* [1KI 18:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:38); [2KI 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:10); [2CH 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:1)

Symbolic significance of fire
-----------------------------

### God's presence and power

**[EXO 3:2](https://ref.ly/Exod3:2)**; **[ACT 2:3](https://ref.ly/Acts2:3)** *See also* [EXO 13:21](https://ref.ly/Exod13:21), [EXO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Exod19:18); [LEV 9:24](https://ref.ly/Lev9:24); [DEU 4:36](https://ref.ly/Deut4:36); [JDG 6:21](https://ref.ly/Judg6:21), [JDG 13:20](https://ref.ly/Judg13:20); [2KI 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:11); [PSA 50:3](https://ref.ly/Ps50:3), [PSA 97:3](https://ref.ly/Ps97:3); [JER 23:29](https://ref.ly/Jer23:29)

### Judgment

**[MAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mal4:1)** *See also* [DEU 4:24](https://ref.ly/Deut4:24); [ISA 66:15–66:16](https://ref.ly/Isa66:15-Isa66:16); [JER 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jer5:14), [JER 48:45](https://ref.ly/Jer48:45); [EZK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:20); [AMO 1:4](https://ref.ly/Amos1:4); [1CO 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:15)

### Purification or refinement

**[1PE 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:7)** *See also* [NUM 31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:23); [ISA 48:10](https://ref.ly/Isa48:10); [ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9); [MAL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2); [1CO 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:13); [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)

### Zeal

**[JHN 2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:17)** *See also* [PSA 69:9](https://ref.ly/Ps69:9); [JER 20:9](https://ref.ly/Jer20:9); [LUK 24:32](https://ref.ly/Luke24:32); [2TI 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:6)

----------------------------

Frost will be absent on the day of the LORD
-------------------------------------------

## 4833: light (id: 4833)

The brightness that enables sight in the darkness. Scripture often uses light as a symbol of the saving presence of God in a fallen world, with darkness being used as a symbol of sin or the absence of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 4834: light, natural (ID: `4834@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4835: light, spiritual (ID: `4835@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4836: light, and people of God (ID: `4836@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4834: light, natural (id: 4834)

Scripture stresses that every source of light (sun, moon and stars) is created by God, and subject to him.

God is the source of natural light
----------------------------------

**[GEN 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:3-Gen1:5)** *See also* [GEN 1:14–1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14-Gen1:18); [2SA 22:13–22:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:13-2Sam22:15); [JOB 41:18–41:21](https://ref.ly/Job41:18-Job41:21); [PSA 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3-Ps8:4), [PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16), [PSA 97:4](https://ref.ly/Ps97:4), [PSA 136:7–136:9](https://ref.ly/Ps136:7-Ps136:9); [ISA 45:7](https://ref.ly/Isa45:7); [JER 31:35](https://ref.ly/Jer31:35); [JAS 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)

God controls natural light
--------------------------

**[PSA 104:19](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19)** *See also* [JOB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Job9:7), [JOB 36:30–36:32](https://ref.ly/Job36:30-Job36:32); [PSA 121:5–121:6](https://ref.ly/Ps121:5-Ps121:6); [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45); [2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:6)

--------------------------------

### The pillars of cloud and fire

### Other miracles

## 4835: light, spiritual (id: 4835)

Light is often used in Scripture as a symbol of the presence of God in the world. It is often especially associated with the word of God, the salvation brought about by God, or the person of Jesus Christ.

Light as a symbol of God
------------------------

### God's purity

**[1JN 1:5](https://ref.ly/1John1:5)** *See also* [JOB 25:4–25:6](https://ref.ly/Job25:4-Job25:6); [ISA 5:20](https://ref.ly/Isa5:20); [1JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/1John1:7)

### God's glory

**[REV 21:23](https://ref.ly/Rev21:23)** *See also* [PSA 76:4](https://ref.ly/Ps76:4), [PSA 84:11](https://ref.ly/Ps84:11); [ISA 60:19–60:20](https://ref.ly/Isa60:19-Isa60:20); [HAB 3:4](https://ref.ly/Hab3:4); [1TI 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15-1Tim6:16)

### God's knowledge

[PSA 90:8](https://ref.ly/Ps90:8), [PSA 139:11–139:12](https://ref.ly/Ps139:11-Ps139:12); ; [DAN 2:22](https://ref.ly/Dan2:22); ; [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)

### God's unchangeability

**[JAS 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)**

### God's vengeance

*See also* [PSA 94:1](https://ref.ly/Ps94:1); [ISA 10:17](https://ref.ly/Isa10:17)

### Satan as an imitator of light

**[2CO 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:14)**

----------------------------------------------------

**[EZR 9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:8)** *See also* [NUM 6:25](https://ref.ly/Num6:25); [JOB 3:20](https://ref.ly/Job3:20), [JOB 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/Job29:2-Job29:3); [PSA 4:6](https://ref.ly/Ps4:6), [PSA 31:16](https://ref.ly/Ps31:16), [PSA 44:3](https://ref.ly/Ps44:3), [PSA 67:1](https://ref.ly/Ps67:1), [PSA 80:3](https://ref.ly/Ps80:3), [PSA 80:7](https://ref.ly/Ps80:7); [MIC 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Mic7:8-Mic7:9); [REV 22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:5)

-------------------------------

------------------------------

---------------------------------

## 4836: light, and people of God (id: 4836)

Scripture often uses light as a symbol of the people of God, and especially the manner in which believers are able and required to reflect the glory of God in a dark world of sin.

The people of God as the light of the world
-------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:14–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:14-Matt5:16)** *See also* [PSA 37:5–37:6](https://ref.ly/Ps37:5-Ps37:6); [PRO 4:18](https://ref.ly/Prov4:18), [PRO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Prov13:9); [ISA 58:8–58:10](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8-Isa58:10), [ISA 60:1–60:5](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1-Isa60:5), [ISA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Isa62:1); [DAN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Dan12:3); [LUK 2:32](https://ref.ly/Luke2:32); [ACT 13:47](https://ref.ly/Acts13:47)

Examples of believers acting as light to the world
--------------------------------------------------

Moses on Mount Sinai: [EXO 34:29–34:30](https://ref.ly/Exod34:29-Exod34:30), [EXO 34:35](https://ref.ly/Exod34:35); [2SA 23:3–23:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:3-2Sam23:4) *the righteous ruler, fully realised in Jesus Christ*; ; [JOB 29:21–29:25](https://ref.ly/Job29:21-Job29:25) *Job as a counsellor*; ; [JHN 5:35](https://ref.ly/John5:35) *John the Baptist*; ; [ACT 6:15](https://ref.ly/Acts6:15) *Stephen*; ; [PHP 2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:15) *the Philippian church*

God is the source of the light of his people
--------------------------------------------

### God and his law as the source of light

**[PSA 27:1](https://ref.ly/Ps27:1), [PSA 118:27](https://ref.ly/Ps118:27)** *See also* [JDG 5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:31); [JOB 11:13–11:17](https://ref.ly/Job11:13-Job11:17), [JOB 22:27–22:28](https://ref.ly/Job22:27-Job22:28); [PSA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Ps19:8), [PSA 34:5](https://ref.ly/Ps34:5), [PSA 56:13](https://ref.ly/Ps56:13), [PSA 97:11](https://ref.ly/Ps97:11); [PRO 6:23](https://ref.ly/Prov6:23); [ECC 8:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:1); [ISA 60:19–60:20](https://ref.ly/Isa60:19-Isa60:20); [JHN 1:5](https://ref.ly/John1:5)

### The gospel of forgiveness as a source of light

**[ACT 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:17-Acts26:18)** *See also* [JOB 33:28–33:30](https://ref.ly/Job33:28-Job33:30); [PSA 112:4](https://ref.ly/Ps112:4); [ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2), [ISA 29:18](https://ref.ly/Isa29:18); [MAT 4:16](https://ref.ly/Matt4:16); [JHN 3:19–3:21](https://ref.ly/John3:19-John3:21); [EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eph1:18), [EPH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:27); [HEB 6:4](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4), [HEB 10:32](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32)

The people of God are called to live in his light
-------------------------------------------------

## 4841: narrowness (id: 4841)

A state in which something is confined and restricted, lacking in breadth. It usually has pejorative implications, but Jesus Christ described the way of salvation in terms of narrowness.

Narrowness equated with no escape
---------------------------------

**[NUM 22:24–22:26](https://ref.ly/Num22:24-Num22:26)** *See also* [PRO 23:26–23:28](https://ref.ly/Prov23:26-Prov23:28)

---------------

--------------------------------------------

## 4842: north (id: 4842)

The geographical direction opposite to south, often used to demonstrate the vast extent between the two.

-------------------------------

**[JOB 37:22](https://ref.ly/Job37:22)** *See also* [GEN 13:14](https://ref.ly/Gen13:14); [DEU 2:3](https://ref.ly/Deut2:3); [JOS 18:5](https://ref.ly/Josh18:5); [ECC 11:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:3); [ISA 14:31](https://ref.ly/Isa14:31); [JER 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jer1:13-Jer1:15), [JER 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jer3:18) *Although geographically situated to the east of Israel, invaders from Mesopotamia had to enter the country from the north, following the land route around the desert areas*; [JER 46:6](https://ref.ly/Jer46:6); [EZK 8:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:5), [EZK 26:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:7); [AMO 8:12](https://ref.ly/Amos8:12); [ZEP 2:13](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:13)

-----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 28:14](https://ref.ly/Gen28:14)** *See also* [JOB 23:9](https://ref.ly/Job23:9); [ISA 43:6](https://ref.ly/Isa43:6); [EZK 20:47](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:47), [EZK 21:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:4); [DAN 8:4](https://ref.ly/Dan8:4); [ZEC 6:6](https://ref.ly/Zech6:6)

## 4843: plague (id: 4843)

Affliction with disease, destruction or environmental disaster as an expression of divine judgment, usually resulting in death. The purpose may be as a punishment or to encourage repentance.

The nature of plague
--------------------

**[EZK 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:11-Ezek6:12)** *See also* [2CH 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:9); [JER 14:12](https://ref.ly/Jer14:12), [JER 27:8](https://ref.ly/Jer27:8); [EZK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:21); [REV 6:8](https://ref.ly/Rev6:8)

----------------------------

Rain as a symbol of spiritual truth

## 4845: rainbow (id: 4845)

An arch of colours in the sky, formed by the sun's rays passing through raindrops. It was the sign of God's covenant with Noah, established in the aftermath of the great flood.

The rainbow as a sign of God's covenant with Noah
-------------------------------------------------

[GEN 9:12–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:12-Gen9:17)

The rainbow as a sign of God's glory
------------------------------------

[EZK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:28); ; [REV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Rev4:3), [REV 10:1](https://ref.ly/Rev10:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 9:12-17; Ezekiel 1:28; Revelation 4:3; Revelation 10:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1045: God, glory of (ID: `1045@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1347: covenant, God's with Noah (ID: `1347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4807: colours (ID: `4807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4844: rain (ID: `4844@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5106: Noah (ID: `5106@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7227: flood, the (ID: `7227@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4846: shadow (id: 4846)

Primarily a symbol of protection, of the brevity of life and of the experience of death, in contrast to God's character and work.

Shadow as a general symbol of protection
----------------------------------------

**[ISA 30:2–30:3](https://ref.ly/Isa30:2-Isa30:3)** *See also* [JDG 9:15](https://ref.ly/Judg9:15); [JER 48:45](https://ref.ly/Jer48:45); [HOS 14:7](https://ref.ly/Hos14:7); [JON 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:5-Jonah4:6); [MRK 4:32](https://ref.ly/Mark4:32)

### A symbol of God's protection

**[PSA 91:1](https://ref.ly/Ps91:1)** *See also* [PSA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8), [PSA 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ps36:7), [PSA 57:1](https://ref.ly/Ps57:1), [PSA 63:7](https://ref.ly/Ps63:7), [PSA 121:5](https://ref.ly/Ps121:5); [ISA 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Isa4:5-Isa4:6), [ISA 25:4–25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4-Isa25:5), [ISA 49:2](https://ref.ly/Isa49:2), [ISA 51:16](https://ref.ly/Isa51:16)

### A symbol of the protection given by God's chosen one

[ISA 32:2](https://ref.ly/Isa32:2); ; [LAM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lam4:20); ; [EZK 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:22-Ezek17:23)

--------------------------

### Of the brevity of life

**[PSA 144:4](https://ref.ly/Ps144:4)** *See also* [1CH 29:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:15); [JOB 8:9](https://ref.ly/Job8:9), [JOB 14:2](https://ref.ly/Job14:2); [PSA 102:11](https://ref.ly/Ps102:11), [PSA 109:23](https://ref.ly/Ps109:23); [ECC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:12), [ECC 8:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:13)

### Of life's sadness and despair

**[ISA 59:9](https://ref.ly/Isa59:9)** *See also* [JOB 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Job3:3-Job3:5), [JOB 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Job7:2-Job7:3), [JOB 16:16](https://ref.ly/Job16:16), [JOB 17:7](https://ref.ly/Job17:7)

---------------------------------------------

-------------------------

**[JAS 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)**

**[JOB 12:22](https://ref.ly/Job12:22)** *See also* [JOB 34:22](https://ref.ly/Job34:22); [PSA 139:11–139:12](https://ref.ly/Ps139:11-Ps139:12); [DAN 2:22](https://ref.ly/Dan2:22)

**[MAT 4:16](https://ref.ly/Matt4:16)** *See also* [ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2), [ISA 60:1–60:2](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1-Isa60:2); [LUK 1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:79)

## 4853: weather (id: 4853)

Patterns of climatic conditions, such as wind and rain, which were of major importance to the rural economies of the Near East. Scripture stresses the sovereignty of God over the weather and sees it as a possible means of judgment or reward.

* **Associated Articles:** 4854: weather, God's sovereignty over (ID: `4854@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4855: weather, as God's judgment (ID: `4855@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4854: weather, God's sovereignty over (id: 4854)

The weather is under the control of God, who alone is able to direct it.

The weather is under God's control
----------------------------------

**[JOB 28:25–28:27](https://ref.ly/Job28:25-Job28:27)**; **[MAT 8:26–8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:26-Matt8:27)** parallels [MRK 4:39–4:41](https://ref.ly/Mark4:39-Mark4:41), [LUK 8:24–8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:24-Luke8:25) *See also* [JOB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Job5:10), [JOB 26:8–26:9](https://ref.ly/Job26:8-Job26:9), [JOB 26:13](https://ref.ly/Job26:13), [JOB 37:1–37:18](https://ref.ly/Job37:1-Job37:18); [PSA 135:7](https://ref.ly/Ps135:7), [PSA 147:8](https://ref.ly/Ps147:8), [PSA 147:16–147:18](https://ref.ly/Ps147:16-Ps147:18), [PSA 148:8](https://ref.ly/Ps148:8); [JER 10:13](https://ref.ly/Jer10:13)

--------------------------------------------------

## 4855: weather, as God's judgment (id: 4855)

The weather can be seen as an expression of God's judgment and grace, as well as providing insights concerning human nature.

----------------------------------------------

### Judgment in battle

**[JOS 10:11](https://ref.ly/Josh10:11)** *See also* [2SA 22:10–22:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:10-2Sam22:15); [ISA 30:30](https://ref.ly/Isa30:30); [EZK 38:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:22)

## 4860: wind (id: 4860)

In Hebrew the same word can denote “breath”, “wind” or “spirit”. Scripture describes the powerful effect of wind, and uses the image figuratively to speak of God's power and presence in the world.

The origins of the wind
-----------------------

**[EXO 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Exod14:21-Exod14:22)**; **[PSA 78:26](https://ref.ly/Ps78:26)**; **[AMO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Amos4:13)** *See also* [GEN 8:1](https://ref.ly/Gen8:1) *A wind caused the flood waters to recede*; [EXO 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/Exod10:13-Exod10:14) *An east wind brought the plague of locusts on Egypt*; [EXO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Exod10:19); [NUM 11:31](https://ref.ly/Num11:31) *A wind brought quail to feed the Israelites*; [JOB 28:25](https://ref.ly/Job28:25); [PSA 107:25](https://ref.ly/Ps107:25), [PSA 135:7](https://ref.ly/Ps135:7), [PSA 148:7–148:8](https://ref.ly/Ps148:7-Ps148:8); [ISA 11:15](https://ref.ly/Isa11:15); [JER 10:13](https://ref.ly/Jer10:13); [EZK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:13); [JON 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:4), [JON 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:8)

The effects of the wind
-----------------------

**[1KI 19:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:11)**; **[EZK 27:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:26)**; **[ACT 27:13–27:15](https://ref.ly/Acts27:13-Acts27:15)** *See also* [GEN 41:5–41:6](https://ref.ly/Gen41:5-Gen41:6); [JOB 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Job1:18-Job1:19), [JOB 37:9](https://ref.ly/Job37:9), [JOB 37:17–37:18](https://ref.ly/Job37:17-Job37:18); [DAN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Dan7:2); [MAT 14:24](https://ref.ly/Matt14:24); [LUK 12:55](https://ref.ly/Luke12:55); [JHN 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/John6:16-John6:18); [JAS 3:4](https://ref.ly/Jas3:4)

Wind used figuratively
----------------------

### As an image of the Holy Spirit

**[JHN 3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:8)** *The Greek word for “Spirit” is the same as that for “wind”, allowing Jesus Christ to play on the double meaning of the word.*

### As an image of spiritual instability

**[JAS 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jas1:6)**

### As an image of destruction

**[PSA 103:15–103:16](https://ref.ly/Ps103:15-Ps103:16)**; **[MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27)** *See also* [JOB 21:17–21:18](https://ref.ly/Job21:17-Job21:18); [PSA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Ps1:4), [PSA 48:7](https://ref.ly/Ps48:7); [ISA 17:13](https://ref.ly/Isa17:13), [ISA 32:2](https://ref.ly/Isa32:2); [JER 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Jer4:11-Jer4:12), [JER 22:22](https://ref.ly/Jer22:22); [EZK 19:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:12); [DAN 2:34–2:35](https://ref.ly/Dan2:34-Dan2:35); [HOS 8:7](https://ref.ly/Hos8:7); [AMO 1:14](https://ref.ly/Amos1:14); [HAB 1:9](https://ref.ly/Hab1:9); [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14); [JUD 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12); [REV 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:13)

### As an image of something insubstantial

**[ECC 1:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:14)** *See also* [JOB 6:26](https://ref.ly/Job6:26); [PRO 11:29](https://ref.ly/Prov11:29), [PRO 27:15–27:16](https://ref.ly/Prov27:15-Prov27:16); [ISA 26:18](https://ref.ly/Isa26:18); [JER 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jer5:13); [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1)

## 4903: time (id: 4903)

A creation of God which marks the duration of life and which is measured by changes in the created order. The flow of time is directed by God who appoints particular “times” within his unfolding purposes. Because human life is brief, time should be used properly, making the most of every opportunity.

Time as a creation of God
-------------------------

### Time began at creation

**[GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5)** *See also* [1CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:7)

### Time is marked by change in the created order

**[GEN 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14)** *The main divisions of time were based upon the phases of the sun and moon.* *See also* [PSA 104:19](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19); [JER 33:20](https://ref.ly/Jer33:20)

### The timelessness of God

**[PSA 90:2](https://ref.ly/Ps90:2)** *See also* [GEN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1); [PSA 93:2](https://ref.ly/Ps93:2); [PRO 8:22–8:26](https://ref.ly/Prov8:22-Prov8:26); [HAB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Hab1:12); [JHN 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/John1:1-John1:2)

### God's perception of time

**[2PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:8)** *See also* [PSA 90:4](https://ref.ly/Ps90:4)

Measurement of time
-------------------

### Divisions of time

[GEN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Gen1:8) *A day was marked by the regular cycle of evening and morning*; ; [NUM 28:14](https://ref.ly/Num28:14) *Months were determined by the regular phases of the moon*; ; [1KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:7) *The year consisted of twelve lunar months*; ; [JHN 11:9](https://ref.ly/John11:9) *An hour was one twelfth of the period of daylight.*

### Indefinite periods of time

[1SA 1:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:22); ; [JER 32:14](https://ref.ly/Jer32:14); ; [LUK 20:9](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9); ; [ACT 14:28](https://ref.ly/Acts14:28), [ACT 18:23](https://ref.ly/Acts18:23)

Recurring times and seasons
---------------------------

### God's seasonal provision

**[DEU 11:14](https://ref.ly/Deut11:14)** *See also* [LEV 26:4](https://ref.ly/Lev26:4); [DEU 28:12](https://ref.ly/Deut28:12); [PSA 145:15–145:16](https://ref.ly/Ps145:15-Ps145:16); [JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24); [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)

### Appointed times for festivals

**[LEV 23:4](https://ref.ly/Lev23:4)** *See also* [EXO 13:10](https://ref.ly/Exod13:10), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15) parallel [EXO 34:18](https://ref.ly/Exod34:18); [NUM 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Num9:2-Num9:3)

### A right time for every activity

**[ECC 3:1–3:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:1-Eccl3:8)** *See also* [ECC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:11), [ECC 8:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:6); [JER 8:7](https://ref.ly/Jer8:7)

### Times of human life in God's hands

**[PSA 31:15](https://ref.ly/Ps31:15), [PSA 139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:16)** *See also* [JOB 14:5](https://ref.ly/Job14:5); [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21); [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)

Appointed times within God's unfolding purpose
----------------------------------------------

### The time of fulfilment of promise

The time of Isaac's birth: [GEN 21:2](https://ref.ly/Gen21:2); ; [ROM 9:9](https://ref.ly/Rom9:9); [LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20) *the time of John the Baptist's birth*; ; [ACT 7:17](https://ref.ly/Acts7:17) *the time of Israel's possession of the land*

### The time of Israel's deliverance

**[PSA 102:13](https://ref.ly/Ps102:13)** *See also* [HAB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3), [HAB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Hab3:16)

### The time of Jesus Christ's coming

**[GAL 4:2–4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:2-Gal4:4)** *See also* [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [LUK 19:44](https://ref.ly/Luke19:44); [1TI 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:6); [TIT 1:3](https://ref.ly/Titus1:3)

### The time of Jesus Christ's death

**[JHN 12:23](https://ref.ly/John12:23)** *See also* [MAT 26:18](https://ref.ly/Matt26:18); [JHN 2:4](https://ref.ly/John2:4), [JHN 8:20](https://ref.ly/John8:20), [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/John13:1); [ROM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6); [1PE 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:11)

### The time of Jesus Christ's return

**[1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)** *See also* [MAT 24:36](https://ref.ly/Matt24:36); [EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10); [HEB 9:28](https://ref.ly/Heb9:28); [1TI 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15)

### The time of God's judgment

[PSA 75:2](https://ref.ly/Ps75:2); ; [EZK 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:7-Ezek7:8), [EZK 24:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:14); ; [ZEP 2:2](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:2); ; [MAT 8:29](https://ref.ly/Matt8:29); ; [REV 11:18](https://ref.ly/Rev11:18)

### The appointed time of the end

[DAN 8:19](https://ref.ly/Dan8:19), [DAN 11:27](https://ref.ly/Dan11:27), [DAN 11:29](https://ref.ly/Dan11:29); ; [MRK 13:32](https://ref.ly/Mark13:32); ; [ACT 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Acts1:6-Acts1:7)

The time between Jesus Christ's first and second coming
-------------------------------------------------------

### The time of salvation

**[2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2)** *The time of salvation lies between the two comings of Jesus Christ.* *See also* [ISA 49:8](https://ref.ly/Isa49:8); [HEB 11:13](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13)

### The end times

[ACT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17); ; [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2); ; [1PE 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:20); ; [1JN 2:18](https://ref.ly/1John2:18)

Proper use of time
------------------

### Recognising the brevity of human life

**[PSA 90:12](https://ref.ly/Ps90:12)** *See also* [PSA 39:4–39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:4-Ps39:6); [PRO 27:1](https://ref.ly/Prov27:1); [JAS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Jas4:14)

### Seeking God

**[ISA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6)** *See also* [PSA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Ps32:6), [PSA 69:13](https://ref.ly/Ps69:13), [PSA 95:7–95:8](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:8); [ECC 12:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:1), [ECC 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:6-Eccl12:7)

### Looking to eternal realities

**[1CO 7:29–7:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:29-1Cor7:31)** *See also* [PSA 10:6](https://ref.ly/Ps10:6); [ISA 56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:12); [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21); [ROM 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:11-Rom13:12); [JAS 5:7–5:9](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7-Jas5:9)

### Making the most of opportunities

**[COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5)** *See also* [GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10); [EPH 5:16](https://ref.ly/Eph5:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:5; 1 Corinthians 2:7; Genesis 1:14; Psalms 104:19; Jeremiah 33:20; Psalms 90:2; Genesis 1:1; Psalms 93:2; Proverbs 8:22-26; Habakkuk 1:12; John 1:1-2; 2 Peter 3:8; Psalms 90:4; Genesis 1:8; Numbers 28:14; 1 Kings 4:7; John 11:9; 1 Samuel 1:22; Jeremiah 32:14; Luke 20:9; Acts 14:28; Acts 18:23; Deuteronomy 11:14; Leviticus 26:4; Deuteronomy 28:12; Psalms 145:15-16; Jeremiah 5:24; Acts 14:17; Leviticus 23:4; Exodus 13:10; Exodus 23:15; Exodus 34:18; Numbers 9:2-3; Ecclesiastes 3:1-8; Ecclesiastes 3:11; Ecclesiastes 8:6; Jeremiah 8:7; Psalms 31:15; Psalms 139:16; Job 14:5; Daniel 2:21; Acts 17:26; Genesis 21:2; Romans 9:9; Luke 1:20; Acts 7:17; Psalms 102:13; Habakkuk 2:3; Habakkuk 3:16; Galatians 4:2-4; Mark 1:15; Luke 19:44; 1 Timothy 2:6; Titus 1:3; John 12:23; Matthew 26:18; John 2:4; John 8:20; John 13:1; Romans 5:6; 1 Peter 1:11; 1 Corinthians 4:5; Matthew 24:36; Ephesians 1:10; Hebrews 9:28; 1 Timothy 6:15; Psalms 75:2; Ezekiel 7:7-8; Ezekiel 24:14; Zephaniah 2:2; Matthew 8:29; Revelation 11:18; Daniel 8:19; Daniel 11:27; Daniel 11:29; Mark 13:32; Acts 1:6-7; 2 Corinthians 6:2; Isaiah 49:8; Hebrews 11:13; Acts 2:17; Hebrews 1:2; 1 Peter 1:20; 1 John 2:18; Psalms 90:12; Psalms 39:4-6; Proverbs 27:1; James 4:14; Isaiah 55:6; Psalms 32:6; Psalms 69:13; Psalms 95:7-8; Ecclesiastes 12:1; Ecclesiastes 12:6-7; 1 Corinthians 7:29-31; Psalms 10:6; Isaiah 56:12; Luke 12:16-21; Romans 13:11-12; James 5:7-9; Colossians 4:5; Galatians 6:10; Ephesians 5:16

## 4906: abolition (id: 4906)

The final ending and elimination of practices or states of affairs. Scripture looks forward to God's final abolition of the present order, through the inauguration of the new Jerusalem.

Things to be abolished in the kingdom of God
--------------------------------------------

### War and destruction

**[HOS 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hos2:18)** *See also* [PSA 46:9–46:10](https://ref.ly/Ps46:9-Ps46:10); [ISA 9:5](https://ref.ly/Isa9:5); [ZEC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Zech9:10)

### Illness and suffering

**[EXO 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Exod23:25-Exod23:26)** *See also* [EXO 15:26](https://ref.ly/Exod15:26); [DEU 7:15](https://ref.ly/Deut7:15); [ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4); [MAT 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Matt8:16-Matt8:17) parallels [MRK 1:32–1:34](https://ref.ly/Mark1:32-Mark1:34), [LUK 4:40–4:41](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40-Luke4:41)

### Sin

**[ROM 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rom6:6)** *See also* [EZK 36:25–36:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:25-Ezek36:26) *God speaks here of his abolition of sinful, human hard\-heartedness*; [ROM 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3-Rom8:4); [HEB 9:26](https://ref.ly/Heb9:26)

### Death and mourning

**[1CO 15:24–15:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24-1Cor15:26)**; **[REV 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:4)** *See also* [ISA 25:7–25:8](https://ref.ly/Isa25:7-Isa25:8), [ISA 65:17–65:20](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17-Isa65:20); [1CO 15:54–15:55](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:54-1Cor15:55); [2TI 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:10); [REV 20:13–20:14](https://ref.ly/Rev20:13-Rev20:14)

The abolition of idolatry in Israel
-----------------------------------

### Israel was commanded to abolish Canaanite worship

**[EXO 23:24](https://ref.ly/Exod23:24)** *See also* [EXO 34:13](https://ref.ly/Exod34:13); [NUM 33:50–33:52](https://ref.ly/Num33:50-Num33:52); [DEU 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2-Deut12:3)

### The abolition of mediums and spiritists

[DEU 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:9-Deut18:12); ; [1SA 28:3–28:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:3-1Sam28:9); ; [2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24)

### In Asa's reign

[1KI 15:11–15:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:11-1Kgs15:13) parallel [2CH 14:2–14:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:2-2Chr14:3)

### In Hezekiah's reign

[2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4) parallel [2CH 31:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:1)

### In Josiah's reign

[2KI 23:4–23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:4-2Kgs23:20) parallels [2CH 34:3–34:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:3-2Chr34:7), [2CH 34:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:33)

The abolition of the temple
---------------------------

### Sacrifice in the temple abolished

**[DAN 11:31](https://ref.ly/Dan11:31)** *Antiochus Epiphanes abolished the daily sacrifice in the temple in 168 B.C., thereby attacking the symbolic centre of Israel's life.* *See also* [DAN 12:11](https://ref.ly/Dan12:11)

### Jesus Christ was accused of seeking to abolish the temple

**[MAT 26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:61)** parallel [MRK 14:58](https://ref.ly/Mark14:58) *See also* [MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13) parallels [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46) *Jesus Christ's action may have been understood as a symbolic abolition of a defiled sacrificial system*; [MAT 27:39–27:40](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39-Matt27:40) parallel [MRK 15:29–15:30](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29-Mark15:30); [ACT 6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:14)

### The final abolition of the temple in the new Jerusalem

**[REV 21:22](https://ref.ly/Rev21:22)**

Abolition and the law
---------------------

### The law has not been abolished

**[MAT 5:17–5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17-Matt5:19)** *See also* [ROM 3:31](https://ref.ly/Rom3:31)

### The law has been abolished as an instrument of condemnation

**[COL 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Col2:13-Col2:14)** *See also* [2CO 3:6–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:6-2Cor3:17); [EPH 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Eph2:14-Eph2:16); [HEB 10:1–10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1-Heb10:10)

### The law does not abolish God's promises

**[GAL 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Gal3:17-Gal3:18)** *God did not abolish his promise of righteousness to Abraham when he gave the law, because the promise was not conditional upon keeping the law but is received by faith.* *See also* [ROM 4:14](https://ref.ly/Rom4:14)

### The offence of the cross is abolished by accepting circumcision

**[GAL 5:11](https://ref.ly/Gal5:11)** *Jews found offensive the Christians' claim that salvation did not come through keeping the law, but instead through faith in a crucified Messiah, whom the law had cursed. Paul regarded “the offence of the cross” as central to the gospel, and refused to make salvation conditional upon circumcision and obedience to the law.* *See also* [1CO 1:22–1:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22-1Cor1:24), [1CO 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:2); [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13), [GAL 5:2](https://ref.ly/Gal5:2), [GAL 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Gal6:14-Gal6:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Hosea 2:18; Psalms 46:9-10; Isaiah 9:5; Zechariah 9:10; Exodus 23:25-26; Exodus 15:26; Deuteronomy 7:15; Isaiah 53:4; Matthew 8:16-17; Mark 1:32-34; Luke 4:40-41; Romans 6:6; Ezekiel 36:25-26; Romans 8:3-4; Hebrews 9:26; 1 Corinthians 15:24-26; Revelation 21:1-4; Isaiah 25:7-8; Isaiah 65:17-20; 1 Corinthians 15:54-55; 2 Timothy 1:10; Revelation 20:13-14; Exodus 23:24; Exodus 34:13; Numbers 33:50-52; Deuteronomy 12:2-3; Deuteronomy 18:9-12; 1 Samuel 28:3-9; 2 Kings 23:24; 1 Kings 15:11-13; 2 Chronicles 14:2-3; 2 Kings 18:4; 2 Chronicles 31:1; 2 Kings 23:4-20; 2 Chronicles 34:3-7; 2 Chronicles 34:33; Daniel 11:31; Daniel 12:11; Matthew 26:61; Mark 14:58; Matthew 21:12-13; Mark 11:15-17; Luke 19:45-46; Matthew 27:39-40; Mark 15:29-30; Acts 6:14; Revelation 21:22; Matthew 5:17-19; Romans 3:31; Colossians 2:13-14; 2 Corinthians 3:6-17; Ephesians 2:14-16; Hebrews 10:1-10; Galatians 3:17-18; Romans 4:14; Galatians 5:11; 1 Corinthians 1:22-24; 1 Corinthians 2:2; Galatians 3:13; Galatians 5:2; Galatians 6:14-15
* **Associated Articles:** 4175: mediums (ID: `4175@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5297: disease (ID: `5297@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7374: high places (ID: `7374@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7466: temples (ID: `7466@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9115: antichrist, the (ID: `9115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9160: new heavens and new earth (ID: `9160@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4909: beginning (id: 4909)

A starting point, origin or source. The beginning often refers to the time of or before the creation of the world. God is described as the Beginning and the End, denoting the eternity of his existence. Jesus Christ's coming marks a new beginning both for God's people and, finally, for all creation.

Beginning as the starting point
-------------------------------

### As the source

**[PSA 111:10](https://ref.ly/Ps111:10)** *See also* [PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7), [PRO 9:10](https://ref.ly/Prov9:10); [MIC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Mic1:13) *the source of Israel's sin*; [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18) *Jesus Christ as the source of the church's life*

### As the origin

[JOB 8:7](https://ref.ly/Job8:7); ; [ACT 26:4](https://ref.ly/Acts26:4); ; [HEB 7:3](https://ref.ly/Heb7:3)

### As the commencement

[LEV 23:5](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5); ; [JDG 7:19](https://ref.ly/Judg7:19); ; [1KI 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:1) parallel [2CH 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:1); ; [LUK 23:54](https://ref.ly/Luke23:54); ; [1JN 2:7](https://ref.ly/1John2:7); ; [2JN 1:5](https://ref.ly/2John1:5)

The beginning: before the creation
----------------------------------

**[PRO 8:22–8:23](https://ref.ly/Prov8:22-Prov8:23)**

### Jesus Christ's existence predates the creation

**[JHN 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5)** *See also* [JHN 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/John1:1-John1:2), [JHN 8:58](https://ref.ly/John8:58); [COL 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:15-Col1:17); [1JN 1:1](https://ref.ly/1John1:1), [1JN 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1John2:13-1John2:14)

### Salvation planned before the creation

**[EPH 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Eph1:3-Eph1:4)** *See also* [1CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:7); [2TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:13); [2TI 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:9); [TIT 1:2](https://ref.ly/Titus1:2)

The beginning: the creation
---------------------------

**[GEN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1)**; **[HEB 1:10](https://ref.ly/Heb1:10)** *See also* [PSA 102:25](https://ref.ly/Ps102:25); [ISA 40:21](https://ref.ly/Isa40:21); [DAN 12:1](https://ref.ly/Dan12:1); [MAT 24:21](https://ref.ly/Matt24:21) parallel [MRK 13:19](https://ref.ly/Mark13:19)

### Marriage ordained from the beginning

**[MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6)** parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9) *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27), [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24)

The beginning of Israel's nationhood
------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Exod12:2)** *See also* [2SA 7:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:10) parallel [1CH 17:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:9); [ISA 52:4](https://ref.ly/Isa52:4); [JER 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2-Jer2:3)

New beginnings for God's people
-------------------------------

### For Israel

**[HOS 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14-Hos2:15)** *See also* [ISA 1:26](https://ref.ly/Isa1:26), [ISA 43:18–43:19](https://ref.ly/Isa43:18-Isa43:19), [ISA 44:26](https://ref.ly/Isa44:26), [ISA 61:4](https://ref.ly/Isa61:4); [JER 33:7–33:9](https://ref.ly/Jer33:7-Jer33:9)

### For believers

**[2CO 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:17)** *See also* [2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:6); [EPH 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eph2:10)

### A new creation

**[ISA 65:17](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17)** *See also* [ISA 66:22](https://ref.ly/Isa66:22); [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13); [REV 21:1](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1)

Beginnings of significant events
--------------------------------

### The rebuilding of Jerusalem

[NEH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Neh2:18); ; [HAG 1:14](https://ref.ly/Hag1:14)

### Jesus Christ's ministry

**[MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17)** *See also* [MRK 1:1](https://ref.ly/Mark1:1), [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [LUK 3:23](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23); [ACT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Acts1:1)

### The beginning of signs of the end

[MAT 24:7–24:8](https://ref.ly/Matt24:7-Matt24:8) parallel [MRK 13:8](https://ref.ly/Mark13:8); ; [LUK 21:28](https://ref.ly/Luke21:28)

The beginning and the end
-------------------------

### Implying all\-inclusiveness

[GEN 44:12](https://ref.ly/Gen44:12); ; [DEU 31:24](https://ref.ly/Deut31:24); ; [1SA 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:12); ; [MAT 20:8](https://ref.ly/Matt20:8)

### The beginning and end contrasted

[PRO 20:21](https://ref.ly/Prov20:21); ; [ECC 7:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:8); ; [2PE 2:20](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:20)

### Completing what is begun

**[PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6)** *See also* [2CO 8:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:6); [GAL 3:3](https://ref.ly/Gal3:3); [HEB 3:14](https://ref.ly/Heb3:14); [REV 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rev2:5), [REV 2:19](https://ref.ly/Rev2:19)

### God as the Beginning and the End

**[ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6)**; **[REV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6)** *See also* [ISA 41:4](https://ref.ly/Isa41:4), [ISA 48:12](https://ref.ly/Isa48:12); [REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17), [REV 2:8](https://ref.ly/Rev2:8), [REV 22:13](https://ref.ly/Rev22:13)

Satan as sinful from the beginning
----------------------------------

**[JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44)** *See also* [1JN 3:8](https://ref.ly/1John3:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 111:10; Proverbs 1:7; Proverbs 9:10; Micah 1:13; Colossians 1:18; Job 8:7; Acts 26:4; Hebrews 7:3; Leviticus 23:5; Judges 7:19; 1 Kings 6:1; 2 Chronicles 3:1; Luke 23:54; 1 John 2:7; 2 John 1:5; Proverbs 8:22-23; John 17:5; John 1:1-2; John 8:58; Colossians 1:15-17; 1 John 1:1; 1 John 2:13-14; Ephesians 1:3-4; 1 Corinthians 2:7; 2 Thessalonians 2:13; 2 Timothy 1:9; Titus 1:2; Genesis 1:1; Hebrews 1:10; Psalms 102:25; Isaiah 40:21; Daniel 12:1; Matthew 24:21; Mark 13:19; Matthew 19:4-6; Mark 10:6-9; Genesis 1:27; Genesis 2:24; Exodus 12:2; 2 Samuel 7:10; 1 Chronicles 17:9; Isaiah 52:4; Jeremiah 2:2-3; Hosea 2:14-15; Isaiah 1:26; Isaiah 43:18-19; Isaiah 44:26; Isaiah 61:4; Jeremiah 33:7-9; 2 Corinthians 5:17; 2 Corinthians 4:6; Ephesians 2:10; Isaiah 65:17; Isaiah 66:22; 2 Peter 3:13; Revelation 21:1; Nehemiah 2:18; Haggai 1:14; Matthew 4:17; Mark 1:1; Mark 1:15; Luke 3:23; Acts 1:1; Matthew 24:7-8; Mark 13:8; Luke 21:28; Genesis 44:12; Deuteronomy 31:24; 1 Samuel 3:12; Matthew 20:8; Proverbs 20:21; Ecclesiastes 7:8; 2 Peter 2:20; Philippians 1:6; 2 Corinthians 8:6; Galatians 3:3; Hebrews 3:14; Revelation 2:5; Revelation 2:19; Isaiah 44:6; Revelation 21:6; Isaiah 41:4; Isaiah 48:12; Revelation 1:17; Revelation 2:8; Revelation 22:13; John 8:44; 1 John 3:8

## 4912: chance (id: 4912)

The occurrence of events at random, as undesigned happenings. While people may perceive events as random, in Scripture all events are subject to God's sovereignty.

Chance in human life
--------------------

### Chance events

**[2CH 18:33–18:34](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33-2Chr18:34)** parallel [1KI 22:34–22:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34-1Kgs22:38) *See also* [1SA 6:7–6:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:7-1Sam6:9); [PRO 26:10](https://ref.ly/Prov26:10)

### Human life seems to be governed by chance, not merit

**[ECC 9:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:11)** *See also* [ECC 2:17–2:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17-Eccl2:19)

### Providence at work through apparent chance

**[JON 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:17)** *See also* [RUT 2:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:3); [LUK 23:44–23:45](https://ref.ly/Luke23:44-Luke23:45) parallels [MAT 27:45](https://ref.ly/Matt27:45), [MAT 27:51](https://ref.ly/Matt27:51), [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33), [MRK 15:38](https://ref.ly/Mark15:38); [ACT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Acts16:26)

Chance events overruled by God's sovereignty
--------------------------------------------

### The casting of lots and the element of chance

**[PRO 16:33](https://ref.ly/Prov16:33)** *See also* [NUM 33:53–33:54](https://ref.ly/Num33:53-Num33:54); [JDG 20:8–20:10](https://ref.ly/Judg20:8-Judg20:10); [NEH 10:34](https://ref.ly/Neh10:34), [NEH 11:1](https://ref.ly/Neh11:1); [LUK 23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:34) parallels [MAT 27:35](https://ref.ly/Matt27:35), [MRK 15:24](https://ref.ly/Mark15:24); [ACT 1:23–1:26](https://ref.ly/Acts1:23-Acts1:26)

### Urim and Thummim, seeming to be dependent on chance

**[EXO 28:29–28:30](https://ref.ly/Exod28:29-Exod28:30)** *The Urim and Thummim were used by the high priest to declare God's will. It was a form of casting lots showing either an affirmative or negative response to an enquiry.* *See also* [NUM 27:21](https://ref.ly/Num27:21); [1SA 28:4–28:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:4-1Sam28:6); [EZR 2:62–2:63](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:62-Ezra2:63) parallel [NEH 7:64–7:65](https://ref.ly/Neh7:64-Neh7:65)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 18:33-34; 1 Kings 22:34-38; 1 Samuel 6:7-9; Proverbs 26:10; Ecclesiastes 9:11; Ecclesiastes 2:17-19; Jonah 1:17; Ruth 2:3; Luke 23:44-45; Matthew 27:45; Matthew 27:51; Mark 15:33; Mark 15:38; Acts 16:26; Proverbs 16:33; Numbers 33:53-54; Judges 20:8-10; Nehemiah 10:34; Nehemiah 11:1; Luke 23:34; Matthew 27:35; Mark 15:24; Acts 1:23-26; Exodus 28:29-30; Numbers 27:21; 1 Samuel 28:4-6; Ezra 2:62-63; Nehemiah 7:64-65
* **Associated Articles:** 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1355: providence (ID: `1355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4937: fate, and fatalism (ID: `4937@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7392: lots, casting of (ID: `7392@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8124: guidance (ID: `8124@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4915: completion (id: 4915)

The bringing to fulfilment of a work, such as the work of creation or redemption.

Creation was completed by the seventh day
-----------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Gen2:1-Gen2:2)**; **[DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4)** *See also* [GEN 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Gen1:3-Gen1:4) *Genesis chapter 1 records the creation completed on each successive day as being “good”*; [EXO 20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:11); [JHN 1:3](https://ref.ly/John1:3); [COL 1:16](https://ref.ly/Col1:16); [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2), [HEB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Heb4:3), [HEB 11:3](https://ref.ly/Heb11:3)

All things are completed in God's time
--------------------------------------

**[GAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4)** *See also* [GEN 15:16](https://ref.ly/Gen15:16); [DAN 9:24](https://ref.ly/Dan9:24); [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [ROM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6); [EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10)

The work of redemption is a completed work
------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ brought his earthly work to completion

**[JHN 17:4](https://ref.ly/John17:4), [JHN 19:30](https://ref.ly/John19:30)** *See also* [JHN 4:34](https://ref.ly/John4:34), [JHN 5:36](https://ref.ly/John5:36)

### Jesus Christ will present his church complete to his Father

[JHN 6:37–6:40](https://ref.ly/John6:37-John6:40), [JHN 6:45](https://ref.ly/John6:45), [JHN 17:24](https://ref.ly/John17:24)

### Jesus Christ makes his people complete in himself

**[EPH 3:19](https://ref.ly/Eph3:19)**; **[PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6)**; **[COL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:10)** *See also* [JHN 15:11](https://ref.ly/John15:11), [JHN 16:24](https://ref.ly/John16:24); [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14); [COL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Col2:2); [JAS 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:4)

Believers are to complete their God\- given work
------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:24)** *See also* [ACT 14:26](https://ref.ly/Acts14:26); [ROM 15:28](https://ref.ly/Rom15:28); [COL 4:17](https://ref.ly/Col4:17); [2TI 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:7)

The completion of the age
-------------------------

### This world will come to an end

**[ISA 65:17](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17)** *See also* [ISA 66:22](https://ref.ly/Isa66:22); [MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28); [EPH 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9-Eph1:10); [HEB 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Heb1:11-Heb1:12); [2PE 3:10–3:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10-2Pet3:12); [REV 21:1–21:5](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:5)

### There will be a final separation of the righteous and wicked

[MAT 13:36–13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:36-Matt13:43), [MAT 13:47–13:50](https://ref.ly/Matt13:47-Matt13:50), [MAT 25:32–25:41](https://ref.ly/Matt25:32-Matt25:41); ; [2TH 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:8-2Thess1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:1-2; Deuteronomy 32:4; Genesis 1:3-4; Exodus 20:11; John 1:3; Colossians 1:16; Hebrews 1:2; Hebrews 4:3; Hebrews 11:3; Galatians 4:4; Genesis 15:16; Daniel 9:24; Mark 1:15; Romans 5:6; Ephesians 1:10; John 17:4; John 19:30; John 4:34; John 5:36; John 6:37-40; John 6:45; John 17:24; Ephesians 3:19; Philippians 1:6; Colossians 2:10; John 15:11; John 16:24; Romans 15:14; Colossians 2:2; James 1:4; Acts 20:24; Acts 14:26; Romans 15:28; Colossians 4:17; 2 Timothy 4:7; Isaiah 65:17; Isaiah 66:22; Matthew 19:28; Ephesians 1:9-10; Hebrews 1:11-12; 2 Peter 3:10-12; Revelation 21:1-5; Matthew 13:36-43; Matthew 13:47-50; Matthew 25:32-41; 2 Thessalonians 1:8-10

## 4918: dawn (id: 4918)

The time marked by the rising of the sun. It signals the end of night and relief from its attendant concerns, and the approach of daylight with the opportunity for renewed daily activity. The light of dawn following darkness is a symbol of hope and vindication, fulfilled especially in the coming of the Messiah through whom God's new age dawns.

Dawn as the end of the night
----------------------------

### It ends the darkness of the night

**[AMO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8)** *See also* [2SA 23:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:4); [JOB 41:18](https://ref.ly/Job41:18)

### It signals the end of nightly activity

[GEN 32:22–32:26](https://ref.ly/Gen32:22-Gen32:26); ; [JDG 19:25–19:26](https://ref.ly/Judg19:25-Judg19:26); ; [1SA 14:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:36), [1SA 25:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:36); ; [2SA 2:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:32), [2SA 17:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:22); ; [SNG 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Song2:16-Song2:17)

### Waiting for the dawn to bring relief

**[PSA 130:6](https://ref.ly/Ps130:6)** *See also* [JOB 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Job7:3-Job7:4); [PSA 46:5](https://ref.ly/Ps46:5); [ISA 21:12](https://ref.ly/Isa21:12), [ISA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Isa38:13); [ACT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Acts27:29)

Dawn as the beginning of a new day
----------------------------------

### It provides the earliest opportunity of the day

**[DAN 6:19](https://ref.ly/Dan6:19)** *First light provides the earliest opportunity for action and implies urgency.* *See also* [GEN 19:15](https://ref.ly/Gen19:15); [JOS 6:15](https://ref.ly/Josh6:15) *Dawn was a common time for military campaigns to begin*; [JDG 9:33](https://ref.ly/Judg9:33), [JDG 16:2](https://ref.ly/Judg16:2); [1SA 19:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:11); [MAT 28:1](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1) parallels [MRK 16:2](https://ref.ly/Mark16:2), [LUK 24:1](https://ref.ly/Luke24:1) *Women visit Jesus Christ's tomb at dawn*; [MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) parallel [LUK 4:42](https://ref.ly/Luke4:42) *Jesus Christ prays at dawn*; [LUK 22:66](https://ref.ly/Luke22:66); [JHN 8:2](https://ref.ly/John8:2); [ACT 27:33](https://ref.ly/Acts27:33)

### Important activity begins at dawn

**[NEH 4:21](https://ref.ly/Neh4:21)** *See also* [NEH 8:3](https://ref.ly/Neh8:3)

Dawn as a symbol of blessings to come
-------------------------------------

### The Messiah brings a new dawn

**[ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2)**; **[REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Rev22:16)** *See also* [NUM 24:17](https://ref.ly/Num24:17); [MAT 4:16](https://ref.ly/Matt4:16); [LUK 1:78–1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78-Luke1:79); [JHN 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/John1:4-John1:5), [JHN 8:12](https://ref.ly/John8:12)

### A symbol of hope and vindication

**[PSA 37:6](https://ref.ly/Ps37:6), [PSA 112:4](https://ref.ly/Ps112:4)** *See also* [JDG 5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:31); [PSA 57:8](https://ref.ly/Ps57:8) parallel [PSA 108:2](https://ref.ly/Ps108:2); [PRO 4:18](https://ref.ly/Prov4:18); [ISA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8), [ISA 60:1–60:3](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1-Isa60:3), [ISA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Isa62:1)

### A symbol of God's coming in glory

**[HAB 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/Hab3:3-Hab3:4)** *See also* [MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2)

### A symbol of renewal

[PSA 110:3](https://ref.ly/Ps110:3)

Dawn as a symbol of God's revelation
------------------------------------

**[1JN 2:8](https://ref.ly/1John2:8)** *See also* [ISA 8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:20); [2PE 1:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:19)

Dawn as a time of reckoning
---------------------------

### Darkness at dawn as a picture of judgment

**[ISA 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:10-Isa13:11)** *See also* [JOB 3:9](https://ref.ly/Job3:9); [AMO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Amos4:13)

### The light of dawn exposes the wicked

[JOB 38:12–38:13](https://ref.ly/Job38:12-Job38:13); ; [JHN 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:19-John3:20); ; [EPH 5:11–5:13](https://ref.ly/Eph5:11-Eph5:13)

## 4921: day (id: 4921)

A period of 24 hours or, as distinct from night, the period of daylight. The term may also be used more generally to refer to a point in time. Daylight is used figuratively to describe the illumination afforded to believers, who are urged to use the opportunities it presents and to avoid the evil usually associated with darkness.

Day and daylight
----------------

### Day as a period of 24 hours

[GEN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Gen1:8); ; [MAT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:19); ; [JHN 4:43](https://ref.ly/John4:43), [JHN 11:6](https://ref.ly/John11:6)

### Day as the period of daylight

**[GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5)** *See also* [GEN 1:14–1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14-Gen1:18), [GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22); [PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16); [JER 33:20](https://ref.ly/Jer33:20)

Daylight gives opportunity for activity
---------------------------------------

### A time for human endeavour

**[JHN 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/John11:9-John11:10)** *See also* [JOS 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Josh10:12-Josh10:13); [ACT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Acts27:29)

### A time of spiritual opportunity

**[JHN 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/John9:4-John9:5)** *See also* [JHN 12:35–12:36](https://ref.ly/John12:35-John12:36)

Daylight allows things to be seen
---------------------------------

### Daylight activities are seen by all

[2SA 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:11-2Sam12:12); ; [EZK 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:3-Ezek12:4); ; [MAT 10:27](https://ref.ly/Matt10:27) parallel [LUK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Luke12:3)

### Examples of those avoiding daylight

[JDG 6:27](https://ref.ly/Judg6:27) *Gideon*; ; [JHN 3:2](https://ref.ly/John3:2) *Nicodemus*

### Daylight activities should be decent

**[ROM 13:12–13:13](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12-Rom13:13)** *See also* [EPH 5:8–5:14](https://ref.ly/Eph5:8-Eph5:14); [1TH 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:5-1Thess5:8); [2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13)

Six working days ordained
-------------------------

**[EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11)** parallel [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15)

Particular days of the week
---------------------------

### The Sabbath (Saturday)

**[EXO 20:8–20:10](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:10)** parallel [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15) *The Sabbath was reckoned from sunset on Friday to sunset on Saturday.* *See also* [LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16); [COL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16); [HEB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Heb4:4)

### The Day of Preparation for the Sabbath (Friday)

[MRK 15:42](https://ref.ly/Mark15:42) parallel [LUK 23:54](https://ref.ly/Luke23:54); ; [JHN 19:31](https://ref.ly/John19:31)

### The first day of the week (Sunday)

**[ACT 20:7](https://ref.ly/Acts20:7)** *See also* [MRK 16:2](https://ref.ly/Mark16:2) parallels [LUK 24:1](https://ref.ly/Luke24:1), [JHN 20:1](https://ref.ly/John20:1); [MRK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Mark16:9); [1CO 16:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:2); [REV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10)

Festival days
-------------

[LEV 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5-Lev23:6) *Passover and Unleavened Bread*; ; [LEV 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27-Lev23:28) *the Day of Atonement*; [LEV 23:34–23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:34-Lev23:36); ; [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [NUM 28:26](https://ref.ly/Num28:26) *the day of firstfruits*

Day as a point in time
----------------------

### The day of salvation

**[2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2)** *See also* [ISA 25:9](https://ref.ly/Isa25:9), [ISA 49:8](https://ref.ly/Isa49:8)

### God sends the day of disaster

[DEU 32:35](https://ref.ly/Deut32:35); ; [JER 17:18](https://ref.ly/Jer17:18), [JER 51:2](https://ref.ly/Jer51:2); ; [MRK 13:19](https://ref.ly/Mark13:19)

### God helps on the day of distress

**[PSA 86:7](https://ref.ly/Ps86:7)** *See also* [GEN 35:3](https://ref.ly/Gen35:3); [2SA 22:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:19) parallel [PSA 18:18](https://ref.ly/Ps18:18); [2KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:3-2Kgs19:4) parallel [ISA 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Isa37:3-Isa37:4); [PSA 94:13](https://ref.ly/Ps94:13); [JER 17:17](https://ref.ly/Jer17:17)

The final days of time
----------------------

### The last days

**[ACT 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17-Acts2:18)** *See also* [JOL 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28-Joel2:29); [ISA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Isa2:2) parallel [MIC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mic4:1); [HOS 3:5](https://ref.ly/Hos3:5); [1TI 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1); [2TI 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1); [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2); [1PE 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:20)

### The day of the LORD

**[ISA 13:9–13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9-Isa13:11)** [JER 30:7–30:9](https://ref.ly/Jer30:7-Jer30:9); ; [ZEP 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:16-Zeph3:17); ; [PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6); ; [2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:12); [JOL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Joel2:1-Joel2:2); ; [OBA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Obad1:15); ; [ZEP 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14-Zeph1:15); [1TH 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:2); ; [2PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10)

### The last day

**[JHN 6:39–6:40](https://ref.ly/John6:39-John6:40)** *See also* [JHN 11:24](https://ref.ly/John11:24), [JHN 12:48](https://ref.ly/John12:48)

------------------------------------------------------------

God's different perception of time
----------------------------------

## 4924: delay (id: 4924)

A postponement or pause, which can result from a deliberate decision or a failure to achieve the intended objective in the time available. Scripture stresses that apparent delays on the part of God are due to his love and wisdom, especially to give sinners time for repentance.

* **Associated Articles:** 4925: delay, divine (ID: `4925@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4926: delay, human (ID: `4926@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4925: delay, divine (id: 4925)

Delays by God do not result from any failure on his part, but reflect unrealistic human expectations concerning his actions. God delays his vengeance in order to give time for human repentance.

God delays to allow for repentance
----------------------------------

### To give time for repentance

**[LUK 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/Luke13:6-Luke13:9)**; **[ROM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rom2:4)**; **[2PE 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:8-2Pet3:9)** *See also* [MAT 3:7–3:11](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7-Matt3:11) parallel [LUK 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/Luke3:7-Luke3:10); [1TI 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:4); [JAS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7); [REV 2:21](https://ref.ly/Rev2:21)

### In response to repentance

**[1KI 21:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:29)** *See also* [1KI 21:21–21:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:21-1Kgs21:28)

### Where there is no repentance, God does not delay

**[EZK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:25)** *See also* [EZK 21:24–21:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:24-Ezek21:26); [REV 2:16](https://ref.ly/Rev2:16)

God delays judgment for the sake of his name
--------------------------------------------

**[ISA 48:9](https://ref.ly/Isa48:9)** *See also* [NEH 9:30–9:31](https://ref.ly/Neh9:30-Neh9:31)

Jesus Christ delayed visiting Lazarus for God's glory
-----------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/John11:1-John11:6)** *See also* [JHN 11:11–11:23](https://ref.ly/John11:11-John11:23), [JHN 11:38–11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:38-John11:44)

The giving of the Holy Spirit was delayed until Pentecost
---------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:49)** *See also* [ACT 1:4](https://ref.ly/Acts1:4), [ACT 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:4)

God acts when he wants and nothing can delay him
------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 10:37](https://ref.ly/Heb10:37)** *Referring to the second coming of Jesus Christ.* *See also* [HAB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3) *Referring to the LORD's delay in sending the Babylonians to conquer Judah and take her inhabitants into exile*; [REV 22:20](https://ref.ly/Rev22:20)

Other examples of delay
-----------------------

[EXO 32:1](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1)

## 4926: delay, human (id: 4926)

Human delay is often the result of fear, indecision or disobedience to God. Scripture indicates that delay is to be avoided.

Examples of delay as a result of fear
-------------------------------------

### Israel entering the promised land

**[NUM 13:31–13:32](https://ref.ly/Num13:31-Num13:32)** *See also* [NUM 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Num13:1-Num13:3), [NUM 13:17–13:20](https://ref.ly/Num13:17-Num13:20), [NUM 13:27–13:30](https://ref.ly/Num13:27-Num13:30), [NUM 14:33–14:35](https://ref.ly/Num14:33-Num14:35)

### Gideon responding to God's call

[JDG 6:36–6:40](https://ref.ly/Judg6:36-Judg6:40)

Examples of delay as a result of disobedience
---------------------------------------------

### Lot escaping from Sodom

**[GEN 19:16](https://ref.ly/Gen19:16)** *See also* [GEN 19:12–19:15](https://ref.ly/Gen19:12-Gen19:15)

### Jonah going to Nineveh

**[JON 1:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:3)** *See also* [PSA 119:60](https://ref.ly/Ps119:60); [JON 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:2), [JON 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1-Jonah3:3) *Jonah obeyed the second time.*

Nothing should delay believers from following Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 8:21–8:22](https://ref.ly/Matt8:21-Matt8:22)** parallel [LUK 9:59–9:60](https://ref.ly/Luke9:59-Luke9:60) *See also* [MAT 9:9](https://ref.ly/Matt9:9); [MRK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Mark1:20); [LUK 14:15–14:24](https://ref.ly/Luke14:15-Luke14:24), [LUK 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Luke19:5-Luke19:6)

People should not delay in the matter of salvation
--------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6)** *See also* [AMO 6:3](https://ref.ly/Amos6:3); [ACT 24:24–24:26](https://ref.ly/Acts24:24-Acts24:26); [2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2); [HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3)

Believers are not to delay in fulfilling their vows to God and obligations to others
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ECC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:4)** *See also* [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13); [DEU 23:21](https://ref.ly/Deut23:21); [PSA 61:8](https://ref.ly/Ps61:8)

Examples of human responses to delay
------------------------------------

### Sarah's wrong response brought trouble

**[GEN 16:1–16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:6)**

### Saul's wrong response cost him the kingship

**[1SA 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:11-1Sam13:12)** *See also* [1SA 13:8–13:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:8-1Sam13:10), [1SA 13:13–13:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:13-1Sam13:14)

### Jacob responded with patience

[GEN 29:20–29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:20-Gen29:30)

### Paul responded by giving instructions to Timothy

**[1TI 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:14-1Tim3:15)**

### Daniel responded by praying

**[DAN 9:19](https://ref.ly/Dan9:19)** *See also* [DAN 9:1–9:23](https://ref.ly/Dan9:1-Dan9:23), [DAN 10:10–10:13](https://ref.ly/Dan10:10-Dan10:13) *Gabriel explains the reason for the delay.*

### David reponded by calling on the LORD

## 4930: end (id: 4930)

A limit in time or space, or the outcome of a sequence of actions or events. God knows the end from the beginning. He is without limit: he exercises control over the furthest reaches of the world, and will bring to an end earthly powers, human life and the present age. Those who persevere to the end will be saved.

God is without limit
--------------------

**[HEB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Heb1:12)** *See also* [PSA 102:26–102:27](https://ref.ly/Ps102:26-Ps102:27); [DAN 6:26](https://ref.ly/Dan6:26), [DAN 7:14](https://ref.ly/Dan7:14); [MAT 24:35](https://ref.ly/Matt24:35) parallels [MRK 13:31](https://ref.ly/Mark13:31), [LUK 21:33](https://ref.ly/Luke21:33); [LUK 1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:33)

God is the Beginning and the End
--------------------------------

**[REV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6)** *See also* [ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6); [REV 22:13](https://ref.ly/Rev22:13)

God brings earthly powers to an end
-----------------------------------

**[ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11)**; **[DAN 2:44](https://ref.ly/Dan2:44)** *See also* [PSA 89:44](https://ref.ly/Ps89:44); [ISA 21:16](https://ref.ly/Isa21:16), [ISA 23:9](https://ref.ly/Isa23:9); [JER 48:2](https://ref.ly/Jer48:2) *Moab*; [JER 49:37](https://ref.ly/Jer49:37) *Elam*; [EZK 7:2–7:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:2-Ezek7:6) *Israel*; [EZK 26:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:21) *Tyre*; [EZK 30:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:18) *Egypt*; [DAN 5:26](https://ref.ly/Dan5:26) *Babylon*

----------------------
