## 4933: evening (id: 4933)

The time between the fading of daylight and nightfall. The onset of darkness marks the end of the working day and is a time for rest and refreshment. It is an assigned time for sacrifice and an opportunity for reflection and prayer. The evening also brings dangers.

Evening marks the end of the day
--------------------------------

### Evening and God's work of creation

[GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5)

### The onset of darkness

**[PRO 7:9](https://ref.ly/Prov7:9)** [PSA 102:11](https://ref.ly/Ps102:11), [PSA 109:23](https://ref.ly/Ps109:23); [JER 6:4](https://ref.ly/Jer6:4)

### Days reckoned from evening to evening

[EXO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Exod12:18); ; [LEV 23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:32) *The Sabbath began at sunset on Friday.*

### Ritual uncleanness lasts until evening

[LEV 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Lev11:39-Lev11:40), [LEV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15); ; [NUM 19:7–19:8](https://ref.ly/Num19:7-Num19:8); ; [DEU 23:11](https://ref.ly/Deut23:11)

### Waiting on God until evening

**[JOS 7:6](https://ref.ly/Josh7:6)** *See also* [JDG 20:23](https://ref.ly/Judg20:23), [JDG 20:26–20:27](https://ref.ly/Judg20:26-Judg20:27), [JDG 21:2–21:3](https://ref.ly/Judg21:2-Judg21:3)

### Mourning until evening

[2SA 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:12)

### City gates shut at evening

[JOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Josh2:5); ; [2KI 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:9-2Kgs7:11); ; [NEH 13:19](https://ref.ly/Neh13:19); ; [EZK 46:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:2)

### Executed criminals to be buried by evening

[DEU 21:22–21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22-Deut21:23); ; [JOS 8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:29), [JOS 10:26](https://ref.ly/Josh10:26); ; [MAT 27:57–27:58](https://ref.ly/Matt27:57-Matt27:58) parallels [MRK 15:42–15:43](https://ref.ly/Mark15:42-Mark15:43), [LUK 23:52–23:54](https://ref.ly/Luke23:52-Luke23:54); ; [JHN 19:31](https://ref.ly/John19:31)

Evening as a time for refreshment
---------------------------------

### A time to rest from labour

**[PSA 104:23](https://ref.ly/Ps104:23)**; **[LUK 21:37](https://ref.ly/Luke21:37)** *See also* [JDG 19:16](https://ref.ly/Judg19:16); [RUT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:17); [JOB 7:2](https://ref.ly/Job7:2); [ZEP 2:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:7); [MAT 20:8](https://ref.ly/Matt20:8); [MRK 4:35–4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:35-Mark4:38) parallels [MAT 8:23](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23), [LUK 8:22–8:23](https://ref.ly/Luke8:22-Luke8:23)

### A time to rest from travel

**[JDG 19:9](https://ref.ly/Judg19:9)** *See also* [GEN 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:2); [NUM 9:21](https://ref.ly/Num9:21); [LUK 24:29](https://ref.ly/Luke24:29)

### A time to return home

**[MAT 21:17](https://ref.ly/Matt21:17)** *See also* [GEN 30:16](https://ref.ly/Gen30:16); [MRK 11:11](https://ref.ly/Mark11:11), [MRK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Mark11:19); [JHN 6:16–6:17](https://ref.ly/John6:16-John6:17)

### A time to eat

[EXO 16:12](https://ref.ly/Exod16:12); ; [1KI 17:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:6); ; [MAT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Matt14:15); ; [JHN 13:2](https://ref.ly/John13:2)

### A time for reflection and prayer

**[GEN 24:63](https://ref.ly/Gen24:63)** *See also* [PSA 55:17](https://ref.ly/Ps55:17), [PSA 141:2](https://ref.ly/Ps141:2); [MAT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Matt14:23) parallel [MRK 6:46](https://ref.ly/Mark6:46)

------------------------

-----------------------------------

------------------------

**[MAT 8:16](https://ref.ly/Matt8:16)** parallels [MRK 1:32](https://ref.ly/Mark1:32), [LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40) *People came to Jesus Christ after sunset, when the Sabbath was over.* *See also* [GEN 24:11](https://ref.ly/Gen24:11) *Water collected in the evening, the coolest time of the day*; [MAT 16:2](https://ref.ly/Matt16:2) *Red sky in the evening as a sign of fair weather*

--------------------------

## 4936: fate (id: 4936)

A hidden power which some believe to control and determine everything that happens in such a way that it is impossible to prevent or change things. Scripture stresses both the sovereignty of God and human responsibility and has no place for this pagan notion. The term is also used in a looser sense, to mean “end” or “destiny”.

* **Associated Articles:** 4937: fate, and fatalism (ID: `4937@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4938: fate, and final destiny (ID: `4938@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4937: fate, and fatalism (id: 4937)

The belief that all things are predetermined, especially by the stars, removing human freedom and responsibility. Scripture affirms human freedom and responsibility and declares that the sun, moon and stars are all part of God's creation and are subject to him.

The heavenly bodies subject to God's authority
----------------------------------------------

### The heavenly bodies are created by God

[GEN 1:14–1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14-Gen1:18); ; [JOB 9:9](https://ref.ly/Job9:9); ; [PSA 8:3](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3), [PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16), [PSA 148:3–148:5](https://ref.ly/Ps148:3-Ps148:5); ; [AMO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Amos5:8)

### The heavenly bodies are subject to the rule and providence of God

**[ISA 40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:26)** *See also* [JOB 38:31–38:32](https://ref.ly/Job38:31-Job38:32); [PSA 104:19](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19); [JER 31:35](https://ref.ly/Jer31:35); [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)

The biblical rejection of fatalism in human choice
--------------------------------------------------

### Human beings are free to choose between right and wrong

**[DEU 11:26–11:28](https://ref.ly/Deut11:26-Deut11:28)** *See also* [DEU 30:15–30:19](https://ref.ly/Deut30:15-Deut30:19); [EZK 18:21–18:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:21-Ezek18:23)

### Human beings are free to call upon the name of the Lord

[JOL 2:32](https://ref.ly/Joel2:32); ; [ACT 2:21](https://ref.ly/Acts2:21); ; [ROM 10:11–10:13](https://ref.ly/Rom10:11-Rom10:13)

### Human beings are free to seek the Lord

[1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); ; [ISA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6)

### Human beings are free to come to the Lord

**[REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)** *See also* [MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:14-18; Job 9:9; Psalms 8:3; Psalms 74:16; Psalms 148:3-5; Amos 5:8; Isaiah 40:26; Job 38:31-32; Psalms 104:19; Jeremiah 31:35; Matthew 5:45; Deuteronomy 11:26-28; Deuteronomy 30:15-19; Ezekiel 18:21-23; Joel 2:32; Acts 2:21; Romans 10:11-13; 1 Chronicles 28:9; Isaiah 55:6; Revelation 22:17; Matthew 11:28
* **Associated Articles:** 1115: God, purpose of (ID: `1115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1355: providence (ID: `1355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4005: creation (ID: `4005@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6209: pagans (ID: `6209@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6622: choice (ID: `6622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6658: freedom (ID: `6658@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8160: seeking God (ID: `8160@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8640: calling upon God (ID: `8640@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8709: astrology (ID: `8709@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

The final destiny or predetermined end of individuals, groups or nations.

---------------------------

## 4942: fulness (id: 4942)

The totality of something, as it is brought to completion by being filled. Jesus Christ is seen as expressing the full nature and purposes of God, so that believers may rest assured that, through Christ, they know God as he really is. Believers are also being brought to fulness through the gospel, although the final fulness of joy and understanding will take place only in the future kingdom of God.

Jesus Christ, the fulness of God
--------------------------------

### Jesus Christ, the fulness of God's nature

**[COL 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:9-Col2:10)** *See also* [COL 1:19](https://ref.ly/Col1:19)

### Jesus Christ, the fulness of God's attributes

**[JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14)** *See also* [JHN 1:16](https://ref.ly/John1:16)

Christians and the church enjoy fulness through Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:13)** *See also* [JHN 10:10](https://ref.ly/John10:10); [EPH 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22-Eph1:23), [EPH 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Eph3:14-Eph3:19); [COL 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:9-Col2:10)

Believers receive fulness of joy from God
-----------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:8-1Pet1:9)** *See also* [PSA 16:11](https://ref.ly/Ps16:11); [JHN 17:13](https://ref.ly/John17:13); [ACT 16:29–16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:29-Acts16:34); [ROM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Rom15:13)

------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Col2:2-Col2:3)** *See also* [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14); [COL 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9); [PHM 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:6)

### Fulness of understanding is only fully realised when believers see God face to face

**[1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:12)**

Believers should be constantly filled with the Holy Spirit
----------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eph5:18)** *See also* [ACT 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:4), [ACT 4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31), [ACT 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Acts6:3-Acts6:4), [ACT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Acts13:52)

----------------------------------------------------------------

God works out his purposes in the fulness of time
-------------------------------------------------

## 4945: history (id: 4945)

In Scripture, more than a record of and reflection on past events, but a process under God's control which has as its goal the eventual fulfilment of his purposes and through which he makes himself known. The goal of history is realised in Jesus Christ, through whom the age of salvation is inaugurated. Both the OT and NT point to the importance of faith rooted in historical events.

God is active in history
------------------------

### God's control over historical events

**[DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21)**; **[ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)** *See also* [GEN 45:5–45:8](https://ref.ly/Gen45:5-Gen45:8); [1KI 12:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:15) parallel [2CH 10:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:15); [DAN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Dan4:25), [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25); [AMO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Amos9:7)

### God's saving purposes worked out through history

**[EPH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eph1:11)** *See also* [ISA 14:24–14:27](https://ref.ly/Isa14:24-Isa14:27), [ISA 37:26](https://ref.ly/Isa37:26), [ISA 41:4](https://ref.ly/Isa41:4), [ISA 46:10–46:11](https://ref.ly/Isa46:10-Isa46:11); [ACT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Acts1:7), [ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23)

God is known through his people's history
-----------------------------------------

### God is known to his people

**[ISA 46:9](https://ref.ly/Isa46:9)** *See also* [EXO 10:2](https://ref.ly/Exod10:2); [DEU 4:32–4:35](https://ref.ly/Deut4:32-Deut4:35); [PSA 77:11–77:15](https://ref.ly/Ps77:11-Ps77:15); [ISA 43:10–43:13](https://ref.ly/Isa43:10-Isa43:13), [ISA 44:6–44:8](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6-Isa44:8); [MIC 6:5](https://ref.ly/Mic6:5)

### God is revealed to the nations

**[PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2)** *See also* [1KI 8:56–8:60](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:56-1Kgs8:60)

The Israelites were called to remember their history
----------------------------------------------------

### The past was recalled in festivals

[LEV 23:42–23:43](https://ref.ly/Lev23:42-Lev23:43); ; [DEU 16:1–16:6](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:6)

### The past was recounted to successive generations

**[PSA 78:2–78:7](https://ref.ly/Ps78:2-Ps78:7)** *See also* [DEU 32:7–32:9](https://ref.ly/Deut32:7-Deut32:9); [JDG 6:13](https://ref.ly/Judg6:13); [PSA 44:1–44:3](https://ref.ly/Ps44:1-Ps44:3)

Jesus Christ as the centre of the history of salvation
------------------------------------------------------

### He is its beginning and end

**[REV 22:13](https://ref.ly/Rev22:13)** *See also* [REV 1:8](https://ref.ly/Rev1:8), [REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17), [REV 2:8](https://ref.ly/Rev2:8)

### God's saving purposes in history fulfilled in Jesus Christ

**[EPH 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9-Eph1:10)** *See also* [ROM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6); [GAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4); [COL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:17)

### Jesus Christ inaugurates God's new age

**[COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18)** *See also* [DAN 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13-Dan7:14); [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [MAT 12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:28) parallel [LUK 11:20](https://ref.ly/Luke11:20); [EPH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Eph2:7)

The Christian faith is based on the historical facts of salvation
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### Testimony passed on by eye\-witnesses

**[LUK 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Luke1:1-Luke1:4)** *See also* [HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3); [1PE 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1); [2PE 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:16); [1JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:3)

### Remembering Jesus Christ's death

**[1CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:25)** parallel [LUK 22:19–22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19-Luke22:20)

### Jesus Christ's resurrection as historical fact

**[ACT 1:3](https://ref.ly/Acts1:3)** *See also* [LUK 24:1–24:3](https://ref.ly/Luke24:1-Luke24:3), [LUK 24:39](https://ref.ly/Luke24:39); [JHN 20:27](https://ref.ly/John20:27); [1CO 15:3–15:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:3-1Cor15:8)

### Historical references in the NT

[LUK 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Luke1:1-Luke1:2); ; [MAT 2:1](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1); ; [LUK 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1-Luke3:2); ; [MAT 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3-Matt14:4) parallel [MRK 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:18); ; [ACT 5:37](https://ref.ly/Acts5:37)

Landmarks in salvation history
------------------------------

### The creation

[GEN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1), [GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7)

### The fall

[GEN 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6-Gen3:7); ; [ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12)

### The call of Abraham

[GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3); ; [HEB 11:8](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8)

### The exodus from Egypt

[EXO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Exod13:3), [EXO 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:4-Exod19:6)

### The birth of Jesus Christ

[JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14); ; [HEB 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:2)

### Jesus Christ's death and resurrection

[1CO 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:3-1Cor15:4); ; [PHP 2:6–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:11); ; [HEB 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3-Heb1:4)

## 4948: hour (id: 4948)

One twelfth of the period of daylight between sunrise and sunset. Measurement of time in biblical days was often approximate, referring most frequently to the broad divisions of time marked by the third, sixth and ninth hours. The term also denotes a significant or appointed moment or period of time, especially the hour of Jesus Christ's suffering and his return.

Hour as a period of time
------------------------

### A measured interval

**[JHN 11:9](https://ref.ly/John11:9)** *See also* [MAT 20:9–20:12](https://ref.ly/Matt20:9-Matt20:12) *The working day lasted twelve hours*; [LUK 22:59](https://ref.ly/Luke22:59); [ACT 19:34](https://ref.ly/Acts19:34); [REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rev8:1)

### Symbolising a brief time

**[MAT 6:27](https://ref.ly/Matt6:27)** parallel [LUK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Luke12:25) *See also* [MAT 26:40](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40) parallel [MRK 14:37](https://ref.ly/Mark14:37)REV [17:12](https://ref.ly/Mark17:12), [REV 18:10](https://ref.ly/Rev18:10), [REV 18:17](https://ref.ly/Rev18:17), [REV 18:19](https://ref.ly/Rev18:19)

### Symbolising a significant period

**[ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12)** *See also* [LUK 22:53](https://ref.ly/Luke22:53) *Darkness provides an hour of opportunity for the powers of evil.*

Hours denoting specific times of day
------------------------------------

[MAT 20:3–20:5](https://ref.ly/Matt20:3-Matt20:5) *The third, sixth and ninth hours marked the principal divisions of the day.*

### The third hour: 9 a.m

**[MRK 15:25](https://ref.ly/Mark15:25)** *See also* [ACT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Acts2:15)

### The sixth hour: noon

**[JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6)** *See also* [GEN 43:16](https://ref.ly/Gen43:16); [1KI 18:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:27); [JOB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Job5:14); [MAT 27:45](https://ref.ly/Matt27:45) parallels [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33), [LUK 23:44](https://ref.ly/Luke23:44)

### The ninth hour: 3 p.m

**[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46)** parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34) *See also* [ACT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Acts3:1), [ACT 10:30](https://ref.ly/Acts10:30)

### Midnight

**[EXO 11:4](https://ref.ly/Exod11:4)** *See also* [PSA 119:62](https://ref.ly/Ps119:62); [MAT 25:6](https://ref.ly/Matt25:6); [MRK 13:35](https://ref.ly/Mark13:35); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25)

### Other hours of the day

[JHN 4:52](https://ref.ly/John4:52) *the seventh hour: 1 p.m*; ; [JHN 1:39](https://ref.ly/John1:39) *the tenth hour: 4 p.m*; ; [MAT 20:6](https://ref.ly/Matt20:6) *the eleventh hour: 5 p.m.*

### A particular unnamed time of day

**[MAT 8:13](https://ref.ly/Matt8:13)** *See also* [MAT 15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:28); [LUK 22:14](https://ref.ly/Luke22:14); [ACT 16:33](https://ref.ly/Acts16:33); [REV 11:13](https://ref.ly/Rev11:13)

Hour as an appointed time in God's purpose
------------------------------------------

### The hour of Jesus Christ's suffering

**[JHN 12:23–12:24](https://ref.ly/John12:23-John12:24)** *See also* [MAT 26:45](https://ref.ly/Matt26:45) parallel [MRK 14:41](https://ref.ly/Mark14:41); [MRK 14:35](https://ref.ly/Mark14:35); [JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/John12:27)

### The unexpected hour of Jesus Christ's return

**[MAT 24:36](https://ref.ly/Matt24:36)** parallel [MRK 13:32](https://ref.ly/Mark13:32) *See also* [MAT 24:42–24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:42-Matt24:44) parallel [LUK 12:39–12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:39-Luke12:40); [MAT 25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:13)

### The time before Jesus Christ's return

**[ROM 13:11](https://ref.ly/Rom13:11)** *See also* [1JN 2:18](https://ref.ly/1John2:18)

### The hour of God's judgment

[REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7)

* **Associated Passages:** John 11:9; Matthew 20:9-12; Luke 22:59; Acts 19:34; Revelation 8:1; Matthew 6:27; Luke 12:25; Matthew 26:40; Mark 14:37; Mark 17:12; Revelation 18:10; Revelation 18:17; Revelation 18:19; Ecclesiastes 9:12; Luke 22:53; Matthew 20:3-5; Mark 15:25; Acts 2:15; John 4:6; Genesis 43:16; 1 Kings 18:27; Job 5:14; Matthew 27:45; Mark 15:33; Luke 23:44; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:34; Acts 3:1; Acts 10:30; Exodus 11:4; Psalms 119:62; Matthew 25:6; Mark 13:35; Acts 16:25; John 4:52; John 1:39; Matthew 20:6; Matthew 8:13; Matthew 15:28; Luke 22:14; Acts 16:33; Revelation 11:13; John 12:23-24; Matthew 26:45; Mark 14:41; Mark 14:35; John 12:27; Matthew 24:36; Mark 13:32; Matthew 24:42-44; Luke 12:39-40; Matthew 25:13; Romans 13:11; 1 John 2:18; Revelation 14:7
* **Associated Articles:** 2530: Jesus Christ, death of (ID: `2530@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4903: time (ID: `4903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4921: day (ID: `4921@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4969: seasons (ID: `4969@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5046: opportunities (ID: `5046@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8490: watchfulness (ID: `8490@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8602: prayer (ID: `8602@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9140: last days (ID: `9140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4951: month (id: 4951)

A thirty\-day period of the year measured from one new moon to the next. There were twelve months in the year, with a thirteenth inserted periodically to bring the lunar calendar into line with the solar year. Early month names may have been linked with the seasons. After the exile, Babylonian names were adopted.

Months marked by the new moon
-----------------------------

**[NUM 28:11–28:14](https://ref.ly/Num28:11-Num28:14)** *Special sacrifices marked the first day of each month.* *See also* [PSA 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3); [ISA 66:23](https://ref.ly/Isa66:23); [EZK 46:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:6); [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5) *No work was done on the first day of the month.*

Months as periods of 30 days
----------------------------

**[DEU 21:13](https://ref.ly/Deut21:13)** *The prescribed period of mourning was 30 days (a month).* *See also* [NUM 20:29](https://ref.ly/Num20:29); [DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8)

The first month: Abib/Nisan
---------------------------

**[EXO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Exod12:2)** *Abib, meaning “young head of grain” was the Canaanite name for Israel's first month. Modern equivalent: March\-April.* *See also* [EXO 40:2](https://ref.ly/Exod40:2); [NUM 9:1](https://ref.ly/Num9:1)

### Feasts in the month of Abib

**[DEU 16:1](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1)** *Passover celebrated on 14th Abib.* *See also* [EXO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Exod13:4), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15) *the Feast of Unleavened bread (15th\-21st)*; [LEV 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5-Lev23:6) parallel [NUM 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Num28:16-Num28:17); [LEV 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Lev23:10-Lev23:11) *The first sheaf of barley was brought to the priest on 16th Abib.*[NEH 2:1](https://ref.ly/Neh2:1); [EST 3:7](https://ref.ly/Esth3:7)

The second month: Ziv
---------------------

**[1KI 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:1)** *Ziv was the pre\-exilic name; the post\-exilic name, Iyyar, does not appear in the Bible. Modern equivalent: April\-May.* *See also* [1KI 6:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:37); [NUM 9:11](https://ref.ly/Num9:11) *A late Passover might be held on 14th Ziv*; [2CH 30:13–30:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:13-2Chr30:15) *Hezekiah's celebration of the Passover was in the second month.*

The third month: Sivan
----------------------

**[EST 8:9](https://ref.ly/Esth8:9)** *Modern equivalent: May\-June.* *See also* [EXO 19:1](https://ref.ly/Exod19:1); [EZK 31:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:1)

### Pentecost celebrated in the month of Sivan

[LEV 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:16) *on 6th Sivan*

The fourth month: Tammuz
------------------------

[EZK 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:1) *modern equivalent: June\-July*; ; [EZK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:14) *Tammuz was a fertility goddess; her annual death was mourned in the month named after her.*

The fifth month
---------------

[NUM 33:38](https://ref.ly/Num33:38) *The name of the fifth month, Ab, does not appear in the Bible. Modern equivalent: July\-August*; ; [2KI 25:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:8)

The sixth month: Elul
---------------------

**[NEH 6:15](https://ref.ly/Neh6:15)** *Modern equivalent: August\-September.* *See also* [EZK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:1); [HAG 1:1](https://ref.ly/Hag1:1); [LUK 1:26](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26)

The seventh month: Ethanim
--------------------------

**[1KI 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:2)** *Ethanim is the pre\-exilic name; its Babylonian equivalent, Tishri, does not appear in the Bible. Modern equivalent: September\-October.*

### Feasts and sacred days in the month of Ethanim

*See also* [LEV 23:23–23:24](https://ref.ly/Lev23:23-Lev23:24) parallel [NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1) *The Feast of Trumpets (1st). This is celebrated today as the Jewish New Year (Rosh Hashanah)*; [LEV 23:27](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27) parallel [NUM 29:7](https://ref.ly/Num29:7) *the Day of Atonement (10th)*; [LEV 23:34](https://ref.ly/Lev23:34) parallel [NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12) *the Feast of Tabernacles (15th\-21st)*; [LEV 23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:36) *Solemn Assembly (22nd)*

The eighth month: Bul
---------------------

**[1KI 6:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:38)** *Bul's post\-exilic equivalent, Marcheshvan, is not mentioned in the Bible. Modern equivalent: October\-November.* *See also* [1KI 12:32–12:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:32-1Kgs12:33) *Jereboam offset Israel's religious calendar by one month. This is the alternative Feast of Tabernacles (15th)*; [ZEC 1:1](https://ref.ly/Zech1:1)

The ninth month: Kislev
-----------------------

**[ZEC 7:1](https://ref.ly/Zech7:1)** *Modern equivalent: November\-December.* *See also* [NEH 1:1](https://ref.ly/Neh1:1); [HAG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hag2:18)

### Hanukkah celebrated in the month of Kislev

[JHN 10:22](https://ref.ly/John10:22) *the Feast of Dedication of the Temple (25th)*

The tenth month: Tebeth
-----------------------

[EST 2:16](https://ref.ly/Esth2:16) *modern equivalent: December\-January*; ; [JER 39:1](https://ref.ly/Jer39:1)

The eleventh month: Shebat
--------------------------

[DEU 1:3](https://ref.ly/Deut1:3) *modern equivalent: January\-February*; ; [ZEC 1:7](https://ref.ly/Zech1:7)

The twelfth month: Adar
-----------------------

**[EST 8:12](https://ref.ly/Esth8:12)** *Modern equivalent: February\-March.* *See also* [EST 3:13](https://ref.ly/Esth3:13), [EST 9:1](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1)

### Purim celebrated in the month of Adar

[EST 9:20–9:22](https://ref.ly/Esth9:20-Esth9:22) *the Feast of Purim (14th\-15th)*

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 28:11-14; Psalms 81:3; Isaiah 66:23; Ezekiel 46:6; Amos 8:5; Deuteronomy 21:13; Numbers 20:29; Deuteronomy 34:8; Exodus 12:2; Exodus 40:2; Numbers 9:1; Deuteronomy 16:1; Exodus 13:4; Exodus 23:15; Leviticus 23:5-6; Numbers 28:16-17; Leviticus 23:10-11; Nehemiah 2:1; Esther 3:7; 1 Kings 6:1; 1 Kings 6:37; Numbers 9:11; 2 Chronicles 30:13-15; Esther 8:9; Exodus 19:1; Ezekiel 31:1; Leviticus 23:15-16; Ezekiel 1:1; Ezekiel 8:14; Numbers 33:38; 2 Kings 25:8; Nehemiah 6:15; Ezekiel 8:1; Haggai 1:1; Luke 1:26; 1 Kings 8:2; Leviticus 23:23-24; Numbers 29:1; Leviticus 23:27; Numbers 29:7; Leviticus 23:34; Numbers 29:12; Leviticus 23:36; 1 Kings 6:38; 1 Kings 12:32-33; Zechariah 1:1; Zechariah 7:1; Nehemiah 1:1; Haggai 2:18; John 10:22; Esther 2:16; Jeremiah 39:1; Deuteronomy 1:3; Zechariah 1:7; Esther 8:12; Esther 3:13; Esther 9:1; Esther 9:20-22

## 4954: morning (id: 4954)

The period of time roughly between dawn and noon. Morning was an assigned time for worship, sacrifice and prayer. The morning light uncovers events of the night and provides opportunity for renewed daily activity. God's blessings are received new every morning, and the start of a new day symbolises renewal and hope.

Morning as the start of a new day
---------------------------------

### The period of time created by God

**[GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5)** *See also* [GEN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Gen1:8); [EXO 18:13–18:14](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13-Exod18:14) *The working day is reckoned from morning till evening*; [EXO 27:21](https://ref.ly/Exod27:21) *The night is reckoned from evening till morning*; [JOB 38:12](https://ref.ly/Job38:12)

### The transience of morning mist

[ISA 44:22](https://ref.ly/Isa44:22); ; [HOS 6:4](https://ref.ly/Hos6:4), [HOS 13:3](https://ref.ly/Hos13:3)

### Morning as a symbol of renewed hope

**[PSA 30:5](https://ref.ly/Ps30:5)** *See also* [PSA 130:6](https://ref.ly/Ps130:6); [ISA 21:11–21:12](https://ref.ly/Isa21:11-Isa21:12), [ISA 26:19](https://ref.ly/Isa26:19)

### The Messiah brings a new morning

**[MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2)**; **[2PE 1:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:19)** *See also* [LUK 1:78–1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78-Luke1:79); [ROM 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12); [1JN 2:8](https://ref.ly/1John2:8); [REV 2:28](https://ref.ly/Rev2:28), [REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Rev22:16)

### The sky in the morning

[MAT 16:3](https://ref.ly/Matt16:3)

Morning as an opportunity for activity
--------------------------------------

### A time for travel

[GEN 19:2](https://ref.ly/Gen19:2), [GEN 22:3](https://ref.ly/Gen22:3), [GEN 31:55](https://ref.ly/Gen31:55); ; [NUM 9:21](https://ref.ly/Num9:21), [NUM 22:21](https://ref.ly/Num22:21); ; [JOS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Josh3:1); ; [1SA 17:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:20)

### A time for battle

[JOS 8:10–8:14](https://ref.ly/Josh8:10-Josh8:14); ; [JDG 9:33](https://ref.ly/Judg9:33); ; [2KI 3:21–3:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:21-2Kgs3:23); ; [2CH 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20)

### A time to begin daily labour

**[RUT 2:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:7)** *See also* [ECC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:6); [MAT 20:1](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1)

### A time to begin important activity

**[EXO 9:13](https://ref.ly/Exod9:13), [EXO 34:2–34:4](https://ref.ly/Exod34:2-Exod34:4)** *See also* [EXO 7:15](https://ref.ly/Exod7:15), [EXO 8:20](https://ref.ly/Exod8:20); [1KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26); [ACT 23:12](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12), [ACT 28:23](https://ref.ly/Acts28:23)

### A time to respond to nightly revelation

[GEN 20:8](https://ref.ly/Gen20:8), [GEN 28:18](https://ref.ly/Gen28:18), [GEN 41:8](https://ref.ly/Gen41:8)

### A time to administer justice

[JER 21:12](https://ref.ly/Jer21:12); ; [ZEP 3:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:5)

Morning as a time of revelation
-------------------------------

### A time of discovery

**[GEN 29:25](https://ref.ly/Gen29:25)**; **[2KI 19:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:35)** parallel [ISA 37:36](https://ref.ly/Isa37:36) *See also* [GEN 19:27–19:28](https://ref.ly/Gen19:27-Gen19:28); [JDG 6:28](https://ref.ly/Judg6:28); [1SA 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:4); [ISA 17:14](https://ref.ly/Isa17:14); [1KI 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:21); [2KI 6:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:15); [ACT 12:18](https://ref.ly/Acts12:18); [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)

### Morning brings God's revelation

**[EXO 16:7–16:8](https://ref.ly/Exod16:7-Exod16:8)** *See also* [NUM 16:5](https://ref.ly/Num16:5); [JOS 7:14](https://ref.ly/Josh7:14); [1SA 9:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:19); [ISA 50:4](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4); [EZK 12:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:8)

Morning devotions
-----------------

### A time for offering sacrifices

**[EZK 46:13–46:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:13-Ezek46:15)** *See also* [EXO 29:38–29:40](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:40), [EXO 36:3](https://ref.ly/Exod36:3); [NUM 28:3–28:5](https://ref.ly/Num28:3-Num28:5); [1CH 16:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:40); [2CH 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:4), [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11), [2CH 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:20-2Chr29:21); [JOB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Job1:5)

### A time for prayer

**[PSA 88:13](https://ref.ly/Ps88:13)** *See also* [PSA 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ps5:3), [PSA 55:17](https://ref.ly/Ps55:17); [MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35)

### A time to praise God

**[PSA 92:1–92:3](https://ref.ly/Ps92:1-Ps92:3)** *See also* [1CH 23:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:30); [PSA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16), [PSA 65:8](https://ref.ly/Ps65:8)

### Priestly duties each morning

[EXO 30:7](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7) *burning incense*; ; [LEV 6:12](https://ref.ly/Lev6:12) *making the burnt offering*

God's blessings are received new each morning
---------------------------------------------

**[LAM 3:22–3:23](https://ref.ly/Lam3:22-Lam3:23)** *See also* [EXO 16:12–16:15](https://ref.ly/Exod16:12-Exod16:15) *God's provision of manna in the desert*; [1KI 17:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:6) *God supplies Elijah with food*; [PSA 90:14](https://ref.ly/Ps90:14); [ISA 33:2](https://ref.ly/Isa33:2)

## 4957: night (id: 4957)

The period of darkness between evening and morning. It is generally a time for the cessation of daily activity and for sleep, but, because darkness also gives an opportunity for evil, there is need to be watchful. The term may also be used figuratively to refer to evil or to a period of distress. Night\-time also provides opportunity for contemplation and for prayer.

Night distinguished from day
----------------------------

**[GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5)** *See also* [GEN 1:14–1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14-Gen1:18); [PSA 74:16](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16)

Night as a time for rest and sleep
----------------------------------

### Rest from daily activity

[JDG 19:9](https://ref.ly/Judg19:9); ; [ECC 2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:23); ; [JHN 9:4](https://ref.ly/John9:4), [JHN 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/John11:9-John11:10)

### Sleep

[GEN 15:12](https://ref.ly/Gen15:12), [GEN 28:11](https://ref.ly/Gen28:11); ; [JOB 4:13](https://ref.ly/Job4:13)

Activity day and night implies continuity and commitment
--------------------------------------------------------

### Weeping

[PSA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Ps42:3); ; [JER 9:1](https://ref.ly/Jer9:1), [JER 14:17](https://ref.ly/Jer14:17)

### Prayer

[NEH 1:6](https://ref.ly/Neh1:6); ; [PSA 88:1](https://ref.ly/Ps88:1); ; [LUK 18:7](https://ref.ly/Luke18:7); ; [1TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:10)

### Meditation

[JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8); ; [PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2)

### Worship

[LUK 2:37](https://ref.ly/Luke2:37); ; [ACT 26:7](https://ref.ly/Acts26:7); ; [REV 4:8](https://ref.ly/Rev4:8), [REV 7:15](https://ref.ly/Rev7:15)

### Work

[1CH 9:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:33); ; [1TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9); ; [2TH 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:8)

The watches of the night
------------------------

### In OT times: three watches

[LAM 2:19](https://ref.ly/Lam2:19) *the first watch: sunset to midnight*; ; [JDG 7:19](https://ref.ly/Judg7:19) *the second watch: midnight to 3 a.m*; ; [EXO 14:24](https://ref.ly/Exod14:24) *the last watch: 3 a.m. to dawn*

### In NT times: four watches

**[MRK 13:35](https://ref.ly/Mark13:35)** *See also* [MAT 14:25](https://ref.ly/Matt14:25) parallel [MRK 6:48](https://ref.ly/Mark6:48); [LUK 12:38](https://ref.ly/Luke12:38)

Night associated with evil
--------------------------

### The period when evil is at work

**[JHN 13:30](https://ref.ly/John13:30)** *Night symbolises the darkness in Judas' soul, and denotes, too, the time of activity of the powers of darkness.* *See also* [JOB 30:26](https://ref.ly/Job30:26); [PRO 4:19](https://ref.ly/Prov4:19); [LUK 22:53](https://ref.ly/Luke22:53)

### Opportunity for evil

**[1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)** *Evil flourishes under cover of darkness.* *See also* [PRO 2:13](https://ref.ly/Prov2:13); [JHN 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:19-John3:20); [JOB 24:14](https://ref.ly/Job24:14) *Murder*; [PRO 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Prov7:8-Prov7:9) *Adultery*; [1TH 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:7) *Drunkenness*; [JER 49:9](https://ref.ly/Jer49:9) parallel [OBA 1:5](https://ref.ly/Obad1:5) *Theft*

### Believers do not belong to the night

**[1TH 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:5)** *See also* [JHN 12:35–12:36](https://ref.ly/John12:35-John12:36); [ROM 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12); [EPH 5:8](https://ref.ly/Eph5:8); [COL 1:13](https://ref.ly/Col1:13)

### Believers are to remain watchful

**[1TH 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:6)** *See also* [MAT 24:42–24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:42-Matt24:44), [MAT 25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:13); [MRK 13:36–13:37](https://ref.ly/Mark13:36-Mark13:37)

Night associated with distress
------------------------------

### Gloom caused by distress

[PSA 107:13–107:14](https://ref.ly/Ps107:13-Ps107:14); ; [ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2), [ISA 59:9](https://ref.ly/Isa59:9)

### Night as a time of weeping

[PSA 6:6](https://ref.ly/Ps6:6), [PSA 30:5](https://ref.ly/Ps30:5); ; [LAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Lam1:2)

Night as an opportunity to seek God
-----------------------------------

### In prayer

**[LUK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12)** *See also* [1SA 15:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:11); [PSA 22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:2), [PSA 119:62](https://ref.ly/Ps119:62)

### In worship

**[ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25)** *See also* [JOB 35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:10); [PSA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Ps42:8), [PSA 77:6](https://ref.ly/Ps77:6), [PSA 139:1–139:2](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:2), [PSA 149:5](https://ref.ly/Ps149:5); [ISA 30:29](https://ref.ly/Isa30:29)

### In contemplation

**[PSA 63:6](https://ref.ly/Ps63:6)** *See also* [PSA 119:55](https://ref.ly/Ps119:55); [ISA 26:9](https://ref.ly/Isa26:9)

Night as an opportunity to receive revelation
---------------------------------------------

### God's voice at night

[1SA 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:4)

### God's revelation through visions

[2CH 7:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:12); ; [DAN 2:19](https://ref.ly/Dan2:19), [DAN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Dan7:2); ; [ZEC 1:8](https://ref.ly/Zech1:8); ; [ACT 18:9](https://ref.ly/Acts18:9), [ACT 23:11](https://ref.ly/Acts23:11)

### God's revelation through dreams

[GEN 20:3](https://ref.ly/Gen20:3), [GEN 31:24](https://ref.ly/Gen31:24); ; [1KI 3:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:5); ; [JOB 33:15](https://ref.ly/Job33:15)

No night in heaven
------------------

[REV 21:25](https://ref.ly/Rev21:25), [REV 22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:5)

## 4960: noon (id: 4960)

The mid\-point between sunrise and sunset when the sun is at its brightest. Noon is a time to seek shelter from the heat of the day. The darkening of the midday sun and the surprise attack of the enemy at noon are signs of God's judgment. The brilliance of the noonday sun is likened to the glory of Jesus Christ and to the vindication of God's people.

Noon as a time of severe heat
-----------------------------

### Shelter from the noonday heat

**[ISA 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Isa4:5-Isa4:6)** *See also* [ISA 16:3](https://ref.ly/Isa16:3) *Moab likens asylum sought from Judah to shelter from the noonday sun*; [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4)

### Time to rest from the sun's heat

**[2SA 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:5)** *See also* [GEN 18:1](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1)**[SNG 1:7](https://ref.ly/Song1:7)** *See also* [RUT 2:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:7) *Ruth rests from her gleaning at noon*; [1SA 11:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:11) *Saul's army halts its destruction of the Ammonites at midday*; [1KI 20:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:16) *Israel's enemies relax at midday, unaware of the imminence of battle*; [MAT 20:12](https://ref.ly/Matt20:12) *Workers in the vineyard might have been expected to rest during the hottest part of the day*; [JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6) *Jesus Christ rests from his journey by the well of Sychar.*

Noon as a natural break in the day
----------------------------------

[1KI 18:26–18:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26-1Kgs18:29); ; [NEH 8:3](https://ref.ly/Neh8:3)

Noon as a time for prayer
-------------------------

**[PSA 55:16–55:17](https://ref.ly/Ps55:16-Ps55:17)** *The devout Jew prayed three times daily.* *See also* [DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10); [ACT 10:9](https://ref.ly/Acts10:9)

Noon as a time of God's judgment
--------------------------------

### A time of unexpected destruction

**[JER 15:8](https://ref.ly/Jer15:8)** *Armies usually attacked at dawn; a noon raid would take the people by surprise.* *See also* [JER 6:4](https://ref.ly/Jer6:4), [JER 20:16](https://ref.ly/Jer20:16); [ZEP 2:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:4)

### Darkness at noon is a sign of God's judgment

**[JOB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Job5:14)**; **[AMO 8:9](https://ref.ly/Amos8:9)** *See also* [DEU 28:29](https://ref.ly/Deut28:29); [ISA 59:9–59:10](https://ref.ly/Isa59:9-Isa59:10)**[MAT 27:45](https://ref.ly/Matt27:45)** parallels [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33), [LUK 23:44–23:45](https://ref.ly/Luke23:44-Luke23:45) *The noonday sun is darkened following the crucifixion.*

The noonday sun as a symbol of glory
------------------------------------

### The glory of Jesus Christ

**[REV 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rev1:16)** *See also* [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2)

### The vindication of God's people

**[JDG 5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:31)** *See also* [JOB 11:17](https://ref.ly/Job11:17); [PSA 37:6](https://ref.ly/Ps37:6); [ISA 58:10](https://ref.ly/Isa58:10); [MAT 13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:43)

Events that occurred at noon
----------------------------

Joseph invites his brothers to eat with him at noon: [GEN 43:16](https://ref.ly/Gen43:16), [GEN 43:26](https://ref.ly/Gen43:26); [JOS 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Josh10:12-Josh10:13) *The sun stops in the middle of the sky, allowing Joshua to defeat the Amorites*; ; [2KI 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:18-2Kgs4:20) *The Shunammite woman's son dies at noon.*; [ACT 22:6](https://ref.ly/Acts22:6), [ACT 26:13](https://ref.ly/Acts26:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 4:5-6; Isaiah 16:3; Isaiah 25:4; 2 Samuel 4:5; Genesis 18:1; Song of Songs 1:7; Ruth 2:7; 1 Samuel 11:11; 1 Kings 20:16; Matthew 20:12; John 4:6; 1 Kings 18:26-29; Nehemiah 8:3; Psalms 55:16-17; Daniel 6:10; Acts 10:9; Jeremiah 15:8; Jeremiah 6:4; Jeremiah 20:16; Zephaniah 2:4; Job 5:14; Amos 8:9; Deuteronomy 28:29; Isaiah 59:9-10; Matthew 27:45; Mark 15:33; Luke 23:44-45; Revelation 1:16; Matthew 17:2; Judges 5:31; Job 11:17; Psalms 37:6; Isaiah 58:10; Matthew 13:43; Genesis 43:16; Genesis 43:26; Joshua 10:12-13; 2 Kings 4:18-20; Acts 22:6; Acts 26:13
* **Associated Articles:** 1193: glory, revelation of (ID: `1193@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4284: sun (ID: `4284@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4829: heat (ID: `4829@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4918: dawn (ID: `4918@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4921: day (ID: `4921@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4954: morning (ID: `4954@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5056: rest (ID: `5056@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5295: destruction (ID: `5295@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8602: prayer (ID: `8602@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4963: past, the (id: 4963)

Jesus Christ transforms the past and sets people free to live in a new way. God's people should remember former blessings but not return to former sins.

Recalling God's work in the past
--------------------------------

### Remembering God's work of creation

[EXO 20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:11); ; [PSA 104:5–104:9](https://ref.ly/Ps104:5-Ps104:9), [PSA 136:1–136:9](https://ref.ly/Ps136:1-Ps136:9)

### Remembering God's saving works

**[PSA 105:5](https://ref.ly/Ps105:5)** parallel [1CH 16:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:12) *See also* [DEU 8:2](https://ref.ly/Deut8:2); [JOS 24:2](https://ref.ly/Josh24:2); [1SA 12:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:6); [NEH 9:7](https://ref.ly/Neh9:7); [PSA 106:6](https://ref.ly/Ps106:6), [PSA 114:1](https://ref.ly/Ps114:1), [PSA 136:10](https://ref.ly/Ps136:10), [PSA 143:5](https://ref.ly/Ps143:5); [ISA 51:1–51:2](https://ref.ly/Isa51:1-Isa51:2); [MIC 6:5](https://ref.ly/Mic6:5)

### Remembering God's work in Jesus Christ

**[2TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:8)** *See also* [1CO 11:23–11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:26) *One of the purposes of the Lord's Supper is to remember Jesus Christ and his death on the cross.*

Learning from the past
----------------------

**[ROM 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rom15:4)** *See also* [DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9), [DEU 32:7](https://ref.ly/Deut32:7); [ZEC 1:4](https://ref.ly/Zech1:4); [1CO 10:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:6), [1CO 10:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:11); [HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16); [1PE 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:5-1Pet3:6); [1JN 3:12](https://ref.ly/1John3:12)

Rediscovering God's word from the past
--------------------------------------

**[2KI 22:8–22:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:8-2Kgs22:13)** parallel [2CH 34:15–34:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:15-2Chr34:21) *See also* [2KI 23:1–23:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:1-2Kgs23:3) parallel [2CH 34:29–34:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:29-2Chr34:31); [2KI 23:21–23:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:21-2Kgs23:23)

Leaving the past behind
-----------------------

### Jesus Christ sets believers free from the past

**[2CO 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:17)** *See also* [ISA 65:16](https://ref.ly/Isa65:16); [GAL 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gal4:3-Gal4:5); [COL 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Col1:13-Col1:14)

### Warnings not to rely on or be preoccupied with the past

**[ISA 43:18](https://ref.ly/Isa43:18)** *This can mean either that God is not to be restricted to his past actions or that Israel should forget their former sins.* *See also* [PHP 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13-Phil3:14)

Dangers of the past
-------------------

### The danger of ignoring the past

**[PSA 78:11](https://ref.ly/Ps78:11)** *See also* [EXO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Exod1:8); [DEU 32:18](https://ref.ly/Deut32:18); [PSA 106:13](https://ref.ly/Ps106:13), [PSA 106:21](https://ref.ly/Ps106:21); [HOS 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:13)

### The danger of lapsing into past sins

**[GAL 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Gal4:8-Gal4:9)** *See also* [HEB 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:6); [2PE 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:9); [REV 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Rev2:4-Rev2:5), [REV 3:3](https://ref.ly/Rev3:3)

Jesus Christ supersedes God's revelation in the past
----------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ supersedes earlier expectations

**[ACT 17:30](https://ref.ly/Acts17:30)** *See also* [ACT 14:15–14:16](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15-Acts14:16)

### Jesus Christ supersedes earlier covenants

**[HEB 7:22](https://ref.ly/Heb7:22)** *See also* [HEB 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:2), [HEB 8:13](https://ref.ly/Heb8:13), [HEB 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:14-Heb9:15), [HEB 10:15–10:18](https://ref.ly/Heb10:15-Heb10:18); [JER 31:33–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33-Jer31:34); [HEB 12:24](https://ref.ly/Heb12:24)

### Jesus Christ reveals God's past mystery

**[COL 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Col1:26-Col1:27)** *See also* [ROM 16:25–16:26](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25-Rom16:26); [1CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:7); [EPH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9), [EPH 3:2–3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:2-Eph3:6), [EPH 3:9](https://ref.ly/Eph3:9)

## 4966: present, the (id: 4966)

God's blessings are available now and are to be received in faith and contentment. It is dangerous to live only for current blessings and pleasures.

Blessings promised for the present
----------------------------------

### The blessing of God's presence now

**[HAG 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hag1:13)** *See also* [DEU 31:6](https://ref.ly/Deut31:6); [1KI 8:57](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:57)

### The blessing of Jesus Christ's presence now

**[MAT 1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:23)** *See also* [ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14), [ISA 8:8](https://ref.ly/Isa8:8), [ISA 8:10](https://ref.ly/Isa8:10); [MAT 18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:20), [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); [JHN 14:18](https://ref.ly/John14:18); [ACT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:10)

### The blessing of the Spirit's presence now

**[JHN 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:16-John14:17)** *See also* [JHN 14:25–14:27](https://ref.ly/John14:25-John14:27), [JHN 16:7](https://ref.ly/John16:7), [JHN 16:13–16:16](https://ref.ly/John16:13-John16:16)

### Other blessings given in the present

**[MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30)** parallels [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29), [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30) *See also* [EXO 23:25](https://ref.ly/Exod23:25); [1KI 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:13) parallel [2CH 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:12); [PSA 121:3–121:8](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:8); [MAT 6:33](https://ref.ly/Matt6:33); [1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:8); [1PE 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:9)

Future blessings are partially fulfilled in the present
-------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14)** *See also* [ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23), [ROM 8:30](https://ref.ly/Rom8:30); [2CO 1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:22), [2CO 5:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:5); [EPH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Eph1:3); [HEB 12:22–12:24](https://ref.ly/Heb12:22-Heb12:24)

Prayer for present provision and blessing
-----------------------------------------

**[LUK 11:3](https://ref.ly/Luke11:3)** parallel [MAT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Matt6:11) [GEN 28:20](https://ref.ly/Gen28:20); ; [PRO 30:8](https://ref.ly/Prov30:8); [MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13) parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4) *prayers for spiritual provision*

The secret of contentment in the present
----------------------------------------

**[PHP 4:10–4:13](https://ref.ly/Phil4:10-Phil4:13)** *See also* [1TI 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:6-1Tim6:8)

The dangers of living for the present
-------------------------------------

**[ECC 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:12-Eccl3:13)** *Ecclesiastes in particular reflects upon the emptiness of life without God. Though the good things of the present life are a gift from God and to be valued as such, they cannot bring ultimate fulfilment*; **[LUK 12:15–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15-Luke12:20)** *See also* [ECC 2:24](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24), [ECC 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:18), [ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15), [ECC 9:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:7), [ECC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:9); [ISA 22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:13); [1CO 15:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:32); [PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19); [1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10); [JAS 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:17); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

The challenge of the present
----------------------------

### The challenge to respond to God now

**[2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2)** *See also* [DEU 4:39–4:40](https://ref.ly/Deut4:39-Deut4:40), [DEU 5:1](https://ref.ly/Deut5:1), [DEU 7:11](https://ref.ly/Deut7:11), [DEU 10:13](https://ref.ly/Deut10:13), [DEU 30:15–30:16](https://ref.ly/Deut30:15-Deut30:16); [PSA 95:7–95:8](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:8); [ISA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6); [HOS 10:12](https://ref.ly/Hos10:12); [HEB 3:7](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7)

### The challenge to serve God now

**[EPH 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Eph5:15-Eph5:16)** *See also* [GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10); [COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5)

## 4969: seasons (id: 4969)

The main periods of the year which each have their own distinctive weather. In life, the varied periods of human experience.

* **Associated Articles:** 4970: seasons, of the year (ID: `4970@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4971: seasons, of life (ID: `4971@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 4970: seasons, of the year (id: 4970)

Distinctive periods of the year marked by certain climatic conditions. Seasons are examples of God's perfect order in the world. They represent the balanced cycle of creation which is sustained by God and through which his provision and mercy are made known.

Seasons are appointed by God
----------------------------

### Seasons are created by God

**[PSA 74:16–74:17](https://ref.ly/Ps74:16-Ps74:17)** *See also* [GEN 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14), [GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22); [PSA 104:19](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19)

### Seasons are sustained by God

**[JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24)** *See also* [JOB 38:32–38:33](https://ref.ly/Job38:32-Job38:33); [EZK 34:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:26); [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)

The agricultural seasons of Palestine
-------------------------------------

### The dry and rainy seasons

**[JOL 2:23](https://ref.ly/Joel2:23)** *The rainy season, accounting for over 90 per cent of the national rainfall, runs from October to March/April. Its beginning is marked by the autumn rains in October/November; the lighter spring showers in April/May announce the coming of the dry season.* *See also* [EZR 10:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:13); [HOS 6:3](https://ref.ly/Hos6:3); [JAS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7)LEV [26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Jas26:3-Jas26:4); [DEU 11:13–11:14](https://ref.ly/Deut11:13-Deut11:14), [DEU 28:12](https://ref.ly/Deut28:12)

### Seedtime and harvest

**[PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4)** *Ploughing and sowing take place from October to December when the autumn rains have begun to soften the ground. Most crops are harvested between April and June.* *See also* [EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21); [NUM 13:20](https://ref.ly/Num13:20); [JOB 5:26](https://ref.ly/Job5:26); [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3)

Spring
------

### Spring rain as a sign of blessing

[JOB 29:23](https://ref.ly/Job29:23) *The gentle spring rains signal the approach of harvest*; ; [PRO 16:15](https://ref.ly/Prov16:15); ; [JER 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jer3:3); ; [ZEC 10:1](https://ref.ly/Zech10:1)

### Spring as a time of growth

**[SNG 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Song2:11-Song2:13)** *Spring marks a pleasant interval between the heavy winter rains and the onset of the dry season.* *See also* [PRO 27:25](https://ref.ly/Prov27:25); [JOL 2:22](https://ref.ly/Joel2:22); [MAT 24:32](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32) parallels [MRK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28), [LUK 21:29–21:30](https://ref.ly/Luke21:29-Luke21:30)

### Spring as a time for war

[2SA 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:1) parallel [1CH 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:1) *The time following the spring harvest (May/June), when agricultural commitments are reduced*; ; [1KI 20:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:26); ; [2KI 13:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:20)

### Festivals celebrated in spring

[LEV 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5-Lev23:6) parallel [NUM 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Num28:16-Num28:17) *Passover and Unleavened Bread*; ; [LEV 23:10](https://ref.ly/Lev23:10) *offering the firstfruits of the barley harvest*; ; [LEV 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:16) parallel [DEU 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:10) *The Feast of Weeks celebrated the ingathering of the grain harvest.*

Summer
------

### A period of intense heat

**[PSA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:4)** *See also* [JOB 6:17](https://ref.ly/Job6:17)

### A time for harvesting summer fruit

**[JER 8:20](https://ref.ly/Jer8:20)** *See also* [PRO 10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:5); [JER 40:10–40:12](https://ref.ly/Jer40:10-Jer40:12) *Grapes, olives and figs are harvested in July/September*; [MIC 7:1](https://ref.ly/Mic7:1)

### A time to store harvested grain

[PRO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Prov6:8), [PRO 30:25](https://ref.ly/Prov30:25)

Autumn
------

### The breaking of the summer drought

**[PSA 84:6](https://ref.ly/Ps84:6)** *Autumn is the start of the rainy season and corresponds to the time of ploughing and sowing.*

### Festivals celebrated in autumn

*See also* [LEV 23:24](https://ref.ly/Lev23:24) parallel [NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1) *the Feast of Trumpets*; [LEV 23:27](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27) parallel [NUM 29:7](https://ref.ly/Num29:7) *the Day of Atonement*; [LEV 23:34](https://ref.ly/Lev23:34) parallel [NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12) *the Feast of Tabernacles*

Winter
------

### A time of inclement weather

**[JOB 37:6–37:10](https://ref.ly/Job37:6-Job37:10)** *See also* [ISA 55:10](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10); [JER 36:22](https://ref.ly/Jer36:22)

### A time when travelling is difficult

[MAT 24:20](https://ref.ly/Matt24:20) parallel [MRK 13:18](https://ref.ly/Mark13:18); ; [2TI 4:21](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:21); ; [ACT 27:12](https://ref.ly/Acts27:12); ; [1CO 16:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:6); ; [TIT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Titus3:12)

### Celebration of the Feast of Dedication

[JHN 10:22](https://ref.ly/John10:22) *The Feast of Dedication (Hannukah) commemorates the rededication of the temple by Judas Maccabeus in 165 B.C.*

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 74:16-17; Genesis 1:14; Genesis 8:22; Psalms 104:19; Jeremiah 5:24; Job 38:32-33; Ezekiel 34:26; Acts 14:17; Joel 2:23; Ezra 10:13; Hosea 6:3; James 5:7; James 26:3-4; Deuteronomy 11:13-14; Deuteronomy 28:12; Proverbs 20:4; Exodus 34:21; Numbers 13:20; Job 5:26; Psalms 1:3; Job 29:23; Proverbs 16:15; Jeremiah 3:3; Zechariah 10:1; Song of Songs 2:11-13; Proverbs 27:25; Joel 2:22; Matthew 24:32; Mark 13:28; Luke 21:29-30; 2 Samuel 11:1; 1 Chronicles 20:1; 1 Kings 20:26; 2 Kings 13:20; Leviticus 23:5-6; Numbers 28:16-17; Leviticus 23:10; Leviticus 23:15-16; Deuteronomy 16:9-10; Psalms 32:4; Job 6:17; Jeremiah 8:20; Proverbs 10:5; Jeremiah 40:10-12; Micah 7:1; Proverbs 6:8; Proverbs 30:25; Psalms 84:6; Leviticus 23:24; Numbers 29:1; Leviticus 23:27; Numbers 29:7; Leviticus 23:34; Numbers 29:12; Job 37:6-10; Isaiah 55:10; Jeremiah 36:22; Matthew 24:20; Mark 13:18; 2 Timothy 4:21; Acts 27:12; 1 Corinthians 16:6; Titus 3:12; John 10:22

## 4971: seasons, of life (id: 4971)

Appropriate or appointed periods of time which are part of the variety and development of human life and experience and which influence human affairs. The times and seasons for individuals and nations are set by God, who works all things together towards the final fulfilment of his purposes.

The seasons of life are appointed by God
----------------------------------------

### The seasons of individual lives

**[ECC 3:1–3:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:1-Eccl3:8)**; **[PSA 31:15](https://ref.ly/Ps31:15)** *See also* [JOB 14:5](https://ref.ly/Job14:5); [PSA 139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:16); [ECC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:11); [ROM 8:28](https://ref.ly/Rom8:28); [JAS 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:15)

### The seasons of nations and the world

**[DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21)**; **[ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)** *See also* [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17), [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25); [ACT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Acts1:7)

A time to die
-------------

### The transience of human life

**[PSA 103:15–103:16](https://ref.ly/Ps103:15-Ps103:16)** *See also* [JOB 7:6–7:8](https://ref.ly/Job7:6-Job7:8), [JOB 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Job14:1-Job14:2); [PSA 37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:2), [PSA 102:11](https://ref.ly/Ps102:11); [ECC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:12)

### Death comes at its appointed time

**[JOB 5:26](https://ref.ly/Job5:26)** *See also* [GEN 47:29](https://ref.ly/Gen47:29); [1KI 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:1); [LUK 12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20)

Times of sadness and joy
------------------------

### A time to mourn

[GEN 27:41](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41), [GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34), [GEN 50:3–50:4](https://ref.ly/Gen50:3-Gen50:4); ; [DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8); ; [JER 4:28](https://ref.ly/Jer4:28)

### A time to rejoice

**[JHN 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/John16:21-John16:22)** *See also* [DEU 28:47](https://ref.ly/Deut28:47); [EST 9:22](https://ref.ly/Esth9:22); [ISA 61:2–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:2-Isa61:3)

A time to speak
---------------

[PRO 15:23](https://ref.ly/Prov15:23), [PRO 25:11](https://ref.ly/Prov25:11); ; [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2) *Christians are to be always ready to share the gospel.*

Times appointed for the world
-----------------------------

### The rise and fall of nations

[DAN 2:37–2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37-Dan2:45), [DAN 8:19–8:25](https://ref.ly/Dan8:19-Dan8:25), [DAN 11:2–11:4](https://ref.ly/Dan11:2-Dan11:4), [DAN 11:24–11:27](https://ref.ly/Dan11:24-Dan11:27); ; [LUK 21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:24)

### The time to receive God's salvation

**[2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2)** *See also* [ISA 49:8](https://ref.ly/Isa49:8), [ISA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6); [HEB 4:7](https://ref.ly/Heb4:7)

### The time of God's judgment

[DEU 32:35](https://ref.ly/Deut32:35); ; [EZK 30:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:3); ; [ZEP 2:2](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:2); ; [ACT 17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:31)

Times appointed for God's people
--------------------------------

### Times for worship

**[LEV 23:4](https://ref.ly/Lev23:4)** *See also* [EXO 34:18](https://ref.ly/Exod34:18); [NUM 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Num9:2-Num9:3), [NUM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Num10:10)

### Times of blessing

**[PSA 102:13](https://ref.ly/Ps102:13)** *See also* [EZK 34:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:26); [ACT 3:19](https://ref.ly/Acts3:19), [ACT 7:17](https://ref.ly/Acts7:17)

### A time of vindication

**[DAN 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:21-Dan7:22)** *See also* [ACT 1:6](https://ref.ly/Acts1:6); [ROM 8:18](https://ref.ly/Rom8:18); [1PE 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:6)

### Times of testing

[PSA 66:10](https://ref.ly/Ps66:10); ; [EZK 21:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:3); ; [MAT 13:21](https://ref.ly/Matt13:21) parallels [MRK 4:17](https://ref.ly/Mark4:17), [LUK 8:13](https://ref.ly/Luke8:13); ; [1PE 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6-1Pet1:7); ; [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

### Times for withdrawal or solitude

**[MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35)** *See also* [1KI 17:2–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:2-1Kgs17:6); [MRK 6:30–6:32](https://ref.ly/Mark6:30-Mark6:32); [GAL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Gal1:17)

### Waiting for God's appointed time

**[HAB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3)** *See also* [ROM 8:23–8:25](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23-Rom8:25); [HEB 9:28](https://ref.ly/Heb9:28), [HEB 11:13](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13)

Times appointed for Jesus Christ
--------------------------------

### The appointed time of his birth

[GAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4)

### The appointed time of his death

**[JHN 12:23](https://ref.ly/John12:23)** *See also* [MAT 26:18](https://ref.ly/Matt26:18); [MRK 14:41](https://ref.ly/Mark14:41); [LUK 9:51](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51); [JHN 2:4](https://ref.ly/John2:4), [JHN 7:6–7:8](https://ref.ly/John7:6-John7:8), [JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/John12:27), [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/John13:1)

### The appointed time of his return

[LUK 21:8](https://ref.ly/Luke21:8); ; [ACT 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Acts3:20-Acts3:21), [ACT 17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:31); ; [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5); ; [REV 1:3](https://ref.ly/Rev1:3)

## 4975: week (id: 4975)

The pattern of weeks provides a useful framework for marking time in agricultural and religious life. The story of creation is described in terms of seven days and illustrates a cycle of work and rest. Feasts and other rituals last for a certain number of weeks.

The seven days of creation
--------------------------

**[GEN 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Gen2:1-Gen2:3)** *See also* [GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5), [GEN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Gen1:8), [GEN 1:13](https://ref.ly/Gen1:13), [GEN 1:19](https://ref.ly/Gen1:19), [GEN 1:23](https://ref.ly/Gen1:23), [GEN 1:31](https://ref.ly/Gen1:31)

The Sabbath\-rest
-----------------

**[DEU 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:14)** parallel [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11) *See also* [EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21), [EXO 35:2](https://ref.ly/Exod35:2); [HEB 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Heb4:3-Heb4:4), [HEB 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Heb4:8-Heb4:10)

Seven\-day intervals or periods
-------------------------------

### After building the ark Noah had seven days' notice of the flood

**[GEN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:4)**

### Noah sent out the dove at seven\-day intervals

*See also* [GEN 8:8–8:12](https://ref.ly/Gen8:8-Gen8:12)

### Marching round Jericho

**[HEB 11:30](https://ref.ly/Heb11:30)** *See also* [JOS 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/Josh6:12-Josh6:15)

### Other examples of seven\-day intervals or periods

[EXO 7:25](https://ref.ly/Exod7:25), [EXO 29:30](https://ref.ly/Exod29:30); ; [1SA 10:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:8), [1SA 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:3), [1SA 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:8); ; [1KI 20:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:29); ; [1CH 9:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:25); ; [ACT 20:6](https://ref.ly/Acts20:6), [ACT 21:4](https://ref.ly/Acts21:4), [ACT 28:14](https://ref.ly/Acts28:14)

--------------------------

### Menstrual impurity

**[LEV 15:19](https://ref.ly/Lev15:19)**

### Initial period of uncleanness after childbirth

**[LEV 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:2)** *This would be fourteen days if the child were a girl ([Lev 12:5](https://ref.ly/Lev12:5)).*

### Mourning

**[GEN 50:10](https://ref.ly/Gen50:10)** *See also* [1SA 31:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:13) parallel [1CH 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:12); [JOB 2:13](https://ref.ly/Job2:13); [EZK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:15)

## 4978: year (id: 4978)

A period of twelve months, with additional periods inserted to bring the lunar year into line with the solar year. Certain calendar years have special significance. The term also denotes an indeterminate time when God will intervene to restore his people and punish his enemies.

The year made up of 12 months
-----------------------------

**[1KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:7)** *See also* [1CH 27:1–27:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:1-1Chr27:15); [EST 2:12](https://ref.ly/Esth2:12); [DAN 4:29](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29); [REV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:2)

The start of the year
---------------------

### The start of the religious year

**[EXO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Exod12:2)** *The exodus from Egypt in Abib (March April) marked a new beginning in the life of God's people.* *See also* [LEV 23:5](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5) parallel [NUM 28:16](https://ref.ly/Num28:16) *Abib is referred to as the first month*; [2CH 35:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:1); [EZR 6:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:19)

### The start of the agricultural year

**[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)** *The Feast of Ingathering (or Tabernacles) was celebrated in the seventh month.* *See also* [EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22); [LEV 23:23–23:24](https://ref.ly/Lev23:23-Lev23:24) parallel [NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1)

God's blessing through the year
-------------------------------

**[PSA 65:9–65:13](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9-Ps65:13)** *See also* [DEU 11:11–11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:11-Deut11:15), [DEU 28:12](https://ref.ly/Deut28:12)

A thousand years as one day
---------------------------

[PSA 90:4](https://ref.ly/Ps90:4); ; [2PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:8)

Special years
-------------

### The sabbatical year

**[LEV 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/Lev25:1-Lev25:7)** *See also* [EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11); [LEV 25:20–25:22](https://ref.ly/Lev25:20-Lev25:22) *God's provision for the seventh year***[DEU 15:1](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1)**[DEU 15:9](https://ref.ly/Deut15:9); [NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31); [DEU 31:10–31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:10-Deut31:13) *the law of God to be read every seventh year at the Feast of Tabernacles*

### The Year of Jubilee

**[LEV 25:8–25:10](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:10)** *The Year of Jubilee began in the seventh month.* *See also* [LEV 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/Lev25:11-Lev25:12) *The Year of Jubilee is a sabbath year*; [LEV 25:13](https://ref.ly/Lev25:13) *Each Hebrew is to return to his family property*; [LEV 25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:28) *Land is returned to its original owner*; [LEV 25:40](https://ref.ly/Lev25:40) *Hebrew slaves are to be released*; [LEV 25:54](https://ref.ly/Lev25:54)

### Hebrew slaves released in the seventh year

**[EXO 21:2](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2)** parallel [DEU 15:12](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12) *See also* [JER 34:14](https://ref.ly/Jer34:14)

The year of the LORD's favour
-----------------------------

**[ISA 61:1–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:3)** *This has echoes of the Year of Jubilee and points to the time when God will restore his people.* *See also* [LUK 4:18–4:21](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:21); [ISA 49:8–49:9](https://ref.ly/Isa49:8-Isa49:9); [2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2)**[ISA 63:4](https://ref.ly/Isa63:4)** *Salvation for God's people is accompanied by judgment on his enemies.*

The year of punishment
----------------------

*See also* [JER 11:23](https://ref.ly/Jer11:23), [JER 23:12](https://ref.ly/Jer23:12), [JER 48:44](https://ref.ly/Jer48:44)
